## <X> domain

> set of objects, each of which is related by a characterizing relationship &lt;X&gt; to a controlling object

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-2:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Foundations, 10.3_

---

## <X> federation

> a community of <x> domains

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-3:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Architecture, 5.1.2_

---

## <X> group

> set of objects with a particular characterizing relationship &lt;X&gt;

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-2:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Foundations, 10.1_

---

## <x> interceptor

> engineering object in a channel, placed at a boundary between <x> domains (ISO/IEC 10746- 3:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Architecture, 8.1.11)

---

## <x> pattern

> abstract specification of a composition of objects that results in any instance of the composition having a given property, named by &lt;X&gt;

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-2:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Foundations, 9.8_

> Application profile

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-1:1998 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference model: Overview_

> technique to determine the effectiveness of minor changes in a product or design where &quot;A&quot; represents the original version and &quot;B&quot; represents the modified version

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 26513:2017 Systems and software engineering--Requirements for testers and reviewers of information for users, 3.1_

> autonomous/intelligent system

_Source: IEEE 7010-2020, IEEE Recommended Practice for Assessing the Impact of Autonomous and Intelligent Systems on Human Well-Being, 2.2_

> person or entity that designs, develops, engineers, programs or similarly creates an autonomous/intelligent system (A/IS)

> achieved availability

_Source: IEEE 982:2024, Standard for Measures of the Software Aspects of Dependability, 3.2_

## abend

> abnormal end

---

## ability

> acquired or natural capacity or talent that enables an individual to perform a particular task (INCOSE Systems Engineering Handbook, 5th ed.)

---

## abnormal end (abend)

> termination of a process prior to completion

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## abort

> to terminate a process prior to completion (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary)

---

## absent variant

> variant that is not determined or developed at the specific time

_Source: ISO/IEC 26554:2018 Information technology--Software and systems engineering-Tools and methods for product line testing, 3.1_

---

## absolute address

> address that is permanently assigned to a device or storage location and that identifies the device or location without the need for translation or calculation (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## absolute assembler

> assembler that produces absolute code (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## absolute code

> code in which all addresses are absolute addresses (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## absolute instruction

> computer instruction in which all addresses are absolute addresses (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## absolute loader

> loader that reads absolute machine code into main memory, beginning at the initial address assigned to the code by the assembler or compiler, and performs no address adjustments on the code (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## absolute time

> tick in a generally agreed chronological frame of reference (IEEE 982:2024, Standard for Measures of the Software Aspects of Dependability, 3.1)

---

## abstract behavior

> description, model, or other general characterization of similar behaviors (IEEE 982:2024, Standard for Measures of the Software Aspects of Dependability, 3.1)

---

## abstract class

> class that cannot be instantiated independently (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.1)

---

## abstract data type

> data type for which only the properties of the data and the operations to be performed on the data are specified, without concern for how the data are represented or how the operations are implemented

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## abstract design

> generic form that needs specialization (further design work) to produce concrete designs

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## abstraction

> view of an object that focuses on the information relevant to a particular purpose and ignores the remainder of the information

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765l:2024_

---

## AC

> actual cost (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## acceptability

> exposure to loss (financial or otherwise) that an organization is willing to tolerate from a risk

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## acceptability criteria

> documented set of characteristics of a program's work products that if satisfied, forms a sufficient basis for judging each product's content to be acceptable to support a successful review or audit

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-8:2019, Systems and software engineering Life cycle management Part 7: Application of systems engineering on defense programs, 3.1_

---

## acceptable

> meeting stakeholder expectations that can be shown to be reasonable or merited

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765c:2014_

---

## acceptance

> action by an authorized representative of the acquirer by which the acquirer assumes ownership of products as partial or complete performance of an agreement

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-5:2017 Systems and software engineering--Life cycle management--Part 5: Software development planning, 3.1_

---

## acceptance criteria

> criteria that a system or component is required to satisfy to be accepted by a user, customer, or other authorized entity

_Source: ISO/IEC 33202:2024, Software and systems engineering Core agile practices, 3.1_

---

## acceptance test

> test of a system or functional unit usually performed by the purchaser on his premises after installation with the participation of the vendor to help ensure that the contractual requirements are met (ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary)

---

## acceptance test-driven development (ATDD)

> development method in which team members with various backgrounds [developers, testers and business analysts] jointly write the acceptance tests before development of the relevant functionality (ISO/IEC TR 7052:2023, Software engineering--Controlling frequently occurring risks during development and maintenance of custom software, 3.1)

---

## acceptance testing

> testing conducted to determine whether a system satisfies its acceptance criteria and to enable the customer to determine whether to accept the system

_Source: IEEE 1012-2024 IEEE Standard for System, Software, and Hardware Verification and Validation, 3_

---

## access

> to obtain the use of a resource

_Source: ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary_

---

## access facility

> set of service primitives that allow a stub objects to negotiate the abstract and transfer syntax to be used for the operation data to be transmitted over the channel

_Source: ISO/IEC 14752:2000 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Protocol support for computational interactions, 3.3.1_

---

## access method

> technique to obtain the use of data, the use of storage in order to read or write data, or the use of an input-output channel to transfer data

_Source: ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary_

---

## access routine

> routine that provides access to a data structure that is hidden, usually because it is a global variable or used in an abstract data type.

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## access transparency

> distribution transparency which masks differences in data representation and invocation mechanisms to enable interworking between objects

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-3:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Architecture, 4.4.1.1_

---

## accessibility

> extent to which products, systems, services, environments, and facilities can be used by people from a population with the widest range of user needs, characteristics, and capabilities to achieve identified goals in identified contexts of use (ISO TR 25060:2023 Systems and software engineering--Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)--General Framework for Common Industry Format (CIF) for usability-related information, 3.1.14) (IEC/IEEE 82079-1:2019 Preparation of information for use (instructions for use) of products: Part 1: Principles and general requirements, 3.1)

---

## accessibility testing

> type of usability testing used to measure the degree to which a test item can be operated by users with the widest possible range of characteristics and capabilities

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-1:2022, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 1: General concepts, 3.2_

---

## accident

> unplanned event or series of events that results in death, injury, illness, environmental damage, or damage to or loss of equipment or property (IEEE 1228-1994 (R2002) IEEE Standard for Software Safety Plans, 3.1.1)

---

## accountability

> capability of a product to enable actions of an entity to be traced uniquely to the entity (ISO/IEC 25010:2023, Systems and software engineering--Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)- -Product quality model, 3.6.4)

---

## accuracy

> qualitative assessment of correctness, or freedom from error

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## accuracy of measurement

> closeness of the agreement between the result of a measurement and the true value of the measurand (ISO/IEC TR 14143-3:2003 Information technology -- Software measurement -- Functional size measurement -- Part 3: Verification of functional size measurement methods, 3.1)

---

## ACIA

> asynchronous communication interface adapter

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765d:2015_

---

## ACID

> Atomicity Consistency Isolation Durability

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-1:1998 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference model: Overview_

---

## acknowledged system of systems

> system of systems (SoS) with recognized objectives, a designated manager, and resources for the SoS (ISO/IEC/IEEE 21841:2019 Systems and software engineering--Taxonomy of systems of systems, 3.2.1)

---

## acquirer

> stakeholder that acquires or procures a product or service from a supplier

_Source: ISO/IEC TS 33064:2025, Information technology Process assessment Process assessment model for safety processes, 4.1_

---

## acquisition

> process of obtaining a system, product, or service (ISO/IEC/IEEE 12207:2026 Systems and software engineering--Software life cycle processes, 3.1.2) (ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288:2023 Systems and software engineering--System life cycle processes, 3.2)

---

## acquisition logistics

> technical and management activities conducted to ensure supportability implications, considered early and throughout the acquisition process to minimize support costs and to provide the user with the resources to sustain the system in the field

_Source: INCOSE Systems Engineering Handbook, 5th ed._

---

## acquisition strategy

> specific approach to acquiring products and services that is based on considerations of supply sources, acquisition methods, requirements specification types, contract or agreement types, and related acquisition risks

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## action

> element of a step that a user performs during a procedure

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 26514:2022, Systems and software engineering -- Design and development of information for users, 3.1.3_

---

## action entry

> indication of the relevance of an action to a particular rule

_Source: ISO 5806:1984 Information processing -- Specification of single-hit decision tables, 3.9_

---

## action of interest

> action in a transaction which leads to a state change of significance to the transaction

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-3:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Architecture, 13.7.1.2_

---

## action signature

> specification of an action that comprises the name for the action, the number, names and types of its parameters, and an indication of the causality of the object that instantiates the action template

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746- 2:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Foundations, 9.14_

---

## action stub

> list of all the actions to be taken in the solution of a problem

_Source: ISO 5806:1984 Information processing -- Specification of single-hit decision tables, 3.11_

---

## activation

> one occurrence of a function's transformation of some subset of its inputs into some subset of its outputs

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765m:2024, 2.1.3_

---

## activation constraint

> function's requirement for the presence of a non-empty object set in a particular arrow role as a precondition for some activation of the function

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765m:2024, 2.1.4_

---

## activation function

> &lt;neural network&gt; formula associated with a node in a neural network that determines the output of the node (activation value) from the inputs to the neuron (ISO/IEC TR 29119-11:2020, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 11: Guidelines on the testing of AI-based systems, 3.1.3)

---

## activation value

> output of an activation function of a node in a neural network

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 29119-11:2020, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 11: Guidelines on the testing of AI-based systems, 3.1.4_

---

## active area

> (on-screen documentation) area that responds to user input

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 26514:2022, Systems and software engineering -- Design and development of information for users, 3.1.4_

---

## active enterprise object

> enterprise object that is able to fill an action role

_Source: ISO/IEC 15414:2015 Information technology -- Open distributed processing -- Reference model -- Enterprise language, 6.3.1_

---

## active interconnection

> physical interaction mechanism allowing the action of one thing to cause a change or to stimulate an action in another thing

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765i:2020_

---

## active redundancy

> in fault tolerance, the use of redundant elements operating simultaneously to prevent, or permit recovery from, failures (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## active text

> text displayed on the screen that responds to user input

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## active white space

> area around textual or graphical elements, not including margins, which breaks up text, separates topic and subtopic groupings, indicates hierarchical and topical relationships, highlights information, or makes text easier to read

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765a:2011_

---

## activities

> events in the software life cycle for which effort data is collected and reported.

---

## activity

> set of cohesive tasks of a process (ISO/IEC/IEEE 12207:2026 Systems and software engineering-- Software life cycle processes, 3.1.3) (ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288:2023 Systems and software engineering--System life cycle

---

## activity group

> set of related activities

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-- Vocabulary_

---

## activity list

> a documented tabulation of schedule activities that shows the activity description, activity identifier, and a sufficiently detailed scope of work description so project team members understand what work is to be performed (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## activity type

> classification of activities defined by the execution of the same algorithm

_Source: ISO/IEC 14756:1999 Information technology -- Measurement and rating of performance of computer-based software systems, 4.2_

---

## activity-based costing (ABC)

> cost accounting method that allocates overhead costs based on specific production activities rather than allocating from a single overhead pool

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## activity-oriented WBS

> work breakdown structure in which activities and tasks are denoted by verbs that indicate work to be accomplished (Software Extension to the PMBOK(R) Guide Fifth Edition)

---

## actor

> role (with respect to that action) in which the enterprise object fulfilling the role participates in the action

_Source: ISO/IEC 15414:2015 Information technology -- Open distributed processing -- Reference model -- Enterprise language, 6.3.2_

---

## actual cost (AC)

> realized cost incurred for the work performed on an activity during a specific time period (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## actual depreciation

> true loss in value of an asset, determined only when the asset is sold

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## actual dollar analysis

> addressing inflation or deflation by using cash-flow amounts that represent actual

---

## actual results

> set of behaviors or conditions of a test item, or set of conditions of associated data or the test environment, observed as a result of test execution (ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-2:2021, Software and systems engineering-- Software testing--Part 2: Test processes, 3.1)

---

## ACWP

> actual cost of work performed

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765c:2014_

---

## ad hoc reviewing

> unstructured independent review technique

_Source: ISO/IEC 20246:2017 Software and systems engineering -- Work product reviews, 3.1_

---

## adaptability

> ability of a system to react to changes in its environment in order to continue meeting both functional and non-functional requirements

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 29119-11:2020, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 11: Guidelines on the testing of AI-based systems, 3.1.5_

---

## adaptation data

> data used to adapt a program to a given installation site or to given conditions in its operational environment

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## adaptation parameter

> variable that is given a specific value to adapt a program to a given installation site or to given conditions in its operational environment

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary_

---

## adapter

> software or device that allows incompatible systems to be interfaced

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765l:2024_

---

## adaptive approach

> development approach in which the requirements are subject to a high level of uncertainty and volatility and are likely to change throughout the project (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## adaptive life cycle

> a project life cycle that is iterative or incremental (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## adaptive maintenance

> modification of a software product, performed after delivery, to keep a software product usable in a changed or changing environment (ISO/IEC/IEEE 14764:2021, Software engineering -- Software life cycle processes -- Maintenance, 3.1.1)

---

## adaptive strategy

> technique that people with disabilities use to improve interaction with the web (ISO/IEC 29110-5-1-2:2025 Systems and software engineering Life cycle profiles for very small entities (VSEs) Part 5-1-2: Software engineering guidelines for the generic Basic profile, 3.4)

---

## adaptive support

> modification of an in-service system of interest performed after installation to keep it usable in a changed or changing runtime environment (IEEE 982:2024, Standard for Measures of the Software Aspects of

---

## added source statements

> count of source statements that were created specifically for the software product

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## additive maintenance

> modification of a software product performed after delivery to add functionality or features to enhance the usage of the product (ISO/IEC/IEEE 14764:2021, Software engineering -- Software life cycle processes -- Maintenance, 3.1.2)

---

## additive support

> modification of an in-service system performed after installation to add functionality or features to a system instance (IEEE 982:2024, Standard for Measures of the Software Aspects of Dependability, 3.1)

---

## additive weighting

> assignment of different values to increase the importance of selected decision attributes (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## address

> number, character, or group of characters that identifies a given device or storage location

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## address field

> field of a computer instruction that contains addresses, information necessary to derive addresses, or values of operands (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## address format

> number and arrangement of address fields in a computer instruction.

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## address modification

> arithmetic, logical, or syntactic operation performed on an address (ISO/IEC/IEEE

---

## address space

> addresses that a computer program can access

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## addressing exception

> exception that occurs when a program calculates an address outside the bounds of the storage available to it (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## addressing mode

> method to search operand position in the instruction set architecture for a central processing unit

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765d:2015_

---

## addressing range

> address space specified and used by the instruction system of a computer (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765d:2015)

---

## ADF

> architecture description framework

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 42010:2022, Software, systems and enterprise Architecture description, 3.5_

---

## adjusted size

> a size based on the functional size multiplied by the technical complexity adjustment (ISO/IEC 20968:2002 Software engineering -- Mk II Function Point Analysis -- Counting Practices Manual, 10)

---

## adjusting leads and lags

> technique used to find ways to bring project activities that are behind into alignment with a plan during project execution

---

## ADL

> architecture description language

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 42010:2022, Software, systems and enterprise Architecture description, 3.6_

---

## adoption process

> set of activities by which an organization brings CASE tools into widespread use

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 14471:2007 Information technology--Software engineering--Guidelines for the adoption of CASE tools, 2.1.2_

---

## advanced profile

> profile targeted at very small entities (VSEs) which want to sustain and grow as a competitive system or software development organization (ISO/IEC 29110-1-2:2024, Systems and software engineering Lifecycle profiles for Very Small Entities (VSEs) Part 1-2: Vocabulary, 3.3)

---

## adversarial attack

> deliberate use of adversarial examples to cause a machine learning model to fail (ISO/IEC TR 29119-11:2020, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 11: Guidelines on the testing of AI-based systems, 3.1.6)

---

## adversarial example

> input to a machine learning model created by applying small perturbations to a working example that results in the model outputting an incorrect result with high confidence

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 29119-11:2020, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 11: Guidelines on the testing of AI-based systems, 3.1.7_

---

## adversarial testing

> testing approach based on the attempted creation and execution of adversarial examples to identify defects in a machine learning model (ISO/IEC TR 29119-11:2020, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 11: Guidelines on the testing of AI-based systems, 3.1.8)

---

> Application Entity (Invocation)

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-1:1998 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference model: Overview_

---

## affect

> feelings felt by humans

_Source: IEEE 7010-2020, IEEE Recommended Practice for Assessing the Impact of Autonomous and Intelligent Systems on Human Well-Being, 2.1_

---

## affective computing

> study and development of systems and devices that can recognize, interpret, process, and simulate human affect

_Source: IEEE 7014-2024, IEEE Standard for Ethical Considerations in Emulated Empathy in Autonomous and Intelligent Systems, 3.1_

---

## affective rights

> normative rights attributed to all humanity which directly relate to communication, thought, conviction, and emotion, and the privacy generally afforded to their development and expression

_Source: IEEE 7014-2024, IEEE Standard for Ethical Considerations in Emulated Empathy in Autonomous and Intelligent Systems, 3.1_

---

## afferent

> pertaining to a flow of data or control from a subordinate module to a superordinate module in a software system (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## affinity diagram

> diagram that shows large numbers of ideas classified into groups for review and analysis (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## affinity grouping

> process of classifying items into similar categories or collections on the basis of their likeness (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## agency

> for users, data subjects, and other stakeholders, the nature and extent of the autonomy, freedom, access, and decision-making capacity with respect to the system (IEEE 7014-2024, IEEE Standard for Ethical Considerations in Emulated Empathy in Autonomous and Intelligent Systems, 3.1)

---

## agent

> active enterprise object that has been delegated something (authorization, responsibility, provision of a service, etc.) by, and acts for, a party (in exercising the authorization, carrying out the responsibility, providing the service, etc.) (ISO/IEC 15414:2015 Information technology -- Open distributed processing -- Reference model -- Enterprise language, 6.6.8)

---

## aggregate

> result of combining one or more physical, logical, or both, system elements

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748- 6:2023 Systems and software engineering Life cycle management Part 6: System and software integration, 3.1.1_

---

## aggregate responsibility

> broadly stated responsibility that is eventually refined as specific properties and

---

## aggregated resource utilization

> degree to which a service utilizes efficiently aggregated resources from resource pooling in order to support multi-tenancy (ISO/IEC TS 25052-1:2022, Systems and software engineering-- Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE): cloud services--Part 1: Quality model, 3.1.1.2)

---

## aggregation

> derived relationship between two elements that are groups of other elements that represents all individual relationships between the grouped elements of the two groups

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765l:2024_

---

## aggregation method

> method that combines a set of measurement values to create a composite value (ISO/IEC 33003:2015 Information technology--Process assessment--Requirements for process measurement frameworks, 3.1)

---

## agile

> approach to development, delivery, and maintenance of products and services by enabling rapid response to feedback (ISO/IEC 33202:2024, Software and systems engineering Core agile practices, 3.2)

---

## agile concept

> fundamental building block of agile

_Source: ISO/IEC 33202:2024, Software and systems engineering Core agile practices, 3.3_

---

## agile development

> development approach based on iterative development, frequent inspection and adaptation, and incremental deliveries, in which requirements and solutions evolve through collaboration in cross-functional teams and through continual stakeholder feedback (ISO/IEC/IEEE 12207:2026 Systems and software engineering--Software life cycle processes, 3.1.4)

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 26515: 2018 Systems and software engineering: Developing information for users in an agile environment, 3.1_

---

## agile environment

> organizational culture, infrastructure, and methodologies that support agile development

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 26515: 2018 Systems and software engineering: Developing information for users in an agile environment, 4.2_

---

## agile maturity

> extent to which an organization, department, project, or team consistently applies agile values and principles that contribute to the achievement of its business needs

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 24587:2021, Software and systems engineering--Agile development--Agile adoption considerations, 3.2_

---

## agile team

> organization or team using agile development methods and approaches

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 26515: 2018 Systems and software engineering: Developing information for users in an agile environment, 3.3_

---

## agile team lead

> individual responsible for ensuring an agile team (3.3) adheres to the organization's agile principles, practices, values, and processes (ISO/IEC TR 24587:2021, Software and systems engineering--Agile

---

## agreement

> mutual acknowledgment of terms and conditions under which a working relationship is conducted (ISO/IEC/IEEE 12207:2026 Systems and software engineering--Software life cycle processes, 3.1.5) (ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288:2023 Systems and software engineering--System life cycle processes, 3.4)

> analytic hierarchy process

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

> artificial intelligence

> artificial intelligence system

> finite set of well-defined rules for the solution of a problem in a finite number of steps

---

## algorithmic drift

> drift that occurs during learning or technical enhancements with a change in the systems processing of the same data (IEEE 7014-2024, IEEE Standard for Ethical Considerations in Emulated Empathy in Autonomous and Intelligent Systems, 3.1)

---

## algorithmic language

> programming language designed for expressing algorithms (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## alias

> alternate name for a construct, such as a class, responsibility, entity, or domain

## aligned

---

## allocated baseline

> approved requirements for a product, subsystem or component, describing the functional, performance, interoperability, and interface requirements that are allocated from higher-level requirements and the verifications required to demonstrate achievement of those requirements, as established at a specific point in time and documented in the allocated configuration documentation (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-7:2019, Systems and software

---

## allocated configuration identification

> in configuration management, the current approved specifications governing the development of configuration items that are part of a higher-level configuration item (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## allocated requirement

> requirement that levies all or part of the performance and functionality of a higher-level requirement on a lower-level architectural element or design component

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## allocation

> distribution of requirements, resources, or other entities among the components of a system or program

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## allocation of an entitlement

> process of assigning some or all of a given entitlement to a subsidiary or other associated organizational unit which manages its own entitlement schema library (ISO/IEC 19770-3:2016 Information technology--IT asset management--Part 3: Entitlement schema, 3.1.1)

> application life cycle management

## alpha testing

> first stage of testing before a product is considered ready for commercial or operational use (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## alphanumeric

> pertaining to data that consists of letters, digits, and usually other characters, such as punctuation marks, as well as to processes and functional units that use the data

## ALS

> Application Layer Structure

> part of a use case that describes its alternative implementations (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## alternate key

> candidate key of an entity other than the primary key (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.5)

## alternatives analysis

> method used to evaluate identified options in order to select the options or approaches to

---

## ambiguity

## analog

> pertaining to continuously variable physical quantities or to data presented in a continuous form, as well as to processes and functional units that use the data

---

## analog computer

> computer whose operations are analogous to the behavior of another system and that accepts, processes, and produces analog data

---

## analogous estimating

> a technique for estimating the duration or cost of an activity or a project using historical data from a similar activity or project (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## analysis

> process of studying a system by partitioning the system into parts (functions, components, or objects) and determining how the parts relate to each other

## analyst

> member of the technical community who is skilled and trained to define problems and to analyze, develop, and express algorithms

---

## analytic hierarchy process (AHP)

> use of matrixes to manage pair-wise relationships in decision-making (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## analytical model

> model that describes mathematical relationships, such as differential equations that support quantifiable analysis about the system parameters (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24641:2023. Systems and Software engineering-- Methods and tools for model-based systems and software engineering, 3.1.1)

---

## analyzability

> capability of a product to be effectively and efficiently assessed regarding the impact of an intended change to one or more of its parts, to diagnose it for deficiencies or causes of failures, or to identify parts to be modified (ISO/IEC 25010:2023, Systems and software engineering--Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)--Product quality model, 3.7.3)

---

> generic ancestor of the class or a parent of the class or an ancestor of a parent of the class (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.6)

---

## ancestral box

> box related to a specific diagram by a hierarchically consecutive sequence of one or more parent/child relationships

---

## ancestral diagram

> diagram that contains an ancestral box

---

## anchor point

> a milestone in software scheduling at which a major project life cycle transition occurs (Software

---

## annotate

> command used for listing the latest version of each program's source code line, along with the date, the file version it was introduced, and the person who committed it

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## annotated topic list (ATL)

> list of all topics to be included in an information-development project with annotations that can include writer, where used, file name, and additional data

---

## annotation

> further documentation accompanying a requirement

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765e:2015_

---

## announcement

> interaction (invocation) initiated by a client object, resulting in the conveyance of information from that client object to a server object, requesting a function to be performed by that server object

> representation of a cash flow as a series of equal annual payments (at a stated interest rate) over the planning horizon (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## annual percentage rate (APR)

> nominal annual interest rate

---

## annuity

> amount of a series of equal payments at regular intervals over a planning horizon

---

> anything observed in the documentation or operation of a system that deviates from expectations based on previously verified system, software, or hardware products or reference documents

> American National Standards Institute

_Source: IEEE 1012-2024 IEEE Standard for System, Software, and Hardware Verification and Validation, 3.2_

> buffering technique in which data are stored in a buffer in anticipation of a need for the data (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## anticipatory paging

> storage allocation technique in which pages are transferred from auxiliary storage to main storage in anticipation of a need for those pages (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary)

> operational availability

> application program interface

> person who has submitted an application to be admitted into the certification process

## application

> system for collecting, saving, processing, and presenting data by means of a computer

---

## application administration function

> functions performed by users which include installation, configuration, application backup, maintenance (patching and upgrading) and de-installation (ISO/IEC 25051:2014 Software engineering -- Software product Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) -- Requirements for quality of Ready to Use Software Product (RUSP) and instructions for testing, 4.1.3)

> architecture including the architectural structure and rules (e.g. common rules and constraints) that constrains a specific member product within a product line

_Source: ISO/IEC 26550:2015 Software and systems engineering--Reference model for product line engineering and management, 3.1_

---

## application area

> group of applications that have common components or characteristics, such as similar technologies or production methods, similar customers (i.e., internal versus external, government versus commercial) or a

---

## application asset

> output of a specific application engineering process (e.g. application realization) that can be exploited in other lifecycle processes of application engineering and can be adapted as a domain asset based on a product management decision (ISO/IEC 26550:2015 Software and systems engineering--Reference model for product line engineering and management, 3.2)

---

## application assets in requirements

> application-specific artifacts produced during application requirements engineering, such as application requirements specifications and application requirements models

_Source: ISO/IEC 26551:2016 Software and systems engineering --Tools and methods for product line requirements engineering, 3.1_

---

## application component

> component that is selected, reused or newly developed for a member product

_Source: ISO/IEC 26553:2018 Information technology -- Software and systems engineering-- Tools and methods for product line realization, 3.1_

---

## application configuration

> derivation for member product specific executables from domain assets in realization

_Source: ISO/IEC 26557:2016 Software and systems engineering -- Methods and tools for variability mechanisms in software and systems product line, 3.1_

---

## application design

> process of application engineering where a single application architecture conforming to the domain architecture is derived

_Source: ISO/IEC 26550:2015 Software and systems engineering--Reference model for product line engineering and management, 3.3_

---

## application domain

> well-defined set of applications

_Source: ISO/IEC 23643:2020, Software and systems engineering-- Capabilities of software safety and security verification tools, 3.1_

---

## application engineering

> life cycle consisting of a set of processes in which the application assets and member products of the product line are implemented and managed by reusing domain assets in conformance to the domain architecture and by binding the variability of the platform

_Source: ISO/IEC 26550:2015 Software and systems engineering-- Reference model for product line engineering and management, 3.4_

---

## application engineering process

> processes for developing a member product in a product line

_Source: ISO/IEC 26555:2015 Software and systems engineering--Tools and methods for product line technical management, 3.1_

---

## application frameworks

> subsystem design made up of a collection of abstract and concrete classes and interfaces between them (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## application function point count

> a count that provides a measure of the functionality the application provides to the end-user

_Source: ISO/IEC 20968:2002 Software engineering -- Mk II Function Point Analysis -- Counting Practices Manual, 10_

---

## application functional size

> measure of the functionality that an application provides to the user, determined by the application function point count

---

## application generator

> code generator that produces programs to solve one or more problems in a particular application area

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## application interface

> interface that is selected, reused or newly developed by a member product

_Source: ISO/IEC 26553:2018 Information technology -- Software and systems engineering-- Tools and methods for product line realization, 3.3_

---

## application management

> domain responsible for all of the tasks and activities that are aimed at managing, supporting, maintaining, and renewing existing applications and related data structures (ISO/IEC 16350-2015 Information technology--Systems and software engineering--Application management, 4.2)

---

## application management organization

> organizational unit that is responsible for application management for one or more applications (ISO/IEC 16350-2015 Information technology--Systems and software engineering-- Application management, 4.3)

---

## application object

> component that is directly related to or forms part of an application

_Source: ISO/IEC 16350-2015 Information technology--Systems and software engineering--Application management, 4.4_

---

## application portfolio

> collection of applications managed by an application management organization or an entity within that application management organization (ISO/IEC 16350-2015 Information technology--Systems and software engineering--Application management, 4.5)

---

## application problem

> problem submitted by an end user and requiring information processing for its solution

_Source: ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary_

---

## application program interface (API) management platform

> proxy for client requests to protect the back-end of an online server from being disabled from too many queries (IEEE 7005 2021, IEEE Standard for Transparent Employer Data Governance, 3.1)

---

## application programming interface (API)

> set of functions, protocols, parameters, and objects of different

---

## application realization

> process of application engineering that develops application assets, some of which can be derived from domain assets, and member products based on the application architecture and the sets of application assets and domain assets

_Source: ISO/IEC 26550:2015 Software and systems engineering--Reference model for product line engineering and management, 3.5_

---

## application requirements analysis

> subprocess that understands all application specific requirements, scrutinizes incorrect and inconsistent application requirements through modelling, and then analyses and negotiates application requirements that cannot be satisfied through the domain requirements

_Source: ISO/IEC 26551:2016 Software and systems engineering --Tools and methods for product line requirements engineering, 3.3_

---

## application requirements elicitation

> subprocess for identifying stakeholders relevant to an application, eliciting application specific requirements, and binding the appropriate variants

_Source: ISO/IEC 26551:2016 Software and systems engineering --Tools and methods for product line requirements engineering, 3.2_

---

## application requirements management

> subprocess that manages traceability and changes on application requirements

_Source: ISO/IEC 26551:2016 Software and systems engineering --Tools and methods for product line requirements engineering, 3.6_

---

## application requirements specification

> subprocess that documents the application specific requirements and integrates it with the domain requirements specification whose variants are bound

_Source: ISO/IEC 26551:2016 Software and systems engineering --Tools and methods for product line requirements engineering, 3.4_

---

## application requirements verification and validation

> subprocess that confirms that the application specific requirements are consistent and feasible and helps ensure that the bound variants satisfy the specific product's requirements

_Source: ISO/IEC 26551:2016 Software and systems engineering --Tools and methods for product line requirements engineering, 3.5_

---

## application software

> software designed to help users perform particular tasks or handle particular types of problems, as distinct from software that controls the computer itself

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary, 4.5_

---

## application specific integrated circuit (ASIC)

> integrated circuit customized for a particular use (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765d:2015)

---

## application test asset

> application-specific test asset that has reuse potential

_Source: ISO/IEC 26554:2018 Information technology--Software and systems engineering-Tools and methods for product line testing, 3.3_

---

## application testing

> sub-process of application engineering where domain test artefacts are reused to uncover evidence of defects in the application

_Source: ISO/IEC 26554:2018 Information technology--Software and systems engineering- Tools and methods for product line testing, 3.2_

---

## application variability model

> variability model for a particular application including variability binding results, application specifically modified variability and application specifically added variability

_Source: ISO/IEC 26558:2017 Software and systems engineering -- Methods and tools for variability modelling in software and systems product line, 3.2_

---

## application-oriented language

> computer language with facilities or notations applicable primarily to a single application area (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## application-specific component

> component that is developed for a specific member product

_Source: ISO/IEC 26553:2018 Information technology -- Software and systems engineering-- Tools and methods for product line realization, 3.5_

---

## application-specific requirements

> requirements specific to an application or requirements not covered in domain requirements (ISO/IEC 26551:2016 Software and systems engineering --Tools and methods for product line requirements engineering, 3.9)

---

## apportioned effort

> an activity where effort is allotted proportionately across certain discrete efforts and not divisible into discrete efforts (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## appraisal findings

> results of an appraisal that identify the most important issues, problems, or opportunities for process improvement within the appraisal scope (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary)

---

## appraisal participants

> members of the organizational unit who participate in providing information during an appraisal

---

## appraisal team leader

> person who leads the activities of an appraisal and has satisfied qualification criteria for experience, knowledge, and skills defined by the appraisal method

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## appropriateness recognizability

> capability of a product to be recognized by users as appropriate for their needs (ISO/IEC 25010:2023, Systems and software engineering--Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)--Product quality model, 3.4.1)

---

## approval

> notification by an authorized representative that an information item appears to satisfy requirements and is complete (ISO/IEC/IEEE 15289:2019 Systems and software engineering--Content of life-cycle information items (documentation), 5.1)

---

## approval authority

> person (or persons) or organization (or organizations) responsible for approving activities, artifacts, and other aspects of the system during its life cycle (ISO/IEC/IEEE 15026-1:2019 Systems and software engineering--Systems and software assurance--Part 1: Concepts and vocabulary, 3.5.2) (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765h:2019)

---

## approved modification

> disposition of one or more proposed changes authorizing change to any SCIs (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765i:2020)

---

## arc

> directed edge of a net which can connect a place to a transition or a transition to a place, normally represented by an arrow

_Source: ISO/IEC 15909-1:2019 Systems and software engineering--High-level Petri nets--Part 1: Concepts, definitions and graphical notation, 2.1.1_

## arc annotation

> expression that can involve constants, variables and operators used to annotate an arc of a net (ISO/IEC 15909-1:2019 Systems and software engineering--High-level Petri nets--Part 1: Concepts, definitions and graphical notation, 2.1.1.3)

---

## architect

> person, team, or organization responsible for systems architecture

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765e:2015_

---

## architecting

> conceiving, defining, expressing, documenting, communicating, certifying proper implementation of, maintaining and improving an architecture throughout the life cycle of an entity of interest (ISO/IEC/IEEE 42010:2022, Software, systems and enterprise Architecture description, 3.1)

---

## architectural design

> definition of a collection of hardware and software components and their interfaces to establish the framework for the development of a computer system (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## architectural design phase

> life-cycle phase in which a system's general architecture is developed, thereby

---

## architectural design review

> joint acquirer-supplier review to evaluate the technical adequacies of the software architectural design as depicted in the software design descriptions

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## architectural structure

> physical or logical layout of the components of a system design and their internal and external connections

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## architectural style

> definition of a family of systems in terms of a pattern of structural organization

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## architecture

> fundamental concepts or properties of an entity in its environment and governing principles for the realization and evolution of this entity and its related life cycle processes (ISO/IEC/IEEE 12207:2026 Systems and software engineering--Software life cycle processes, 3.1.6) (ISO/IEC/IEEE 42020:2019 Software, systems and enterprise -- Architecture processes, 3.3)

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 42010:2022, Software, systems and enterprise Architecture description, 3.2_

---

## architecture collection

> group of architectures held by an organization that is subject to governance and management by the organization as a whole (ISO/IEC/IEEE 42020:2019 Software, systems and enterprise -- Architecture processes, 3.4)

---

## architecture description (AD)

> work product used to express an architecture (ISO/IEC/IEEE 42010:2022, Software, systems and enterprise Architecture description, 3.3)

---

## architecture description element

> identified or named part of an architecture description (ISO/IEC/IEEE 42010:2022, Software, systems and enterprise Architecture description, 3.4)

---

## architecture description framework (ADF)

> conventions, principles, and practices for the description of architectures established within a specific domain of application or community of stakeholders (ISO/IEC/IEEE 12207:2026 Systems and software engineering--Software life cycle processes, 3.1.7) (ISO/IEC/IEEE 42010:2022, Software, systems and enterprise Architecture description, 3.5)

---

## architecture description language (ADL)

> means of expression, with syntax and semantics, consisting of a set of representations, conventions, and associated rules intended to be used to describe an architecture

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 42010:2022, Software, systems and enterprise Architecture description, 3.6_

---

## architecture entity

> thing being considered, described, discussed, studied or otherwise addressed during the architecting effort

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 42020:2019 Software, systems and enterprise -- Architecture processes, 3.6_

---

## architecture evaluation (AE)

> individual responsible for ensuring an agile team (3.3) adheres to the organization's agile principles, practices, values, and processes

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 24587:2021, Software and systems engineering--Agile development--Agile adoption considerations, 3.4_

---

## architecture evaluation framework

> conventions, principles and practices for evaluating architectures in a consistent and repeatable manner (ISO/IEC/IEEE 42030:2019 Software, systems, and enterprise--Architecture evaluation framework, 3.5)

---

## architecture framework

> conventions, principles and practices for the description of architectures established within a specific domain of application or community of stakeholders

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 12207:2026 Systems and software engineering--Software life cycle processes, 3.1.7_

---

## architecture view

> information part comprising portion of an architecture description (ISO/IEC/IEEE 12207:2026 Systems and software engineering--Software life cycle processes, 3.1.8) (ISO/IEC/IEEE 42010:2022, Software, systems and enterprise Architecture description, 3.7)

---

## architecture viewpoint

> set of conventions for the creation, interpretation, and use of an architecture view to frame one or more concerns (ISO/IEC/IEEE 12207:2026 Systems and software engineering--Software life cycle processes, 3.1.9) (ISO/IEC/IEEE 42010:2022, Software, systems and enterprise Architecture description, 3.8)

---

## archival page

> content that is preserved as a record and not expected to change (ISO/IEC/IEEE 23026:2023 Systems and software engineering--Engineering and management of websites for systems, software, and services information, 3.1.1)

---

## archive

> Location of system elements that are no longer present in runtime environments, but are available for examination for audit, regulatory, and other processes

_Source: IEEE 2675-2021, IEEE Standard for DevOps: Building Reliable and Secure Systems Including Application Build, Package, and Deployment, 3.1_

---

## archiving

> process of placing a version of a document in a less frequently used storage area

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 26511:2018 Systems and software engineering--Requirements for managers of information for users of systems, software, and services, 3.1.3_

---

## argument

> independent variable

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## ARIA

> accessible rich internet application

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 23026:2023 Systems and software engineering-- Engineering and management of websites for systems, software, and services information, 3.2_

---

## arity

> number of roles that participate in a relationship (ISO/IEC 15474-1:2002 Information technology -- CDIF framework -- Part 1: Overview, 4.2)

---

## arranging

> activity of sequencing attributes in a transactional function

_Source: ISO/IEC 20926:2009 Software and systems engineering -- Software measurement -- IFPUG functional size measurement method 2009, 3.5_

---

## array

> n-dimensional ordered set of data items identified by a single name and one or more indices, so that each element of the set is individually addressable

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## arrow

> directed line, composed of one or more connected arrow segments in a single diagram from a single source (box or diagram boundary) to a single use (box or diagram boundary)

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765m:2024, 2.1.8_

---

## arrow label

> noun or noun phrase associated with an arrow segment to signify the arrow meaning of the arrow segment (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765m:2024, 2.1.9)

---

## arrow meaning

> object types of an object type set, regardless of how these object types can be collected, aggregated, grouped, bundled, or otherwise joined within the object type set

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765m:2024, 2.1.10_

---

## arrow role

> relationship between an object type set represented by an arrow segment and the activity represented by the box to which the arrow segment is attached (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765m:2024, 2.1.12)

---

## arrow segment

> directed line that originates at a box side, arrow junction (branch or join), or diagram boundary and terminates at the next box side, arrow junction (branch or join), or diagram boundary that occurs in the path of the line

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765m:2024, 2.1.13_

---

## artifact

> work product that is produced and used during a project to capture and convey information (ISO/IEC/IEEE 12207:2026 Systems and software engineering--Software life cycle processes, 3.1.10)

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288:2023 Systems and software engineering--System life cycle processes, 3.6_

> branch of computer science devoted to developing data processing systems that perform functions normally associated with human intelligence, such as reasoning, learning, and self-improvement

> situation when a previously labelled artificial intelligence (AI) system is no longer considered to be AI as technology advances (ISO/IEC TR 29119-11:2020, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 11: Guidelines on the testing of AI-based systems, 3.1.10)

## artificial intelligence-based system

> application-specific integrated circuit

> Application Service Object

## ASOI

## aspect

> part of an entitys character or nature

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 42010:2022, Software, systems and enterprise Architecture description, 3.9_

---

## ASR

> alternative systems review

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-8:2019, Systems and software engineering Life cycle management Part 7: Application of systems engineering on defense programs, 3.2_

---

## assemble

> to translate a computer program expressed in an assembly language into its machine language equivalent (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## assemble-and-go

> operating technique in which there are no stops between the assembling, linking, loading, and execution of a computer program

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## assembled origin

> address of the initial storage location assigned to a computer program by an assembler, a compiler, or a linkage editor (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## assembler

> computer program that translates programs expressed in assembly language into their machine language equivalents (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## assembly code

> computer instructions and data definitions expressed in a form that can be recognized and processed by an assembler (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## assembly language

> programming language that corresponds closely to the instruction set of a given computer, allows symbolic naming of operations and addresses, and usually results in a one-to-one translation of program instructions into machine instructions (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## assertion

> logical expression specifying a program state that exists or a set of conditions that program variables satisfy at a particular point during program execution

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary_

---

## assessment

> action of applying specific documented criteria to a specific software module, package or product for the purpose of determining acceptance or release of the software module, package or product

_Source: ISO/IEC 14102:2008 Information Technology - Guideline for the evaluation and selection of CASE tools, 3.1_

---

## assessment body

> body that performs an assessment (ISO/IEC 33001:2015 Information technology--Process assessment--Concepts and terminology, 3.2.1)

---

## assessment constraint

> restriction placed on the use of the assessment outputs or on the assessment team's freedom of choice regarding the conduct of the assessment

_Source: ISO/IEC 33001:2015 Information technology--Process assessment--Concepts and terminology, 3.2.2_

---

## assessment indicator

> sources of objective evidence used to support the assessors' judgment in rating process attributes

_Source: ISO/IEC 33001:2015 Information technology--Process assessment--Concepts and terminology, 3.3.1_

---

## assessment input

> information required before a process assessment can commence

_Source: ISO/IEC 33001:2015 Information technology--Process assessment--Concepts and terminology, 3.2.3_

---

## assessment output

> tangible results from an assessment (ISO/IEC 33001:2015 Information technology--Process assessment--Concepts and terminology, 3.2.4)

---

## assessment participant

> individual who has responsibilities within the scope of the assessment

_Source: ISO/IEC 33001:2015 Information technology--Process assessment--Concepts and terminology, 3.2.5_

---

## assessment purpose

> statement, provided as part of the assessment input, which defines the reasons for performing the assessment

_Source: ISO/IEC 33001:2015 Information technology--Process assessment--Concepts and terminology, 3.2.6_

---

## assessment record

> orderly, documented collection of information which is pertinent to the assessment and adds to the understanding and verification of the process profiles generated by the assessment

_Source: ISO/IEC 33001:2015 Information technology--Process assessment--Concepts and terminology, 3.2.7_

---

## assessment scope

> definition of the boundaries of the assessment, provided as part of the assessment input, encompassing the boundaries of the organizational unit for the assessment, the processes to be included, the quality level for each process to be assessed, and the context within which the processes operate

_Source: ISO/IEC 33001:2015 Information technology--Process assessment--Concepts and terminology, 3.2.8_

---

## assessment sponsor

> individual or entity, internal or external to the organizational unit being assessed, who requires the assessment to be performed, and provides financial or other resources to carry it out

_Source: ISO/IEC 33001:2015 Information technology--Process assessment--Concepts and terminology, 3.2.9_

---

## assessment team

> one or more individuals who jointly perform a process assessment

_Source: ISO/IEC 33001:2015 Information technology--Process assessment--Concepts and terminology, 3.2.10_

---

## assessor

> individual who participates in the rating of process attributes

_Source: ISO/IEC 33001:2015 Information technology--Process assessment--Concepts and terminology, 3.2.11_

---

## asset

> item, thing, or entity that has potential or actual value to an organization

_Source: ISO/IEC 19770-5:2015 Information technology--IT asset management--Overview and vocabulary, 3.2_

---

## asset base

> reusable assets produced from both domain and application engineering

_Source: ISO/IEC 26550:2015 Software and systems engineering--Reference model for product line engineering and management, 3.6_

---

## asset life

> period from asset creation to asset end-of-life

_Source: ISO/IEC 19770-1:2017 Information technology -- IT asset management -- Part 1: IT asset management systems--Requirements, 3.2_

---

## asset management

> coordinated activity of an organization to realize value from assets

_Source: ISO/IEC 19770-5:2015 Information technology--IT asset management--Overview and vocabulary, 3.3_

---

## asset management plan

> documented information that specifies the activities, resources and timescales required for an individual asset, or a grouping of assets, to achieve the organization's asset management objectives

_Source: ISO/IEC 19770-1:2017 Information technology -- IT asset management -- Part 1: IT asset management systems-- Requirements, 3.4_

---

## asset management system

> management system for asset management whose function is to establish the asset management policy and asset management objectives

_Source: ISO/IEC 19770-1:2017 Information technology -- IT asset management -- Part 1: IT asset management systems--Requirements, 3.5_

---

## asset portfolio

> assets that are within the scope of the asset management system (ISO/IEC 19770-1:2017 Information technology -- IT asset management -- Part 1: IT asset management systems--Requirements, 3.6)

---

## asset proposal

> artifact that includes major assets (functional areas and high-level common and variable features of all applications) that can be included in a product line with their quantified costs and benefits, and estimate results (ISO/IEC 26551:2016 Software and systems engineering --Tools and methods for product line requirements engineering,

---

## asset protection

> for services, degree to which a service has processes to protect physical facilities used to provide the covered services from loss of data, connectivity, and availability of necessary infrastructure and IT equipment, and to secure the covered services during operation (ISO/IEC TS 25052-1:2022, Systems and software engineering-- Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE): cloud services--Part 1: Quality model, 3.1.5.6)

---

## asset scoping

> process of identifying the potential domain assets and estimating the returns of investments in the assets (ISO/IEC 26550:2015 Software and systems engineering--Reference model for product line engineering and management, 3.7)

---

## asset system

> set of assets that interact or are interrelated

_Source: ISO/IEC 19770-1:2017 Information technology -- IT asset management -- Part 1: IT asset management systems--Requirements, 3.7_

---

## asset type

> grouping of assets having common characteristics that distinguish those assets as a group or class

_Source: ISO/IEC 19770-1:2017 Information technology -- IT asset management -- Part 1: IT asset management systems-- Requirements, 3.8_

---

## assignment

> for a set of variables, the association of a value (of correct type) to each variable (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765i:2020)

---

## assignment statement

> computer program statement that assigns a value to a variable (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## assistive technologies

> hardware or software that is added to or incorporated within a system that increases accessibility for an individual

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025_

---

## assistive technology

> equipment, product, system, hardware, software, or service that is used to increase, maintain or improve capabilities of individuals (ISO/IEC 29110-5-1-2:2025 Systems and software engineering Life cycle profiles for very small entities (VSEs) Part 5-1-2: Software engineering guidelines for the generic Basic profile, 3.6)

---

## association

> in UML, a relationship between an actor and a use case that indicates that the actor interacts with the system by means of the use case

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## association management facility

> set of service primitives which support the management of an association between protocol objects

_Source: ISO/IEC 14752:2000 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Protocol support for computational interactions, 3.3.3_

---

## associative class

> class introduced to resolve a many-to-many relationship

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.7_

---

## associative entity

> entity used to represent a relationship between other entities (ISO/IEC 15474-1:2002 Information technology -- CDIF framework -- Part 1: Overview, 4.2)

---

## associative entity type

> entity type that contains attributes which further describe a many-to-many relationship between two other entity types (ISO/IEC 20926:2009 Software and systems engineering -- Software measurement -- IFPUG functional size measurement method 2009, 3.6)

---

## associative literal

> literal that denotes an instance in terms of its value (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.8)

---

## assumption

> factor in the planning process that is considered to be true, real, or certain, without proof or demonstration (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## assumption and constraint analysis

> assessment to help ensure that assumptions and constraints are integrated into the project plans and documents, and that there is consistency among them (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## assumption log

> project document used to record all assumptions and constraints throughout the project (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## assurance

> grounds for justified confidence that a claim has been or will be achieved (ISO/IEC/IEEE 15026-1:2019 Systems and software engineering--Systems and software assurance--Part 1: Concepts and vocabulary, 3.1.1)

---

## assurance argument

> artifact that links tangible evidence and assumptions to provide a convincing and valid argument of a claim under a given context (ISO/IEC/IEEE 15026-4:2021, Systems and software engineering Systems and software assurance Part 4: Assurance in the life cycle, 3.2)

---

## assurance case

> reasoned, auditable artifact created that supports the contention that its top-level claim (or set of claims), is satisfied, including systematic argumentation and its underlying evidence and explicit assumptions that support the claim(s)

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 15026-1:2019 Systems and software engineering--Systems and software assurance--Part 1: Concepts and vocabulary, 3.1.3_

---

## assurance case report

> auditable artifact that provides the claim of an assurance case and a complete index of the argument and evidence of the assurance case such that the assurance case of interest can be assembled from the index (ISO/IEC/IEEE 15026-2:2022, Systems and software engineering Systems and software assurance Part 2: Assurance case, 3.1.2)

---

## assurance claim

> claim for which assurance is considered

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 15026-4:2021, Systems and software engineering Systems and software assurance Part 4: Assurance in the life cycle, 3.3_

---

## assurance information

> information including a claim about a system, evidence supporting the claim, an argument showing how the evidence supports the achievement of the claim, and the context for these items (ISO/IEC/IEEE 15026-4:2021, Systems and software engineering Systems and software assurance Part 4: Assurance in the life cycle, 3.4)

---

## assurance objective

> purpose of achievement of the assurance claim (ISO/IEC/IEEE 15026-4:2021, Systems and software engineering Systems and software assurance Part 4: Assurance in the life cycle, 3.5)

---

## assure

> to promise or state with certainty by one person to another person or group (IEEE 730-2014 IEEE Standard for Software Quality Assurance Processes, 3.2)

---

## asynchronous

> pertaining to two or more processes that do not depend upon the occurrence of specific events such as common timing

_Source: ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary_

---

## asynchronous communication interface adapter

> functional unit to connect interfaces for asynchronous communications (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765d:2015)

---

## asynchronous communication interface adapter (ACIA)

> functional unit to connect interfaces for asynchronous communications

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765d:2015_

---

## asynchronous I/O device

> I/O device that generates an interrupt after producing some input or generating some output

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## asynchronous I/O device interface task

> task that interfaces to an I/O device and is activated by interrupts from that device

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## asynchronous message communication

> communication in which a producer task sends a message to a consumer task and does not wait for a response (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary)

---

## ATDD

> acceptance-test driven development

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 7052:2023, Software engineering--Controlling frequently occurring risks during development and maintenance of custom software, 3.1_

---

## ATL

> annotated topic list

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 26511:2018 Systems and software engineering--Requirements for managers of information for users of systems, software, and services, 3.2_

---

## atomic type

> data type, each of whose members consists of a single, nondecomposable data item (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## atomicity

> entity at a given level of abstraction that cannot be subdivided at that level of abstraction (ISO/IEC

---

## attached process

> process definitions how each asset will be used in application

_Source: ISO/IEC 26555:2015 Software and systems engineering--Tools and methods for product line technical management, 3.2_

---

## attack

> malicious action or interaction with the system or its environment that has the potential to result in a fault or an error (and thereby possibly in a failure) or an adverse consequence

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 15026-1:2019 Systems and software engineering--Systems and software assurance--Part 1: Concepts and vocabulary, 3.4.7_

---

## attribute

> inherent property or characteristic of an entity that can be distinguished quantitatively or qualitatively by human or automated means (ISO/IEC 25000:2014 Systems and software Engineering--Systems and software product Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) -- Guide to SQuaRE, 4.1)

---

## attribute name

> role name for the value class of the attribute

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.10_

---

## attributed relationship

> relationship that has attributes

_Source: ISO/IEC 15474-1:2002 Information technology -- CDIF framework -- Part 1: Overview, 4.2_

---

## attributive entity type

> entity type that further describes one or more attributes of another entity type (ISO/IEC 20926:2009 Software and systems engineering -- Software measurement -- IFPUG functional size measurement method 2009, 3.7)

---

## ATWS

> anticipated transient without scram

_Source: IEEE 1012-2024 IEEE Standard for System, Software, and Hardware Verification and Validation, 3.2_

---

## AUC

> area under curve

_Source: IEEE 1012-2024 IEEE Standard for System, Software, and Hardware Verification and Validation, 3.2_

---

## audience

> category of users sharing the same or similar characteristics and needs (for example, purpose in using the information for users, tasks, education level, abilities, training, and experience) that determine the content, structure, and use of the intended information (ISO/IEC/IEEE 23026:2023 Systems and software engineering--Engineering and management of websites for systems, software, and services information, 3.1.2)

---

## audience research

> planned activity of interviewing representative users and analyzing interview records and personnel records (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765a:2011)

---

## audit

> independent examination of a work product or set of work products to assess compliance with specifications,

---

## audit team

> one or more auditors conducting an audit, supported if needed by technical experts (ISO/IEC 29110-1- 2:2024, Systems and software engineering Lifecycle profiles for Very Small Entities (VSEs) Part 1-2: Vocabulary, 3.13)

---

## auditability

> for a service, degree to which it collects and provides available necessary evidential information related to the operation and use of the service, for the purpose of conducting an audit (ISO/IEC TS 25052-1:2022, Systems and software engineering--Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE): cloud services--Part 1: Quality model, 3.1.8.2)

---

## auditee

> organization being audited (ISO/IEC 29110-1-2:2024, Systems and software engineering Lifecycle profiles for Very Small Entities (VSEs) Part 1-2: Vocabulary, 3.11)

---

## auditor

> person who conducts an audit (ISO/IEC 29110-1-2:2024, Systems and software engineering Lifecycle profiles for Very Small Entities (VSEs) Part 1-2: Vocabulary, 3.12)

---

## augmented reality system

> view of the physical world that is supplemented by computer-generated text, images, data, or other media

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 26511:2018 Systems and software engineering--Requirements for managers of information for users of systems, software, and services, 3.1.4_

---

## authenticity

> capability of a product to prove that the identity of a subject or resource is the one claimed (ISO/IEC 25010:2023, Systems and software engineering--Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)- -Product quality model, 3.6.5)

---

## author check

> informal review performed by the author of the work product

_Source: ISO/IEC 20246:2017 Software and systems engineering -- Work product reviews, 3.2_

---

## authoring environment

> toolset used to create, store, and manage content units (ISO/IEC/IEEE 26511:2018

---

## authoring language

> high-level programming language used to develop courseware for computer-assisted instruction (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## authoring system

> programming system that incorporates an authoring language

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## authority
