> right to apply project resources, expend funds, make decisions, or give approvals (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## authorization

> action indicating that a particular behavior is not prevented (ISO/IEC 15414:2015 Information technology -- Open distributed processing -- Reference model -- Enterprise language, 6.6.4)

---

## authorized individual

> individual identified by an organization to make decisions, allocate resources, and accept risk, within a domain of responsibility

_Source: IEEE 7002:2022, IEEE Standard for Data Privacy Process, 3.1_

---

## automate

> to make a process or equipment automatic

_Source: ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary_

---

## automated systems process

> systems or software process that is performed either fully or partially supported by CASE tools (ISO/IEC 15940:2013 Systems and software engineering--Software Engineering Environment Services, 2.2.3, 2.9)

---

## automated verification system

> software tool that accepts as input a computer program and a representation of its specification and produces, possibly with human help, a proof or disproof of the correctness of the program

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## automatic

> pertaining to a process or equipment that, under specified conditions, functions without human intervention

_Source: ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary_

---

## automation

> conversion of processes or equipment to automatic operation, or the results of the conversion

_Source: ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary_

---

## autonomous system

> system capable of working without human intervention for sustained periods (ISO/IEC TR 29119-11:2020, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 11: Guidelines on the testing of AI-based systems, 3.1.14)

---

## autonomous/intelligent system (A/IS)

> semi-autonomous or autonomous computer-controlled system programmed to carry out some task with or without limited human intervention, capable of decision making by independent inference and successfully adapting to its context (IEEE 7010-2020, IEEE Recommended Practice for Assessing the Impact of Autonomous and Intelligent Systems on Human Well-Being, 2.1)

---

## autonomy

> ability of a system to work for sustained periods without human intervention

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 29119- 11:2020, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 11: Guidelines on the testing of AI-based systems, 3.1.15_

---

## autonomy-based improvement

> self-motivated and self-determined professional process improvement with an understanding of the work (process) objectives, latest technology, and outcomes from product use (ISO/IEC 29110-1- 2:2024, Systems and software engineering Lifecycle profiles for Very Small Entities (VSEs) Part 1-2: Vocabulary, 3.14)

---

## AV

> autonomous vehicle

_Source: IEEE 7010-2020, IEEE Recommended Practice for Assessing the Impact of Autonomous and Intelligent Systems on Human Well-Being, 2.2_

---

## availability

> capability of a product to be operational and accessible when required for use (ISO/IEC 25010:2023, Systems and software engineering--Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)--Product quality model, 3.5.2)

---

## AVI

> availability threshold

_Source: IEEE 982:2024, Standard for Measures of the Software Aspects of Dependability, 3.2_

---

## awareness requirement

> reference to other requirements or domain assumptions and their success or failure

_Source: IEEE 7005 2021, IEEE Standard for Transparent Employer Data Governance, 3.1_

---

## BABOK

> Business Analysis Book of Knowledge

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018 Systems and software engineering-Life cycle processes-Requirements engineering, 3.2_

---

## BAC

> budget at completion (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## back matter

> material that appears at the end of printed documentation, such as an index

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765a:2011_

---

## back-to-back testing

> testing in which two or more variants of a program are executed with the same inputs, the outputs are compared, and errors are analyzed in case of discrepancies (ISO/IEC TR 19759:2016 Software Engineering - - Guide to the Software Engineering Body of Knowledge (SWEBOK), 4.2.2.9)

---

## background

> in job scheduling, the computing environment in which low-priority processes or those not requiring user interaction are executed (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## background processing

> execution of a low-priority process while higher priority processes are not using

---

## backlog

> collection of agile features or stories of both functional and nonfunctional requirements that are typically sorted in an order based on value priority

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 26515: 2018 Systems and software engineering: Developing information for users in an agile environment, 3.4_

---

## backlog refinement

> progressive elaboration of the content in the backlog and (re)prioritization of it to identify the work that can be accomplished in an upcoming iteration (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## backout

> to undo the effects of a commit (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary)

---

## backup

> system, component, file, procedure, or person available to replace or help restore a primary item in the event of a failure or externally caused disaster

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary_

---

## backup and recovery testing

> type of reliability testing that measures the degree to which system state can be restored from backup within specified parameters of time, cost, completeness, and accuracy in the event of failure

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765k:2022_

---

## backup programmer

> assistant leader of a chief programmer team (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## Backus-Naur Form

> formal meta-language used for defining the syntax of a language in a textual format

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-4:2021 Software and systems engineering -- Software testing -- Part 4: Test techniques, 3.1_

---

## backward propagation

> method used in artificial neural networks to determine the weights to be used on the network connections based on the computed error at the output of the network (ISO/IEC TR 29119-11:2020, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 11: Guidelines on the testing of AI-based systems, 3.1.17)

---

## backward recovery

> reconstruction of a file to a given state by reversing all changes made to the file since it was in that state

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## bag

> a collection class whose members are unordered but in which duplicates are meaningful (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.11)

---

## ball grid array (BGA)

> surface-mounted integrated circuit package with multiple connections in a grid pattern on the bottom surface (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765c:2014)

---

## base address

> address used as a reference point to which a relative address is added to determine the address of the storage location to be accessed (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## base choice

> input parameter value that is normally selected based on being a representative or typical value for the parameter (ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-1:2022, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 1: General concepts, 3.11)

---

## base class

> relationship between a template class CB of instances of B and template class CA of instances of A, where template A is an incremental modification of template B (ISO/IEC 10746-2:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Foundations, 9.24)

---

## base functional component (BFC)

> an elementary unit of functional user requirements defined by and used by an FSM method for measurement purposes (ISO/IEC 19761:2011 Software engineering -- COSMIC: a functional size measurement method, 2.1) (ISO/IEC 20926:2009 Software and systems engineering -- Software measurement -- IFPUG functional size measurement method 2009, 3.8) (ISO/IEC 14143-1:2007 Information technology--Software measurement-- Functional size measurement; Part 1: Definition of concepts, 3.1)

---

## base functional component type (BFC type)

> defined category of Base Functional Component

_Source: ISO/IEC 19761:2011 Software engineering -- COSMIC: a functional size measurement method, 2.2_

---

## base measure

> measure defined in terms of an attribute and the method for quantifying it (ISO/IEC/IEEE 15939:2017 Systems and software engineering--Measurement process, 3.3)

---

## base practice (BP)

> activity that, when consistently performed, contributes to achieving a specific process purpose

_Source: ISO/IEC 33001:2015 Information technology--Process assessment--Concepts and terminology, 3.3.2_

---

## base standard

> approved International Standard or Telecommunication Standardization Sector of the International Telecommunications Union (ITU-T) Recommendation (ISO/IEC 29110-1-2:2024, Systems and software engineering Lifecycle profiles for Very Small Entities (VSEs) Part 1-2: Vocabulary, 3.17)

---

## baseline

> formally approved version of a configuration item, regardless of media, formally designated and fixed at a specific time during the configuration item's life cycle (ISO/IEC/IEEE 12207:2026 Systems and software engineering-- Software life cycle processes, 3.1.12)

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288:2023 Systems and software engineering--System life cycle processes, 3.8_

---

## baseline data

> data collected at the beginning of a process and used for comparison to subsequently collected data

_Source: IEEE 7010-2020, IEEE Recommended Practice for Assessing the Impact of Autonomous and Intelligent Systems on Human Well-Being, 2.1_

---

## baseline design

> system design that has been agreed on by all stakeholders interested in the system development

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## baseline document

> system or software document that defines a work product that has been placed under configuration management

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## baseline function point count

> application function point count taken of the functionality at a point in time, from which changes can be measured

_Source: ISO/IEC 20968:2002 Software engineering -- Mk II Function Point Analysis -- Counting Practices Manual, 10_

---

## baseline management

> in configuration management, the application of technical and administrative direction to designate the documents and changes to those documents that formally identify and establish baselines at specific times during the life cycle of a configuration item

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## basic assumption

> assumption in a collection of claims shared by assurance cases in a specific field (ISO/IEC/IEEE 15026-2:2022, Systems and software engineering Systems and software assurance Part 2: Assurance case, 3.1.3)

---

## basic engineering object

> engineering object that requires the support of a distributed infrastructure (ISO/IEC 10746-3:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Architecture, 8.1.1)

---

## basic flow

> part of a use case that describes its most common implementation (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## basic interworking facility

> a set of service primitives which have a direct correspondence with computational signals which model computational operations

_Source: ISO/IEC 14752:2000 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Protocol support for computational interactions, 3.3.4_

---

## basic maturity level

> lowest level of achievement in a scale of organizational process maturity

_Source: ISO/IEC 33001:2015 Information technology--Process assessment--Concepts and terminology, 3.3.3_

---

## basic process set

> set of processes, which help ensure the achievement of the basic maturity level (ISO/IEC

---

## basic profile

> profile targeted at very small entities (VSE) developing a single product by a single work team (ISO/IEC 29110-1-2:2024, Systems and software engineering Lifecycle profiles for Very Small Entities (VSEs) Part 1-2: Vocabulary, 3.18)

---

## basic symbol

> symbol used when the precise nature or form of, for example, the process or data media is not known or when it is not necessary to depict the actual medium

_Source: ISO 5807:1985 Information processing -- Documentation symbols and conventions for data, program and system flowcharts, program network charts and system resources charts, 3.1_

---

## basis of estimates

> supporting documentation outlining the details used in establishing project estimates such as assumptions, constraints, level of detail, ranges, and confidence levels (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## basis set

> set of objects used to create a multiset

_Source: ISO/IEC 15909-1:2019 Systems and software engineering-- High-level Petri nets--Part 1: Concepts, definitions and graphical notation, 2.1.4_

---

## BAT

> best available technique

_Source: IEEE 7000:2021, IEEE Standard Model Process for Addressing Ethical Concerns during System Design, 3.2_

---

## batch

> pertaining to a system or mode of operation in which inputs are collected and processed all at one time, rather than being processed as they arrive, and a job, once started, proceeds to completion without additional input or user interaction (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## bathtub curve

> graph of the number of failures in a system or component as a function of time (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## Bayes' rule

> statistical formula that relates the conditional probability P(A | B) to the inverse conditional probability P(B | A)

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765j:2021_

---

## BCWP

> budgeted cost of work performed

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765c:2014_

---

## BCWS

> budgeted cost of work scheduled

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765c:2014_

---

## BDD

> behavior-driven development

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 7052:2023, Software engineering--Controlling frequently occurring risks during development and maintenance of custom software, 3.5_

---

## BDIR

> biometric data interchange record

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 29119-13:2022, Software and systems engineering Software testing Part 13: Using the ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119 series in the testing of biometric systems, 3.2_

---

## behavior

> observable activity of a system, measurable in terms of quantifiable effects on the environment whether arising from internal or external stimulus

---

## behavior-driven development (BDD)

> development method in which team members with various backgrounds [developers, testers and business analysts] jointly describe the behavior of the intended functionality prior to development of the relevant functionality (ISO/IEC TR 7052:2023, Software engineering--Controlling frequently occurring risks during development and maintenance of custom software, 3.5)

---

## behavioral compatibility

> identical behavior of two objects, such that one object can replace the other with respect to a set of criteria without the environment being able to notice the difference in the objects' behavior on the basis of the set of criteria

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-2:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Foundations, 9.4_

---

## benchmark

> reference point against which comparisons can be made

_Source: ISO/IEC 29155-1:2017 Systems and software engineering--Information technology project performance benchmarking framework--Part 1: Concepts and definitions, 2.1_

---

## benchmark suite

> collection of tests used to compare the performance of alternatives

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 29119- 11:2020, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 11: Guidelines on the testing of AI-based systems, 3.1.18_

---

## benchmarking

> activity of comparing objects of interest to each other or against a benchmark to evaluate characteristic(s)

_Source: ISO/IEC 29155-1:2017 Systems and software engineering--Information technology project performance benchmarking framework--Part 1: Concepts and definitions, 2.2_

---

## benchmarking analyst

> person or organization that conducts benchmarking activity

_Source: ISO/IEC 29155-3:2015 Systems and software engineering--Information technology project performance benchmarking framework--Part 3: Guidance for reporting_

---

## benchmarking experience base

> information store that contains the evaluation of the information products and the benchmarking activity, as well as any lessons learned during benchmarking and analysis

_Source: ISO/IEC 29155-1:2017 Systems and software engineering--Information technology project performance benchmarking framework--Part 1: Concepts and definitions, 2.3_

---

## benchmarking method

> logical sequence of general steps to describe the process of comparing one or more attributes against a reference attribute with respect to a specified scale

_Source: ISO/IEC 29155-1:2017 Systems and software engineering--Information technology project performance benchmarking framework--Part 1: Concepts and definitions, 2.4_

---

## benchmarking report

> document of the results of an instance of benchmarking (ISO/IEC 29155-1:2017 Systems and software engineering--Information technology project performance benchmarking framework--Part 1: Concepts and definitions, 3.7)

---

## benchmarking repository

> organized and persistent data storage which is designated for benchmarking

_Source: ISO/IEC 29155-1:2017 Systems and software engineering--Information technology project performance benchmarking framework--Part 1: Concepts and definitions, 3.8_

---

## benchmarking user

> person or organization that utilizes the outcome of benchmarking

_Source: ISO/IEC 29155-1:2017 Systems and software engineering--Information technology project performance benchmarking framework--Part 1: Concepts and definitions, 2.5_

---

## beneficialness

> extent of benefit resulting from the use of a product, system, or service (ISO/IEC 25019:2023, Systems and software engineering Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) Quality-in- use model, 3.2.1)

---

## benefit

> positive outcome that is voluntarily or involuntarily created by a system or process (IEEE 7000:2021, IEEE Standard Model Process for Addressing Ethical Concerns during System Design, 3.1)

---

## benefit cost analysis

> in not-for-profit decision analysis, evaluating the desirability of an alternative on the ratio of the net benefits to the population to the net costs to the sponsor

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## benefits management plan

> documented explanation defining the processes for creating, maximizing, and sustaining the benefits provided by a project or program (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## BEO

> Basic Engineering Object (ISO/IEC 10746-1:1998 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference model: Overview)

_Source: ISO/IEC 19793:2015 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Use of UML for ODP system specifications, 4_

---

## beta test

> second stage of testing when a product is in limited production use (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## beta testing

> final stage of testing for a computer product prior to commercial or operational release

---

## BFC

> base functional component (ISO/IEC 19761:2011 Software engineering -- COSMIC: a functional size measurement method, 2.1)

_Source: ISO/IEC 14143-1:2007 Information technology--Software measurement--Functional size measurement; Part 1: Definition of concepts, 4_

---

## BFC class

> defined group of BFC types

_Source: ISO/IEC 29881:2010 Information technology--Software and systems engineering--FiSMA 1.1 functional size measurement method, 3.1_

---

## BFC Type

> a defined category of BFCs

_Source: ISO/IEC 14143-1:2007 Information technology--Software measurement-- Functional size measurement; Part 1: Definition of concepts, 3.2_

---

## BGA

> ball grid array

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765c:2014_

---

## bias

> in machine learning (ML), a measure of the distance between the predicted value provided by the ML model and a desired fair prediction

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 29119-11:2020, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 11: Guidelines on the testing of AI-based systems, 3.1.19_

---

## bid documents

> documents used to solicit information, quotations, or proposals from prospective sellers (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## bidder conference

> meetings with prospective sellers prior to the preparation of a bid or proposal to help ensure all prospective vendors have a clear and common understanding of the procurement (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## bidirectional traceability

> association among two or more logical entities that is discernible in either direction (to and from an entity) (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## big-bang testing

> type of integration testing in which software elements, hardware elements, or both are combined all at once into an overall system, rather than in stages

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## bill of materials (BOM)

> documented formal hierarchical tabulation of the physical assemblies, subassemblies, and components needed to fabricate a product

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary_

---

## bill-of-features

> specification for a member product in the product line, rendered in terms of the specific features from the feature catalogue that are chosen for that member product (ISO/IEC 26580:2021, Software and systems engineering Methods and tools for the feature-based approach to software and systems product line engineering, 3.1)

---

## bill-of-features portfolio

> specification for a member product in the product line, rendered in terms of the specific features from the feature catalogue that are chosen for that member product

_Source: ISO/IEC 26580:2021, Software and systems engineering Methods and tools for the feature-based approach to software and systems product line engineering, 3.2_

---

## binary digit (bit)

> unit of information that can be represented by either a zero or a one

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## binary digit (bit)

>

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## binary digit (bit)

>

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## bind

> to assign a value to an identifier (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## binder

> engineering object in a channel, which maintains a distributed binding between interacting basic engineering objects

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-3:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Architecture, 8.1.10_

---

## binding

> contractual context resulting from a given establishing behavior

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-2:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Foundations, 13.5.2_

---

## binding behavior

> establishing behavior between two or more interfaces, and hence between their supporting objects

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-2:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Foundations, 13.5.1_

---

## binding endpoint identifier

> identifier, in the naming context of a capsule, used by a basic engineering object to select one of the bindings in which it is involved, for the purpose of interaction (ISO/IEC 10746-3:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Architecture, 8.1.15)

---

## binding object

> computational object which supports a binding between a set of other computational objects

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-3:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Architecture, 7.1.14_

---

## binding precondition

> set of conditions required for the successful execution of a binding behavior

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-2:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Foundations, 13.5.3_

---

## binding time

> moment of variability resolution (ISO/IEC 26555:2015 Software and systems engineering--Tools and methods for product line technical management, 3.3)

---

## binding time decision

> selection for variability defined in platforms in accordance with the functional distinction between variability in time and variability in space

_Source: ISO/IEC 26557:2016 Software and systems engineering -- Methods and tools for variability mechanisms in software and systems product line, 3.5_

---

## binding time of variability

> stage when the value of variability is determined

_Source: ISO/IEC 26553:2018 Information technology -- Software and systems engineering-- Tools and methods for product line realization, 3.7_

---

## biometric characteristic

> biological and behavioural characteristic of an individual from which distinguishing, repeatable biometric features can be extracted for the purpose of biometric recognition

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 29119-13:2022, Software and systems engineering Software testing Part 13: Using the ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119 series in the testing of biometric systems, 3.1.1_

---

## biometric data

> biometric sample or aggregation of biometric samples at any stage of processing (ISO/IEC TR 29119-13:2022, Software and systems engineering Software testing Part 13: Using the ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119 series in the testing of biometric systems, 3.1.2)

---

## biometric feature

> number or label extracted from biometric samples and used for comparison

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 29119-13:2022, Software and systems engineering Software testing Part 13: Using the ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119 series in the testing of biometric systems, 3.1.3_

---

## biometric identification

> process of searching against a biometric enrollment database to find and return the biometric reference identifiers attributable to a single individual

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 29119-13:2022, Software and systems engineering Software testing Part 13: Using the ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119 series in the testing of biometric systems, 3.1.4_

---

## biometric probe

> biometric sample or biometric feature set input to an algorithm for biometric comparison to a

---

## biometric recognition

> automated recognition of individuals based on their biological and behavioral characteristics

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 29119-13:2022, Software and systems engineering Software testing Part 13: Using the ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119 series in the testing of biometric systems, 3.1.6_

---

## biometric reference

> one or more stored biometric samples, biometric templates, or biometric models attributed to a biometric data subject and used as the object of biometric comparison (ISO/IEC TR 29119-13:2022, Software and systems engineering Software testing Part 13: Using the ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119 series in the testing of biometric systems, 3.1.7)

---

## biometric reference adaptation

> automatic incremental updating of a biometric reference (ISO/IEC TR 29119- 13:2022, Software and systems engineering Software testing Part 13: Using the ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119 series in the testing of biometric systems, 3.1.8)

---

## biometric sample

> analogue or digital representation of biometric characteristics prior to biometric feature extraction

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 29119-13:2022, Software and systems engineering Software testing Part 13: Using the ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119 series in the testing of biometric systems, 3.1.9_

---

## biometric system

> system for the purpose of the biometric recognition of individuals based on their behavioral and biological characteristics

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 29119-13:2022, Software and systems engineering Software testing Part 13: Using the ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119 series in the testing of biometric systems, 3.1.10_

---

## biometric template

> set of stored biometric features comparable directly to a biometric probe (ISO/IEC TR 29119-13:2022, Software and systems engineering Software testing Part 13: Using the ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119 series in the testing of biometric systems, 3.1.11)

---

## biometric verification

> process of confirming a biometric claim through comparison (ISO/IEC TR 29119-

---

## bit

> either of the digits 0 or 1 when used in the binary numeration system

_Source: ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary_

---

## bit steering

> microprogramming technique in which the meaning of a field in a microinstruction is dependent on the value of another field in the microinstruction (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## black box

> system or component whose inputs, outputs, and general function are known but whose contents or implementation are unknown or irrelevant

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## block

> group of contiguous storage locations, computer program statements, records, words, characters, or bits that are treated as a unit

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## block diagram

> diagram of a system in which the principal parts or functions are represented by blocks connected by lines that show the relationships of the blocks

_Source: ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary_

---

## block-structured language

> design or programming language in which sequences of statements, called blocks, are defined, usually with begin and end delimiters, and variables or labels defined in one block are not recognized outside that block (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## blocking bug

> software defect that obstructs further work on a particular task or feature until it is resolved

_Source: IEEE 982:2024, Standard for Measures of the Software Aspects of Dependability, 3.1_

---

## blocking factor

> number of records, words, characters, or bits in a block

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## BMP

> bitmap image file

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 26531:2023 Systems and software engineering -- Content management for product lifecycle, user and service management information for users, 3.2_

---

## BMT

> Bench Mark Test

_Source: ISO/IEC 14102:2008 Information Technology - Guideline for the evaluation and selection of CASE tools, 8.2_

---

## body metadata

> elements in the body of an HTML document providing administrative or navigational facilities for the user or administrator

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765l:2024_

---

## body of knowledge

> collection of knowledge items or areas generally agreed to be essential to understanding a

---

## BOK

> body of knowledge

_Source: ISO/IEC 24773-1:2019 Software and systems engineering-Certification of software and systems engineering professionals-Part 1: General requirements, 3.11_

---

## BOM

> bill of materials

_Source: IEEE 7014-2024, IEEE Standard for Ethical Considerations in Emulated Empathy in Autonomous and Intelligent Systems, 3.2_

---

## Boolean expression

> expression that evaluates to true or false

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765i:2020_

---

## Boolean signature

> signature where one of the sorts is Bool, corresponding to the carrier Boolean in any associated algebra, and one of the constants is true sub Bool, corresponding to the value true in the algebra

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765i:2020_

---

## boot

> to initialize a computer system by clearing memory and reloading the operating system (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## boot mode

> initialized mode of program operations when a computer is turned on

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765d:2015_

---

## bootstrap

> short computer program that is permanently resident or easily loaded into a computer and whose execution brings a larger program, such as an operating system or its loader, into memory

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## bootstrap loader

> short computer program used to load a bootstrap

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## bottom-up

> pertaining to an activity that starts with the lowest-level components of a hierarchy and proceeds through progressively higher-levels

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## bottom-up design

> design approach in which low-level pieces of a system are combined into an overall design

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## boundary

> conceptual interface between the software under study and its users (ISO/IEC 20926:2009 Software and systems engineering -- Software measurement -- IFPUG functional size measurement method 2009, 3.9)

_Source: ISO/IEC 29881:2010 Information technology--Software and systems engineering--FiSMA 1.1 functional size measurement method, 3.2_

---

## boundary arrow

> arrow with one end (source or use) not connected to any box in a diagram (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765m:2024, 2.1.14)

---

## boundary value

> data value that corresponds to a minimum or maximum input, internal, or output value specified for a system or component (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## boundary value analysis

> specification-based test design technique based on exercising the boundaries of equivalence partitions

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-1:2022, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 1: General concepts, 3.12_

---

## box

> rectangle containing a box name, a box number, and possibly a box detail reference and representing a function in a diagram

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765m:2024, 2.1.16_

---

## box detail reference

> square enclosure encompassing a box number, which indicates that the box is decomposed or detailed by a child diagram

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765m:2024, 2.1.17_

---

## box diagram

> control flow diagram consisting of a rectangle that is subdivided to show sequential steps, if-then- else conditions, repetition, and case conditions (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary)

---

## box ICOM code

> ICOM code that maps a tunneled boundary arrow to an arrow attached to some ancestral box

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765m:2024, 2.1.18_

---

## box name

> verb or verb phrase placed inside a box that names the modeled function (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765m:2024, 2.1.19)

---

## box number

> single digit (0, 1, 2, ..., 9) placed in the lower right corner of a box to uniquely identify that box in a diagram (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765m:2024, 2.1.20)

---

## BPMN

> business process model and notation

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24641:2023. Systems and Software engineering-- Methods and tools for model-based systems and software engineering, 3.2_

---

## brainstorming

> data-gathering and creativity technique that can be used to identify risks, ideas, or solutions to issues by using a group of team members or subject-matter experts

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765h:2019_

---

## branch

> computer program construct in which one of two or more alternative sets of program statements is selected for execution

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## branch

>

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## branch condition combination testing

> structure-based test case design technique based on exercising combinations of Boolean values of conditions within a decision

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-4:2021 Software and systems engineering -- Software testing -- Part 4: Test techniques, 3.4_

---

## branch condition testing

> structure-based test case design technique based on exercising Boolean values of the conditions within decisions and the decision outcomes

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-4:2021 Software and systems engineering -- Software testing -- Part 4: Test techniques, 3.5_

---

## branch testing

> testing designed to execute each outcome of each decision point in a computer program

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## branching

> method of development in which a set of components is duplicated so the components may be modified in parallel and optionally synchronized at a later time

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 26531:2023 Systems and software engineering -- Content management for product lifecycle, user and service management information for users, 3.1.2_

---

## breadcrumb trail

> navigational aid with a displayed series of links which lead from the home page or another page to the current page

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 23026:2023 Systems and software engineering--Engineering and management of websites for systems, software, and services information, 3.1.3_

---

## break-even analysis

> analysis of two or more objective functions to find where, if at all, they have the same value

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## breakpoint

> point in a computer program at which execution can be suspended to permit manual or automated monitoring of program performance or results (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## brownfield system engineering

> development of future system or system elements in the presence of an existing or legacy system or system elements (INCOSE Systems Engineering Handbook, 5th ed.)

---

## browser

> application allowing a person to retrieve and read hypertext, to view the contents of hypertext nodes (Web pages), to navigate from one web page to another, and to interact with the content, such as changing the visual appearance of the displayed content (ISO/IEC/IEEE 23026:2023 Systems and software engineering--Engineering and

---

## BRS

> business requirements specification

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018 Systems and software engineering-Life cycle processes-Requirements engineering, 4.2_

---

## bubble chart

> data flow, data structure, or other diagram in which entities are depicted with circles (bubbles) and relationships are represented by links drawn between the circles (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## buddy check

> informal review performed independently by a colleague of the author (ISO/IEC 20246:2017 Software and systems engineering -- Work product reviews, 3.3)

---

## budget

> approved estimate for the project or any work breakdown structure component or any schedule activity (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## budget at completion (BAC)

> sum of all the budgets established for the work to be performed (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## buffer

> device, routine, or storage area used to store data temporarily to compensate for differences in rates of data flow, time of occurrence of events, or amounts of data that can be handled by the devices or processes involved in the transfer or use of the data

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## build

> operational version of a system or component that incorporates a specified subset of the capabilities that the final product will provide

_Source: IEEE 828-2012 IEEE Standard for Configuration Management in Systems and Software Engineering, 2.1_

---

## built-in random access memory (RAM)

> RAM embedded in a microcontroller unit (MCU) chip (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765e:2015)

---

## built-in read only memory

> read-only memory embedded in a microcontroller unit (MCU) chip (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765d:2015)

---

## bundle

> grouping of products which is the result of a marketing/licensing strategy to sell entitlements to multiple products as one purchased item

_Source: ISO/IEC 19770-5:2015 Information technology--IT asset management--Overview and vocabulary, 3.5_

---

## burn chart

> graphical representation of the work remaining in a timebox or the work completed toward the release of a product or project deliverable (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## burndown

> indicator of the work completed and an estimate of remaining work to be completed or remaining effort needed to complete a product development iteration cycle (Software Extension to the PMBOK(R) Guide Fifth Edition)

---

## burndown chart

> graph that represents the work remaining to do on a project

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 26511:2018 Systems and software engineering--Requirements for managers of information for users of systems, software, and services, 3.1.6_

---

## burndown rate

> the number of software story points, features, functions, user stories, use cases, or requirements completed per work unit (week or iteration) (Software Extension to the PMBOK(R) Guide Fifth Edition)

---

## burnup

> indicator of the number of story points, features, functions, user stories, use cases, or requirements completed and the work remaining or remaining effort needed to complete a product development iteration cycle (Software Extension to the PMBOK(R) Guide Fifth Edition)

---

## bus

> data communication path in a computer or system

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765c:2014_

---

## business case

> value proposition for a proposed project that may include financial and non-financial benefits (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## business critical product

> product that is essential to the operation of a business or organization, whose sustained failure would result in significant business impacts e.g., loss of revenue or loss of reputation (ISO/IEC 29110-1- 2:2024, Systems and software engineering Lifecycle profiles for Very Small Entities (VSEs) Part 1-2: Vocabulary, 3.21)

---

## business impact analysis

> process of analyzing the impact over time of a disruption on the organization (ISO/IEC TR 7052:2023, Software engineering--Controlling frequently occurring risks during development and maintenance of custom software, 3.8)

---

## business information management

> domain responsible for all of the tasks and activities that are aimed at supporting the end users in the use of the application and at acting as the customer of the IT organizations (ISO/IEC 16350-2015 Information technology--Systems and software engineering--Application management, 4.8)

---

## business model canvas

> one-page visual summary that describes the value proposition, infrastructure, customers, and finances (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## business objective

> strategy designed by senior management to help ensure an organization's continued existence and enhance its profitability, market share, and other factors influencing the organization's success

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 41062:2024, Software engineering Life cycle processes Software acquisition, 3.1.4_

---

## business process

> partially ordered set of enterprise activities that can be executed to achieve some desired end-result in pursuit of a given objective of an organization

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 12207:2026 Systems and software engineering--Software life cycle processes, 3.1.13_

---

## business requirement

> business need that the organization aims to meet

_Source: IEEE 7002:2022, IEEE Standard for Data Privacy Process, 3.1_

---

## business requirements specification (BRS)

> structured collection of the requirements (business or mission problem or opportunity definition, concepts, and required conditions of solutions) of the business or mission and its relation to the external environment

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018 Systems and software engineering-Life cycle processes-Requirements engineering, 3.1.4_

---

## business value

> net quantifiable benefit derived from a business endeavor that may be tangible, intangible, or both (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## busy

> pertaining to a system or component that is operational, in service, and in use (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## busy time

> in computer performance engineering, the period of time during which a system or component is operational, in service, and in use (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## buyer

> an individual or organization responsible for acquiring a product or service for use by themselves or other users

---

## byte

> group of adjacent binary digits operated upon as a unit and usually shorter than a computer word

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## C4I

> command, control, communications, computer, and intelligence

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-8:2019, Systems and software engineering Life cycle management Part 7: Application of systems engineering on defense programs, 3.2_

---

## cache

> temporary storage in computer memory, to improve operations by having frequently used data readily available for retrieval

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765c:2014_

---

## CAD

> Computer Aided Design

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-1:1998 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference model: Overview_

---

## cadence

> frequency of performing a periodic activity, such as incremental product release (Software Extension to the PMBOK(R) Guide Fifth Edition)

---

## CAE

> claims arguments evidence

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 15026-2:2022, Systems and software engineering Systems and software assurance Part 2: Assurance case, 3.2_

---

## CAI

> critical application item

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-7:2019, Systems and software engineering Life cycle management Part 7: Application of systems engineering on defense programs, 3.2_

---

## calculator

> device that is suitable for performing arithmetic operations, but that requires human intervention to alter its stored program, if any, and to initiate each operation or sequence of operations (ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary)

---

## calendar unit

> smallest unit of time used in scheduling a project (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765c:2014)

---

## call

> transfer of control from one software module to another, usually with the implication that control will be returned to the calling module

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## call

> and, often, to pass parameters to the other module

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## call by name

> method for passing parameters, in which the calling module provides to the called module a symbolic expression representing the parameter to be passed, and a service routine evaluates the expression and provides the resulting value to the called module (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary)

---

## call by reference

> a method for passing parameters, in which the calling module provides to the called module the address of the parameter to be passed (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## call by value

> method of passing parameters, in which the calling module provides to the called module the actual value of the parameter to be passed (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## call graph

> diagram that identifies the modules in a system or computer program and shows which modules call one another (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## call list

> ordered list of arguments used in a call to a software module

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## call reference

> page reference attached to a call arrow

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765m:2024, 2.1.24_

---

## called diagram

> decomposition diagram invoked by a calling box and identified by a page reference attached to a call arrow

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765m:2024, 2.1.25_

---

## calling box

> box that is detailed by a decomposition diagram that is not the box's child diagram (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765m:2024, 2.1.26)

---

## calling sequence

> sequence of computer instructions and, possibly, data necessary to perform a call to another module

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## CAN

> controller area network

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765d:2015_

---

## candidate

> applicant who has fulfilled prerequisites and has been admitted to the certification process

_Source: ISO/IEC 24773-1:2019 Software and systems engineering-Certification of software and systems engineering professionals-Part 1: General requirements, 3,3_

---

## candidate FSM method

> documented software size measurement method submitted for conformity evaluation

_Source: ISO/IEC 14143-2:2011 Information technology -- Software measurement -- Functional size measurement -- Part 2: Conformity evaluation of software size measurement methods to ISO/IEC 14143-1, 3.1_

---

## candidate key

> attribute, or combination of attributes, of an entity for which no two instances agree on the values (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.14)

---

## CAPA

> corrective and preventative actions

_Source: IEEE 982:2024, Standard for Measures of the Software Aspects of Dependability, 3.2_

---

## capability

> ability to do something useful under a particular set of conditions (ISO/IEC/IEEE 12207:2026 Systems and software engineering--Software life cycle processes, 3.1.14)

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24641:2023. Systems and Software engineering--Methods and tools for model-based systems and software engineering, 3.1.3_

---

## capability maturity model

> model that contains the essential elements of effective processes for one or more disciplines and describes an evolutionary improvement path from ad hoc, immature processes to disciplined, mature processes with improved quality and effectiveness

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary_

---

## capability requirement

> requirement that specifies a condition or behavior that a system is to achieve under stated conditions (IEEE 982:2024, Standard for Measures of the Software Aspects of Dependability, 3.1)

---

## capable process

> process that can satisfy specified product quality, service quality, and process-performance objectives (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## capacity

> capability of a product to meet requirements for the maximum limits of a product parameter (ISO/IEC 25010:2023, Systems and software engineering--Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)- -Product quality model, 3.2.3)

---

## capacity place

> special kind of place that can hold no more than a specified capacity

_Source: ISO/IEC 15909-3:2021. Systems and software engineering--High-level Petri nets--Part 3: Extensions and structuring mechanisms, 3.2_

---

## capacity testing

> type of performance efficiency testing conducted to evaluate the level at which increasing load (of users, transactions, data storage, etc.) compromises a test item's ability to sustain required performance

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765k:2022_

---

## capital expenditure

> spending by an enterprise to acquire tangible infrastructure or facilities items, such as furniture, computers, and the like (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765a:2011)

---

## capsule

> configuration of engineering objects forming a single unit for the purpose of encapsulation of processing and storage

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-3:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Architecture, 8.1.4_

---

## capsule manager

> engineering object which manages the engineering objects in a capsule

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746- 3:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Architecture, 8.1.5_

---

## captured biometric sample

> biometric sample resulting from a biometric capture process (ISO/IEC TR 29119- 13:2022, Software and systems engineering Software testing Part 13: Using the ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119 series in the

---

## CARD

> cost analysis requirements description

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-8:2019, Systems and software engineering Life cycle management Part 7: Application of systems engineering on defense programs, 3.2_

---

## cardinality

> constraint on the number of entity instances that are related to the subject entity through a relationship

_Source: ISO/IEC 15474-1:2002 Information technology -- CDIF framework -- Part 1: Overview, 4.2_

---

## cardinality constraint

> constraint that limits the number of instances that can be associated with each other in a relationship

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.16_

---

## carrier

> set of a many-sorted algebra

_Source: ISO/IEC 15909-1:2019 Systems and software engineering--High-level Petri nets--Part 1: Concepts, definitions and graphical notation, 3.4_

---

## case

> single-entry, single-exit multiple-way branch that defines a control expression, specifies the processing to be performed for each value of the control expression, and returns control in all instances to the statement immediately following the overall construct

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## CASE needs

> organizational requirements which are met by CASE tool characteristics (ISO/IEC TR 14471:2007 Information technology--Software engineering--Guidelines for the adoption of CASE tools, 2.1.3)

---

## CASE tool

> software product that can assist software and system engineers by providing automated support for software and system engineering life-cycle activities

_Source: ISO/IEC 15940:2013 Systems and software engineering--Software Engineering Environment Services, 2.3_

---

## cast

> to treat an object of one type as an object of another type (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.17)

---

## catastrophic failure (CF)

> failure of critical software

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025_

---

## categorization

> specific way to allocate a target system into a category (ISO/IEC TR 12182:2015 Systems and software engineering--Framework for categorization of IT systems and software, and guide for applying it, 3.5)

---

## categorization scheme

> orderly combination of views and categories related to software

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## categorization space

> universal set of systems and software which has one or more classification axes as its individual dimension, by which stakeholder's concerns on categorization are expressed

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 12182:2015 Systems and software engineering--Framework for categorization of IT systems and software, and guide for applying it, 3.6_

---

## category

> specifically defined division or grouping of software based upon one or more attributes or characteristics

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## category entity

> entity whose instances represent a subtype or subclassification of another entity (generic entity) (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.21)

---

## causal analysis

> analysis of a defect to determine its cause

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## cause-and-effect diagram

> visual representation that helps trace an undesirable effect back to its root cause (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## cause-effect graph

> graphical representation of decision rules between causes (inputs described as Boolean conditions) and effects (outputs described as Boolean expressions)

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-4:2021 Software and systems engineering -- Software testing -- Part 4: Test techniques, 3.8_

---

## cause-effect graphing

> specification-based test case design technique based on exercising decision rules in a cause-effect graph

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-4:2021 Software and systems engineering -- Software testing -- Part 4: Test techniques, 3.9_

---

## caution

> hazardous situation which, if not avoided, can result in minor or moderate injury (ISO/IEC/IEEE 26513:2017 Systems and software engineering--Requirements for testers and reviewers of information for users, 3.5)

---

## CBa

> conduct benchmarking activity

_Source: ISO/IEC 29155-2:2013 Systems and software engineering--Information technology project performance benchmarking framework--Part 2: Requirements for benchmarking, 4_

---

## CBT

> computer-based training

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 26511:2018 Systems and software engineering--Requirements for managers of information for users of systems, software, and services, 3.2_

---

## CCB

> configuration control board

_Source: IEEE 828-2012 IEEE Standard for Configuration Management in Systems and Software Engineering, 2.2_

---

## CCMS

> component content management system

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 26531:2023 Systems and software engineering -- Content management for product lifecycle, user and service management information for users, 3.1.4_

---

## CCTV

> closed-circuit television

_Source: IEEE 1012-2024 IEEE Standard for System, Software, and Hardware Verification and Validation, 3.2_

---

## CD

> compact disk

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765a:2011_

---

## CD-ROM

> compact disc, read-only memory (IEEE 828-2012 IEEE Standard for Configuration Management in Systems and Software Engineering, 2.2)

---

## CDD

> capability development document

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-8:2019, Systems and software engineering Life cycle management Part 7: Application of systems engineering on defense programs, 3.2_

---

## CDIF

> CASE Data Interchange Format (originally)

_Source: ISO/IEC 15474-1:2002 Information technology -- CDIF framework -- Part 1: Overview, 5.2_

---

## CDIF clear text encoding

> clear text file encoding of a CDIF transfer file

_Source: ISO/IEC 15474-1:2002 Information technology -- CDIF framework -- Part 1: Overview, 4.2_

---

## CDIF exporter

> tool that creates a CDIF transfer file

_Source: ISO/IEC 15474-1:2002 Information technology -- CDIF framework -- Part 1: Overview, 4.2_

---

## CDIF family of standards

> set of standards that, when used together, provide a standard definition for the interchange of information between modeling tools

_Source: ISO/IEC 15474-1:2002 Information technology -- CDIF framework -- Part 1: Overview, 4.2_

---

## CDIF graphical notation

> set of rules governing the representation of CDIF modeling concepts in diagrams

_Source: ISO/IEC 15474-1:2002 Information technology -- CDIF framework -- Part 1: Overview, 4.2_

---

## CDIF identifier

> attribute that uniquely identifies an object in the model section of a transfer

_Source: ISO/IEC 15474- 1:2002 Information technology -- CDIF framework -- Part 1: Overview, 4.2_

---

## CDIF importer

> tool that reads a CDIF transfer file and uses it to create or modify a model

_Source: ISO/IEC 15474-1:2002 Information technology -- CDIF framework -- Part 1: Overview, 4.2_

---

## CDIF meta-metamodel

> description of the set of concepts and notations used to define a metamodel (ISO/IEC 15474-1:2002 Information technology -- CDIF framework -- Part 1: Overview, 4.2)

---

## CDIF metaidentifier

> meta-meta-attribute that uniquely identifies a meta-object in the metamodel section of a transfer

_Source: ISO/IEC 15474-1:2002 Information technology -- CDIF framework -- Part 1: Overview, 4.2_

---

## CDIF semantic metamodel

> description of the set of concepts and notations used to define a model (ISO/IEC 15474-1:2002 Information technology -- CDIF framework -- Part 1: Overview, 4.2)

---

## CDIF transfer

> combination of a particular syntax, a particular encoding of that syntax, and a metamodel (ISO/IEC 15474-1:2002 Information technology -- CDIF framework -- Part 1: Overview, 4.2)

---

## CDIF transfer file

> transfer file conforming to ISO/IEC 15475

_Source: ISO/IEC 15474-1:2002 Information technology -- CDIF framework -- Part 1: Overview, 4.1_

---

## CDIF transfer format

> combination of a particular syntax and a particular encoding of that syntax which together provides a complete definition of the transfer format

_Source: ISO/IEC 15474-1:2002 Information technology -- CDIF framework -- Part 1: Overview, 4.2_

---

## CDIF transfer syntax and encoding

> standard vehicle format supported by CDIF (ISO/IEC 15474-1:2002 Information technology -- CDIF framework -- Part 1: Overview, 4.2)

---

## CDR

> critical design review

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-7:2019, Systems and software engineering Life cycle management Part 7: Application of systems engineering on defense programs, 3.2_

---

## CDRL

> contract data requirements list

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 16326:2019 Systems and software engineering -- Life cycle processes -- Project management, 3_

---

## CE

> Conformite' Europe'enne

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 26511:2018 Systems and software engineering--Requirements for managers of information for users of systems, software, and services, 3.2_

---

## CEN

> European Committee for Standardization (ISO/IEC TR 29119-11:2020, Software and systems engineering-- Software testing--Part 11: Guidelines on the testing of AI-based systems, 3.2)

---

## CENELEC

> European Committee for Electrotechnical Standardization (ISO/IEC TR 29119-11:2020, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 11: Guidelines on the testing of AI-based systems, 3.2)

---

## central processing unit (CPU)

> functional unit that consists of one or more processors and their internal storage

_Source: ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary_

---

## central tendency

> property of the central limit theorem predicting that the data observations in a distribution will tend to group around a central location (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765h:2019)

---

## certificant

> recipient or holder of a certification

_Source: ISO/IEC 24773-1:2019 Software and systems engineering- Certification of software and systems engineering professionals-Part 1: General requirements, 3.4_

---

## certificate

> attestation document issued by an independent third-party certification body

_Source: ISO/IEC 23643:2020, Software and systems engineering--Capabilities of software safety and security verification tools, 3.3_

---

## certification

> third-party attestation related to an object of conformity assessment, with the exception of accreditation (ISO/IEC 29110-1-2:2024, Systems and software engineering Lifecycle profiles for Very Small Entities (VSEs) Part 1-2: Vocabulary, 3.22)

---

## certification artifact

> tangible results from a certification process

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## certification body

> third-party conformity assessment body operating certification schemes (ISO/IEC 29110-1- 2:2024, Systems and software engineering Lifecycle profiles for Very Small Entities (VSEs) Part 1-2: Vocabulary, 3.23)

---

## certification criteria

> set of standards, rules, or properties to which an asset conforms in order to be certified to a certain level (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## certification process

> activities by which a certification body determines that a person fulfils certification requirements, including application, assessment, decision on certification, recertification and use of certificates and logos/marks

_Source: ISO/IEC 24773-1:2019 Software and systems engineering-Certification of software and systems engineering professionals-Part 1: General requirements, 3.6_

---

## certification property

> a statement about some feature or characteristic of an asset that can be assessed as being true or false during a certification process (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary)

---

## certification requirements

> set of specified requirements, including requirements of the scheme to be fulfilled in order to establish or maintain certification of a candidate

_Source: ISO/IEC 24773-1:2019 Software and systems engineering- Certification of software and systems engineering professionals-Part 1: General requirements, 3.7_

---

## certification scheme

> competence and other requirements related to specific occupational or skilled categories of persons

_Source: ISO/IEC 24773-1:2019 Software and systems engineering-Certification of software and systems engineering professionals-Part 1: General requirements, 3.8_

---

## certification scheme owner

> person or organization that is responsible for developing and maintaining a specific certification scheme (ISO/IEC 29110-1-2:2024, Systems and software engineering Lifecycle profiles for Very Small Entities (VSEs) Part 1-2: Vocabulary, 3.25)

---

## CFD

> cumulative flow diagram (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## CFP

> COSMIC function point

_Source: ISO/IEC 19761:2011 Software engineering -- COSMIC: a functional size measurement method, 3_

---

## CFR

> Code of Federal Regulations (US)

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 23026:2023 Systems and software engineering--Engineering and management of websites for systems, software, and services information, 3.2_

---

## chain

> one or more tasks submitted to the SUT in a defined sequence

_Source: ISO/IEC 14756:1999 Information technology -- Measurement and rating of performance of computer-based software systems, 4.3_

---

## chain of custody

> process of maintaining and documenting the handling of evidence (IEEE 7005 2021, IEEE Standard for Transparent Employer Data Governance, 3.1)

---

## chain type

> classification of chains which is defined by the sequence of tasks types (ISO/IEC 14756:1999 Information technology -- Measurement and rating of performance of computer-based software systems, 4.4)

---

## change

> modification to any formally controlled deliverable, project management plan component, or project document (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## change control

> process whereby modifications to documents, deliverables, or baselines associated with the project are identified, documented, approved, or rejected (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## change control board (CCB)

> formally chartered group responsible for reviewing, evaluating, approving, delaying, or rejecting changes to a project, and for recording and communicating such decisions (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## change control plan

> component of the project management plan that establishes the change control board, documents the extent of its authority, and describes how the change control system will be implemented (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## change control procedure

> actions taken to identify, document, review, and authorize changes to a software or documentation product that is being developed (ISO/IEC/IEEE 26514:2022, Systems and software engineering -- Design and development of information for users, 4.8)

---

## change control system

> set of procedures that describes how modifications to the project deliverables and documentation are managed and controlled (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## change dump

> selective dump of those storage locations whose contents have changed since some specified time or event (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## change log

> comprehensive list of changes submitted during the project and their current status (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## change management

> judicious use of means to effect a change, or a proposed change, to a product or service

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## change package

> collection of objects that have been changed and approved and will be transferred to the production environment

_Source: ISO/IEC 16350-2015 Information technology--Systems and software engineering--Application management, 4.10_

---

## change project function point count

> a count that measures the work-output arising from modifications to an existing application that add, change or delete user functions delivered when the project is complete

_Source: ISO/IEC 20968:2002 Software engineering -- Mk II Function Point Analysis -- Counting Practices Manual, 10_

---

## change record

> record containing details of which configuration items are affected and how they are affected by an authorized change

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765a:2011_

---

## change request

> formal proposal to modify a document, deliverable, or baseline (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## change set

> collection of objects which can undergo change as the result of a release

_Source: ISO/IEC 16350-2015 Information technology--Systems and software engineering--Application management, 4.11_

---

## changeover system

> temporary information processing system used to facilitate the transition from an operational system to its successor

_Source: ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary_

---

## channel

> approach to distributing products and services from the original supplier to the end-user organization

_Source: ISO/IEC 19770-3:2016 Information technology--IT asset management--Part 3: Entitlement schema, 3.1.3_

---

## channel capacity

> maximum amount of information that can be transferred on a given channel per unit of time;

---

## channel partner

> person or entity working with a software licensor or another person/entity within the channel who facilitates the sale of software to the end-user

_Source: ISO/IEC 19770-3:2016 Information technology--IT asset management-- Part 3: Entitlement schema, 3.1.4_

---

## character

> letter, digit, or other symbol that is used to represent information

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## character set

> collection of characters used in an encoding to represent terminal symbols (ISO/IEC 15474-1:2002 Information technology -- CDIF framework -- Part 1: Overview, 4.2)

---

## character type

> data type whose members can assume the values of specified characters and can be operated on by character operators, such as concatenation (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary)

---

## characteristic

> inherent factor of software development that may have a significant impact on productivity

---

## characteristic entity

> meta-entity that provides additional attribution for another meta-object (ISO/IEC 15474- 1:2002 Information technology -- CDIF framework -- Part 1: Overview, 4.2)

---

## characteristic of FUR

> a distinctive property of the FUR that is important for identifying the functional domain to which a specific set of FUR belongs

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 14143-5:2004 Information technology -- Software measurement -- Functional size measurement -- Part 5: Determination of functional domains for use with functional size measurement, 3.1_

---

## chart of accounts

> numbering system used by a project or organization to identify costs by category, such as labor, supplies, materials, and equipment (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## check sheet

> tally sheet that can be used as a checklist when gathering data (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## checklist-based reviewing

> review technique guided by a list of questions or required attributes

_Source: ISO/IEC 20246:2017 Software and systems engineering -- Work product reviews, 3.4_

---

## checkout

> testing conducted in the operational or support environment to help ensure that a software product performs as required after installation

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering--Vocabulary_

---

## checkpoint

> point in a computer program at which program state, status, or results are checked or recorded

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## checkpointing

> creating a checkpoint (ISO/IEC 10746-3:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Architecture, 8.1.21)

---

## chief programmer

> leader of a chief programmer team; a senior-level programmer whose responsibilities include producing key portions of the software assigned to the team, coordinating the activities of the team, reviewing the work of the other team members, and having an overall technical understanding of the software being developed (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## chief programmer team

> software development group that consists of a chief programmer, a backup programmer, a secretary/librarian, and additional programmers and specialists as needed, and that employs procedures designed to enhance group communication and to make optimum use of each member's skills (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## child box

> box in a child diagram

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765m:2024, 2.1.27_

---

## child diagram

> decomposition diagram related to a specific box by exactly one child/parent relationship

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765m:2024, 2.1.28_

---

## child entity

> entity in a specific relationship whose instances can be related to zero or one instance of the other entity (parent entity)

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.22_

---

## child tag

> tag that has a subsidiary relationship to another tag

---

## CI

> configuration item

_Source: IEEE 2675-2021, IEEE Standard for DevOps: Building Reliable and Secure Systems Including Application Build, Package, and Deployment, 3.2_

---

## CI/CD

> continuous integration/continuous delivery

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 29119-11:2020, Software and systems engineering-- Software testing--Part 11: Guidelines on the testing of AI-based systems, 3.2_

---

## CIA

> confidentiality, integrity, availability

_Source: IEEE 7002:2022, IEEE Standard for Data Privacy Process, 3.2_

---

## CIF

> common industry format (ISO/IEC 25062:2025 Systems and software engineering -- Systems and software product Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) -- Common Industry Format (CIF) for reporting usability evaluations)

---

## CIM

> Computer Integrated Manufacturing

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-1:1998 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference model: Overview_

---

## CIR

> configuration item record

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24641:2023. Systems and Software engineering--Methods and tools for model-based systems and software engineering, 3.2_

---

## clabject

> dual entity that is a class and an object at the same time (ISO/IEC 24744:2014 Software Engineering-- Metamodel for development methodologies, 3.13)

---

## claim

> true-false statement about the limitations on the values of an unambiguously defined property--called the

---

## class

> abstraction of the knowledge and behavior of a set of similar things

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.23_

---

## class hierarchy

> ordering of classes, in which a subclass is a specialization of its superclass (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## class-level attribute

> mapping from the class itself to the instances of a value class

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.24_

---

## class-level operation

> mapping from the (cross product of the) class itself and the instances of the input argument types to the (cross product of the) instances of the other (output) argument types

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.25_

---

## class-level responsibility

> responsibility that represents some aspect of the knowledge, behavior, or rules of the class as a whole (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.26)

---

## classification

> machine learning function that predicts the output class for a given input

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 29119- 11:2020, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 11: Guidelines on the testing of AI-based systems, 3.1.20_

---

## classification axis

> total range of a mapping of systems and software for categorizing them from a particular perspective

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 12182:2015 Systems and software engineering--Framework for categorization of IT systems and software, and guide for applying it, 3.7_

---

## classification tree

> hierarchical tree model of the input data to a program in which the inputs are represented by distinct classifications (relevant test aspects) and classes (input values)

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-4:2021 Software and systems engineering -- Software testing -- Part 4: Test techniques, 3.10_

---

## classification tree method

> specification-based test case design technique based on exercising classes in a classification tree

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-4:2021 Software and systems engineering -- Software testing -- Part 4: Test techniques, 3.11_

---

## classifier

> machine learning model used for classification

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 29119-11:2020, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 11: Guidelines on the testing of AI-based systems, 3.1.20_

---

## clear

> to set a variable, register, or other storage location to zero, blank, or other null value (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## clear text file encoding

> class of techniques for representing data based on first defining a human readable representation using some specific character repertoire and then defining an encoding for that repertoire

_Source: ISO/IEC 15474- 1:2002 Information technology -- CDIF framework -- Part 1: Overview, 4.1_

---

## client

> entity, code or process that invokes an operation on an object or requests a service from the server

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765l:2024_

---

## client object

> object which requests that a service be performed by another object

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-2:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Foundations, 13.4.5_

---

## client-side
