> type of cost-reimbursable contract where the buyer reimburses the seller for the seller's allowable costs (allowable costs are defined by the contract), and the seller earns its profit if it meets defined performance criteria (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## cost variance (CV)

> amount of budget deficit or surplus at a given point in time, expressed as the difference between the earned value and the actual cost (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## cost-benefit analysis

> financial analysis tool used to determine the benefits provided by a project against its costs (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## cost-plus-fee (CPF)

> contract in which the acquirer reimburses the supplier's allowable costs for performing the contract work and also pays a fee (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## cost-reimbursable contract

> type of contract involving payment to the seller for the seller's actual costs, plus a fee typically representing the seller's profit (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## COTS

> commercial-off-the-shelf

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 90003:2018 Software engineering -- Guidelines for the application of ISO 9001:2015 to computer software, 3.4_

---

## countable failure

> anomalous behavior that does not satisfy one or more of a systems explicit or revealed capability requirements; meets or exceeds the severity criteria of its reliability class; and the tick at which it occurred is imputed or recorded

_Source: IEEE 982:2024, Standard for Measures of the Software Aspects of Dependability, 3.1_

---

## counter

> variable used to record the number of occurrences of a given event during the execution of a computer program

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## counting rule

> conditions and procedures under which the measurement value is obtained

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## counting scope

> set of Functional User Requirements to be included in the function point count

_Source: ISO/IEC 20926:2009 Software and systems engineering -- Software measurement -- IFPUG functional size measurement method 2009, 3.14_

---

## coupling

> manner and degree of interdependence between software modules

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## courtesy

> degree to which the IT service is provided in a polite, respectful and friendly way

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 33014:2013 Information technology--Process assessment--Guide for process improvement, 3.2.2.6_

---

## CPAF

> cost plus award fee (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## CPC

> computer program component (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## CPCI

> computer program configuration item (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary)

---

## CPD

> continuing professional development

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765j:2021_

---

## CPF

> cost-plus-fee

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765c:2014_

---

## CPFF

> cost plus fixed fee (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## CPG

> clock pulse generator

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765d:2015_

---

## CPI

> cost performance index (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## CPIF

> cost plus incentive fee (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## CPLD

> complex programmable logic device

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765d:2015_

---

## CPM

> critical path method (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## CPPC

> cost plus percentage of cost

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## CPU

> central processing unit

_Source: IEEE 1012-2024 IEEE Standard for System, Software, and Hardware Verification and Validation, 3.1_

---

## crash

> sudden and complete failure of a computer system or component (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## crashing

> method used to shorten the schedule duration for the least incremental cost by adding resources (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## CRC

> cyclic redundancy check

_Source: IEEE 1012-2024 IEEE Standard for System, Software, and Hardware Verification and Validation, 3.2_

---

## create WBS (work breakdown structure)

> the process of subdividing project deliverables and project work into smaller, more manageable components (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## creation

> of an &lt;X&gt;, instantiating by an action of objects in the model (ISO/IEC 10746-2:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Foundations, 9.18)

---

## crisis

> critical state of affairs in which a decisive, probably undesirable outcome is impending

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## crisis management

> steps to take when a contingency plan does not solve the associated problem

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## criteria

> standards, rules, or tests on which a judgment or decision can be based, or by which a product, service, result, or process can be evaluated (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## critical asset

> asset having potential to significantly impact on the achievement of the organization's objectives (ISO/IEC 19770-1:2017 Information technology -- IT asset management -- Part 1: IT asset management systems-- Requirements, 3.15)

---

## critical chain method

> schedule method that allows the project team to place buffers on any project schedule path to account for limited resources and project uncertainties

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765h:2019_

---

## critical design review (CDR)

> review conducted to verify that the detailed design of one or more configuration items satisfy specified requirements; to establish the compatibility among the configuration items and other items of equipment, facilities, software, and personnel; to assess risk areas for each configuration item; and, as applicable, to assess the results of producibility analyses, review preliminary hardware product specifications, evaluate preliminary test planning, and evaluate the adequacy of preliminary operation and support documents

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## critical information

> information describing the safe use of the software, the security of the information created with the software, or the protection of the sensitive personal information created by or stored with the software (ISO/IEC/IEEE 15289:2019 Systems and software engineering--Content of life-cycle information items (documentation), 5.6)

---

## critical item

> in configuration management, an item within a configuration item that, because of special engineering or logistic considerations, requires an approved specification to establish technical or inventory control at the component level

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## critical path

> sequence of activities that represents the longest path through a project, which determines the shortest possible duration (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## critical path method (CPM)

> method used to estimate the minimum project duration and determine the amount of scheduling flexibility on the logical network paths within the schedule model (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## critical piece first

> system development approach in which the most critical aspects of a system are implemented first (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## critical property

> property that is agreed by primary stakeholders as having serious consequence

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 15026-4:2021, Systems and software engineering Systems and software assurance Part 4: Assurance in the life cycle, 3.6_

---

## critical range

> values used to classify software into the categories of acceptable, marginal, or unacceptable

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765j:2021_

---

## critical section

> section of a task's internal logic that is executed mutually exclusively with other tasks

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## critical system

> system having the potential for serious impact on the users or environment, due to factors including safety, performance, and security

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025_

---

## critical value

> value of a validated metric that is used to identify software that has unacceptable quality

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765j:2021_

---

## criticality

> degree of impact that a requirement, module, error, fault, failure, or other item has on the development or operation of a system

_Source: IEEE 1012-2024 IEEE Standard for System, Software, and Hardware Verification and Validation, 3.1_

---

## cross-assembler

> assembler that executes on one computer but generates machine code for a different computer

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## cross-compiler

> compiler that executes on one computer but generates machine code for a different computer

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## cross-reference generator

> software tool that accepts as input the source code of a computer program and produces as output a listing that identifies each of the program's variables, labels, and other identifiers and indicates which statements in the program define, set, or use each one (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## cross-reference list

> list that identifies each of the variables, labels, and other identifiers in a computer program and indicates which statements in the program define, set, or use each one

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## cross-reference tool

> software maintenance tool that lets the user determine where a variable is used or where a particular procedure is called on

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## CRS

> commonality and reuse strategy

_Source: ISO/IEC 26554:2018 Information technology--Software and systems engineering-Tools and methods for product line testing, 4_

---

## CRUD

> create, read, update, delete

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24641:2023. Systems and Software engineering--Methods and tools for model-based systems and software engineering, 3.2_

---

## cryptographic hash

> Method to verify the authenticity of a system element or software via the production of a checksum

_Source: IEEE 2675-2021, IEEE Standard for DevOps: Building Reliable and Secure Systems Including Application Build, Package, and Deployment, 3.1_

---

## CS

> constituent system (ISO/IEC/IEEE 21840:2019 Systems and software engineering--Guidelines for the utilization of ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288 in the context of system of systems (SoS), 3.2)

---

## CSA

> configuration status accounting

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 18018:2010 Information technology--Systems and software engineering--Guide for configuration management tool capabilities, 3.10_

---

## CSAS

> containment spray actuation signal

_Source: IEEE 1012-2024 IEEE Standard for System, Software, and Hardware Verification and Validation, 3.2_

---

## CSC

> computer software component

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## CSCI

> computer software configuration item (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary)

---

## CSF

> critical success factor

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 14471:2007 Information technology--Software engineering--Guidelines for the adoption of CASE tools, 2.2_

---

## CSN

> cloud service partner (ISO/IEC TS 25052-1:2022, Systems and software engineering--Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE): cloud services--Part 1: Quality model, 4)

---

## CSS

> cascading style sheets (ISO/IEC 15909-2:2011 Software and system engineering--High-level Petri nets--Part 2: Transfer format, 4.2.1)

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 23026:2023 Systems and software engineering--Engineering and management of websites for systems, software, and services information, 3.2_

---

## CT

> communication technology

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765c:2014_

---

## cumulative flow diagram (CFD)

> chart indicating features completed over time, features in other states of development, and those features in the backlog (Software Extension to the PMBOK(R) Guide Fifth Edition)

---

## curriculum standard

> standard that describes the characteristics of a course of study on a body of knowledge that is offered by an educational institution

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## CUS

> customer (ISO/IEC 29110-5-4:2025, Systems and software engineering Life cycle profiles for very small entities (VSEs) Part 5-4: Agile software development guidelines, 3.2)

---

## custom software

> software product developed for a specific application from a user requirements specification (ISO/IEC 25000:2014 Systems and software Engineering--Systems and software product Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) -- Guide to SQuaRE, 4.3)

---

## customer

> organization or person that receives a product or service (ISO/IEC/IEEE 12207:2026 Systems and software engineering--Software life cycle processes, 3.1.16)

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288:2023 Systems and software engineering--System life cycle processes, 3.12_

---

## customer requirement

> result of eliciting, consolidating, and resolving conflicts among the needs, expectations, constraints, and interfaces of the product's relevant stakeholders in a way that is acceptable to the customer

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## customer satisfaction

> state of fulfillment in which the needs of a customer are met or exceeded for the customer's expected experiences as assessed by the customer at the moment of evaluation

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765h:2019_

---

## customizability

> degree to which the IT service can be customized at the request of users (ISO/IEC TS 25011:2017 Information technology--Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)--Service quality models, 3.2.7.1)

---

## customization

> adaptation of a software or information product to the needs of a particular audience

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 26512:2018 Systems and software engineering--Requirements for acquirers and suppliers of information for users, 3.7_

---

## customize

> adapt a software or information product to the needs of a particular audience

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 26514:2022, Systems and software engineering -- Design and development of information for users, 3.1.15_

---

## cut-off date

> date after which changes to the software are reflected in the next, rather than the current, software release or issue of the documentation

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765a:2011_

---

## cutover

> transfer of functions of a system to its successor at a given moment

_Source: ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary_

---

## CV

> cost variance (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## CVE

> common vulnerabilities and exposures (ISO/IEC/IEEE 23026:2023 Systems and software engineering--

---

## CVSS

> Common Vulnerability Scoring System

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 23026:2023 Systems and software engineering-- Engineering and management of websites for systems, software, and services information, 3.2_

---

## CWBS

> contract work breakdown structure

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-7:2019, Systems and software engineering Life cycle management Part 7: Application of systems engineering on defense programs, 3.2_

---

## cycle

> period of time during which a set of events is completed

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## cycle stealing

> suspending the operation of a central processing unit for one or more cycles to permit the occurrence of other operations, such as transferring data from main memory in response to an output request from an input/output controller

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## cycle time

> time associated with one complete operation of a repetitive process

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 26511:2018 Systems and software engineering--Requirements for managers of information for users of systems, software, and services, 3.1.10_

---

## cycle time chart

> diagram that shows the average cycle time of the work items completed over time (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## cyclic search

> storage allocation technique in which each search for a suitable block of storage begins with the block following the one last allocated

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## DAC

> digital-to-analogue converter

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 29119-13:2022, Software and systems engineering Software testing Part 13: Using the ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119 series in the testing of biometric systems, 3.2_

---

## daily standup

> short, daily, time-boxed meeting used to discuss progress, plans, and any blocking issues with each member of an agile team

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 24587:2021, Software and systems engineering--Agile development--Agile adoption considerations, 3.7_

---

## danger

> hazardous situation, which if not avoided, can result in death or serious injury

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 26513:2017 Systems and software engineering--Requirements for testers and reviewers of information for users, 3.8_

---

## dangerous condition

> state of a system which, in combination with some states of the environment, will result in adverse consequence (ISO/IEC/IEEE 15026-1:2019 Systems and software engineering--Systems and software assurance--Part 1: Concepts and vocabulary, 3.4.11)

---

## dark matter

> work missed in the original project plan that is required to complete the deliverable product (Software Extension to the PMBOK(R) Guide Fifth Edition)

---

## Darwin Information Typing Architecture (DITA)

> XML-based architecture for authoring, producing, and delivering topic-oriented, information-typed content that can be reused and single-sourced in a variety of ways

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 26511:2018 Systems and software engineering--Requirements for managers of information for users of systems, software, and services, 3.1.13_

---

## dashboard

> set of charts and graphs showing progress or performance against important measures of the project (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## data

> representation of facts, concepts, or instructions in a manner suitable for communication, interpretation, or processing by humans or by automatic means

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary_

---

## data abstraction

> extracting the essential characteristics of data by defining data types and their associated functional characteristics and disregarding representation details

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## data abstraction

> (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## data action

> system operation on data elements

_Source: IEEE 7002:2022, IEEE Standard for Data Privacy Process, 3.1_

---

## data analysis

> systematic investigation of the data and their flow in a real or planned system

_Source: ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary_

---

## data attribute

> smallest parcel of information, within an identified data group, carrying a meaning from the perspective of the software's functional user requirements

_Source: ISO/IEC 19761:2011 Software engineering -- COSMIC: a functional size measurement method, 3.4_

---

## data bank

> set of data related to a given subject and organized in such a way that it can be consulted by subscribers

_Source: ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary_

---

## data breakpoint

> breakpoint that is initiated when a specified data item is accessed (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## data buffer register (DBR)

> region of a physical memory storage used to temporarily store data while it is being moved, e.g., from input to processing

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765d:2015_

---

## data characteristic

> inherent, possibly accidental, trait, quality, or property of data

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering--Vocabulary_

---

## data communication

> transfer of data among functional units according to sets of rules governing data transmission and the coordination of the exchange

_Source: ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary_

---

## data configuration

> process of adding, changing or deleting reference data or code data information from the database or data storage with no change in software code or the database structure

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 32430:2025, Software engineering Software non-functional size measurement, 3.1.5_

---

## data corruption

> Incorrect and un-recovered alteration to a data item, including malicious encryption

_Source: IEEE 982:2024, Standard for Measures of the Software Aspects of Dependability, 3.1_

---

## data coupling

> type of coupling in which output from one software module serves as input to another module (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## data declaration source statement

> source statement that reserves or initializes memory at compilation time

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## data definition

> statement where a variable is assigned a value (ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-4:2021 Software and systems engineering -- Software testing -- Part 4: Test techniques, 3.16)

---

## data definition c-use pair

> data definition and subsequent computation data use, where the data use uses the value defined in the data definition

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-4:2021 Software and systems engineering -- Software testing -- Part 4: Test techniques, 3.17_

---

## data definition p-use pair

> data definition and subsequent predicate data use, where the data use uses the value defined in the data definition

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-4:2021 Software and systems engineering -- Software testing -- Part 4: Test techniques, 3.18_

---

## data definition-use pair

> data definition and subsequent data use, where the data use uses the value defined in the data definition

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-4:2021 Software and systems engineering -- Software testing -- Part 4: Test techniques, 4.12_

---

## data design

> design of a program's data, especially table design in database applications

---

## data dictionary

> collection of information about data such as name, description, creator, owner, provenance, translation in different languages, and usage (ISO/IEC 25024:2015 Systems and software engineering - Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) - Measurement of data, 4.7)

---

## data drift

> drift that occurs when data changes after the model is deployed (IEEE 7014-2024, IEEE Standard for Ethical Considerations in Emulated Empathy in Autonomous and Intelligent Systems, 3.1)

---

## data element

> unique, user-recognizable, non-repeated field in a BFC

_Source: ISO/IEC 29881:2010 Information technology--Software and systems engineering--FiSMA 1.1 functional size measurement method, 3.3_

---

## data element type (DET)

> unique, user-recognizable, non-repeated attribute

_Source: ISO/IEC 20926:2009 Software and systems engineering -- Software measurement -- IFPUG functional size measurement method 2009, 3.15_

---

## data erasure

> irrevocable loss of a data item

_Source: IEEE 982:2024, Standard for Measures of the Software Aspects of Dependability, 3.1_

---

## data exception

> exception that occurs when a program attempts to use or access data incorrectly (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## data exposure

> unpermitted access to a data item, without actual unpermitted usage or transfer

_Source: IEEE 982:2024, Standard for Measures of the Software Aspects of Dependability, 3.1_

---

## data failure (DF)

> occurrence of data corruption, erasure, exposure, or leakage occurring during any IN-SERVICE or REMOVED mode of a system of interest instance

_Source: IEEE 982:2024, Standard for Measures of the Software Aspects of Dependability, 3.1_

---

## data file

> set of related data records treated as a unit (ISO/IEC 25024:2015 Systems and software engineering - Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) - Measurement of data, 4.7)

---

## data flow

> sequence in which data transfer, use, and transformation are performed during the execution of a computer program

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## data flow diagram (DFD)

> diagram that depicts data sources, data sinks, data storage, and processes performed on data as nodes, and logical flow of data as links between the nodes (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## data flow testing

> class of structure-based test case design techniques based on exercising definition-use pairs

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-4:2021 Software and systems engineering -- Software testing -- Part 4: Test techniques, 3.19_

---

## data format

> specified arrangement and encoding for data to be communicated or stored and retrieved

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765d:2015_

---

## data function

> functionality provided to the user to meet internal or external data storage requirements (ISO/IEC 20926:2009 Software and systems engineering -- Software measurement -- IFPUG functional size measurement method 2009, 3.16)

---

## data gathering and analysis methods

> methods used to collect, assess, and evaluate data and information to gain a deeper understanding of a situation (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## data governance

> execution and enforcement of authority over the definition, production, and usage of data and data related assets

_Source: IEEE 7005 2021, IEEE Standard for Transparent Employer Data Governance, 3.1_

---

## data group

> a distinct, non-empty, non-ordered and non-redundant set of data elements

_Source: ISO/IEC 29881:2010 Information technology--Software and systems engineering--FiSMA 1.1 functional size measurement method, A.5_

---

## data input sheet

> user documentation that describes, in a worksheet format, the required and optional input data for a system or component (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## data inventory

> in an information processing system, all the data and their characteristics, including interdependencies

_Source: ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary_

---

## data item

> smallest identifiable unit of data within a certain context for which the definition, identification, permissible values, and other information is specified by means of a set of properties (ISO/IEC 25024:2015 Systems and software engineering - Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) - Measurement of data, 4.9)

---

## data leakage

> unpermitted usage or transfer of a data item

_Source: IEEE 982:2024, Standard for Measures of the Software Aspects of Dependability, 3.1_

---

## data management

> in a data processing system, the functions that provide access to data, perform or monitor the storage of data, and control input-output operations

_Source: ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary_

---

## data manipulation

> any processing of the data other than a movement of the data into or out of a functional process, or between a functional process and persistent storage (ISO/IEC 19761:2011 Software engineering -- COSMIC:

---

## data map

> visual representation of data processing within the system

_Source: IEEE 7002:2022, IEEE Standard for Data Privacy Process, 3.1_

---

## data medium

> material in or on which data can be recorded and from which data can be retrieved. Plural: data media

_Source: ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary_

---

## data model

> graphical and textual representation of analysis that identifies the data needed by an organization to achieve its mission, functions, goals, objectives, and strategies and to manage and rate the organization

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.44_

---

## data movement (-type)

> base functional component which moves a single data group (ISO/IEC 19761:2011 Software engineering -- COSMIC: a functional size measurement method, 3.6)

---

## data privacy

> fair and legitimate processing of personal data

_Source: IEEE 7002:2022, IEEE Standard for Data Privacy Process, 3.1_

---

## data processing (DP)

> systematic performance of operations upon data (ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary)

---

## data processing system

> one or more computers, peripheral equipment, and software that perform data processing (ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary)

---

## data protection

> implementation of appropriate administrative, technical, or physical means to guard against unauthorized intentional or accidental disclosure, modification, or destruction of data (ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary)

---

## data protection impact assessment

> tool described in the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) to assess in advance the privacy risks of data processing and then to be able to implement controls to reduce the risks (ISO/IEC TR 7052:2023, Software engineering--Controlling frequently occurring risks during development and maintenance of custom software, 3.14)

---

## data provider

> individual or organization that is a source of data

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 15939:2017 Systems and software engineering--Measurement process, 3.5_

---

## data quality

> capability of the characteristics of data to satisfy stated and implied needs when used under specified conditions (ISO/IEC 25019:2023, Systems and software engineering Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) Quality-in-use model, 3.1.6)

---

## data quality characteristic

> category of data quality attributes that bears on data quality (ISO/IEC 25024:2015 Systems and software engineering - Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) - Measurement of data, 4.12)

---

## data quality measure

> variable to which a value is assigned as the result of measurement of a data quality characteristic (ISO/IEC 25024:2015 Systems and software engineering - Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) - Measurement of data, 4.13)

---

## data quality model

> defined set of characteristics which provides a framework for specifying data quality requirements and evaluating data quality (ISO/IEC 25024:2015 Systems and software engineering - Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) - Measurement of data, 4.14)

---

## data record

> defined group of related data elements, in which all the necessary data elements are included to represent attributes of interest

_Source: ISO/IEC 29155-1:2017 Systems and software engineering--Information technology project performance benchmarking framework--Part 1: Concepts and definitions, 3.12_

---

## data repository

> object providing the storage function

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-3:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Architecture, 14.1.1.1_

---

## data sequencer

> component that replaces keyword parameters with data, if necessary, across several hierarchy levels

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-5:2024 Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 5: Keyword-driven testing, 3.3_

---

## data store

> organized and persistent collection of data and information that allows for its retrieval

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 15939:2017 Systems and software engineering--Measurement process, 3.6_

---

## data structure

> physical or logical relationship among data elements, designed to support specific data manipulation functions (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## data structure diagram

> diagram that depicts a set of data elements, their attributes, and the logical relationships among them (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## data structure-centered design

> software design technique in which the architecture of a system is derived from analysis of the structure of the data sets with which the system deals (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## data subject

> natural person to whom personal data relates

_Source: IEEE 7002:2022, IEEE Standard for Data Privacy Process, 3.1_

---

## data submitter

> person or organization that provides IT project data to be included into a benchmarking repository

_Source: ISO/IEC 29155-2:2013 Systems and software engineering--Information technology project performance benchmarking framework--Part 2: Requirements for benchmarking, 3.1_

---

## data system

> discrete set of resources organized for the collection, processing, maintenance, use, sharing, dissemination, or disposition of data

_Source: IEEE 7002:2022, IEEE Standard for Data Privacy Process, 3.1_

---

## data transfer controller (DTC)

> functional unit to control data communication without going through the central processing unit (CPU)

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765e:2015_

---

## data type

> class of data, characterized by the members of the class and the operations that can be applied to them

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## data use

> executable statement where the value of a variable is accessed

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-4:2021 Software and systems engineering -- Software testing -- Part 4: Test techniques, 3.20_

---

## data value

> content of data item (ISO/IEC 25024:2015 Systems and software engineering - Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) - Measurement of data, 4.17)

---

## data-driven

> informing an activity by evidence

_Source: IEEE 2675-2021, IEEE Standard for DevOps: Building Reliable and Secure Systems Including Application Build, Package, and Deployment, 3.1_

---

## data-sensitive fault

> fault that causes a failure in response to some particular pattern of data (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## data-structure-oriented design

> design methodology used for business applications by basing the design on the logical data structures of the program specification

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary_

---

## database

> collection of interrelated data stored together in one or more computerized files

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## database design specification

> document that describes the content and format of the permanent or semi- permanent data necessary for the software to carry out its functions

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## database management system

> organized collection of structured data (ISO/IEC 25024:2015 Systems and software engineering - Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) - Measurement of data, 4.18)

---

## database view

> result set of a stored query on the data, which the database users can query just as they would in a persistent database collection object (ISO/IEC/IEEE 32430:2025, Software engineering Software non-functional size measurement, 3.1.8)

---

## datum

> singular of "data" (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## DBMS

> database management system (ISO/IEC 25024:2015 Systems and software engineering - Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) - Measurement of data, 5)

---

## DBR

> data buffer register

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765d:2015_

---

## DCS

> digital computer system

_Source: IEEE 1012-2024 IEEE Standard for System, Software, and Hardware Verification and Validation, 3.2_

---

## DDOS

> distributed denial of service

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 7052:2023, Software engineering--Controlling frequently occurring risks during development and maintenance of custom software, 3.22_

---

## DDR

> detailed design review

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765d:2015_

---

## DDR2

> double data rate x 2 (twice as fast as DDR)

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765c:2014_

---

## DDR2 SDRAM

> double data rate synchronous dynamic random access memory unit with higher performance than DDR SDRAM, because the device transfers data four times (four consecutive words) in one internal clock cycle.

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765c:2014_

---

## DDR3

> double data rate 3, which transfers data 2 to the third power (8 times) faster than DDR

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765c:2014_

---

## DDR3 SDRAM

> double data rate synchronous dynamic random access memory unit with higher performance because it transfers data 2 to the third power

---

## DDR4

> double data rate 4; data transfer is 2 to the 4th power = 16 times that of a SDRAM

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765c:2014_

---

## DDR4 SDRAM

> double data rate synchronous dynamic random access memory unit with higher performance, because it transfers data at the rate of 2 to the 4th power

---

## de-identification

> removal of personally identifiable data from a data set

_Source: IEEE 7002:2022, IEEE Standard for Data Privacy Process, 3.1_

---

## deactivation

> checkpointing a cluster, followed by deletion of the cluster (ISO/IEC 10746-3:2009 Information

---

## deadlock

> situation in which computer processing is suspended because two or more devices or processes are each awaiting resources assigned to the others

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary_

---

## deblock

> to separate the parts of a block (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary)

---

## debug

> to detect, locate, and correct faults in a computer program

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## decision

> types of statements in which a choice between two or more possible outcomes controls which set of actions will result (ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-1:2022, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 1: General concepts, 3.23)

---

## decision criteria

> thresholds, targets, or patterns used to determine the need for action or further investigation, or to describe the level of confidence in a given result

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 15939:2017 Systems and software engineering-- Measurement process, 3.7_

---

## decision gate

> approval event (INCOSE Systems Engineering Handbook, 5th ed.)

---

## decision outcome

> result of a decision that determines the branch to be executed

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-4:2021 Software and systems engineering -- Software testing -- Part 4: Test techniques, 3.21_

---

## decision policy

> one or more rules used to determine whether a biometric comparison results in a positive or negative match (ISO/IEC TR 29119-13:2022, Software and systems engineering Software testing Part 13: Using the ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119 series in the testing of biometric systems, 3.1.15)

---

## decision rule

> combination of conditions (also known as causes) and actions (also known as effects) that produce a specific outcome in decision table testing and cause-effect graphing

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-4:2021 Software and systems engineering -- Software testing -- Part 4: Test techniques, 3.22_

---

## decision table

> table of all contingencies that are to be considered in the description of a problem together with the action to be taken

_Source: ISO 5806:1984 Information processing -- Specification of single-hit decision tables, 3.1_

---

## decision table testing

> specification-based test case design technique based on exercising decision rules in a decision table

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-4:2021 Software and systems engineering -- Software testing -- Part 4: Test techniques, 3.24_

---

## decision testing

> structure-based test case design technique based on exercising decision outcomes in the control flow of the test item

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-1:2022, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 1: General concepts, 3.28_

---

## decision tree

> supervised-learning model for which inference can be represented by traversing one or more tree- like structures

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 29119-11:2020, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 11: Guidelines on the testing of AI-based systems, 3.1.25_

---

## decision tree analysis

> diagramming and calculation method for evaluating the implications of a chain of multiple options in the presence of uncertainty (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## decision variable

> representation of different values for a decision which the decision-maker can choose (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## declaration

> action that establishes a state of affairs in the environment of the object making the declaration

_Source: ISO/IEC 15414:2015 Information technology -- Open distributed processing -- Reference model -- Enterprise language, 6.6.5_

---

## declarative language

> nonprocedural language that permits the user to declare a set of facts and to express queries or problems that use these facts (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## decompile

> to translate a compiled computer program from its machine language version into a form that resembles, but is not necessarily identical to, the original high-order language program (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## decompiler

> software tool that decompiles computer programs

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## decomposer

> component that deconstructs the high-level keywords across all levels into a sequence of low-level keywords that eventually comprise it

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-5:2024 Software and systems engineering--Software testing-- Part 5: Keyword-driven testing, 3.4_

---

## decomposition

> method used for dividing and subdividing the project scope and project deliverables into smaller, more manageable parts (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## decomposition diagram

> diagram that details its parent box

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765m:2024, 2.1.36_

---

## decoupling

> making software modules more independent of one another to decrease the impact of changes to, and errors in, the individual modules (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## deep learning

> approach to creating rich hierarchical representations through the training of neural networks with one or more hidden layers (ISO/IEC TR 29119-11:2020, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 11: Guidelines on the testing of AI-based systems, 3.1.26)

---

## deep neural net

> neural network with more than two layers (ISO/IEC TR 29119-11:2020, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 11: Guidelines on the testing of AI-based systems, 3.1.27)

---

## defect

> imperfection or deficiency in a work product or characteristic that does not meet its requirements or specifications

_Source: IEEE 2675-2021, IEEE Standard for DevOps: Building Reliable and Secure Systems Including Application Build, Package, and Deployment, 3.1_

---

## defect density

> number of defects per unit of product size

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## defensive programming

> a general approach to programming that assumes that errors will occur during both initial development and maintenance and, as a result, creates code in such a way that the program still operates properly when errors occur

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## defined process

> implemented process that is managed and tailored from the organization's set of standard processes according to the organization's tailoring guidelines (ISO/IEC 33001:2015 Information technology--Process assessment--Concepts and terminology, 3.4.2)

---

## definition-use pair

> data definition and subsequent predicate or computational data use, where the data use uses the value defined in the data definition (ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-4:2021 Software and systems engineering -- Software testing -- Part 4: Test techniques, 3.26)

---

## definition-use path

> data definition and subsequent predicate or computational data use, where the data use uses the value defined in the data definition

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-4:2021 Software and systems engineering -- Software testing -- Part 4: Test techniques, 4.16_

---

## definitive software library (DSL)

> secure storage environment, formed of physical media or of one or more electronic software repositories, capable of control and protection of definitive authorized versions of all software configuration items and masters of all software controlled by SAM

_Source: ISO/IEC 19770-5:2015 Information technology--IT asset management--Overview and vocabulary, 3.11_

---

## degree of influence (DI)

> a numerical indicator of the impact of each of the 19 (or more) technical complexity adjustment factors, ranging from 0 (no influence) to 5 (strong influence, throughout) (ISO/IEC 20968:2002 Software engineering -- Mk II Function Point Analysis -- Counting Practices Manual, 10)

---

## delegation

> action that assigns something, such as authorization, responsibility or provision of a service, to another object (ISO/IEC 15414:2015 Information technology -- Open distributed processing -- Reference model -- Enterprise language, 6.6.6)

---

## deleted source statement

> source statement that is removed or modified from an existing software product as a new product is constructed

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## deletion

> of an &lt;X&gt;, the action of destroying an instantiated &lt;X&gt;

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-2:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Foundations, 9.20_

---

## delimiter

> character or set of characters used to denote the beginning or end of a group of related bits, characters, words, or statements

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## deliver primitive

> service primitive for which the protocol object is the responding object of the corresponding communication

_Source: ISO/IEC 14752:2000 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Protocol support for computational interactions, 3.3.6_

---

## deliverable

> any unique and verifiable product, result, or capability to perform a service that must be produced to complete a process, phase, or project (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## deliverable product

> unique and verifiable system or software product to perform a service, that is subject to approval by the project sponsor or customer (ISO/IEC 25041: 2012 Systems and software engineering--Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)--Evaluation guide for developers, acquirers and independent evaluators, 4.1)

---

## delivered source statement

> source statement that is incorporated into the product delivered to the customer

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## delivery

> release of a system or component to its customer or intended user (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## delivery performance domain

> performance domain that addresses activities and functions associated with delivering the scope and quality that the project was undertaken to achieve (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## Delphi method

> information-gathering technique used as a way to reach consensus of experts on a subject (ISO/IEC TR 7052:2023, Software engineering--Controlling frequently occurring risks during development and maintenance of custom software, 3.16)

---

## delphi technique

> information-gathering technique used as a way to reach a consensus of experts on a subject (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765h:2019)

---

## delta

> difference between two versions

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 18018:2010 Information technology--Systems and software engineering--Guide for configuration management tool capabilities, 3.11_

---

## demand paging

> storage allocation technique in which pages are transferred from auxiliary storage to main storage only when those pages are needed (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## DEMIL

> demilitarization

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-7:2019, Systems and software engineering Life cycle management Part 7: Application of systems engineering on defense programs, 3.2_

---

## demodularization

> in software design, combining related software modules, usually to optimize system performance (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## demonstration

> dynamic analysis technique that relies on observation of system or component behavior during execution, without need for post-execution analysis, to detect errors, violations of development standards, and other problems (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## dependability

> extent to which measured values of reliability, availability, recoverability, and supportability meet or exceed their required thresholds during an in-service interval

_Source: IEEE 982:2024, Standard for Measures of the Software Aspects of Dependability, 3.1_

---

## dependability measurement

> data collection and processing to produce values for reliability, availability, supportability, or recoverability of a system

_Source: IEEE 982:2024, Standard for Measures of the Software Aspects of Dependability, 3.1_

---

## dependability requirement

> requirement statement that specifies a bounded threshold for one or more parameters of reliability, availability, supportability, or recoverability for a system (IEEE 982:2024, Standard for Measures of the Software Aspects of Dependability, 3.1)

---

## dependency export

> operation in which a component and all of its dependencies are exported from the CCMS as a single process

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 26531:2023 Systems and software engineering -- Content management for product lifecycle, user and service management information for users, 3.1.10_

---

## dependent entity

> entity for which the unique identification of an instance depends upon its relationship to another entity (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.46)

---

## dependent state class

> class whose instances are, by their very nature, intrinsically related to certain other state class instances (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.47)

---

## deployment

> stage of a project in which a system is put into operation and transition issues are resolved

_Source: IEEE 2675-2021, IEEE Standard for DevOps: Building Reliable and Secure Systems Including Application Build, Package, and Deployment, 3.1_

---

## deployment package

> set of artifacts developed to facilitate the implementation of a set of practices of the selected framework in a very small entity (ISO/IEC 29110-1-2:2024, Systems and software engineering Lifecycle profiles for Very Small Entities (VSEs) Part 1-2: Vocabulary, 3.35)

---

## derivation of requirements

> translation and elaboration of requirements from one type of requirements to another in the same system level (ISO/IEC 25030:2019 Systems and software engineering--Systems and software quality requirements and evaluation (SQuaRE)--Quality requirements framework, 3.4)

---

## derived class

> relation between a template class CA of instances of A, and template class CB of instances of B, where template A is an incremental modification of template B (ISO/IEC 10746-2:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Foundations, 9.24)

---

## derived data

> data created as a result of processing that involves steps other than or in addition to direct retrieval and validation of information from data functions

_Source: ISO/IEC 20926:2009 Software and systems engineering -- Software measurement -- IFPUG functional size measurement method 2009, 3.17_

---

## derived measure

> measure that is defined as a function of two or more values of base measures (ISO/IEC/IEEE 15939:2017 Systems and software engineering--Measurement process, 3.8)

---

## derived property

> designation given to a property whose value is determined by computation (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.50)

---

## derived requirement

> requirement deduced or inferred from the collection and organization of requirements into a particular system configuration and solution

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018 Systems and software engineering-Life cycle processes-Requirements engineering, 4.1.8_

---

## derived type

> data type whose members and operations are taken from those of another data type according to some specified rule (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## descendent box

> box in a descendent diagram

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765m:2024, 2.1.37_

---

## descendent diagram

> decomposition diagram related to a specific box by a hierarchically consecutive sequence of one or more child/parent relationships

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765m:2024, 2.1.38_

---

## description

> information item that represents a planned or actual concept, function, design, or object (ISO/IEC/IEEE 15289:2019 Systems and software engineering--Content of life-cycle information items (documentation), 5.8)

---

## description standard

> standard that describes the characteristics of product information or procedures provided to help understand, test, install, operate, or maintain the product

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## descriptive model

> model that shows an interconnected set of model elements which represent key system aspects including its structure, behavior, parameters, and requirements

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24641:2023. Systems and Software engineering--Methods and tools for model-based systems and software engineering, 3.1.6_

---

## design

> specification of system elements and their relationships, that is sufficiently complete to support a compliant implementation of the architecture (ISO/IEC/IEEE 12207:2026 Systems and software engineering--Software life cycle processes, 3.1.20)

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288:2023 Systems and software engineering--System life cycle processes, 3.13_

---

## design analyzer

> automated design tool that accepts information about a program's design and produces such outputs as module hierarchy diagrams, graphical representations of control and data structure, and lists of accessed data

---

## design attribute

> element of a design view that names a characteristic or property of a design entity, design relationship, or design constraint (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765j:2021)

---

## design authority

> person or organization that is responsible for the design of the product

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 15026- 1:2019 Systems and software engineering--Systems and software assurance--Part 1: Concepts and vocabulary, 3.5.3_

---

## design characteristic

> design attribute or distinguishing feature that pertains to a measurable description of a product or service (ISO/IEC/IEEE 12207:2026 Systems and software engineering--Software life cycle processes, 3.1.21)

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288:2023 Systems and software engineering--System life cycle processes, 3.17_

---

## design concept

> fundamental idea that can be applied to designing a system

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## design constraint

> explicit and direct restriction regarding the choice of design ideas

_Source: IEEE 730-2014 IEEE Standard for Software Quality Assurance Processes, 3.2_

---

## design description

> model or information item that specifies the design of a system or component (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## design fault

> design (specification, coding) fault that results from a human error during system design and that can result in a design failure

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## design language

> specification language with special constructs and, sometimes, verification protocols, used to develop, analyze, and document a hardware or software design

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## design level

> design decomposition of the configuration item (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## design methodology

> systematic approach to creating a design consisting of the ordered application of a specific collection of tools, techniques, and guidelines

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary_

---

## design pattern

> description of the problem and the essence of its solution to enable the solution to be reused in different settings (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## design phase

> period in the software life cycle during which definitions for architecture, software components,

---

## design rationale

> information capturing the reasoning of the designer that led to the system as designed, including design options, trade-offs considered, decisions made, and the justifications of those decisions

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765j:2021_

---

## design requirement

> requirement that specifies or constrains the design of a system or system component (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## design review

> formal, documented, comprehensive, and systematic examination of a design to determine if the design meets the applicable requirements, to identify problems, and to propose solutions

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## design standard

> standard that describes the characteristics of a design or a design description of data or program components

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## design strategy

> overall plan and direction for performing design

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## design thinking

> methodology for solving complex problems where these are defined from the human needs (ISO/IEC TR 7052:2023, Software engineering--Controlling frequently occurring risks during development and maintenance of custom software, 3.17)

---

## design unit

> logically related collection of design elements

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## design view

> representation comprised of one or more design elements to address a set of design concerns from a specified design viewpoint (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765j:2021)

---

## design viewpoint

> specification of the elements and conventions available for constructing and using a design view (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765j:2021)

---

## design-to-cost

> approach to managing a system or software project so as to hold the project to a predetermined cost (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## desirable consequence

> consequence associated with a benefit or gain or avoiding an adverse consequence (ISO/IEC/IEEE 15026-1:2019 Systems and software engineering--Systems and software assurance--Part 1: Concepts and vocabulary, 3.4.4)

---

## desk checking

> manual simulation of program execution to detect faults through step-by-step examination of the

---

## desktop publishing

> electronic publishing using a microcomputer

_Source: ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary_

---

## destination address

> address of the device or storage location to which data is to be transferred (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## destructive read

> read operation that alters the data in the accessed location (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## DET

> data element type

_Source: ISO/IEC 20926:2009 Software and systems engineering -- Software measurement -- IFPUG functional size measurement method 2009, 4_

---

## detailed design

> refinement and expansion of the preliminary design of a system or component to the extent that the design is sufficiently complete to be implemented (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary)

---

## detailed design description

> document that describes the exact detailed configuration of a computer program (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## detailed design phase

> software development lifecycle phase during which the detailed design process takes place, using the software system design and software architecture from the previous phase (architectural design) to produce the detailed logic for each unit such that it is ready for coding

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## detailed design review

> milestone review to determine the acceptability of the detailed software design (as depicted in the detailed design description) to satisfy the requirements of the software requirements document

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## detection error trade-off

> relationship between false-negative and false-positive errors of a binary classification system as the discrimination threshold varies (ISO/IEC TR 29119-13:2022, Software and systems engineering Software testing Part 13: Using the ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119 series in the testing of biometric systems, 3.1.16)

---

## deterministic system

> system which, given a particular set of inputs and starting state, will always produce the same set of outputs and final state

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 29119-11:2020, Software and systems engineering--Software testing-- Part 11: Guidelines on the testing of AI-based systems, 3.1.28_

---

## developed source statement

> source statement that is newly created for, added to, or modified for a software product

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## developer

> individual or organization that performs development activities (including requirements analysis, design, testing through acceptance) during the system or software life-cycle process (ISO/IEC 25000:2014 Systems and software Engineering--Systems and software product Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) -- Guide to SQuaRE, 4.6)

---

## development

> specification, construction, testing and delivery of a new application or of a discrete addition to an existing application

_Source: ISO/IEC 20968:2002 Software engineering -- Mk II Function Point Analysis -- Counting Practices Manual, 10_

---

## development approach

> method used to create and evolve the product, service, or result during the project life cycle, such as predictive, adaptive, or a hybrid method (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## development approach and life cycle performance domain

> performance domain that addresses activities and functions associated with the development approach, cadence, and life cycle phases of the project (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## development branch

> branch where active product development takes place (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## development environment

> hardware, software, platform and tools for designers and developers of computer solutions

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765c:2014_

---

## development pipeline

> set of tools aimed at the managed and controlled build of a package with which an application can be taken into use (ISO/IEC TR 7052:2023, Software engineering--Controlling frequently occurring risks during development and maintenance of custom software, 3.19)

---

## development plan

> plan for guiding, implementing, and controlling the design and development of one or more products or services (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## development project

> project to develop and deliver the first release of a software application (ISO/IEC 20926:2009 Software and systems engineering -- Software measurement -- IFPUG functional size measurement method 2009, 3.18)

---

## development project function point count (DFP)

> a count that measures the functionality provided to the end users with the first installation of the software, developed when the project is complete

_Source: ISO/IEC 20968:2002 Software engineering -- Mk II Function Point Analysis -- Counting Practices Manual, 10_

---

## development project functional size

> measure of the functionality provided to the users with the first release of the software, as measured by the development project function point count (ISO/IEC 20926:2009 Software and systems engineering -- Software measurement -- IFPUG functional size measurement method 2009, 3.19)

---

## development team

> team that develops or maintains software (ISO/IEC TR 7052:2023, Software engineering-- Controlling frequently occurring risks during development and maintenance of custom software, 3.20)

---

## development testing

> formal or informal testing conducted during the development of a system or component, usually in the development environment by the developer

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary_

---

## development tool

> hardware and software for developing or modifying applications

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765c:2014_

---

## developmental baseline

> specifications that are in effect at a given time for a system under development

_Source: ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary_

---

## developmental configuration

> in configuration management, the software and associated technical documentation that define the evolving configuration of a computer software configuration item during development (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## deviation

> departure from a specified requirement

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary_

---

## device

> mechanism or piece of equipment designed to serve a purpose or perform a function (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## device interface task

> concurrent task that hides the characteristics of and interfaces to an external I/O device

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## DevOps

> set of principles and practices which enable better communication and collaboration between relevant

---

## DF

> data failure

---

## DFD

> data flow diagram

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## diagnostic

> pertaining to the detection and isolation of faults or failures

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## diagnostic manual

> document that presents the information necessary to execute diagnostic procedures for a system or component, identify malfunctions, and remedy those malfunctions (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## diagonal microinstruction

> microinstruction capable of specifying a limited number of simultaneous operations needed to carry out a machine language instruction (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary)

---

## diagram

> logically coherent fragment of a design view, using selected graphical icons and conventions for visual representation from an associated design language, to be used for representing selected design elements of interest for a system under design from a single viewpoint (diagram type)

---

## diagram boundary

> edge of a diagram in a diagram page

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765m:2024, 2.1.40_

---

## diagram feature

> element of a diagram (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765m:2024, 2.1.41)

---

## diagram number

> that part of a diagram reference that corresponds to a diagram's parent function's node number (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765m:2024, 2.1.43)

---

## diagram page

> model page that contains a context diagram or a decomposition diagram

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765m:2024, 2.1.44_

---

## diagram reference

> expression that unambiguously identifies a diagram and specifies the diagram's position in a specific model hierarchy (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765m:2024, 2.1.45)

---

## diagram title

> verb or verb phrase that describes the overall function presented by a diagram (ISO/IEC/IEEE

---

## dialog

> interaction between a user and an interactive system as a sequence of user actions (inputs) and system responses (outputs) in order to achieve a goal (ISO TR 25060:2023 Systems and software engineering--Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)--General Framework for Common Industry Format (CIF) for usability-related information, 2.4)

> directory information base
