## differential cash flow

> representation of the difference between cash flows of two alternatives or proposals (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## digit numeric character

> character that represents a nonnegative integer (ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary)

---

## digital

> pertaining to data that consists of digits as well as to processes and functional units that use the data

_Source: ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary_

---

## digital asset

> IT asset expressed electronically in a digital format

_Source: ISO/IEC 19770-1:2017 Information technology -- IT asset management -- Part 1: IT asset management systems--Requirements, 3.17_

---

## digital computer

> computer that is controlled by internally stored programs and that is capable of using common storage for all or part of a program and also for all or part of the data necessary for the execution of the programs; executing user-written or user-designated programs; performing user-designated manipulation of digitally represented discrete data, including arithmetic operations and logic operations; and executing programs that modify themselves during their execution

_Source: ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary_

---

## digital information content asset

> digital asset with information content (ISO/IEC 19770-1:2017 Information technology -- IT asset management -- Part 1: IT asset management systems--Requirements, 3.18)

---

## digital product

> product or service that is delivered, used, and stored in an electronic format (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## digital signal processing (DSP)

> modification of an information signal represented by a sequence of digits or symbols to affect the representation of discrete time, discrete frequency, or other attributes

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765d:2015_

---

## digital signal processor (DSP)

> microprocessor designed to perform digital signal processing

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765d:2015_

---

## DIKW

> data, information, knowledge, wisdom

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24641:2023. Systems and Software engineering-- Methods and tools for model-based systems and software engineering, 3.2_

---

## dimension

> distinct components that a multidimensional construct encompasses

_Source: ISO/IEC 33003:2015 Information technology--Process assessment--Requirements for process measurement frameworks, 3.6_

---

## DIP

> dual inline package

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765c:2014_

---

## direct address

> address that identifies the storage location of an operand (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems

---

## direct instruction

> computer instruction that contains the direct addresses of its operands (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## direct labor

> personnel efforts that are directly related to the units of production (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## direct measure

> measure of an attribute that does not depend upon a measure of any other attribute

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## direct memory access (DMA)

> technique in which a peripheral takes direct control of a central processing unit's memory bus to transfer data to or from memory

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765d:2015_

---

## direct memory access controller (DMAC)

> functional unit that performs direct memory access

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765d:2015_

---

## direct metric

> metric that does not depend upon a measure of any other attribute

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765l:2024_

---

## direct staff-hour

> amount of effort directly expended in creating a specific output product

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## direct user

> person who directly interacts with the product (ISO/IEC 25019:2023, Systems and software engineering Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) Quality-in-use model, 3.1.14) (ISO/IEC 25002:2024, Systems and software engineering Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) Quality model overview and usage, 3.1.8)

---

## directed graph

> a graph (sense 2) in which direction is implied in the internode connections (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## directed system of systems

> system of systems (SoS) created and managed to fulfill specific purposes and the constituent systems are subordinated to the SoS (ISO/IEC/IEEE 21841:2019 Systems and software engineering-- Taxonomy of systems of systems, 3.2.3)

---

## directory

> list of data items and information about those data items

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## disassemble

> to translate an assembled computer program from its machine language version into a form that resembles, but is not necessarily identical to, the original assembly language program (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## disassembler

> software tool that disassembles computer programs

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## disaster recovery

> in computer system operations, the return to normal operation after a hardware or software failure

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## discipline-specific model

> representation of a system or system elements from the perspective of a discipline addressing domain-specific concerns where the model elements come from a specific discipline

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24641:2023. Systems and Software engineering--Methods and tools for model-based systems and software engineering, 3.1.7_

---

## disclaimer

> notice that renounces or repudiates a legal claim or right

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## discounted payback period

> time it will take to recover a project's initial investment including interest (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## discrete

> pertaining to data that consist of distinct elements, such as characters, or to physical quantities having a finite number of distinctly recognizable values, as well as to processes and functional units that use those data

_Source: ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary_

---

## discrete data

> data that arrives at specific time intervals

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## discrete effort

> work effort that is separate, distinct, and related to the completion of specific work breakdown structure components and deliverables, and that can be directly planned and measured (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## discrete type

> data type whose members can assume any of a set of distinct values (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## discretionary dependency

> relationship that is based on best practices or project preferences (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## discrimination (threshold)

> largest change in a stimulus that produces no detectable change in the response of a measuring instrument, the change in the stimulus taking place slowly and monotonically (ISO/IEC TR 14143-3:2003 Information technology -- Software measurement -- Functional size measurement -- Part 3: Verification of functional size measurement methods, 3.4)

---

## discriminator

> property of a superclass, associated with a cluster of that superclass, whose value identifies to which subclass a specific instance belongs

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.51_

---

## disk

> data medium originally consisting of a flat circular plate that is rotated in order to read or write data on one or both sides

_Source: ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary_

---

## display

> information presented on a screen or in a window of a screen

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765g:2018_

---

## disposal

> removal or archiving, but not deletion, of an artifact so it can be made available for traceability and auditability

_Source: IEEE 2675-2021, IEEE Standard for DevOps: Building Reliable and Secure Systems Including Application Build, Package, and Deployment, 3.1_

---

## disposition

> range of processes associated with implementing retention, destruction or transfer decisions which are documented in disposition or other instruments

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 26511:2018 Systems and software engineering-- Requirements for managers of information for users of systems, software, and services, 3.1.12_

---

## distribute information

> [Process] the process of making relevant information available (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## distributed computing

> spreading of computation and data across a number of computers connected by a network

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## distributed denial of service attack

> unauthorized access to a system resource or the delaying of system operations and functions in the way of compromising multiple systems to flood the bandwidth or resources of the targeted system, with resultant loss of availability to authorized users (ISO/IEC TR 7052:2023, Software engineering--Controlling frequently occurring risks during development and maintenance of custom software, 3.22)

---

## distributed processing

> information processing in which discrete components can be located in different places, and where communication between components can suffer delay or can fail

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-2:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Foundations, 3.2.2_

---

## distribution transparency

> property of hiding from the user some specific aspects of the system's complexity needed to support distribution

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-2:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Foundations, 11.1.1_

---

## distributional shift

> in machine learning, the distance between the training data distribution and the desired data distribution (ISO/IEC TR 29119-11:2020, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 11: Guidelines on the testing of AI-based systems, 3.1.29)

---

## disturbance

> operational fault or event or anything that can change the state of the system (ISO/IEC 25045:2010 Systems and software engineering--Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)--Evaluation module for recoverability, 4.2)

---

## DIT

> directory information tree

_Source: ISO/IEC 13235-3:1998 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Trading Function -- Part 3: Provision of Trading Function using OSI Directory service, 4_

---

## DITA

> Darwin Information Typing Architecture

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 26531:2023 Systems and software engineering -- Content management for product lifecycle, user and service management information for users, 3.2_

---

## diversity

> in fault tolerance, realization of the same function by different means (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## dividing action

> action which enables two or more chains

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-2:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Foundations, 13.1.4_

---

## division of standards

> division forms a family of standards serving complementary purposes (ISO/IEC 25000:2014 Systems and software Engineering--Systems and software product Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) -- Guide to SQuaRE, 4.13)

---

## DL

> Definition Language

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-1:1998 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference model: Overview_

---

## DLC

> data-life-cycle (ISO/IEC 25024:2015 Systems and software engineering - Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) - Measurement of data, 5)

---

## DMA

> direct memory access

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765d:2015_

---

## DMAC

> direct memory access controller

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765d:2015_

---

## DMS

> document management system

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765h:2019_

---

## DN

> distinguished name

_Source: ISO/IEC 13235-3:1998 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Trading Function -- Part 3: Provision of Trading Function using OSI Directory service, 4_

---

## DNN

> deep neural network

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 29119-11:2020, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 11: Guidelines on the testing of AI-based systems, 3.2_

---

## DNS

> Domain Name Service

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 23026:2023 Systems and software engineering--Engineering and management of websites for systems, software, and services information, 3.2_

---

## do nothing alternative

> in a decision analysis, the alternative of not investing in any of the proposed alternatives (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## document

> uniquely identified unit of information for human use (ISO/IEC/IEEE 15289:2019 Systems and software engineering--Content of life-cycle information items (documentation), 3.1.10)

---

## document

>

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## document control

> application of configuration management to the control of documents

## document management system

> system that supports the storage, retrieval, versioning, and manipulation of

---

## document management system (DMS)

> system that supports the storage, retrieval, versioning, and manipulation of whole documents, images, and other media (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765h:2019)

---

## document set

> documentation that has been segmented into separately identified volumes or products for ease of distribution or use

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 26514:2022, Systems and software engineering -- Design and development of information for users, 3.1.20_

---

## document type definition (DTD)

> template for the structure, content, and semantics of documents

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 26531:2023 Systems and software engineering -- Content management for product lifecycle, user and service management information for users, 3.1.12_

---

## documentation

> collection of documents related to a given subject (IEC/IEEE 82079-1:2019 Preparation of information for use (instructions for use) of products: Part 1: Principles and general requirements, 3.10)

---

## documentation tree

> diagram that depicts all the documents for a given system and shows their relationships to one another (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## documented information

> information required to be controlled and maintained by an organization and the medium on which it is contained (ISO/IEC 19770-1:2017 Information technology -- IT asset management -- Part 1: IT asset management systems--Requirements, 3.19)

---

## DoDAF

> Department of Defense architecture framework

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-7:2019, Systems and software engineering Life cycle management Part 7: Application of systems engineering on defense programs, 3.2_

---

## DOI

> digital object identifier

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 23026:2023 Systems and software engineering--Engineering and management of websites for systems, software, and services information, 3.2_

---

## domain

> aspect or area of knowledge or activity characterized by a set of concepts and terminology understood by practitioners in that area (IEEE 7010-2020, IEEE Recommended Practice for Assessing the Impact of Autonomous and

---

## domain analysis

> analysis of systems within a domain to discover commonalities and differences among them

_Source: IEEE 1012-2024 IEEE Standard for System, Software, and Hardware Verification and Validation, 3.1_

---

## domain architecture

> common architecture for a product line that can embrace variability of member products

_Source: ISO/IEC 26552:2019 Software and systems engineering--Tools and methods for product line architecture design, 3.5_

---

## domain asset

> output of domain engineering life cycle processes that can be reused in producing products during application engineering (ISO/IEC 26550:2015 Software and systems engineering--Reference model for product line engineering and management, 3.11)

---

## domain assets in requirements

> reusable artifacts produced during domain requirements engineering

_Source: ISO/IEC 26551:2016 Software and systems engineering --Tools and methods for product line requirements engineering, 3.10_

---

## domain component

> reusable component among member products within a product line

_Source: ISO/IEC 26553:2018 Information technology -- Software and systems engineering-- Tools and methods for product line realization, 3.10_

---

## domain engineering

> reuse-based approach to defining the scope (i.e., domain definition), specifying the structure (i.e., domain architecture), and building the assets for a class of systems, subsystems, or applications

_Source: IEEE 1012-2024 IEEE Standard for System, Software, and Hardware Verification and Validation, 3.1_

---

## domain engineering process

> processes for domain asset development

_Source: ISO/IEC 26555:2015 Software and systems engineering--Tools and methods for product line technical management, 3.5_

---

## domain interface

> reusable interface among the components of a member product within a product line

_Source: ISO/IEC 26553:2018 Information technology -- Software and systems engineering-- Tools and methods for product line realization, 3.11_

---

## domain layer

> highest level of abstraction for the test item (ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-5:2024 Software and systems

---

## domain model

> product of domain analysis that provides a representation of the requirements of the domain (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765j:2021)

---

## domain realization

> one of the domain engineering processes that include detailed design and implementation

_Source: ISO/IEC 26553:2018 Information technology -- Software and systems engineering-- Tools and methods for product line realization, 3.12_

---

## domain requirements analysis

> subprocess that models domain requirements so as to analyze and scrutinize commonality/variability of a product line in requirements

_Source: ISO/IEC 26551:2016 Software and systems engineering --Tools and methods for product line requirements engineering, 3.12_

---

## domain requirements elicitation

> subprocess that identifies initial requirements from domain stakeholders for a product line

_Source: ISO/IEC 26551:2016 Software and systems engineering --Tools and methods for product line requirements engineering, 3,11_

---

## domain requirements management

> subprocess that manages traceability and changes with respect to domain requirements and their relevant domain/application artifacts

_Source: ISO/IEC 26551:2016 Software and systems engineering --Tools and methods for product line requirements engineering, 3.15_

---

## domain requirements specification

> subprocess that documents domain requirements for a product line based on domain analysis results

_Source: ISO/IEC 26551:2016 Software and systems engineering --Tools and methods for product line requirements engineering, 3.13_

---

## domain requirements verification and validation

> subprocess that confirms that domain requirements are correct, consistent, and complete

_Source: ISO/IEC 26551:2016 Software and systems engineering --Tools and methods for product line requirements engineering, 3.14_

---

## domain scoping

> subprocess for identifying and bounding the functional domains that are important to an envisioned product line and provide sufficient reuse potential to justify the product line creation (ISO/IEC 26550:2015 Software and systems engineering--Reference model for product line engineering and management, 3.13)

---

## domain supersets

> collection comprising the feature catalogue and shared asset supersets

_Source: ISO/IEC 26580:2021, Software and systems engineering Methods and tools for the feature-based approach to software and systems product line engineering, 3.3_

---

## domain test asset

> domain test artifacts that will be reused in application testing (ISO/IEC 26554:2018 Information technology--Software and systems engineering-Tools and methods for product line testing, 3.6)

---

## domain test requirement

> specific element of a domain artifact that should be covered by domain testing (ISO/IEC 26554:2018 Information technology--Software and systems engineering-Tools and methods for product line

---

## domain testing

> domain engineering phase whose role is to test domain artefacts (ISO/IEC 26554:2018 Information technology--Software and systems engineering-Tools and methods for product line testing, 3.5)

---

## domain variability model

> explicit definition of product line variability

_Source: ISO/IEC 26558:2017 Software and systems engineering -- Methods and tools for variability modelling in software and systems product line, 3.4_

---

## domain-based requirement

> requirement originated from its application domain (ISO/IEC 25030:2019 Systems and software engineering--Systems and software quality requirements and evaluation (SQuaRE)--Quality requirements framework, 3.5)

---

## dominance

> decision technique that looks for an alternative that is at least as good in every attribute and better in at least one attribute (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## done

> regarded by the agile team as complete and ready to use

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 26515: 2018 Systems and software engineering: Developing information for users in an agile environment, 3.5_

---

## dot notation

> technique for naming that joins the name of a parent class to the name of a dependent class with the period character

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765m:2024, 2.1.47_

---

## double data rate (DDR) SDRAM

> synchronous dynamic random access memory unit with higher access speed and bandwidth, because it transfers two consecutive words in one internal clock cycle

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765c:2014_

---

## down

> pertaining to a system or component that is not operational or has been taken out of service (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## down time

> in-service interval during which a system has not performed or cannot perform its required capabilities (IEEE 982:2024, Standard for Measures of the Software Aspects of Dependability, 3.1)

---

## downgrade right

> right granted to receive, install, or use an installation of a previous version of software than the currently granted entitlement

_Source: ISO/IEC 19770-3:2016 Information technology--IT asset management--Part 3: Entitlement schema, 3.1.7_

---

## download

> to transfer programs or data from a computer to a connected computer with fewer resources (ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary)

---

## downward compatible

> pertaining to hardware or software that is compatible with an earlier or less complex version of itself (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## downward compression

> in software design, a form of demodularization in which a superordinate module is copied into the body of a subordinate module (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## DP

> data processing

_Source: ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary_

---

## DPIA

> data protection impact assessment

_Source: IEEE 7002:2022, IEEE Standard for Data Privacy Process, 3.2_

---

## DR

> decision review

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-8:2019, Systems and software engineering Life cycle management Part 7: Application of systems engineering on defense programs, 3.2_

---

## DRAM

> dynamic random access memory

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765c:2014_

---

## drift

> changes to machine learning model behavior that occur over time

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 29119-11:2020, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 11: Guidelines on the testing of AI-based systems, 6.1.30_

---

## driver

> external dynamic component or simulator that activates the performing of functions of an aggregate of system elements (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-6:2023 Systems and software engineering Life cycle management Part 6: System and software integration, 3.1.2)

---

## DRP

> disaster recovery plan

_Source: IEEE 2675-2021, IEEE Standard for DevOps: Building Reliable and Secure Systems Including Application Build, Package, and Deployment, 3.2_

---

## DSA

> Directory System Agent

_Source: ISO/IEC 13235-3:1998 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Trading Function -- Part 3: Provision of Trading Function using OSI Directory service, 4_

---

## DSL

> definitive software library

_Source: ISO/IEC 19770-5:2015 Information technology--IT asset management--Overview and vocabulary, 3.11_

---

## DSP

> digital signal processing

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765d:2015_

---

## DT

> development test

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-8:2019, Systems and software engineering Life cycle management Part 7: Application of systems engineering on defense programs, 3.2_

---

## DT&E

> developmental test and evaluation

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-7:2019, Systems and software engineering Life cycle management Part 7: Application of systems engineering on defense programs, 3.2_

---

## DTC

> data transfer controller

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765d:2015_

---

## DTCF

> downtime cycle finish

_Source: IEEE 982:2024, Standard for Measures of the Software Aspects of Dependability, 3.2_

---

## DTCS

> downtime cycle start

_Source: IEEE 982:2024, Standard for Measures of the Software Aspects of Dependability, 3.2_
