## job

> user-defined unit of work that is to be accomplished by a computer (ISO/IEC 25023:2016, Systems and software engineering--Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)--Measurement of system and software product quality, 4.3)

---

## job control language (JCL)

> language used to identify a sequence of jobs, describe their requirements to an operating system, and control their execution

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## job function

> group of engineering processes that is identified as a unit for the purposes of work organization, assignment, or evaluation

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## job step

> user-defined portion of a job, explicitly identified by a job control statement (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## job stream

> sequence of programs or jobs set up so that a computer can proceed from one to the next without the need for operator intervention (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## join

> junction at which an arrow segment (going from source to use) merges with one or more other arrow segments to form a root arrow segment (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765m:2024, 2.1.66)

---

## joining action

> action shared between two or more chains resulting in a single chain

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-2:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Foundations, 13.1.3_

---

## JPEG

> Joint Photographic Experts Group (ISO/IEC/IEEE 23026:2023 Systems and software engineering-- Engineering and management of websites for systems, software, and services information, 3.2)

## JPG

> Joint Photographic Group (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765l:2024)

> JavaScript Object Notation

---

## JTA

> Java Transaction API

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765l:2024_

---

## jump

> to depart from the implicit or declared order in which computer program statements are being executed

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## junction

> point at which either a root arrow segment divides into branching arrow segments or arrow segments join into a root arrow segment

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765m:2024, 2.1.67_

---

## JWT

> JSON (JavaScript Object Notation) web token

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 26531:2023 Systems and software engineering - - Content management for product lifecycle, user and service management information for users, 3.2_

## KA

> knowledge area

_Source: ISO/IEC 24773-4:2023 Software and systems engineering Certification of software and systems engineering professionals Part 4: Software engineering, 3.1_

---

## Kanban board

> visualization tool that shows work in progress to help identify bottlenecks and overcommitments, thereby allowing the team to optimize the workflow (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## kernel

> that portion of an operating system that is kept in main memory at all times

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## kernel entity

> a classification used for a meta-entity whose instances can exist without the occurrences of other meta-entities

_Source: ISO/IEC 15474-1:2002 Information technology -- CDIF framework -- Part 1: Overview, 4.2_

---

## key migration

> in data modeling, placing the primary key of a parent or generic entity in its child or category entity as a foreign key (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.96)

---

## key-style view

> view that represents the structure and semantics of data within an enterprise (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.97)

---

## keyword

> one or more words used as a reference to a specific set of actions intended to be performed during the execution of one or more test cases (ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-5:2024 Software and systems engineering--Software testing-- Part 5: Keyword-driven testing, 3.5)

---

## keyword dictionary

> repository containing a set of keywords reflecting the language and level of abstraction used to write test cases (ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-5:2024 Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 5: Keyword-driven testing, 3.9)

---

## keyword execution code

> implementation of a keyword that is intended to be executed by a test execution engine

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-5:2024 Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 5: Keyword-driven testing, 3.12_

---

## keyword test case

> sequence of keywords and the required values for their associated parameters (as applicable) that are composed to describe the actions of a test case

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-5:2024 Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 5: Keyword-driven testing, 3.13_

---

## keyword-driven testing

> testing using test cases composed from keywords

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-5:2024 Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 5: Keyword-driven testing, 3.10_

---

## keyword-driven testing framework

> test framework covering the functional capabilities of a keyword-driven editor, decomposer, data sequencer, keyword library, data repository, test environment, and including organizational processes that define their interaction and use

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-5:2024 Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 5: Keyword-driven testing, 3.11_

---

## kickoff meeting

> gathering of team members and other key stakeholders at the outset of a project to formally set expectations, gain a common understanding, and commence work (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## kNN

> k-nearest neighbors

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765j:2021_

---

## knowledge

> aspect of an instance's specification that is determined by the values of its attributes, participant properties, and constant, read-only operations

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.98_

---

## knowledge area (KA)

> identified group of knowledge

_Source: ISO/IEC 24773-4:2023 Software and systems engineering Certification of software and systems engineering professionals Part 4: Software engineering, 3.1_

---

## knowledge base (K-base)

> database that contains inference rules and information about human experience and expertise in a domain (ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary)

---

## knowledge management

> multi-disciplinary process of obtaining, preserving, sharing, using, and refreshing knowledge (IEEE 2675-2021, IEEE Standard for DevOps: Building Reliable and Secure Systems Including Application Build, Package, and Deployment, 3.1)

---

## known error

> result of a problem with an identified root cause or an identified workaround that reduces or

---

## KOPS

> kilo-operations per second; that is, thousands of operations per second (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## KPI

> key performance indicator

_Source: IEEE 2675-2021, IEEE Standard for DevOps: Building Reliable and Secure Systems Including Application Build, Package, and Deployment, 3.2_

---

## KPP

> key performance parameter

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-8:2019, Systems and software engineering Life cycle management Part 7: Application of systems engineering on defense programs, 3.2_

---

## KSA

> key system attribute

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-8:2019, Systems and software engineering Life cycle management Part 7: Application of systems engineering on defense programs, 3.2_

---

## label

> a name or identifier assigned to a computer program statement to enable other statements to refer to that statement

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## lag

> amount of time whereby a successor activity is required to be delayed with respect to a predecessor activity (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## language

> systematic means of communicating ideas by the use of conventionalized signs, sounds, gestures, or marks and rules for the formation of admissible expressions

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## language binding

> wrapper for a programming language to allow access to a library written in another programming language (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765l:2024)

---

## language processor

> computer program that translates, interprets, or performs other tasks required to process statements expressed in a given language (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## language standard

> standard that describes the characteristics of a language used to describe a requirements specification, a design, or test data

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## last responsible moment

> concept of deferring a decision to allow the team to consider multiple options until the cost of further delay would exceed the benefit (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## late binding

> the assignment of tasks to specific resources when the resources are available to start work, rather than when the project is planned (Software Extension to the PMBOK(R) Guide Fifth Edition)

---

## latency

> time interval between the instant at which an instruction control unit issues a call for data and the instant at which the transfer of data is started

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## latent variable

> variable representing a unidimensional construct

_Source: ISO/IEC 33003:2015 Information technology-- Process assessment--Requirements for process measurement frameworks, 3.8_

---

## lateral compression

> in software design, a form of demodularization in which two or more modules that execute one after the other are combined into a single module (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary)

---

## layer

> partition resulting from the functional division of a software system, where layers are organized in a hierarchy; there is only one layer at each level in the hierarchy; there is a superior/subordinate hierarchical dependency between the functional services provided by software in any two layers in the software system that exchange data directly; and the software in any two layers in the software system that exchange data interpret only part of that data identically

_Source: ISO/IEC 19761:2011 Software engineering -- COSMIC: a functional size measurement method, 2.15_

---

## layout

> physical organization of source code including the use of white space, grouping, blank lines, alignment, indentation, and parentheses

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## LCA

> life cycle assessment (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## LCC

> life-cycle cost

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-8:2019, Systems and software engineering Life cycle management Part 7: Application of systems engineering on defense programs, 3.2_

---

## LCCE

> life-cycle cost estimate

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-8:2019, Systems and software engineering Life cycle management Part 7: Application of systems engineering on defense programs, 3.2_

---

## LCL

> local coincidence logic

_Source: IEEE 1012-2024 IEEE Standard for System, Software, and Hardware Verification and Validation, 3.2_

---

## LCMS

> Learning Content Management System

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765l:2024_

---

## LCSP

> life cycle sustainment plan

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-7:2019, Systems and software engineering Life cycle management Part 7: Application of systems engineering on defense programs, 3.2_

---

## lead

> amount of time whereby a successor activity can be advanced with respect to a predecessor activity (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## lead startup canvas

> one-page template designed to communicate a business plan with key stakeholders in an efficient and effective manner (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## lead time

> time between a customer request and the actual delivery (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## lead time chart

> diagram showing the trend over time of the average lead time of the items completed in work (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## leadframe

> framework of a semiconductor device, made of plated metal (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765e:2015)

---

## leading decision

> loop control that is executed before the loop body (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## leaf diagram

> diagram that has no descendent diagrams (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765m:2024, 2.1.68)

---

## leaf node

> function that is not decomposed (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765m:2024, 2.1.69)

---

## learnability

> capability of a product to have specified users learn to use specified product functions within a specified amount of time (ISO/IEC 25010:2023, Systems and software engineering--Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)--Product quality model, 3.4.2)

---

## left-shift

> prioritizing the involvement of relevant stakeholders in applying quality activities, security, privacy, performance, verification, and validation earlier in the life cycle

_Source: IEEE 2675-2021, IEEE Standard for DevOps: Building Reliable and Secure Systems Including Application Build, Package, and Deployment, 3.1_

---

## legacy hardware

> hardware originally created without native information structures

_Source: ISO/IEC 19770-6:2024, Information technology IT asset management Part 6: Hardware identification tag, 3.1.5_

---

## legacy software

> software originally created without information structures

_Source: ISO/IEC 19770-5:2015 Information technology--IT asset management--Overview and vocabulary, 3.17_

---

## legal feasibility

> determination that the system of interest is consistent with applicable laws and regulations

_Source: IEEE 7000:2021, IEEE Standard Model Process for Addressing Ethical Concerns during System Design, 3.1_

---

## legend

> in the context of an architecture description, an informal documentation of conventions

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 42010:2022, Software, systems and enterprise Architecture description, 3.19_

---

## lessons learned

> knowledge gained during a project which shows how project events were addressed or should be addressed in the future with the purpose of improving future performance (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## lessons learned register

> project document used to record knowledge gained during a project so that it can be used in the current project and entered into the lessons learned repository (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## lessons learned repository

> a project document used to record knowledge gained during a project so that it can be used in the current project and entered into the lessons learned repository (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## level

> designation of the coverage and detail of a view (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.100)

---

## level of abstraction

> view of an object at a specific level of detail

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018 Systems and software engineering-Life cycle processes-Requirements engineering, 4.1.12_

---

## level of effort (LOE)

> support activity which does not produce definitive end products, generally characterized by a uniform rate of work performance over a period of time determined by the activities supported (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## level of risk

> magnitude of a risk or combination of risks, expressed in terms of the combination of consequences and their likelihood

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 15026-1:2019 Systems and software engineering--Systems and software assurance-- Part 1: Concepts and vocabulary, 3.3.5_

---

## level of service

> parameters, or combination of parameters, which reflect social, political, environmental and economic outcomes that the organization delivers

_Source: ISO/IEC 19770-1:2017 Information technology -- IT asset management -- Part 1: IT asset management systems--Requirements, 3.31_

---

## Levenshtein distance

> measure of the difference between two character sequences based on the minimum number of single character edits (insertion, deletion, or substitution) needed to convert one word to the other

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 26531:2023 Systems and software engineering -- Content management for product lifecycle, user and service management information for users, 3.1.21_

---

## lexicography

> decision technique that prioritizes the decision attributes (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## LFT&E

> live fire test and evaluation

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-8:2019, Systems and software engineering Life cycle management Part 7: Application of systems engineering on defense programs, 3.2_

---

## liaison

> relationship between a set of objects which results from the performance of some establishing behavior

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-2:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Foundations, 13.2.4_

---

## library

> place containing collections of work products and useful information items for people to read, borrow or refer to, and for machines to access and retrieve data from (ISO/IEC/IEEE 42020:2019 Software, systems and enterprise -- Architecture processes, 3.10)

---

## license

> legal agreement between two parties, the licensor and the licensee, as to the terms and conditions for the use or transfer of an intellectual property right from the licensor to the licensee

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## license compliance audit

> audit that reconciles license-related information from multiple information sources, such as entitlement consumption against entitlement rights

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## license model

> class of licenses with common characteristics

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## licensing standard

> standard that describes the characteristics of an authorization given by an official or a legal authority to an individual or organization to do or own a specific thing

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## life cycle

> evolution of a system, product, service, project or other human-made entity from conception through retirement (ISO/IEC/IEEE 12207:2026 Systems and software engineering--Software life cycle processes, 3.1.26)

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288:2023 Systems and software engineering--System life cycle processes, 3.21_

---

## life cycle assessment (LCA)

> tool used to evaluate the total environmental impact of a product, process, or system (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## life cycle cost

> total cost of a system over its entire life (INCOSE Systems Engineering Handbook, 5th ed.)

---

## life cycle model

> framework of processes and activities concerned with the life cycle that can be organized into stages, acting as a common reference for communication and understanding (ISO/IEC/IEEE 12207:2026 Systems and software engineering--Software life cycle processes, 3.1.37)

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288:2023 Systems and software engineering--System life cycle processes, 3.22_

---

## life cycle processes

> set of interrelated or interacting activities that result in the development or assessment of system, software, or hardware products (IEEE 1012-2024 IEEE Standard for System, Software, and Hardware Verification and Validation, 3.1)

---

## lightweight process

> process with a single thread of control; a task

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## likelihood

> probability of something happening

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 15026-1:2019 Systems and software engineering-- Systems and software assurance--Part 1: Concepts and vocabulary, 3.3.6_

---

## limit

> restriction on rights or privileges granted by a software entitlement

_Source: ISO/IEC 19770-3:2016 Information technology--IT asset management--Part 3: Entitlement schema, 3.1.16_

---

## limited entry table

> decision table where all the conditions and actions are completely described without reference to the rules

_Source: ISO 5806:1984 Information processing -- Specification of single-hit decision tables, 3.14_

---

## line of code

> programming-language statement; a non-comment, nonblank deliverable source statement

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## linear interpolation

> approximation of the value of a function at a given point, based on values on a straight line between two known points

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## link

> reference from some part of one document to some part of another document or another part of the same document

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 23026:2023 Systems and software engineering--Engineering and management of websites for systems, software, and services information, 3.1.15_

---

## linkage editor

> computer program that creates a single load module from two or more independently translated object modules or load modules by resolving cross-references among the modules and, possibly, by relocating elements (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## linking loader

> computer program that reads one or more object modules into main memory in preparation for execution, creates a single load module by resolving cross-references among the separate modules, and, in some cases, adjusts the addresses to reflect the storage locations into which the code has been loaded (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## list

> set of data items, each of which has the same data definition

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## list processing language

> programming language designed to facilitate the manipulation of data expressed in the form of lists (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## listing

> ordered display or printout of data items, program statements, or other information

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## literal

> in a source program, an explicit representation of the value of an item

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## LITSR

> level interim test status report

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering--Vocabulary_

---

## load

> to read machine code into main memory in preparation for execution and, in some cases, to perform address adjustment and linking of modules

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## load map

> computer-generated list that identifies the location or size of all or selected parts of memory-resident code or data

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## load module

> computer program or subprogram in a form suitable for loading into main storage for execution by a computer; usually the output of a linkage editor (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary)

---

## load testing

> type of performance efficiency testing conducted to evaluate the behavior of a test item under anticipated conditions of varying load, usually between anticipated conditions of low, typical, and peak usage

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-1:2022, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 1: General concepts, 3.43_

---

## load-and-go

> operating technique in which there are no stops between the loading and execution phases of a computer program

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## loaded origin

> address of the initial storage location of a computer program at the time the program is loaded into main memory (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## loader

> computer program that reads machine code into main memory in preparation for execution and, in some cases, adjusts the addresses and links the modules

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary_

---

## LOC

> lines of code

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-5:2017 Systems and software engineering--Life cycle management--Part 5: Software development planning, 4_

---

## LOCA

> loss of coolant accident

_Source: IEEE 1012-2024 IEEE Standard for System, Software, and Hardware Verification and Validation, 3.2_

---

## local area network (LAN)

> computer network located on a user's premises within a limited geographical area (ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary)

---

## local compaction

> in microprogramming, compaction in which microoperations are not moved beyond the boundaries of the single-entry, single-exit sequential blocks in which they occur (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## local customization

> FSM method that has been modified for local use, such that it can produce different functional sizes from those obtained prior to modification

_Source: ISO/IEC 14143-1:2007 Information technology--Software measurement--Functional size measurement; Part 1: Definition of concepts, 3.9_

---

## local data

> data that can be accessed by only one module or set of nested modules in a computer program

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## local SAM owner

> individual at a level of the organization below that of the SAM owner who is identified as being responsible for SAM for a defined part of the organization

_Source: ISO/IEC 19770-5:2015 Information technology--IT asset management--Overview and vocabulary, 3.21_

---

## local variable

> variable that can be accessed by only one module or set of nested modules in a computer program (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## locality of quality responsibility

> assignment of responsibility for specific quality-related requirements or

---

## localization

> creation of a national or specific regional version of a product and its information for users

---

## location facility

> set of service primitives that allow a client-side binder object to ask a server-side if it will accept requests carrying invocations to a particular (computational) server object (ISO/IEC 14752:2000 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Protocol support for computational interactions, 3.3.9)

---

## location in space

> interval of arbitrary size in space at which an action can occur

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-2:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Foundations, 8.10_

---

## location in time

> interval of arbitrary size in time at which an action can occur

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-2:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Foundations, 8.11_

---

## location reference

> indicator following a heading or subheading in an index or table of contents, showing to which part of the document the heading or subheading refers

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765a:2011_

---

## location transparency

> distribution transparency which masks the use of information about location in space when identifying and binding to interfaces

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-3:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Architecture, 4.4.1.3_

---

## lockout

> computer resource allocation technique in which shared resources (especially data) are protected by permitting access by only one device or process at a time (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary)

---

## LOE

> level of effort

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025_

---

## log

> document used to record and describe or denote selected items identified during execution of a process or activity. Usually used with a modifier, such as issue, change, or assumption (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## log off

> to end a session (ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary)

---

## log on

> to initiate a session (ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary)

---

## logic programming language

> a programming language used to express programs in terms of control

---

## logical cohesion

> type of cohesion in which the tasks performed by a software module perform logically similar functions (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## logical file

> logical group of data as seen by the user

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 32430:2025, Software engineering Software non-functional size measurement, 3.1.22_

---

## logical interface

> input-output flow between two or more system elements or software system elements and the function that determines it

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-6:2023 Systems and software engineering Life cycle management Part 6: System and software integration, 3.1.5_

---

## logical record

> set of data which is processed in a single iteration of the main procedure (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765a:2011)

---

## logical source statement (LSS)

> software instruction, independent of the physical format (lines of code) in which it appears (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## logical trace

> execution trace that records only branch or jump instructions (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## logical transaction

> the basic functional component of Mk II FPA (ISO/IEC 20968:2002 Software engineering -- Mk II Function Point Analysis -- Counting Practices Manual, 10)

---

## logical type

> data type whose members can assume only logical values (usually TRUE and FALSE) and can be operated on only by logical operators, such as and, OR, and NOT (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## loop

> sequence of computer program statements that is executed repeatedly until a given condition is met or while a given condition is true

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## loop assertion

> logical expression specifying one or more conditions that are met each time a particular point in a program loop is executed (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## loop body

> part of a loop that accomplishes the loop's primary purpose (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## loop control

> part of a loop that determines whether to exit from the loop (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## loop-control variable

> program variable used to determine whether to exit from a loop

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## loopback

> an internal arrow that is the output of a box whose box number is greater than the box number of the box that uses that arrow as input, control, or mechanism (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765m:2024, 2.1.70)

---

## loopback testing

> testing in which signals or data from a test device are input to a system or component, and results are returned to the test device for measurement or comparison

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## lossage

> detrimental effect on stored data of a failure or an impairment

_Source: IEEE 982:2024, Standard for Measures of the Software Aspects of Dependability, 3.1_

---

## LOU

> letter of understanding

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 41062:2024, Software engineering Life cycle processes Software acquisition, 3.2_

---

## low level

> specific; detailed

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## low-level design

> design at the individual-routine or, sometimes, class level under the guidance of a more general design (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## low-level keyword

> keyword that covers only one or very few simple actions and is not composed from other keywords

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-4:2021 Software and systems engineering -- Software testing -- Part 4: Test techniques, 4.9_

---

## low-profile quad flat package (LQFP)

> semiconductor device based on a leadframe with gull wing-shaped leads on all four sides (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765e:2015)

---

## lowclass

> if an instance is in a class S and not in any subclass of S, then S is the lowclass for the instance

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.103_

---

## LQFP

> low-profile quad flat package

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765d:2015_

---

## LRIP

> low-rate initial production

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-8:2019, Systems and software engineering Life cycle management Part 7: Application of systems engineering on defense programs, 3.2_

---

## LT

> less than

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 29119-13:2022, Software and systems engineering Software testing Part 13: Using the ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119 series in the testing of biometric systems, 3.2_

---

## LTC

> level test case

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering--Vocabulary_

---

## LTD

> level test design

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering--Vocabulary_

---

## LTE

> less than or equal

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 29119-13:2022, Software and systems engineering Software testing Part 13: Using the ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119 series in the testing of biometric systems, 3.2_

---

## LTL

> level test log

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering--Vocabulary_

---

## LTP

> level test plan (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering--Vocabulary)

---

## LTPr

> level test procedure (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering--Vocabulary)

---

## LTR

> level test report

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering--Vocabulary_

---

## M&S

> modeling and simulation (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-8:2019, Systems and software engineering Life cycle

---

## MAC

> Media Access Control

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 23026:2023 Systems and software engineering--Engineering and management of websites for systems, software, and services information, 3.2_

---

## machine code

> computer instructions and data definitions expressed in a form that can be recognized by the processing unit of a computer (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## machine language

> language that can be recognized by the processing unit of a computer (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## machine learning (ML)

> process using computational techniques to enable systems to learn from data or experience

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 29119-11:2020, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 11: Guidelines on the testing of AI-based systems, 3.1.43_

---

## machine-dependent

> pertaining to software that relies on features unique to a particular type of computer and therefore executes only on computers of that type (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary)

---

## machine-independent

> pertaining to software that does not rely on features unique to a particular type of computer, and therefore executes on computers of more than one type (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## machine-readable

> pertaining to data in a form that can be automatically generated by and input to a computer (IEEE 2675-2021, IEEE Standard for DevOps: Building Reliable and Secure Systems Including Application Build, Package, and Deployment, 3.1)

---

## macro

> in software engineering, a predefined sequence of computer instructions that is inserted into a program, usually during assembly or compilation, at each place that its corresponding macroinstruction appears in the program (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## macro library

> collection of macros available for use by a macrogenerator (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## macroassembler

> assembler that includes, or performs the functions of, a macrogenerator

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## macrogenerator

> routine, often part of an assembler or compiler, that replaces each macroinstruction in a source program with the predefined sequence of instructions that the macroinstruction represents (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## macroinstruction

> source code instruction that is replaced by a predefined sequence of source instructions, usually in the same language as the rest of the program and usually during assembly or compilation (ISO/IEC/IEEE

---

## macroprocessor

> routine or set of routines provided in some assemblers and compilers to support the definition and use of macros

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## macroprogramming

> computer programming using macros and macroinstructions

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## magic number

> literal value that is used by a program directly rather than being embedded in a named constant or variable (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## magnetic core memory

> volatile memory that uses magnetic rings as the storage element

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765c:2014_

---

## main library

> software library containing controlled versions of software and documentation from which working copies can be made for distribution and use (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765k:2022)

---

## main probe

> phase to perform repetitive cycle for gathering and analyzing data for finding strengths and challenges of an organization

_Source: ISO/IEC 26561:2019 Software systems engineering--Methods and tools for product line technical probe, 3.2_

---

## main procedure

> all those activities subsequent to the general initiation routine and prior to the general termination routine within the complete procedure

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765a:2011_

---

## main program

> software component that is called by the operating system of a computer and that usually calls other software components (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## main schedule

> high-level schedule that includes the major milestones, deliverables, and work breakdown structure elements (activities) (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025)

---

## mainframe

> computer intended to run in a computer center, with extensive capabilities and resources to which other computers can be connected so that they can share facilities

_Source: ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary_

---

## maintain

> add, change or delete data through an elementary process

_Source: ISO/IEC 20926:2009 Software and systems engineering -- Software measurement -- IFPUG functional size measurement method 2009, 3.40_

---

## maintainability

> degree of effectiveness and efficiency with which a product or system can be modified

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 14764:2021, Software engineering -- Software life cycle processes -- Maintenance, 3.1.6_

---

## maintainability plan

> document setting out the specific maintainability practices, resources and sequence of activities relevant to software (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## maintainability testing

> test type conducted to evaluate the degree of effectiveness and efficiency with which a test item can be modified

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-1:2022, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 1: General concepts, 3.45_

---

## maintainer

> individual or organization that performs maintenance activities (ISO/IEC 25000:2014 Systems and software Engineering--Systems and software product Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) -- Guide to SQuaRE, 4.31)

---

## maintenance

> process of modifying a software system or component after delivery to correct faults, improve performance or other attributes, or adapt to a changed environment (ISO/IEC 25051:2014 Software engineering -- Software product Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) -- Requirements for quality of Ready to Use Software Product (RUSP) and instructions for testing, 4.1.9)

---

## maintenance branch

> branch where most development concerns bug fixes

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## maintenance compliance and versioning

> degree to which a service provides maintenance according to the service level agreement (SLA), and a new version is assigned and published after maintenance (ISO/IEC TS 25052- 1:2022, Systems and software engineering--Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE): cloud services--Part 1: Quality model, 3.1.6.1)

---

## maintenance manual (MM)

> software engineering project-deliverable document that enables a system's maintenance personnel (rather than users) to maintain the system (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## maintenance personnel

> software engineers who maintain software systems

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## maintenance plan

> document setting out the specific maintenance practices, resources, and sequence of activities relevant to maintaining a software product

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary_

---

## maintenance program

> organizational structure, responsibilities, procedures, processes, and resources used for implementing the maintenance plan (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## maintenance project

> software development project described as maintenance to correct errors in an original requirements specification, to adapt a system to a new environment, or to enhance a system

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## make or buy analysis

> process of gathering and organizing data about product requirements and analyzing them against available alternatives including the purchase or internal manufacture of the product (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## malfunction

> behavior of a system or component that deviates from the specifications

_Source: ISO/IEC 23643:2020, Software and systems engineering--Capabilities of software safety and security verification tools, 3.11_

---

## manage

> [requirements] provide storing and editing capabilities, tracking history of edition, versioning, author identification, change management, time stamping, user notification for content changes, security rights control

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 24766:2009 Information technology--Systems and software engineering--Guide for requirements engineering tool capabilities, 3.2_

---

## manage stakeholder expectations

> [Process] the process of communicating and working with stakeholders to meet their needs and addressing issues as they occur (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## manageability

> degree to which IT infrastructure management can attain and keep an application in its operational state (ISO/IEC 16350-2015 Information technology--Systems and software engineering--Application management, 4.24)

---

## managed network

> network or set of networks established and controlled by one or more organizations to meet specific organizational or business needs

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 23026:2023 Systems and software engineering--Engineering and management of websites for systems, software, and services information, 3.1.16_

---

## managed process

> performed process that is planned and executed in accordance with policy; employs skilled people having adequate resources to produce controlled outputs; involves relevant stakeholders; is monitored, controlled, and reviewed; and is evaluated for adherence to its process description (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## managed role

> view of the management interface of an object which is being managed within an ODP system

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-2:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Foundations, 14.4_

---

## managed website

> site created and maintained based on organizational guidelines (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765l:2024)

---

## management

> coordinated activities to direct and control an organization (ISO/IEC 29110-1-2:2024, Systems and software engineering Lifecycle profiles for Very Small Entities (VSEs) Part 1-2: Vocabulary, 3.52)

---

## management information

> knowledge concerning objects which are of relevance to management (ISO/IEC

---

## management notification

> event notification initiated by an object operating in a managed role

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746- 2:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Foundations, 14.6_

---

## management process

> activities that are undertaken to help ensure that the software engineering processes are performed in a manner consistent with the organization's policies, goals, and standards

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## management reserve

> amount of the project budget or project schedule held outside the performance measurement baseline for management control purposes that is reserved for unforeseen work that is within the project scope (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## management review

> systematic evaluation of a software acquisition, supply, development, operation, or maintenance process performed by or on behalf of management that monitors progress, determines the status of plans and schedules, confirms requirements and their system allocation, or evaluates the effectiveness of management approaches used to achieve fitness for purpose

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765i:2020_

---

## management skills

> ability to plan, organize, direct, and control individuals or groups of people to achieve specific goals

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765h:2019_

---

## management system

> set of interrelated or interacting elements to establish policy and objectives and to achieve those objectives (ISO/IEC 19770-1:2017 Information technology -- IT asset management -- Part 1: IT asset management systems--Requirements, 3.33)

---

## managerial independence

> of software quality assurance (SQA), situation in which the responsibility of the SQA effort is vested in an organization separate from the development and project management organizations

_Source: IEEE 730- 2014 IEEE Standard for Software Quality Assurance Processes, 3.2_

---

## managing role

> view of an object which is performing managing actions

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-2:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Foundations, 14.5_

---

## mandatory

> syntax keyword used to specify a total mapping (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.104)

---

## mandatory dependency

> relationship that is contractually required or inherent in the nature of the work (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## mandatory nonidentifying relationship

> nonidentifying relationship in which an instance of the child entity is related to an instance of the parent entity (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.105)

---

## manual testing

> humans performing tests by entering information into a test item and verifying the results

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-5:2024 Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 5: Keyword-driven testing, 3.15_

---

## manufacture

> in software engineering, to copy software to disks, chips, or other devices for distribution to customers or users

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## manufacturing phase

> stage in the software life cycle during which the basic version of a software product is adapted to a specified set of operational environments and is distributed to a customer base

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## many-sorted algebra

> mathematical structure comprising a set of sets and a set of functions taking these sets as domains and co-domains

_Source: ISO/IEC 15909-1:2019 Systems and software engineering--High-level Petri nets--Part 1: Concepts, definitions and graphical notation, 3.14_

---

## many-to-many relationship

> relationship between two state classes (not necessarily distinct) in which each instance of one class can be associated with any number of instances of a second class (possibly none), and each instance of the second class can be related to any number of instances of the first class (possibly none)

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.106_

---

## MAO

> mean operational availability

_Source: IEEE 982:2024, Standard for Measures of the Software Aspects of Dependability, 3.2_

---

## map program

> software tool, often part of a compiler or assembler, that generates a load map

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## mapping

> assigned correspondence between two things that is represented as a set of ordered pairs

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.107_

---

## mapping completeness

> a designation of whether a mapping is complete (totally mapped) or incomplete (partial) (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.108)

---

## mapping document

> document that relates a standard and an existing industry practice

_Source: ISO/IEC 19770-8:2020, Information technology IT asset management Part 8: Guidelines for mapping of industry practices to/from the ISO/IEC 19770 family of standards, 3.1_

---

## marking

> symbols, pictograms, warnings, logos or inscriptions on the product, label, or packaging to identify its type, which can also include short textual messages (IEC/IEEE 82079-1:2019 Preparation of information for use (instructions for use) of products: Part 1: Principles and general requirements, 3.24)

---

## marking (of a net)

> the set of the place markings for all places of the net

_Source: ISO/IEC 15909-1:2019 Systems and software engineering--High-level Petri nets--Part 1: Concepts, definitions and graphical notation, 3.15_

---

## marking of a place

> a multiset of tokens associated with ('residing in') the place

_Source: ISO/IEC 15909-1:2019 Systems and software engineering--High-level Petri nets--Part 1: Concepts, definitions and graphical notation, 3.16_

---

## markup language

> method of defining and describing the structure of different types of electronic documents

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 26511:2018 Systems and software engineering--Requirements for managers of information for users of systems, software, and services, 3.1.20_

---

## MARR

> minimum attractive rate of return (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-

---

## mask

> a pattern of bits or characters designed to be logically combined with an unknown data item to retain or suppress portions of the data item (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## mask ROM

> read-only memory unit whose circuits are programmed during the manufacturing process

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765c:2014_

---

## master library

> a software library containing master copies of software and documentation from which working copies can be made for distribution and use (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## material

> aggregate of things used by an organization in an undertaking (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765h:2019)

---

## maturity level

> point on an ordinal scale of organizational process maturity that characterizes the maturity of the organizational unit assessed in the scope of the maturity model used

_Source: ISO/IEC 33001:2015 Information technology-- Process assessment--Concepts and terminology, 3.4.1_

---

## maturity model

> model derived from one or more specified process assessment model(s) that identifies the process sets associated with the levels in a specified scale of organizational process maturity

_Source: ISO/IEC 33001:2015 Information technology--Process assessment--Concepts and terminology, 3.3.7_

---

## maximax rule

> in decision making under uncertainty, assuming that the best state of nature will happen, selection of the alternative that has the best payoff from all of the best payoffs (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## maximin rule

> in decision making under uncertainty, assuming that the worst state of nature will happen, selection of the alternative that has the best payoff from all of the worst payoffs (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## MBLa

> manage benchmarking business level activity

_Source: ISO/IEC 29155-2:2013 Systems and software engineering-- Information technology project performance benchmarking framework--Part 2: Requirements for benchmarking, 4_

---

## MBSA

> model-based safety assessment

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24641:2023. Systems and Software engineering--Methods and tools for model-based systems and software engineering, 3.2_

---

## MBSE

> model-based systems engineering

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 14764:2021, Software engineering -- Software life cycle processes -- Maintenance, 3.2_

---

## MBSSE

> model-based systems and software engineering

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24641:2023. Systems and Software engineering--Methods and tools for model-based systems and software engineering, 3.1.16_

---

## MCDC

> modified condition/decision coverage

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-4:2021 Software and systems engineering -- Software testing -- Part 4: Test techniques, 3.36_

---

## MCDM

> multiple-criteria decision making

_Source: ISO/IEC 33003:2015 Information technology--Process assessment-- Requirements for process measurement frameworks, 3.9_

---

## MCI

> model configuration item (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24641:2023. Systems and Software engineering--Methods and tools for model-based systems and software engineering, 3.1.18)

---

## MCU

> microcontroller unit

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765c:2014_

---

## MD5

> message digest 5

_Source: ISO/IEC 19770-5:2015 Information technology--IT asset management--Overview and vocabulary, 3.22_

---

## MDD

> model driven development

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018 Systems and software engineering-Life cycle processes-Requirements engineering, 3.2_

---

## MDT

> mean downtime

_Source: IEEE 982:2024, Standard for Measures of the Software Aspects of Dependability, 3.2_

---

## mean

> arithmetic average of a collection of numbers (IEEE 982:2024, Standard for Measures of the Software Aspects of Dependability, 3.1)

---

## mean execution time

> the mean value of all execution times of tasks of the j-the task type which were submitted within the rating interval

_Source: ISO/IEC 14756:1999 Information technology -- Measurement and rating of performance of computer-based software systems, 4.8_

---

## mean execution time rating value

> the quotient (corresponding to the j-th task type) of the mean execution time reference value and the measured mean execution time

_Source: ISO/IEC 14756:1999 Information technology -- Measurement and rating of performance of computer-based software systems, 4.9_

---

## mean execution time reference value

> the mean execution time maximally accepted by the emulated user

_Source: ISO/IEC 14756:1999 Information technology -- Measurement and rating of performance of computer-based software systems, 4.1_

---

## mean time between failures (MTBF)

> the expected or observed time between consecutive failures in a system or component (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## mean time to repair (MTTR)

> expected or observed duration required to return a malfunctioning system or component to normal operations

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## meaningful

> user-recognizable and satisfies a functional user requirement

_Source: ISO/IEC 20926:2009 Software and systems engineering -- Software measurement -- IFPUG functional size measurement method 2009, 3.41_

---

## measurable concept

> abstract relationship between attributes of entities and information needs

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 15939:2017 Systems and software engineering--Measurement process, 3.14_

---

## measurand

> particular quantity subject to measurement (ISO/IEC TR 14143-3:2003 Information technology -- Software measurement -- Functional size measurement -- Part 3: Verification of functional size measurement methods, 3.5)

---

## measure

> variable to which a value is assigned as the result of measurement (ISO/IEC 25000:2014 Systems and software Engineering--Systems and software product Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) -- Guide to SQuaRE, 4.18)

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 15939:2017 Systems and software engineering--Measurement process, 3.15_

---

## measure of effectiveness (MOE)

> operational measure of success that is closely related to the achievement of the operational objective being evaluated in the intended operational environment under a specified set of conditions

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-4:2016 Systems and software engineering-Life cycle management-Part 4: Systems engineering planning, 4.7_

---

## measure of performance (MOP)

> engineering parameter that provides critical performance requirements to satisfy a measure of effectiveness (MOE)

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-4:2016 Systems and software engineering-Life cycle management-Part 4: Systems engineering planning, 4.8_

---

## measurement

> set of operations having the object of determining a value of a measure (ISO/IEC 25000:2014 Systems and software Engineering--Systems and software product Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) -- Guide to SQuaRE, 4.20)

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 15939:2017 Systems and software engineering--Measurement process, 3.17_

---

## measurement analyst

> individual or organization that is responsible for the planning, performance, evaluation, and improvement of measurement

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 15939:2017 Systems and software engineering--Measurement process, 3.18_

---

## measurement experience base

> data store that contains the evaluation of the information products and the measurement process as well as any lessons learned during the measurement process (ISO/IEC/IEEE 15939:2017

---

## measurement function

> algorithm or calculation performed to combine two or more base measures

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 15939:2017 Systems and software engineering--Measurement process, 3.20_

---

## measurement librarian

> individual or organization that is responsible for managing measurement data stores

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## measurement method

> logical sequence of operations, described generically, used in quantifying an attribute with respect to a specified scale

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 15939:2017 Systems and software engineering--Measurement process, 3.21_

---

## measurement model

> implicit or explicit relationship between a latent variable and its (multi-item) measures

_Source: ISO/IEC 33001:2015 Information technology--Process assessment--Concepts and terminology, 3.10_

---

## measurement performance domain

> performance domain that addresses activities and functions associated with assessing project performance and taking appropriate actions to maintain acceptable performance (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## measurement procedure

> set of operations, described specifically, used in the performance of a particular measurement according to a given method

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 15939:2017 Systems and software engineering--Measurement process, 3.22_

---

## measurement process

> process for establishing, planning, performing and evaluating software measurement within an overall project or organizational measurement structure

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 15939:2017 Systems and software engineering--Measurement process, 3.23_

---

## measurement process owner

> individual or organization responsible for the measurement process

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 15939:2017 Systems and software engineering--Measurement process, 3.24_

---

## measurement source

> set of artifacts used for quality measures when performing a quality evaluation (ISO/IEC 25040:2024 Systems and software engineering--Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)-- Quality evaluation framework, 3.5)

---

## measurement sponsor

> individual or organization that authorizes and supports the establishment of the measurement process

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 15939:2017 Systems and software engineering--Measurement process, 3.25_

---

## measurement standard

> standard that describes the characteristics of evaluating a process or product

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## measurement user

> individual or organization that uses the information products

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 15939:2017 Systems and software engineering--Measurement process, 3.26_

---

## measuring instrument

> device intended to be used to make measurements, alone or in conjunction with supplementary devices

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 14143-3:2003 Information technology -- Software measurement -- Functional size measurement -- Part 3: Verification of functional size measurement methods, 3.6_

---

## mechanism arrow

> arrow or arrow segment that expresses IDEF0 mechanism (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765m:2024, 2.1.72)

---

## mechanism loopback

> loopback of output from one function to be mechanism for another function in the same diagram

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765m:2024, 2.1.73_

---

## member product

> product belonging to the product line (ISO/IEC 26550:2015 Software and systems engineering- -Reference model for product line engineering and management, 3.15)

---

## memory

> addressable storage space in a processing unit and all other internal storage that is used to execute instructions

_Source: ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary_

---

## memory capacity

> maximum number of items that can be held in a given computer memory; usually measured in words or bytes (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## memory compaction

> storage allocation technique in which the contents of all allocated storage areas are moved to the beginning of the storage space and the remaining storage blocks are combined into a single block

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## memory dump

> display of the contents of all or part of a computer's internal storage, usually in binary, octal, or hexadecimal form (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## memory map

> diagram that shows where programs and data are stored in a computer's memory

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## menu

> list displayed on a screen showing available functions from which the user can select an action to be initiated

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 26514:2022, Systems and software engineering -- Design and development of information for users, 3.1.33_

---

## menu by-pass

> in a menu-driven system, a feature that permits advanced users to perform functions in a command-driven mode without selecting options from the menus

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## menu-driven

> pertaining to a system or mode of operation in which the user directs the system through menu selections (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## merge

> to combine different changes to the same file (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## merge from current

> to merge changes from the current branch into the stable branches (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## message

> communication sent from one object to another (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.110)

---

## message digest 5 (MD5)

> algorithm that is used to verify data integrity through the creation of a 128-bit message digest from data input (which may be a message of any length) that is claimed to be as unique to that specific data as a fingerprint is to the specific individual (ISO/IEC 19770-5:2015 Information technology--IT asset management-- Overview and vocabulary, 3.22)

---

## meta-

> prefix to a concept to imply definition information about the concept (ISO/IEC 15474-1:2002 Information technology -- CDIF framework -- Part 1: Overview, 4.2)

---

## meta-attribute

> definition of a characteristic of a meta-entity or meta-relationship (ISO/IEC 15474-1:2002 Information technology -- CDIF framework -- Part 1: Overview, 4.2)

---

## meta-entity

> definition of a type of data object that occurs in CDIF models (ISO/IEC 15474-1:2002 Information technology -- CDIF framework -- Part 1: Overview, 4.2)

---

## meta-meta-attribute

> definition of a characteristic of a meta-meta-entity or meta-meta-relationship (ISO/IEC 15474-1:2002 Information technology -- CDIF framework -- Part 1: Overview, 4.2)

---

## meta-meta-entity

> a definition of the behavior and structure of meta-entities, meta-relationships, meta-attributes, or subject areas (ISO/IEC 15474-1:2002 Information technology -- CDIF framework -- Part 1: Overview, 4.2)

---

## meta-meta-relationship

> definition of a type of data object that occurs in CDIF metamodels (ISO/IEC 15474- 1:2002 Information technology -- CDIF framework -- Part 1: Overview, 4.2)

---

## meta-object

> generic term for meta-entities, meta-relationships and meta-attributes (ISO/IEC 15474-1:2002

---

## meta-object facility

> specification of the object management group for repositories of type information for arbitrary type systems (ISO/IEC 14769:2001 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Type Repository Function, 3.3.1)

---

## meta-relationship

> definition of a type of data object that occurs in CDIF models (ISO/IEC 15474-1:2002 Information technology -- CDIF framework -- Part 1: Overview, 4.2)

---

## metadata

> data that describe other data (ISO/IEC 25024:2015 Systems and software engineering - Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) - Measurement of data, 4.29)

---

## metalanguage

> language used to specify some or all aspects of a language (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## metamodel

> special kind of model that specifies the abstract syntax of a modeling language

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24641:2023. Systems and Software engineering--Methods and tools for model-based systems and software engineering, 3.1.12_

---

## metamodel element

> element of a meta-model from which model elements are instantiated

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765l:2024_

---

## metamorphic relation

> description of how a change in the test inputs from the source test case to the follow-up test case affects a change (or not) in the expected outputs from the source test case to the follow-up test case

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 29119-11:2020, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 11: Guidelines on the testing of AI-based systems, 3.1.44_

---

## metamorphic testing

> testing where the expected results are not based on the specification but are instead extrapolated from previous actual results

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 29119-11:2020, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 11: Guidelines on the testing of AI-based systems, 3.1.45_

---

## method

> code that is executed to perform a service

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765l:2024_

---

## method engineer

> person who designs, builds, extends and maintains methodologies (ISO/IEC 24744:2014

---

## method standard

> standard that describes the characteristics of the orderly process or procedure used in the engineering of a product or performing a service

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary_

---

## methodology

> system of practices, techniques, procedures, and rules used by those who work in a discipline (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## methodology element

> simple component of a methodology (ISO/IEC 24744:2014 Software Engineering-- Metamodel for development methodologies, 3.6)

---

## metric

> measure or unit of measure that is designed to facilitate decision-making and improve performance and accountability through collection, analysis, and reporting of relevant data

_Source: IEEE 7005 2021, IEEE Standard for Transparent Employer Data Governance, 3.1_

---

## MFA

> multi-factor authentication

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 23026:2023 Systems and software engineering--Engineering and management of websites for systems, software, and services information, 3.2_

---

## MFLOPS

> millions of floating-point operations per second

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## micro code assembler

> computer program that translates microprograms from symbolic form to binary form

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## microarchitecture

> microword definition, data flow, timing constraints, and precedence constraints that characterize a given microprogrammed computer

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary_

---

## microcode

> collection of microinstructions, comprising part of, or all of, a set of microprograms (IEEE 1012-2024 IEEE Standard for System, Software, and Hardware Verification and Validation, 3.1)

---

## microcomputer

> digital computer whose processing unit consists of one or more microprocessors, and includes

---

## microcontroller (unit) (MCU)

> unit with application control function on a single chip (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765c:2014)

---

## microinstruction

> in microprogramming, an instruction that specifies one or more of the basic operations needed to carry out a machine language instruction (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## microoperation

> in microprogramming, one of the basic operations needed to carry out a machine language instruction (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## microprocessor

> processor whose elements have been miniaturized into one or a few integrated circuits

_Source: ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary_

---

## microprogram

> sequence of instructions, called microinstructions, specifying the basic operations needed to carry out a machine language instruction

_Source: IEEE 1012-2024 IEEE Standard for System, Software, and Hardware Verification and Validation_

---

## microprogrammable computer

> microprogrammed computer in which microprograms can be created or altered by the user

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## microprogrammed computer

> computer in which machine language instructions are implemented by microprograms rather than by hard-wired logic (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary)

---

## microprogramming

> designing and implementing the control logic of a computer by identifying the basic operations needed to carry out each machine language instruction and representing these operations as sequences of instructions in a special memory called control store (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary)

---

## microword

> addressable element in the control store of a microprogrammed computer

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## middleware

> software layer between an operating system and the software applications

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765d:2015_

---

## migratability

> ability to change the configuration, substituting one reference point of an object for another while the object is being used (ISO/IEC 10746-2:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Foundations, 15.4.2)

---

## migrated attribute

> foreign key attribute of a child entity (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.113)

---

## migration

> moving a cluster to a different capsule

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-3:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Architecture, 8.1.27_

---

## migration transparency

> distribution transparency which masks from an object, the ability of a system to change the location of that object (ISO/IEC 10746-3:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Architecture, 4.4.1.4)

---

## MIL

> mean impairment lossage

_Source: IEEE 982:2024, Standard for Measures of the Software Aspects of Dependability, 3.2_

---

## milestone

> significant point or event in a project, program, or portfolio (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## milestone review

> formal review of a work product and supporting evidence used to determine its acceptability for use in the next stage of development or for delivery (ISO/IEC 20246:2017 Software and systems engineering -- Work product reviews, 3.10)

---

## milestone schedule

> type of schedule that presents milestones with planned dates (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## MIM

> Management Information Model

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-1:1998 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference model: Overview_

---

## minicomputer

> digital computer that is functionally intermediate between a microcomputer and a mainframe (ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary)

---

## minimalism

> principle for the selection of information for users that supports task performance, troubleshooting, and problem resolution

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 26511:2018 Systems and software engineering--Requirements for managers of information for users of systems, software, and services, 3.1.22_

---

## minimax regret rule

> in decision making under uncertainty, selection of the alternative that minimizes the regret that one would have, if one chose the wrong alternative under each state of nature; that is, the alternative that has the smallest maximum regret (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## minimum attractive rate of return (MARR)

> lowest rate of return at which an organization will consider investing; the interest rate used in business decision analysis (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## minimum delay programming

> programming technique in which storage locations for computer instructions and data are chosen so that access time is minimized

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary_

---

## minimum tasks

> those verification and validation tasks required for the integrity level assigned to the system, software, or hardware to be verified and validated

_Source: IEEE 1012-2024 IEEE Standard for System, Software, and Hardware Verification and Validation, 3.1_

---

## minimum viable product

> version of a work product with just enough features and requirements to satisfy early customers and provide feedback for future development (IEEE 2675-2021, IEEE Standard for DevOps: Building Reliable and Secure Systems Including Application Build, Package, and Deployment, 3.1)

---

## MIPS

> million instructions per second (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## mirror site

> duplicate copy of a main site maintained on a different host typically to provide redundancy, higher performance, or local access

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765l:2024_

---

## MIS

> management information system

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765b:2013_

---

## mission

> important operational job or duty assigned to a resource or a group of resources or certain groups of people

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24641:2023. Systems and Software engineering--Methods and tools for model-based systems and software engineering, 3.1.13_

---

## mistake

> human action that produces an incorrect result (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## mixed entry table

> decision table whose stub consists of rows in which limited and extended entries are written

_Source: ISO 5806:1984 Information processing -- Specification of single-hit decision tables, 3.16_

---

## mixed mode

> pertaining to an expression that contains two or more different data types (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## ML

> machine learning

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 29119-11:2020, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 11: Guidelines on the testing of AI-based systems, 3.1.43_

---

## MLDT

> mean logistic downtime

_Source: IEEE 982:2024, Standard for Measures of the Software Aspects of Dependability, 3.2_

---

## MMC(S)

> Multimedia Conferencing (System)

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-1:1998 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference model: Overview_

---

## MMI

> man-machine interface (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## MO

> member of

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 29119-13:2022, Software and systems engineering Software testing Part 13: Using the ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119 series in the testing of biometric systems, 3.2_

---

## mobile device

> portable computing device, typically having a wireless internet connection and a display screen with touch, pen, or keyboard input, and possibly auditory input and output features (ISO/IEC/IEEE 26513:2017 Systems and software engineering--Requirements for testers and reviewers of information for users, 3.26)

---

## mobility schema

> specification putting constraints on the mobility of an object

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-3:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Architecture, 16.4.1.1_

---

## mock object

> temporary dummy objects created to aid testing until the real objects become available

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## mock-up

> throw-away product (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765i:2020)

---

## mode

> definition of the expected behavior of the system, its actors, or its components in situations foreseen at design time
