_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24641:2023. Systems and Software engineering--Methods and tools for model-based systems and software engineering, 3.1.14_

---

## model

> representation of a real-world process, device, or concept

_Source: IEEE 7014-2024, IEEE Standard for Ethical Considerations in Emulated Empathy in Autonomous and Intelligent Systems, 3.1_

---

## model baseline

> immutable set of model configuration items with their associated versions and variants

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24641:2023. Systems and Software engineering--Methods and tools for model-based systems and software engineering, 3.1.17_

---

## model configuration item (MCI)

> logical part of the model that is maintained in a controlled fashion, having a trackable revision history

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24641:2023. Systems and Software engineering--Methods and tools for model- based systems and software engineering, 3.1.18_

---

## model element

> instance of a meta-model element (ISO/IEC 19506:2012 Information technology -- Object Management Group Architecture-Driven Modernization (ADM) -- Knowledge Discovery Meta-Model (KDM), 4)

---

## model element library

> set or catalogue of non-modifiable model elements usable within any project, packaged in a single artifact

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24641:2023. Systems and Software engineering--Methods and tools for model-based systems and software engineering, 3.1.20_

---

## model glossary

> collection of the names and definitions of all defined concepts that appear within the views of a model

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.116_

---

## model kind

> category of model distinguished by its key characteristics and modelling conventions

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 42010:2022, Software, systems and enterprise Architecture description, 3.15_

---

## model layers

> different layers of definition (or abstraction) used in defining the CDIF family of standards (ISO/IEC 15474-1:2002 Information technology -- CDIF framework -- Part 1: Overview, 4.2)

---

## model name abbreviation

> unique short form of a model name that is used to construct diagram references

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765m:2024, 2.1.76_

---

## model note

> textual or graphical component of a diagram that records a fact not otherwise depicted by a diagram's boxes and arrows

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765m:2024, 2.1.77_

---

## model note number

> integer number, placed inside a small square, that unambiguously identifies a model note in a specific diagram

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765m:2024, 2.1.78_

---

## model pattern

> general, reusable model or model part that can be used as a solution to a commonly occurring problem within a given context in system or software design

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24641:2023. Systems and Software engineering--Methods and tools for model-based systems and software engineering, 3.1.21_

---

## model repository

> means to store different models at different levels of abstraction and to facilitate understanding and cooperation between stakeholders and practitioners at different levels

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24641:2023. Systems and Software engineering--Methods and tools for model-based systems and software engineering, 3.1.22_

---

## model-based systems and software engineering (MBSSE)

> formalized applications of modelling to support systems and software engineering

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24641:2023. Systems and Software engineering--Methods and tools for model-based systems and software engineering, 3.1.16_

---

## modeling

> activity of representing some elements of a process, device, or concept (Software Extension to the PMBOK(R) Guide Fifth Edition)

---

## modeling tool

> tool that provides support for modeling, i.e., representing, a software product or an information system

_Source: ISO/IEC 15474-1:2002 Information technology -- CDIF framework -- Part 1: Overview, 4.2_

---

## modifiability

> ease with which a system can be changed without introducing defects

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## modifiable

> structured and has a style such that changes can be made completely, consistently, and correctly while retaining the structure (ISO/IEC/IEEE 15289:2019 Systems and software engineering--Content of life-cycle information items (documentation), 5.15)

---

## modification request (MR)

> information item that identifies and describes proposed changes(s) to a product or service (ISO/IEC/IEEE 14764:2021, Software engineering -- Software life cycle processes -- Maintenance, 3.1.8)

---

## modified condition/decision coverage testing

> structure-based test case design technique based on demonstrating that a single Boolean condition within a decision can independently affect the outcome of the decision (ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-4:2021 Software and systems engineering -- Software testing -- Part 4: Test techniques, 3.36)

---

## modified source statement

> source statement that has been changed from the original source

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## modified-off-the-shelf (MOTS)

> software product that is already developed and available, usable either as is or with modification, and provided by the supplier, acquirer, or a third party

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765l:2024_

---

## MODL

> Meta-Object Definition Language

_Source: ISO/IEC 14769:2001 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Type Repository Function, 4_

---

## modular

> composed of discrete parts (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## modular decomposition

> breaking a system into components to facilitate design and development; an element of modular programming (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## modular programming

> software development technique in which software is developed as a collection of modules (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## modularity

> capability of a product to limit changes to one component from affecting other components (ISO/IEC 25010:2023, Systems and software engineering--Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)-

---

## module

> program unit that is discrete and identifiable with respect to compiling, combining with other units, and loading

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765l:2024_

---

## module data

> data that can be accessed by any routine within the module in which it is declared but not by routines in other modules (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## MOE

> measure of effectiveness (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-8:2019, Systems and software engineering Life cycle management Part 7: Application of systems engineering on defense programs, 3.2)

---

## MOF

> meta-object facility (ISO/IEC 14769:2001 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Type Repository Function, 4) (ISO/IEC 19793:2015 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Use of UML for ODP system specifications, 4)

---

## monadic selective construct

> if-then-else construct in which processing is specified for only one outcome of the branch, the other outcome resulting in skipping this processing (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## monitor

> software tool or hardware device that operates concurrently with a system or component and supervises, records, analyzes, or verifies the operation of the system or component

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## monitor and control risks

> [Process] the process of implementing risk response plans, tracking identified risks, monitoring residual risks, identifying new risks, and evaluating risk process throughout the project (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## monitorability

> degree to which a service provides monitoring parameters and tools to monitor the performance of the service (ISO/IEC TS 25052-1:2022, Systems and software engineering--Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE): cloud services--Part 1: Quality model, 3.1.6.3)

---

## monitoring

> determining the status of a system, a process, or an activity

_Source: ISO/IEC 19770-1:2017 Information technology -- IT asset management -- Part 1: IT asset management systems--Requirements, 3.35_

---

## monitoring and controlling process group

> those processes required to track, review, and regulate the progress and performance of the project; identify any areas in which changes to the plan are required; and initiate the

---

## monitoring and controlling processes

> [Process Group] those processes required to track, review, and regulate the progress and performance of the project, identify any areas in which changes to the plan are required, and initiate the corresponding changes (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## monolithic executor

> executor consisting of a single artifact

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765l:2024_

---

## Monte Carlo simulation

> method of identifying the potential impacts of risk and uncertainty using multiple iterations of a computer model to develop a probability distribution of a range of outcomes that could result from a decision or course of action (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## mood chart

> visualization chart for tracking moods or reactions to identify areas for improvement (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## MOP

> measure of performance

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018 Systems and software engineering-Life cycle processes- Requirements engineering, 4.2_

---

## MOPS

> million operations per second

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765c:2014_

---

## MOS

> measure of suitability

_Source: IEEE 982:2024, Standard for Measures of the Software Aspects of Dependability, 3.2_

---

## MOTS

> modified-off-the-shelf

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765l:2024_

---

## MOU

> memorandum of understanding

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 41062:2024, Software engineering Life cycle processes Software acquisition, 3.2_

---

## move

> to read data from a source, altering the contents of the source location, and to write the same data elsewhere in a physical form that can differ from that of the source (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary)

---

## MP3

> MPEG Audio Layer 3

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 26531:2023 Systems and software engineering -- Content management for product lifecycle, user and service management information for users, 3.2_

---

## MP4

> MPED-V AVC (Advanced Video Coding)

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 26531:2023 Systems and software engineering -- Content management for product lifecycle, user and service management information for users, 3.2_

---

## MPa

> measure IT project activity

_Source: ISO/IEC 29155-2:2013 Systems and software engineering--Information technology project performance benchmarking framework--Part 2: Requirements for benchmarking, 4_

---

## MPEG

> Moving Picture Experts Group

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 26531:2023 Systems and software engineering -- Content management for product lifecycle, user and service management information for users, 3.2_

---

## MPLa

> manage benchmarking program level activity

_Source: ISO/IEC 29155-2:2013 Systems and software engineering-- Information technology project performance benchmarking framework--Part 2: Requirements for benchmarking, 4_

---

## MR

> metamorphic relation

_Source: IEEE 1012-2024 IEEE Standard for System, Software, and Hardware Verification and Validation, 3.2_

---

## MRa

> maintain repository activity

_Source: ISO/IEC 29155-2:2013 Systems and software engineering--Information technology project performance benchmarking framework--Part 2: Requirements for benchmarking, 4_

---

## MRP

> machine-readable passport (ISO/IEC TR 29119-13:2022, Software and systems engineering Software testing

---

## MSP

> managed service provider

_Source: IEEE 7005 2021, IEEE Standard for Transparent Employer Data Governance, 3.2_

---

## MSS

> management system standard

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765l:2024_

---

## MT

> machine translation

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 26511:2018 Systems and software engineering--Requirements for managers of information for users of systems, software, and services, 3.2_

---

## MTBF

> mean time between failures

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## MTBS

> mean time between support

_Source: IEEE 982:2024, Standard for Measures of the Software Aspects of Dependability, 3.2_

---

## MTBSF

> mean time between software failures

_Source: IEEE 982:2024, Standard for Measures of the Software Aspects of Dependability, 3.2_

---

## MTIR

> mean time to impairment recovery

_Source: IEEE 982:2024, Standard for Measures of the Software Aspects of Dependability, 3.2_

---

## MTP

> master test plan

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering--Vocabulary_

---

## MTR

> master test report

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering--Vocabulary_

---

## MTTD

> mean time to detection

_Source: IEEE 2675-2021, IEEE Standard for DevOps: Building Reliable and Secure Systems Including Application Build, Package, and Deployment, 3.2_

---

## MTTF

> mean time to failure

_Source: IEEE 982:2024, Standard for Measures of the Software Aspects of Dependability, 3.2_

---

## MTTR

> mean time to repair

_Source: IEEE 982:2024, Standard for Measures of the Software Aspects of Dependability, 3.2_

---

## MTX

> mean time to x

_Source: IEEE 982:2024, Standard for Measures of the Software Aspects of Dependability, 3.2_

---

## multi-attribute decision

> decision that considers more than just one criterion (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## multi-component system

> measured system consisting of more than one piece of software (ISO/IEC 20968:2002 Software engineering -- Mk II Function Point Analysis -- Counting Practices Manual, A.13)

_Source: ISO/IEC 29881:2010 Information technology--Software and systems engineering--FiSMA 1.1 functional size measurement method, A.10_

---

## multi-core

> chip with two or more microprocessor units

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765e:2015_

---

## multi-core processor

> single integrated circuit chip with more than one processing unit

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765c:2014_

---

## multi-level cache

> layered cache with progressively larger size and slower access (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765c:2014)

---

## multi-modal biometric system

> biometric system based on multiple biometric characteristics

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 29119-13:2022, Software and systems engineering Software testing Part 13: Using the ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119 series in the testing of biometric systems, 3.1.31_

---

## multi-valued

> a mapping that is not a function (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.117)

---

## multi-valued property

> a property with a multi-valued mapping (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.118)

---

## multiaddress instruction

> computer instruction that contains more than one address field (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## multidimensional construct

> construct that consists of a number of unidimensional constructs

_Source: ISO/IEC 33003:2015 Information technology--Process assessment--Requirements for process measurement frameworks, 3.11_

---

## multiple inclusive selective construct

> special instance of the case construct in which two or more different values of the control expression result in the same processing

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## multiple inheritance

> ability of a subclass to inherit responsibilities from more than one superclass

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.119_

---

## multiple instance approach

> case where each method of delivery of the same functionality is counted separately (ISO/IEC/IEEE 32430:2025, Software engineering Software non-functional size measurement, 3.1.23)

---

## multiple readers and writers

> algorithm that lets multiple readers access a shared data repository concurrently; however, writers have mutually exclusive access to update the repository

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## multiple-criteria decision making (MCDM)

> making preference decisions (e.g., evaluation, prioritization, and selection) of available alternatives characterized by multiple criteria (ISO/IEC 33003:2015 Information technology-- Process assessment--Requirements for process measurement frameworks, 3.9)

---

## multiple-hit decision table

> decision table where at least one set of conditions will be satisfied by more than one rule

_Source: ISO 5806:1984 Information processing -- Specification of single-hit decision tables, 3.3_

---

## multiplex receptacle

> specialization of a receptacle that allows multiple simultaneous connections

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765l:2024_

---

## multiplicity

> natural number (i.e., non-negative integer) which describes the number of repetitions of an item in a multiset

_Source: ISO/IEC 15909-1:2019 Systems and software engineering--High-level Petri nets--Part 1: Concepts, definitions and graphical notation, 3.18_

---

## multipoint estimating

> method used to estimate cost or duration by applying an average or weighted average of optimistic, pessimistic, and most likely estimates when there is uncertainty with the individual activity estimates (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## multiprocessing

> mode of operation in which two or more processes are executed concurrently by separate processing units that have access (usually) to a common main storage (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software

---

## multiprogramming

> mode of operation in which two or more computer programs are executed in an interleaved manner by a single processing unit (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## multiset

> collection of items where repetition of items is allowed (ISO/IEC 15909-1:2019 Systems and software engineering--High-level Petri nets--Part 1: Concepts, definitions and graphical notation, 3.17)

---

## multiset cardinality

> sum of the multiplicities of each of the members of the multiset (ISO/IEC 15909-1:2019 Systems and software engineering--High-level Petri nets--Part 1: Concepts, definitions and graphical notation, 3.19)

---

## multitasking

> mode of operation in which two or more tasks are executed in an interleaved manner (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## mutable class

> class for which the set of instances is not fixed; its instances come and go over time (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.120)

---

## mutation testing

> testing methodology in which two or more program mutations are executed using the same test cases to evaluate the ability of the test cases to detect differences in the mutations

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## mutual exclusion

> giving access to shared data only to one task at a time (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## mutually exclusive

> alternatives from which at most one is selected (ISO/IEC 26580:2021, Software and systems engineering Methods and tools for the feature-based approach to software and systems product line engineering, 3.9)

---

## mutually exclusive clustering

> task structuring criterion in which a group of objects are combined into one task because only one object can be executed at any one time

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## mutually inclusive

> alternatives from which zero or more are selected (ISO/IEC 26580:2021, Software and systems engineering Methods and tools for the feature-based approach to software and systems product line engineering, 3.10)

---

## MVP

> minimum viable product

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 7052:2023, Software engineering--Controlling frequently occurring risks during development and maintenance of custom software, 3.29_

---

## N 2 diagram

> system engineering or software engineering tool for tabulating, defining, analyzing, and describing functional interfaces and interactions among system components (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765e:2015)

---

## n-address instruction

> computer instruction that contains n address fields, where n is any non-negative integer (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## N-ary relationship

> relationship with arity (degree) n &gt; 2 (ISO/IEC 15474-1:2002 Information technology --

---

## n-level address

> indirect address that specifies the first of a chain of n storage locations, the first n-1 of which contains the address of the next location in the chain and the last of which contains the desired operand (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## n-plus-one address instruction

> computer instruction that contains n+1 address fields, the last containing the address of the instruction to be executed next (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## N/A

> not applicable

_Source: IEEE 1012-2024 IEEE Standard for System, Software, and Hardware Verification and Validation, 3.2_

---

## N/R

> not required

_Source: IEEE 1012-2024 IEEE Standard for System, Software, and Hardware Verification and Validation, 3.2_

---

## N2 diagram

> graphical representation used to define the internal operational relationships or external interfaces of the system of interest (INCOSE Systems Engineering Handbook, 5th ed.)

---

## name

> word or phrase that designates some model construct

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.121_

---

## name resolution

> process by which, given an initial name and an initial naming context, an association between a name and the entity designated by the initial name can be found

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-2:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Foundations, 12.8_

---

## name space

> set of terms usable as names

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-2:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Foundations, 12.3_

---

## named constant

> identifier that refers to a numeric or string value that does not change during program execution

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## named constraint

> constraint that is specific to a particular model, rather than being inherent in some modeling construct (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.122)

---

## naming action

> action that associates a term from a name space with a given entity

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-2:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Foundations, 12.5_

---

## naming context

> relation between a set of names and a set of entities

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-2:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Foundations, 12.4_

---

## naming domain

> subset of a naming context such that all naming actions are performed by the controlling object of the domain (the name authority object)

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-2:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing - - Reference Model: Foundations, 12.6_

---

## naming graph

> directed graph where each vertex denotes a naming context, and where each edge denotes an association between a name appearing in the source naming context and the target naming context

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746- 2:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Foundations, 12.7_

---

## nano code

> collection of nanoinstructions

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary_

---

## nanoinstruction

> in a two-level implementation of microprogramming, an instruction that specifies one or more of the basic operations needed to carry out a microinstruction

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## nanostore

> in a two-level implementation of microprogramming, a secondary control store in which nanoinstructions reside

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## narrow artificial intelligence

> artificial intelligence focused on a single well-defined task to address a specific problem (ISO/IEC TR 29119-11:2020, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 11: Guidelines on the testing of AI-based systems, 3.1.47)

---

## natural language

> language whose rules are based on usage rather than being pre-established prior to the language's use

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## natural unit

> application-specific alternative to absolute or relative time to quantify the usage frequency of a software-based product

_Source: IEEE 982:2024, Standard for Measures of the Software Aspects of Dependability, 3.1_

---

## navigation

> process of accessing on-screen information by moving between different locations in a website or electronic document

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 23026:2023 Systems and software engineering--Engineering and management of websites for systems, software, and services information, 3.1.17_

---

## navigational aids

> features of software that help the user to navigate around a computer application

_Source: ISO/IEC 20968:2002 Software engineering -- Mk II Function Point Analysis -- Counting Practices Manual, 10_

---

## NDA

> non-disclosure agreement

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 41062:2024, Software engineering Life cycle processes Software acquisition, 3.2_

---

## NDI

> non-developmental item

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-6:2023 Systems and software engineering Life cycle management Part 6: System and software integration, 3.2_

---

## need statement

> result of a formal transformation of one or more life cycle concepts into an agreed-to expectation for an entity to perform some function or possess some quality

_Source: INCOSE Systems Engineering Handbook, 5th ed._

---

## negotiation

> resolution of disputes through consultations between involved parties

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765h:2019_

---

## NEQ

> not equal

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 29119-13:2022, Software and systems engineering Software testing Part 13: Using the ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119 series in the testing of biometric systems, 3.2_

---

## nest

> to incorporate a computer program construct into another construct of the same kind

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## nesting

> embedding one construct inside another (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-

---

## network

> arrangement of nodes and interconnecting branches

_Source: ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary_

---

## network chart

> directed graph used for describing and scheduling events, activities, and their relationships in project control

_Source: ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary_

---

## network open end

> schedule activity without any predecessor activities or successor activities, causing a break in a schedule network path (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## network path

> sequence of activities connected by logical relationships in a project schedule network diagram (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## network planning

> technique that uses network charts for planning, scheduling. and controlling a project

_Source: ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary_

---

## networking

> developing relationships with persons who can assist in the achievement of objectives and responsibilities

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## neural network

> network of primitive processing elements connected by weighted links with adjustable weights, in which each element produces a value by applying a nonlinear function to its input values, and transmits it to other elements or presents it as an output value (ISO/IEC TR 29119-11:2020, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 11: Guidelines on the testing of AI-based systems, 3.1.48)

---

## neuron coverage

> proportion of activated neurons divided by the total number of neurons in the neural network (normally expressed as a percentage) for a set of tests (ISO/IEC TR 29119-11:2020, Software and systems engineering-- Software testing--Part 11: Guidelines on the testing of AI-based systems, 3.1.49)

---

## new source statements

> sum of the added and modified source statements

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## NFR

> non-functional requirement

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 32430:2025, Software engineering Software non-functional size measurement, 3.1.25_

---

## NFSM

> non-functional size measurement

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 32430:2025, Software engineering Software non- functional size measurement, 3.2_

---

## no-op

> no-operation

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## no-operation

> computer operation whose execution has no effect except to advance the instruction counter to the next instruction (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## node

> vertex of a net graph, i.e. either a place or a transition

_Source: ISO/IEC 15909-1:2019 Systems and software engineering--High-level Petri nets--Part 1: Concepts, definitions and graphical notation, 3.20_

---

## node letter

> letter that is the first character of a node number

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765m:2024, 2.1.82_

---

## node number

> expression that unambiguously identifies a function's position in a model hierarchy (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765m:2024, 2.1.83)

---

## nomenclature standard

> standard that describes the characteristics of a system or set of names, or designations, or symbols

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## nominal scale

> scale in which the measurement values are categorical (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## non-compensatory model

> multiple-criteria decision-making model that does not allow criteria to compensate for each other in proportion to their weights (ISO/IEC 33003:2015 Information technology--Process assessment-- Requirements for process measurement frameworks, 3.12)

---

## non-deliverable item

> hardware or software product that is not required to be delivered under the contract, but may be employed in the development of a product (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765g:2018)

---

## non-deterministic system

> system which, given a particular set of inputs and starting state, will not always produce the same set of outputs and final state (ISO/IEC TR 29119-11:2020, Software and systems engineering-- Software testing--Part 11: Guidelines on the testing of AI-based systems, 3.1.50)

---

## non-functional requirement (NFR)

> requirement for a software-intensive system or for a software product, including how it should be developed and maintained, and how it should perform in operation, except any functional user requirement for the software

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 32430:2025, Software engineering Software non-functional size measurement, 3.1.25_

---

## non-functional requirements for software

> portion of the non-functional requirements (NFR) that are realized by the software (ISO/IEC/IEEE 32430:2025, Software engineering Software non-functional size measurement, 3.1.26)

---

## non-functional size

> size of the software derived by quantifying the non-functional requirements (NFR), defined by a set of rules (ISO/IEC/IEEE 32430:2025, Software engineering Software non-functional size measurement, 3.1.24)

---

## non-primary entity

> a data entity-type arrived at by Third Normal Form analysis which is not one of the main entity-types for which the application in question has been built (ISO/IEC 20968:2002 Software engineering -- Mk II Function Point Analysis -- Counting Practices Manual, 10)

---

## non-repudiation

> capability of a product to prove that actions or events have taken place, so that the events or

---

## non-terminal symbol

> part of the hierarchical definition of a syntax that is further decomposed in the hierarchy

_Source: ISO/IEC 15474-1:2002 Information technology -- CDIF framework -- Part 1: Overview, 4.2_

---

## non-time-critical computationally intensive task

> low-priority compute-bound task that consumes spare CPU cycles

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## non-volatile memory

> unit that stores data whether power is on or off

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765c:2014_

---

## noncompensatory decision technique

> a multi-attribute decision technique that weighs all attributes equally, without allowing lower performance in one attribute to be traded off against better performance in another attribute (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## nonconformity

> non-fulfillment of a requirement (ISO/IEC 19770-1:2017 Information technology -- IT asset management -- Part 1: IT asset management systems--Requirements, 3.36)

---

## nondelivered source statement

> source statement that is developed in support of the final product, but not delivered to the customer (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## nondestructive read

> read operation that does not erase the data in the accessed location (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## nondeveloped source statement

> existing source statement that is reused or deleted (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## nondevelopmental

> developed prior to its current use in an acquisition or development process (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## nondimensional scaling

> decision technique in which attribute values are converted into a common scale where they can be added together to make a composite score for each alternative (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## nonfunctional requirement

> software requirement that describes not what the software will do but how the software will do it

_Source: IEEE 7000:2021, IEEE Standard Model Process for Addressing Ethical Concerns during System Design, 3.1_

---

## nonidentifying relationship

> a specific (not many-to-many) relationship in which some or all of the attributes contained in the primary key of the parent entity do not participate in the primary key of the child entity (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.123)

---

## nonintrinsic relationship

> relationship that is partial, is multi-valued, or changeable. (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.125)

---

## nonkey attribute

> attribute that is not the primary or a part of a composite primary key of an entity (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.125)

---

## nonprocedural language

> language in which the user states what is to be achieved without having to state specific instructions that the computer executes in a given sequence (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## nonprocedural programming language

> computer programming language used to express the parameters of a problem rather than the steps in a solution (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-- Vocabulary)

---

## nonprogrammable terminal

> user terminal that has no independent data processing capability (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025)

---

## nontechnical requirement

> requirement affecting product and service acquisition or development that is not a property of the product or service

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## NOR

> notice of revision

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## norm

> something that is usual, typical, or standard

_Source: IEEE 7005 2021, IEEE Standard for Transparent Employer Data Governance, 3.1_

---

## normalization

> process by which a data structure can be transformed by a database designer into a set of relations that have no repeating groups

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765a:2011_

---

## not printable

> not a <GeneralPrintableChar>, ", #, ], <EscapeCharacter> or <WhiteSpace>

_Source: ISO/IEC 15475- 3:2002 Information technology -- CDIF transfer format -- Part 3: Encoding ENCODING.1, 7.2.11_

---

## notation

> means of concrete representation for a particular type of a model, expressed as a grammar and suitable glyphs for its terminal symbols

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-2:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Foundations, 7.5_

---

## notation standard

> standard that describes the characteristics of formal interfaces within a profession

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## note

> helpful hint or other information that assists the user by emphasizing or supplementing important points of the main text

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765g:2018_

---

## notebook computer

> battery-powered portable computer small and light enough to be operated anywhere (ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary)

---

## notice of revision (NOR)

> form used in configuration management to propose revisions to a drawing or list, and, after approval, to notify users that the drawing or list has been, or will be, revised accordingly (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## NRR

> normal run response

_Source: IEEE 982:2024, Standard for Measures of the Software Aspects of Dependability, 3.2_

---

## NRT

> normal run termination

_Source: IEEE 982:2024, Standard for Measures of the Software Aspects of Dependability, 3.2_

---

## NSBGM

> non-state-based grievance mechanism

_Source: IEEE 7005 2021, IEEE Standard for Transparent Employer Data Governance, 3.2_

---

## NSR

> normal support response

_Source: IEEE 982:2024, Standard for Measures of the Software Aspects of Dependability, 3.2_

---

## NTF

> non-terminal failure

_Source: IEEE 982:2024, Standard for Measures of the Software Aspects of Dependability, 3.2_

---

## nth of a kind

> re-manufacturing or re-installation of a previously verified and validated hardware or software design (IEEE 1012-2024 IEEE Standard for System, Software, and Hardware Verification and Validation, 3.1)

---

## nucleus

> engineering object which coordinates processing, storage and communications functions for use by other engineering objects within the node to which it belongs (ISO/IEC 10746-3:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Architecture, 8.1.6)

---

## number of data element types

> sum of all data element types (DETs) that are part of the input/output/query of the elementary process(EP), plus the data elements which are read or maintained internally to the boundary (ISO/IEC/IEEE 32430:2025, Software engineering Software non-functional size measurement, 3.1.27)

---

## number of data element types (DETs)

> sum of all data element types (DETs) which are part of the input/output/query of the elementary process(EP), plus the data elements which are read or updated internally to the boundary. (IEEE 2430-2019 Trial-Use Standard for Software Non-Functional Sizing Measurements, 3.1)

---

## numeric

> pertaining to data that consists of numerals as well as functional units that use the data

_Source: ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary_

---

## NVR

> network video recorder

_Source: IEEE 1012-2024 IEEE Standard for System, Software, and Hardware Verification and Validation, 3.2_

---

## O&S

> operations and support (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-8:2019, Systems and software engineering Life cycle management Part 7: Application of systems engineering on defense programs, 3.2)

---

## OASIS

> Organization for the Advancement of Structured Information Standards

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 26511:2018 Systems and software engineering--Requirements for managers of information for users of systems, software, and services, 3.2_

---

## OAUTH

> open authentication

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 23026:2023 Systems and software engineering--Engineering and management of websites for systems, software, and services information, 3.2_

---

## object

> encapsulation of data and services that manipulate that data

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765l:2024_

---

## object code

> computer instructions and data definitions in a form output by an assembler or compiler (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## object identifier

> some concrete representation for the identity of an object (instance) (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.128)

---

## object implementation

> definition that provides the information needed to create an object and to allow the object to participate in providing an appropriate set of services

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765l:2024_

---

## Object Management Group (OMG)

> international standards organization that owns and maintains CORBA(R) and UML(R) standards

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## object module

> computer program or subprogram that is the output of an assembler or compiler (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## object of interest (-type)

> anything that is identified from the point of view of the functional user requirements about which the software is required to process or store data (ISO/IEC 19761:2011 Software engineering -- COSMIC: a functional size measurement method, 2.19)

---

## object program

> computer program that is the output of an assembler or compiler (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## object reference

> value that unambiguously stores a reference for accessing an object

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765l:2024_

---

## object set

> subset of instantiations from the set of all possible instantiations of all object types within an object type set (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765m:2024, 2.1.86)

---

## object type set

> named set of one or more object types (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765m:2024, 2.1.88)

---

## object-oriented design

> software development technique in which a system or component is expressed in terms of objects and connections between those objects (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary)

---

## object-oriented language

> programming language that allows the user to express a program in terms of objects and messages between those objects

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## objective

> something toward which work is to be directed, a strategic position to be attained, a purpose to be achieved, a result to be obtained, a product to be produced, or a service to be performed (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## objective evidence

> data supporting the existence or verity of something (ISO/IEC 33001:2015 Information technology--Process assessment--Concepts and terminology, 3.2.13)

---

## objective function

> formula that relates a decision variable to either the cost or the revenue of an alternative (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## obligation

> prescription that a particular behavior is required

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-2:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Foundations, 11.2.4_

---

## OBS

> organizational breakdown structure (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## observation

> instance of applying a measurement procedure to produce a value for a base measure

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 15939:2017 Systems and software engineering--Measurement process, 3.28_

---

## observation period

> time interval, where the measurement procedure is observed for collecting (logging) measurement results for rating or validation, consisting of the rating interval and the supplementary run

_Source: ISO/IEC 14756:1999 Information technology -- Measurement and rating of performance of computer-based software systems, 4.11_

---

## occupational title standard

> standard that describes the characteristics of the general areas of work or profession

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## OCD

> operational concept document (IEEE 1012-2024 IEEE Standard for System, Software, and Hardware Verification and Validation, 3.2)

---

## OCL

> Object Constraint Language (ISO/IEC 14769:2001 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Type Repository Function, 4)

_Source: ISO/IEC 19793:2015 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Use of UML for ODP system specifications, 4_

---

## octet

> byte that consists of eight bits (ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary)

---

## ODP

> Open Distributed Processing

## ODP function

> function required to support Open Distributed Processing (ISO/IEC 10746-3:2009 Information

---

## ODP IDL

> Open Distributed Processing Interface Definition Language

> standard that complies with the ODP Reference Model, directly or indirectly

## ODP system

> system which conforms to the requirements of ODP standards

> Open Distributed Processing: Reference Model (ISO/IEC 14769:2001 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Type Repository Function, 4)

## OF

> operational failure

## off-the-shelf

> product or system already developed and available (ISO/IEC/IEEE 41062:2024, Software engineering Life cycle processes Software acquisition, 3.1.12)

---

## office automation (OA)

> integration of office activities by means of an information processing system (ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary)

---

## offline

> pertaining to a device or process that is not under the direct control of the central processing unit of a computer

---

## offset

> difference between the loaded origin and the assembled origin of a computer program

---

> object identifier

> on-the-job-training

> operations level agreement

> operational level grievance mechanism

---

> Object Management Group (ISO/IEC 10746-1:1998 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference model: Overview)

## OMT

> Object Modeling Technique (ISO/IEC 10746-1:1998 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing --

---

## on-chip oscillator

> electronic circuit on a microcomputer that produces a periodic electronic signal, often used for a device clock

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765d:2015_

---

## on-demand scheduling

> scheduling approach in which work is pulled from a backlog according to the perceived value to customers and is assigned as resources become available (Software Extension to the PMBOK(R) Guide Fifth Edition)

---

## on-screen documentation

> information that is intended to be read on the screen by the user while using the software (ISO/IEC/IEEE 26512:2018 Systems and software engineering--Requirements for acquirers and suppliers of information for users, 3.15)

---

## one-address instruction

> computer instruction that contains one address field (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## one-ahead addressing

> method of implied addressing in which the operands for a computer instruction are understood to be in the storage locations following the locations of the operands used for the last instruction executed (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## one-plus-one address instruction

> computer instruction that contains two address fields, the second containing the address of the instruction to be executed next (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## one-time programming (OTP)

> method of recording data in ROM which can only be written once

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765c:2014_

---

## one-to-many relationship

> relationship between two state classes in which each instance of one class, referred to as the child class, is specifically constrained to relate to no more than one instance of a second class, referred to as the parent class

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.131_

---

## online

> pertaining to a system or mode of operation in which input data enter the computer directly from the point of origin or output data are transmitted directly to the point where they are used

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## online documentation

> information accessed by the user through the use of software (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765a:2011)

---

## online help

> information about the software that is intended to be read on the screen by the user while using the software (ISO/IEC/IEEE 26513:2017 Systems and software engineering--Requirements for testers and reviewers of information for users, 3.28)

---

## onscreen information for users

> information for users that is intended to be read on the screen by the user while using the software (ISO/IEC/IEEE 26514:2022, Systems and software engineering -- Design and development of information for users, 3.1.36)

---

## ontology

> logical structure of the terms used to describe a domain of knowledge, including both the definitions of the applicable terms and their relationships

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24641:2023. Systems and Software engineering--Methods and tools for model-based systems and software engineering, 3.1.23_

---

## OOD

> object-oriented design

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## OOSEM

> object-oriented systems engineering method

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24641:2023. Systems and Software engineering--Methods and tools for model-based systems and software engineering, 3.2_

---

## OPA

> organizational process asset (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## open distributed processing

> distributed processing designed to conform to ODP standards (ISO/IEC 10746- 2:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Foundations, 3.2.4)

---

## open subroutine

> subroutine that is copied into a computer program at each place that it is called (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## operability

> capability of a product to have functions and attributes that make it easy to operate and control (ISO/IEC 25010:2023, Systems and software engineering--Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)--Product quality model, 3.4.3)

---

## operable

> state of being able to perform the intended function

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## operand

> variable, constant, or function upon which an operation is to be performed

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## operating environment (software)

> set of software operating concurrently on a specified computer system (ISO/IEC 19761:2011 Software engineering -- COSMIC: a functional size measurement method, 2.20)

---

## operating mode

> type of operation for a system

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765d:2015_

---

## operating system

> collection of software, firmware, and hardware elements that controls the execution of computer programs and provides such services as computer resource allocation, job control, input/output control, and file management in a computer system (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## operation

> in computer mathematics, the action specified by an operator on one or more operands

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## operation and maintenance costs

> costs associated with using an asset as well as costs of keeping it in a usable condition

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## operation and maintenance phase

> period of time in the software life cycle during which a software product is employed in its operational environment, monitored for satisfactory performance, and modified as necessary to correct problems or to respond to changing requirements

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary_

---

## operation code

> character or set of characters that specifies a computer operation (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## operation exception

> exception that occurs when a program encounters an invalid operation code (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## operation field

> field of a computer instruction that specifies the operation to be performed (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## operation interface

> interface in which all the interactions are operations

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-3:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Architecture, 7.1.7_

---

## operation interface signature

> interface signature for an operation interface (ISO/IEC 10746-3:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Architecture, 7.1.12)

---

## operational

> pertaining to a system or component that is ready for use in its intended environment

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering--Vocabulary_

---

## operational concept (OpsCon)

> verbal and graphic statement of an organizations assumptions or intent in regard to an operation or series of operations of a specific system or a related set of specific new, existing or modified systems (ISO/IEC/IEEE 12207:2026 Systems and software engineering--Software life cycle processes, 3.1.39) (ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288:2023 Systems and software engineering--System life cycle processes, 3.23)

---

## operational constraint

> capability of a product to constrain its operation to within safe parameters or states when encountering an operational hazard (ISO/IEC 25010:2023, Systems and software engineering--Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)--Product quality model, 3.9.1)

---

## operational environment

> physical context, setting, and circumstances used to support the in-service operation of a system (IEEE 982:2024, Standard for Measures of the Software Aspects of Dependability, 3.1)

---

## operational scenario

> description of an imagined sequence of events that includes the interaction of the product or service with its environment and users, as well as interaction among its product or service components (ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018 Systems and software engineering-Life cycle processes-Requirements engineering, 4.1.15)

---

## operational testing

> testing conducted to evaluate a system or component in its operational environment (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering--Vocabulary)

---

## operations

> ongoing execution of activities that produce the same product or provide a repetitive service

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## operator

> entity that performs the operation of a system

---

## operator manual

> document that provides the information necessary to initiate and operate a system or component (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## OPM

> object-process methodology (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24641:2023. Systems and Software engineering--Methods and tools for model-based systems and software engineering, 3.2)

---

## opportunity

> risk that would have a positive effect on one or more project objectives (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## opportunity cost

> implicit cost associated with investing money in a certain activity, so that it is no longer available for investing elsewhere (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## opportunity study

> study to examine a problem and determine whether or not it requires being solved during the time period under consideration

_Source: ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary_

---

## OpsCon

> operational concept (ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018 Systems and software engineering-Life cycle processes- Requirements engineering, 4.2)

---

## optical disc (OD)

> disk which stores binary data in the form of pits which interrupt the reflection of light from a laser

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765c:2014_

---

## optimistic duration

> estimate of the shortest activity duration that takes into account the known variables that can affect performance

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765h:2019_

---

## optimization analysis

> balance of competing components to achieve the best performance under the situation (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## optimizing process

> quantitatively managed process that is improved based on an understanding of the common causes of variation inherent in the process. (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary)

---

## optional

> syntax keyword used to specify a partial mapping (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.133)

---

## optional attribute

> attribute that can have no value for an instance

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.134_

---

## optional nonidentifying relationship

> nonidentifying relationship in which an instance of the child entity can exist without being related to an instance of the parent entity (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.135)

---

## optional requirement

> requirement of a normative document that is fulfilled in order to comply with a particular

---

## optional task

> verification and validation (V&V) task that can be added to the minimum V&V tasks to address specific application requirements

_Source: IEEE 1012-2024 IEEE Standard for System, Software, and Hardware Verification and Validation, 3.1_

---

## optional tasks

> verification and validation tasks that can be added to the minimum required verification and validation tasks to address specific application requirements

_Source: IEEE 1012-2024 IEEE Standard for System, Software, and Hardware Verification and Validation, 3.1_

---

## order clash

> in software design, a type of structure clash in which a program deal with two or more data sets that have been sorted in different orders (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## ordinal scale

> scale in which the measurement values are rankings (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## organization

> person or group of people that has its own functions with responsibilities, authorities, and relationships to achieve its objectives (ISO/IEC/IEEE 12207:2026 Systems and software engineering--Software life cycle processes, 3.1.41)

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288:2023 Systems and software engineering--System life cycle processes, 3.25_

---

## organization chain

> constellation of organizations that have business relationships with one another (ISO/IEC 16350-2015 Information technology--Systems and software engineering--Application management, 4.25)

---

## organization chart

> graphical depiction of hierarchies and interrelationships among persons working together

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## organization level

> management level or levels responsible for managing one or more data processing or information systems organizations

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765a:2011_

---

## organizational breakdown structure (OBS)

> hierarchical representation of the project organization, which illustrates the relationship between project activities and the organizational units that will perform those activities (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## organizational management profile

> profile targeted at very small entities (VSE) to provide additional organizational management guidance (ISO/IEC 29110-1-2:2024, Systems and software engineering Lifecycle profiles for Very Small Entities (VSEs) Part 1-2: Vocabulary, 3.55)

---

## organizational maturity

> extent to which an organization has explicitly and consistently deployed processes that are documented, managed, measured, controlled, and continually improved. (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## organizational objective

> overarching objective that sets the context and direction for an organization's activities (ISO/IEC 19770-1:2017 Information technology -- IT asset management -- Part 1: IT asset management systems--Requirements, 3.39)

---

## organizational plan

> documented information that specifies the programs to achieve the organizational objectives

_Source: ISO/IEC 19770-1:2017 Information technology -- IT asset management -- Part 1: IT asset management systems--Requirements, 3.40_

---

## organizational policy

> guiding principle typically established by senior management that is adopted by an organization to influence and determine decisions

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary_

---

## organizational privacy framework

> set of principles, policies, and processes that describe what an organization may or may not do with personal data

_Source: IEEE 7002:2022, IEEE Standard for Data Privacy Process, 3.1_

---

## organizational privacy requirements

> statements that reference key privacy objectives and specify capabilities and functions that a system performs

_Source: IEEE 7002:2022, IEEE Standard for Data Privacy Process, 3.1_

---

## organizational process assets

> plans, processes, policies, procedures and knowledge bases, specific to and used by the performing organization (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## organizational process maturity

> extent to which an organizational unit consistently implements processes within a defined scope that contributes to the achievement of its business needs (current or projected) (ISO/IEC 33001:2015 Information technology--Process assessment--Concepts and terminology, 3.4.2)

---

## organizational risk

> risks that inhibit the achievement of business values and product line objectives (ISO/IEC 26556:2018 Information technology--Software and systems engineering--Tools and methods for product line

---

## organizational test practice

> documentation that expresses the recommended approaches or methods for the testing to be performed within an organization, providing detail on how the testing is to be performed (ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-2:2021, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 2: Test processes, 3.8)

---

## organizational test process

> test process for developing and managing organizational test specifications

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-2:2021, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 2: Test processes, 3.9_

---

## organizational test specification

> document that provides information about testing for an organization, i.e. information that is not project-specific

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-2:2021, Software and systems engineering--Software testing- -Part 2: Test processes, 3.10_

---

## organizational unit

> part of an organization that is the subject of measurement

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 15939:2017 Systems and software engineering--Measurement process, 3.30_

---

## origin

> address of the initial storage location assigned to a computer program in main memory (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## origin attribute

> classification of software as either developed or nondeveloped

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## original equipment manufacturer license

> license for products or components that are created or manufactured by one company and licensed by another company (ISO/IEC 19770-3:2016 Information technology--IT asset management--Part 3: Entitlement schema, 3.1.17)

---

## original source statement

> source statement that is obtained from an external product

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## orphan page

> page on a website with no link from any other page on the website

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 23026:2023 Systems and software engineering--Engineering and management of websites for systems, software, and services information, 3.1.18_

---

## OS

> operating system

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 29119-13:2022, Software and systems engineering Software testing Part 13: Using the ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119 series in the testing of biometric systems, 3.2_

---

## OSE

> Open System Environment

## OSF

> Open Software Foundation

---

## OSI

> Open Systems Interconnection

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-1:1998 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing - - Reference model: Overview_

## osmotic communication

> means of receiving information without direct communication by overhearing and through nonverbal cues (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## OSS

> open source software (IEEE 2675-2021, IEEE Standard for DevOps: Building Reliable and Secure Systems Including Application Build, Package, and Deployment, 3.2)

---

## OT

> operational test

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-8:2019, Systems and software engineering Life cycle management Part 7: Application of systems engineering on defense programs, 3.2_

---

## OTE

> operational test and evaluation (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-8:2019, Systems and software engineering Life cycle management Part 7: Application of systems engineering on defense programs, 3.2)

---

## OTP

> one-time password

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 23026:2023 Systems and software engineering--Engineering and management of websites for systems, software, and services information, 3.2_

## OTRR

> operational test readiness review

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-8:2019, Systems and software engineering Life cycle management Part 7: Application of systems engineering on defense programs, 3.2_

---

## OTS

> off the shelf

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 41062:2024, Software engineering Life cycle processes Software acquisition, 3.1.12_

---

## outcome

> significant change of state or the meeting of specified constraints. e.g., requirements or goals.

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765l:2024_

---

## outer cardinality

> number of allowed instances of a participating data object from the viewpoint of the other participants in the relationship (ISO/IEC 15476-4:2005 Information technology--CDIF semantic metamodel--Part 4: Data models, 6.6.1)

---

## output

> product, result, or service generated by a process

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24774:2021, Systems and software engineering--Life cycle management--Specification for process description, 3.6_

---

## output arc (of a transition)

> arc directed from the transition to a place

_Source: ISO/IEC 15909-1:2019 Systems and software engineering--High-level Petri nets--Part 1: Concepts, definitions and graphical notation, 3.22_

---

## output argument

> an argument that has not been specified as an input argument (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.136)

---

## output assertion

> logical expression specifying one or more conditions that program outputs satisfy in order for the program to be correct (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## output place (of a transition)

> place connected to the transition by an output arc

_Source: ISO/IEC 15909-1:2019 Systems and software engineering--High-level Petri nets--Part 1: Concepts, definitions and graphical notation, 3.23_

---

## output primitive

> primitive that includes source statements, function points, and documents (ISO/IEC/IEEE

---

## outsider's viewpoint

> perspective of a potential acquirer who does not own either an existing system nor its proposed replacement, and who evaluates the alternatives of buying the existing system at its salvage value or buying a replacement candidate system (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## outsource

> make an arrangement in which an external organization performs part of an organization's function or process

_Source: ISO/IEC 19770-1:2017 Information technology -- IT asset management -- Part 1: IT asset management systems- -Requirements, 3.41_

---

## overfitting

> generation of a machine learning model that corresponds too closely to the training data, resulting in a model that finds it difficult to generalize to new data

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 29119-11:2020, Software and systems engineering-- Software testing--Part 11: Guidelines on the testing of AI-based systems, 3.1.51_

---

## overflow exception

> exception that occurs when the result of an arithmetic operation exceeds the size of the storage location designated to receive it (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## overhead time

> amount of time a computer system spends performing tasks that do not contribute directly to the progress of any user task

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## overlay

> storage allocation technique in which computer program segments are loaded from auxiliary storage to main storage when needed, overwriting other segments not currently in use

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## overlay supervisor

> routine that controls the sequencing and positioning of overlays

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## overload

> to assign an operator, identifier, or literal more than one meaning, depending upon the data types associated with it at any given time during program execution

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## override

> the ability of a property in a subclass to respecify the realization of an inherited property of the same name while retaining the same meaning

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.137_

---

## overriding property

> property in a subclass that has the same meaning and signature as a similarly named property in one of its superclasses, but has a different realization

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.138_

---

## OVM

> orthogonal variability model

_Source: ISO/IEC 26559:2017 Software and systems engineering -- Methods and tools for variability traceability in software and systems product line, 4_

---

## owned attribute

> attribute of an entity that has not migrated into the entity (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.139)

---

## owner

> person or organization that owns the copyright for the Candidate FSM method (ISO/IEC 14143-2:2011

---

## owner of the FSM method

> the person or organization that owns the intellectual property rights for the FSM method

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 14143-3:2003 Information technology -- Software measurement -- Functional size measurement -- Part 3: Verification of functional size measurement methods, 3.7_

---

## P&D

> production and deployment (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-7:2019, Systems and software engineering Life cycle management Part 7: Application of systems engineering on defense programs, 3.2)

---

## P&P

> policies and procedures (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765k:2022)

---

## P/T net

> Place/Transition net

_Source: ISO/IEC 15909-2:2011 Software and system engineering--High-level Petri nets--Part 2: Transfer format, 4.2.4_

---

## PaaS

> platform as a service (IEEE 2675-2021, IEEE Standard for DevOps: Building Reliable and Secure Systems Including Application Build, Package, and Deployment, 3.1)

---

## pack

> to store data in a compact form in a storage medium, using known characteristics of the data and medium in such a way as to permit recovery of the data (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## package

> separately compilable software component consisting of related data types, data objects, and subprograms

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## packaging

> in software development, the assignment of modules to segments to be handled as distinct physical units for execution by a computer

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## padding

> technique of filling out a fixed-length block of data with dummy characters, words, or records

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## page

> fixed-length segment of data or of a computer program treated as a unit in storage allocation

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## page breakage

> portion of main storage that is unused when the last page of data or of a computer program does not fill the entire block of storage allocated to it (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary)

---

## page frame

> block of main storage having the size of, and used to hold, a page (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017

---

## page reference

> expression that unambiguously identifies a model page (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765m:2024, 2.1.91)

---

## page swapping

> exchange of pages between main storage and auxiliary storage (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## page table

> table that identifies the location of pages in storage and gives significant attributes of those pages (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## page type letter

> uppercase letter in a page reference that denotes a specific type of model page

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765m:2024, 2.1.92_

---

## page zero

> in the paging method of storage allocation, the first page in a series of pages

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## page-by-page reviewing

> technique in which reviewers review a work product in a sequential order

_Source: ISO/IEC 20246:2017 Software and systems engineering -- Work product reviews, 3.11_

---

## pager

> routine that initiates and controls the transfer of pages between main and auxiliary storage (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## paging

> storage allocation technique in which programs or data are divided into fixed-length blocks called pages, main storage is divided into blocks of the same length called page frames, and pages are stored in page frames, not necessarily contiguously or in logical order

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## pair programming

> practice that typically involves one developer writing source code or configuring a system while a second developer observes their work to look for errors and to determine whether the completed work is accurate and meets its requirements (ISO/IEC 29110-1-2:2024, Systems and software engineering Lifecycle profiles for Very Small Entities (VSEs) Part 1-2: Vocabulary, 3.56)

---

## pair review

> informal review of a work product performed by two suitably qualified people other than the author working together

_Source: ISO/IEC 20246:2017 Software and systems engineering -- Work product reviews, 3.12_

---

## pairwise testing

> black-box test design technique in which test cases are designed to execute all possible discrete combinations of each pair of input parameters (ISO/IEC TR 29119-11:2020, Software and systems engineering-- Software testing--Part 11: Guidelines on the testing of AI-based systems, 3.1.52)

---

## PAM

> process assessment model (ISO/IEC 29110-1-2:2024, Systems and software engineering Lifecycle profiles for Very Small Entities (VSEs) Part 1-2: Vocabulary, 3.62)

---

## parallel

> pertaining to the simultaneous transfer, occurrence, or processing of the individual parts of a whole, such

---

## parallel classes

> pair of classes that are distinct, are not mutually exclusive and have a common generic ancestor class and for which neither is a generic ancestor of the other

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.140_

---

## parallel construct

> program construct consisting of two or more procedures that can occur simultaneously

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## parallel run operation

> operation of two information processing systems, a given one and its intended replacement, with the same application and source data, for comparison and confidence

_Source: ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary_

---

## parameter

> variable that is given a constant value for a specified application

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## parameter-value pair

> combination of a test item parameter with a value assigned to that parameter, used as a test coverage item in combinatorial test design techniques (ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-4:2021 Software and systems engineering -- Software testing -- Part 4: Test techniques, 3.38)

---

## parameterized collection class

> collection class restricted to hold only instances of a specified type (class)

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.141_

---

## parametric estimating

> estimating technique in which an algorithm is used to calculate cost or duration based on historical data and project parameters (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## parent box

> ancestral box related to its child diagram by exactly one parent/child relationship (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765m:2024, 2.1.93)

---

## parent diagram

> diagram that contains a parent box

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765m:2024, 2.1.94_

---

## parent entity

> entity in a specific relationship whose instances can be related to a number of instances of another entity (child entity) (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.142)

---

## parent function

> function modeled by a parent box

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765m:2024, 2.1.95_

---

## Pareto chart

> [Tool] a histogram, ordered by frequency of occurrence, that shows how many results were generated by each identified cause. (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## Pareto diagram

> histogram, ordered by frequency of occurrence, that shows how many results were generated by each identified cause (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765h:2019)

---

## parking lot

> register of deferred, unresolved issues or concerns

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025_

---

## parking lot diagram

> displayed listing of incomplete tasks or user stories not yet being worked or completed (Software Extension to the PMBOK(R) Guide Fifth Edition)

---

## parse

> to determine the syntactic structure of a language unit by decomposing it into more elementary subunits and establishing the relationships among the subunits

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary_

---

## parser
