> software tool that parses computer programs or other text, often as the first step of assembly, compilation, interpretation, or analysis

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## partial

> incomplete mapping (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.143)

---

## partial cluster

> subclass cluster in which an instance of the superclass exists without also being an instance of any of the subclasses (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.144)

---

## partial correctness

> in proof of correctness, a designation indicating that a program's output assertions follow logically from its input assertions and processing steps (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary)

---

## participant property

> property of a state class that reflects that class' knowledge of a relationship in which instances of the class participate (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.145)

---

## participatory design

> system design process that aims at investigating, understanding, reflecting upon, establishing, developing, and supporting mutual learning between multiple system stakeholders and system developers in collective reflection-in-action (IEEE 7000:2021, IEEE Standard Model Process for Addressing Ethical Concerns during System Design, 3.1)

---

## partition

> set of software functions within an application boundary that share homogeneous criteria and values (ISO/IEC/IEEE 32430:2025, Software engineering Software non-functional size measurement, 3.1.28)

---

## partitioning

> decomposition; the separation of the whole into its parts

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## party

> organization entering into an agreement

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 12207:2026 Systems and software engineering-- Software life cycle processes, 3.1.42_

---

## pass

> single cycle in the processing of a set of data, usually performing part of an overall process

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## pass/fail criteria

> decision rules used to determine whether a software item or a software feature passes or fails a test (ISO/IEC 25051:2014 Software engineering -- Software product Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) -- Requirements for quality of Ready to Use Software Product (RUSP) and instructions for testing, 4.1.10)

---

## passive I/O device

> device that does not generate an interrupt on completion of an input or output operation (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## passive I/O device interface task

> task that interfaces to a passive I/O device and either reads from it or writes to it on demand

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## passive object

> object with no thread of control (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary)

---

## patch

> modification made directly to an object program without reassembling or recompiling from the source program

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## patch

> ,

---

## path

> in software engineering, a sequence of instructions that are performed in the execution of a computer program

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## path analysis

> analysis of a computer program to identify all possible paths through the program, to detect incomplete paths, or to discover portions of the program that are not on any path

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## path condition

> set of conditions required for a particular program path to be executed

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## path divergence

> a relationship in which a schedule activity has more than one successor (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## path expression

> logical expression indicating the input conditions that are required for a particular program path to be executed

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## path testing

> testing designed to execute all or selected paths through a computer program (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## pathological coupling

> type of coupling in which one software module affects or depends upon the internal implementation of another (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## pause

> to suspend the execution of a computer program (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## payoff matrix

> in decisions under uncertainty, a table relating the desirability of a set of alternatives to a set of future states

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## PBS

> product breakdown structure

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 7052:2023, Software engineering--Controlling frequently occurring risks during development and maintenance of custom software, 3.32_

---

## PCA

> physical configuration audit

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## PCB

> printed circuit board

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765d:2015_

---

## PCBA

> printed circuit board assembly

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765d:2015_

---

## PCI DSS

> payment card industry data security standard

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 23026:2023 Systems and software engineering--Engineering and management of websites for systems, software, and services information, 3.2_

---

## PCO

> Point of Control and Observation

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-1:1998 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference model: Overview_

---

## PDCA

> Plan-Do-Check-Act

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765c:2014_

---

## PDF

> Portable Document Format (ISO/IEC/IEEE 26531:2023 Systems and software engineering -- Content management for product lifecycle, user and service management information for users, 3.2)

---

## PDL

> program design language

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## PDR

> preliminary design review

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-7:2019, Systems and software engineering Life cycle management Part 7: Application of systems engineering on defense programs, 3.2_

---

## peer desk check

> informal review where the author and a colleague walk through a work product

_Source: ISO/IEC 20246:2017 Software and systems engineering -- Work product reviews, 3.13_

---

## peer review

> review of work products performed by others qualified to do the same work (ISO/IEC 20246:2017 Software and systems engineering -- Work product reviews, 3.14)

---

## peer software

> piece of software that resides in the same layer as, and exchanges data with, another piece of software

_Source: ISO/IEC 19761:2011 Software engineering -- COSMIC: a functional size measurement method, 2.21_

---

## PEO

> program executive office

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-8:2019, Systems and software engineering Life cycle management Part 7: Application of systems engineering on defense programs, 3.2_

---

## perceptual reference point

> reference point at which there is some interaction between the system and the physical world (ISO/IEC 10746-2:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model:

---

## perfective maintenance

> modification of a software product to provide enhancements for users, improvements of information for users, and recording to improve software performance, maintainability, or other software attributes (ISO/IEC/IEEE 14764:2021, Software engineering -- Software life cycle processes -- Maintenance, 3.1.9)

---

## perfective support

> in-service modification of a system to provide enhancements (IEEE 982:2024, Standard for Measures of the Software Aspects of Dependability, 3.1)

---

## perform quality control

> [Process] the process of monitoring and recording results of executing the quality activities to assess performance and recommend necessary changes (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## perform quantitative analysis

> [Process] the process of numerically analyzing the effect of identified risks on overall project objectives (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## performance

> degree to which a system or component accomplishes its designated functions within given constraints, such as speed, accuracy, or memory usage

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary_

---

## performance analysis

> quantitative analysis of a real-time system (or software design) executing on a given hardware configuration with a given external workload applied to it

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## performance baseline

> result from a normal execution of a performance workload against a system without performing disturbance injection (ISO/IEC 25045:2010 Systems and software engineering--Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)--Evaluation module for recoverability, 4.1)

---

## performance deficiency

> difference between the required (or desired) level of performance and the actual performance (ISO/IEC 25064:2013 Systems and software engineering--Software product Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)--Common Industry Format (CIF) for usability: User needs report, 4.9)

---

## performance efficiency

> capability of a product to perform its functions within specified time and throughput parameters and be efficient in the use of resources under specified conditions (ISO/IEC 25010:2023, Systems and software engineering--Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)--Product quality model,

---

## performance measurement baseline

> integrated scope, schedule, and cost baselines used for comparison to manage, measure, and control project execution (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## performance metrics

> metrics used to evaluate machine learning models that are used for classification (ISO/IEC TR 29119-11:2020, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 11: Guidelines on the testing of AI-based systems, 3.1.54)

---

## performance reports

> [Output/Input] documents and presentations that provide organized and summarized work performance information, earned value management parameters and calculations, and analyses of project work progress and status (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## performance requirement

> measurable criterion that identifies a quality attribute of a function or how well a functional requirement is accomplished

_Source: IEEE 730-2014 IEEE Standard for Software Quality Assurance Processes, 3.2_

---

## performance specification

> information item that identifies the performance characteristics of a system or component (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## performance testing

> type of testing conducted to evaluate the degree to which a test item accomplishes its designated functions within given constraints of time and other resources (ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-2:2021, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 2: Test processes, 3.11)

---

## performed process

> process that accomplishes the needed work to produce work products (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## performing organization

> an enterprise whose personnel are most directly involved in doing the work of the project or program

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765h:2019_

---

## periodic I/O device interface task

> task that interfaces to a passive I/O device and polls it regularly

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## periodic task

> task that a timer event activates at regular intervals

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## peripheral equipment

> device that is controlled by and can communicate with a particular computer

_Source: ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary_

---

## permanence

> degree to which failures can affect object state changes due to completed transactions (ISO/IEC

---

## perpetual license

> license for a software entitlement granted in perpetuity (ISO/IEC 19770-3:2016 Information technology--IT asset management--Part 3: Entitlement schema, 3.1.18)

---

## persistence

> property that an object continues to exist across changes of contractual context or of epoch

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-2:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Foundations, 11.3.1_

---

## persistence schema

> specification of constraints on the use of specific processing, storage and communication functions

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-3:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Architecture, 16.5.1.1_

---

## persistence transparency

> distribution transparency which masks, from an object, the deactivation and reactivation of other objects (or itself) (ISO/IEC 10746-3:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Architecture, 4.4.1.7)

---

## persistent storage

> storage which enables a functional process to store data beyond the life of the functional process, or which enables a functional process to retrieve data stored by another functional process, or stored by an earlier occurrence of the same functional process, or stored by some other process (ISO/IEC 19761:2011 Software engineering -- COSMIC: a functional size measurement method, 2.22)

---

## persistent URI

> a reference that does not need to change at the link in a document, and can still reach the desired object even though that object may have changed locations (ISO/IEC/IEEE 23026:2023 Systems and software engineering--Engineering and management of websites for systems, software, and services information, 3.1.19)

---

## persona

> model of a user with defined characteristics, based on research

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 26513:2017 Systems and software engineering--Requirements for testers and reviewers of information for users, 3.29_

---

## personal computer (PC)

> microcomputer primarily intended for stand-alone use by an individual

_Source: ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary_

---

## personal data

> information related to a natural person

_Source: IEEE 7002:2022, IEEE Standard for Data Privacy Process, 3.1_

---

## personal maxim

> personal principle of what one wishes for, acts upon, and thinks that it should be applicable to everyone

_Source: IEEE 7000:2021, IEEE Standard Model Process for Addressing Ethical Concerns during System Design, 3.1_

---

## personal protective equipment

> any device or appliance designed to be worn or held by an individual for protection against one or more health and safety hazards (IEC/IEEE 82079-1:2019 Preparation of information for use (instructions for use) of products: Part 1: Principles and general requirements, 3.26)

---

## personally identifiable information protection conformance

> degree to which a service conforms to the standards, laws, or regulations applied to collection, processing, and disposal of PII (personally identifiable information) (ISO/IEC TS 25052-1:2022, Systems and software engineering--Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE): cloud services--Part 1: Quality model, 3.1.5.4)

---

## personnel

> individual expected to perform duties on behalf of the organization, including officers, employees, and contractors

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765g:2018_

---

## personnel management

> management of activities involving hiring, retaining, promoting, training, and terminating personnel

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## perspective-based reading

> form of role-based reviewing that uses checklists and involves the creation of prototype deliverables to check the completeness and other quality characteristics of the work product

_Source: ISO/IEC 20246:2017 Software and systems engineering -- Work product reviews, 3.15_

---

## PERT

> program evaluation review technique

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 16326:2019 Systems and software engineering -- Life cycle processes -- Project management, 3_

---

## pessimistic duration

> estimate of the longest activity duration that takes into account all of the known variables that can affect performance

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765h:2019_

---

## PESTEL

> political, economic, social, technological, environmental, and legal

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765f:2016_

---

## Petri net

> algebraic structure with two sets, one called places and the other called transitions, together with their associated relations and functions, and named after their inventor, Carl Adam Petri (ISO/IEC 15909-1:2019 Systems and software engineering--High-level Petri nets--Part 1: Concepts, definitions and graphical notation, 3.25)

---

## Petri net with priorities

> Petri net having priorities which can be used for selecting the enabled transitions according to the given priority scheme

_Source: ISO/IEC 15909-1:2019 Systems and software engineering--High-level Petri nets-- Part 1: Concepts, definitions and graphical notation, 3.26_

---

## Petri net with time

> Petri net having timing constraints associated with the nodes or arcs

_Source: ISO/IEC 15909-1:2019 Systems and software engineering--High-level Petri nets--Part 1: Concepts, definitions and graphical notation, 3.27_

---

## phase

> period of time in the life cycle during which activities are performed that enable achievement of objectives for that phase (ISO/IEC/IEEE 42020:2019 Software, systems and enterprise -- Architecture processes, 3.15)

---

## phase gate

> review at the end of a phase in which a decision is made to continue to the next phase, to continue with modification, or to end a project or program (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## PHP

> hypertext preprocessor

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 23026:2023 Systems and software engineering--Engineering and management of websites for systems, software, and services information, 3.2_

---

## physical configuration audit (PCA)

> audit conducted to verify that a configuration item, as built, conforms to the technical documentation that defines it

_Source: IEEE 828-2012 IEEE Standard for Configuration Management in Systems and Software Engineering, 2.1_

---

## physical interface

> physical link between two or more system elements (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-6:2023 Systems and software engineering Life cycle management Part 6: System and software integration, 3.1.6)

---

## physical requirement

> requirement that specifies a tangible, measurable characteristic of a system or system component (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## physical source statement (PSS)

> source statement considered as a line of code (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## PI

> personal information

_Source: IEEE 7002:2022, IEEE Standard for Data Privacy Process, 3.1_

---

## PICS

> Protocol Implementation Conformance Statement

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-2:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Foundations, 4_

---

## picture

> illustration that shows the actual appearance of physical objects

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 26514:2022, Systems and software engineering -- Design and development of information for users, 3.1.37_

---

## PII

> personally identifiable information (ISO/IEC/IEEE 23026:2023 Systems and software engineering--Engineering

---

## pilot project

> project designed to test a preliminary version of an information processing system under actual but limited operating conditions and which will then be used to test the definitive version of the system

_Source: ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary_

---

## PIN

> personal identification number

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 23026:2023 Systems and software engineering--Engineering and management of websites for systems, software, and services information, 3.2_

---

## PIPEDA

> Personal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Act

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 23026:2023 Systems and software engineering--Engineering and management of websites for systems, software, and services information, 3.2_

---

## pipeline

> software or hardware design technique in which the output of one process serves as input to a second, the output of the second process serves as input to a third, and so on, often with simultaneity within a single cycle time

_Source: IEEE 2675-2021, IEEE Standard for DevOps: Building Reliable and Secure Systems Including Application Build, Package, and Deployment, 3.1_

---

## pixel

> smallest element of a screen display; short for 'picture element'

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765a:2011_

---

## PIXIT

> Protocol Implementation Extra Information for Testing

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-2:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Foundations, 4_

---

## place

> node of a net, usually represented by an ellipse

_Source: ISO/IEC 15909-1:2019 Systems and software engineering-- High-level Petri nets--Part 1: Concepts, definitions and graphical notation, 3.28_

---

## place/transition net

> Petri Net comprising a net graph with positive integers associated with arcs and an initial marking function which associates a natural number of simple tokens ('black dots') with places (ISO/IEC 15909-2:2011 Software and system engineering--High-level Petri nets--Part 2: Transfer format, 3.29)

---

## plan

> information item that presents a systematic course of action for achieving a declared purpose, including when, how, and by whom specific activities are to be performed (ISO/IEC/IEEE 15289:2019 Systems and software engineering-- Content of life-cycle information items (documentation), 5.16)

---

## plan communications

> [Process] the process of determining project stakeholder information needs and defining a communication approach (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## plan procurement

> [Process] the process of documenting project purchasing decisions, specifying the approach, and identifying potential sellers (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## plan quality

> [Process] the process of identifying quality requirements and/or standards for the project and product, and documenting how the project will demonstrate compliance (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## plan risk management

> the process of defining how to conduct risk management activities for a project (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## plan standard

> standard that describes the characteristics of a scheme for accomplishing defined objectives or work within specified resources

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## planned process

> process that is documented by both a description and a plan (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## planned value (PV)

> authorized budget assigned to scheduled work (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## planning

> activities concerned with the specification of a plan

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-5:2017 Systems and software engineering--Life cycle management--Part 5: Software development planning, 3.9_

---

## planning horizon

> consistent time span used to compare the cost of alternatives

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## planning performance domain

> performance domain that addresses activities and functions associated with the initial, ongoing, and evolving organization and coordination necessary for delivering project deliverables and results (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## planning poker

> team-based estimation approach whereby relative estimates of the effort required to develop and test each user story are set via consensus (ISO/IEC 29110-1-2:2024, Systems and software engineering Lifecycle profiles for Very Small Entities (VSEs) Part 1-2: Vocabulary, 3.57)

---

## planning process group

> those processes required to establish the scope of the project, refine the objectives, and define the course of action required to attain the objectives that the project was undertaken to achieve (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## planning processes

> [Process Group] those processes performed to establish the total scope of the effort, define and refine the project objectives, and develop the course of action required to attain those objectives (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## plant

> assembly of different systems on a specific site (IEC/IEEE 82079-1:2019 Preparation of information for use (instructions for use) of products: Part 1: Principles and general requirements, 3.27)

---

## plastic leaded chip carrier (PLCC)

> rectangular chip unit, made of plastic for use in low-heat devices, usually with surface-mount or J-shaped (J-lead) connectors

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765c:2014_

---

## platform

> type of computer or hardware device and/or associated operating system, or a virtual environment, on which software can be installed or run

---

## platform as a service (PaaS)

> provision of a complete environment of IT resources, such as programming languages, libraries, services, and tools supported by the service provider (IEEE 2675-2021, IEEE Standard for DevOps: Building Reliable and Secure Systems Including Application Build, Package, and Deployment, 3.1)

---

## platform provider

> organization responsible for the platform (ISO/IEC 19770-2:2015 (corr 2017), Information technology -- Software asset management -- Part 2: Software identification tag, 3.1.2)

---

## PLC

> programmable logic controller

_Source: IEEE 1012-2024 IEEE Standard for System, Software, and Hardware Verification and Validation, 3.2_

---

## PLCC

> plastic leaded chip carrier

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765c:2014_

---

## PM

> project manager

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 16326:2019 Systems and software engineering -- Life cycle processes -- Project management, 5_

---

## PM&P

> parts, materials, and processes

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-8:2019, Systems and software engineering Life cycle management Part 7: Application of systems engineering on defense programs, 3.2_

---

## PMB

> performance measurement baseline (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## PMBOK®

> Project Management Body of Knowledge (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## PMI

> Project Management Institute

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765i:2020_

---

## PMO

> project management office (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## PMP

> Project Management Plan

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 16326:2019 Systems and software engineering -- Life cycle processes -- Project management, 3_

---

## PNG

> Portable Network Graphics

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 23026:2023 Systems and software engineering--Engineering and management of websites for systems, software, and services information, 3.2_

---

## PNML

> Petri Net Markup Language

_Source: ISO/IEC 15909-2:2011 Software and system engineering--High-level Petri nets- -Part 2: Transfer format, 4.2.5_

---

## PNML Core Model

> metamodel defining the basic concepts and structure of net graph models that are common to all versions of Petri nets

_Source: ISO/IEC 15909-2:2011 Software and system engineering--High-level Petri nets--Part 2: Transfer format, 4.1.9_

---

## PNML document

> XML document that contains one or more net graphs (ISO/IEC 15909-2:2011 Software and system engineering--High-level Petri nets--Part 2: Transfer format, 4.1.10)

---

## PNML high-level net document

> PNML Document that contains one or more net graphs, where all net graphs conform to high-level Petri nets

_Source: ISO/IEC 15909-2:2011 Software and system engineering--High-level Petri nets--Part 2: Transfer format, 4.1.11_

---

## PNML symmetric net document

> PNML Document that contains one or more net graphs, where all net graphs conform to symmetric nets

_Source: ISO/IEC 15909-2:2011 Software and system engineering--High-level Petri nets--Part 2: Transfer format_

---

## PO

> purchase order

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765l:2024_

---

## point

> measure of vertical distance; there are approximately 28 points to the millimeter (72 points to the inch)

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765a:2011_

---

## point design

> selection of one design that satisfies the requirements without examining other, potentially more effective, designs

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## pointer

> data item that specifies the location of another data item

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## policy

> clear and measurable statements of preferred direction and behavior to condition the decisions made within an organization (ISO/IEC/IEEE 15289:2019 Systems and software engineering--Content of life-cycle information items (documentation), 5.17)

---

## policy declaration

> element in a specification defined in order to allow incorporation of future constraints, together with rules determining the allowed form of acceptable constraints and the circumstances in which such constraints can be applied

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-2:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Foundations, 11.2.9_

---

## policy envelope

> set of acceptable policy values that can be applied at a particular policy declaration

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-2:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Foundations, 11.2.11_

---

## policy value

> specific constraints associated with a policy in some particular epoch

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-2:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Foundations, 11.2.10_

---

## policy-setting behavior

> behavior defined in a specification via which a policy can be changed (ISO/IEC 10746- 2:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Foundations, 11.2.12)

---

## pop-up

> embedded, context-sensitive information that is displayed when invoked by user action

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765g:2018_

---

## port

> surface feature through which clients and other elements of an application environment can interact with a component

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765l:2024_

---

## port alias

> replacement relationship in a build specification that identifies a port of one unit with a port of a sub-unit and indicates that interactions at the two ports can be paired identically or compatibly (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765i:2020)

---

## port-to-port time

> elapsed time between the application of a stimulus to an input interface and the appearance of

---

## portability

> ease with which a system or component can be transferred from one hardware or software environment to another

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## portability testing

> type of testing conducted to evaluate the ease with which a test item can be transferred from one hardware or software environment to another, including the level of modification needed for it to be executed in various types of environments

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-2:2021, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 2: Test processes, 3.59_

---

## portable computer

> microcomputer that can be hand-carried for use in more than one location (ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary)

---

## portfolio

> projects, programs, subsidiary portfolios, and operations managed as a group to achieve strategic objectives (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## portfolio management

> the centralized management of one or more portfolios to achieve strategic objectives (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## positive control

> state of affirmative physical or non-physical control (IEEE 7005 2021, IEEE Standard for Transparent Employer Data Governance, 3.1)

---

## post-closure activities

> activities that occur after a software system has been formally accepted by its customer (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## post-compile time

> collective name for link time and load time that are right after the compilation of components

_Source: ISO/IEC 26557:2016 Software and systems engineering -- Methods and tools for variability mechanisms in software and systems product line, 3.9_

---

## post-probe

> optional phase to prepare action plans for addressing challenge

_Source: ISO/IEC 26561:2019 Software systems engineering--Methods and tools for product line technical probe, 3.3_

---

## postcondition

> condition that is true after a successful property request

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.147_

---

## postmortem dump

> dump that is produced upon abnormal termination of a computer program (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## postprocessor

> computer program or routine that carries out some final processing step after the completion of the primary process (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## power-down mode

> energy-saving operational state for a microcontroller unit (MCU)

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765e:2015_

---

## powertype

> type, the instances of which are subtypes of another type called the 'partitioned type'

_Source: ISO/IEC 24744:2014 Software Engineering--Metamodel for development methodologies, 3.12_

---

## PP

> prioritization processor (IEEE 7005 2021, IEEE Standard for Transparent Employer Data Governance, 3.2)

---

## PPE

> personal protective equipment

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025_

---

## PPL

> Product Parts List

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 16326:2019 Systems and software engineering -- Life cycle processes -- Project management, 3_

---

## PPP

> program protection plan

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-8:2019, Systems and software engineering Life cycle management Part 7: Application of systems engineering on defense programs, 3.2_

---

## PPSL

> program parts selection list

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-8:2019, Systems and software engineering Life cycle management Part 7: Application of systems engineering on defense programs, 3.2_

---

## PR&RPI

> Problem Reporting and Resolution Planned Information

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering--Vocabulary_

---

## practice

> specific type of activity that contributes to the execution of a process

_Source: ISO/IEC 33001:2015 Information technology--Process assessment--Concepts and terminology, 3.3.8_

---

## practitioner (PT)

> person or team performing the activities within one or more process areas (ISO/IEC 29110-1- 2:2024, Systems and software engineering Lifecycle profiles for Very Small Entities (VSEs) Part 1-2: Vocabulary, 3.58)

---

## pre-probe

> phase to understand an organization's basic context such as current structure, terminology, product maturity level, implementation, and documentation

_Source: ISO/IEC 26561:2019 Software systems engineering--Methods and tools for product line technical probe, 3.4_

---

## pre-processing

> part of the machine learning (ML) workflow that transforms raw data into a state ready for use by the ML algorithm to create the ML model (ISO/IEC TR 29119-11:2020, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 11: Guidelines on the testing of AI-based systems, 3.1.57)

---

## precautionary principle

> principle that the introduction of a new product or process whose ultimate effects are unknown or disputed should be resisted until such risks are appropriately mitigated (ISO/IEC/IEEE 32430:2025, Software engineering Software non-functional size measurement, 3.1.30)

---

## precision

> degree of exactness or discrimination with which a quantity is stated

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## precompiler

> computer program or routine that processes source code and generates equivalent code that is acceptable to a compiler (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## precondition

> condition that is required to be true before making a property request

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.148_

---

## predicate

> logical expression which evaluates to TRUE or FALSE, normally to direct the execution path in code

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-4:2021 Software and systems engineering -- Software testing -- Part 4: Test techniques, 3.40_

---

## predicate data use

> data use associated with the decision outcome of the predicate portion of a decision statement (ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-4:2021 Software and systems engineering -- Software testing -- Part 4: Test techniques, 3.37)

---

## prediction

> machine learning function that results in a predicted target value for a given input

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 29119- 11:2020, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 11: Guidelines on the testing of AI-based systems, 3.1.56_

---

## predictive action

> action to monitor the condition of an asset and predict the need for preventive action or corrective action (ISO/IEC 19770-1:2017 Information technology -- IT asset management -- Part 1: IT asset management systems--Requirements, 3.44)

---

## predictive approach

> development approach in which the project scope, time, and cost are determined in the early phases of the life cycle (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## preliminary design

> analysis of design alternatives and definition of the architecture, components, interfaces, and timing and sizing estimates for a system or component (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## preliminary design review (PDR)

> review conducted to evaluate the progress, technical adequacy, and risk resolution of the selected design approach for one or more configuration items; to determine each design's compatibility

---

## preparation time

> time which elapses before the task submission (ISO/IEC 14756:1999 Information technology -- Measurement and rating of performance of computer-based software systems, 4.12)

---

## preprocessor

> computer program or routine that carries out some processing step prior to the primary process (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## prescription

> action that establishes a rule

_Source: ISO/IEC 15414:2015 Information technology -- Open distributed processing -- Reference model -- Enterprise language, 6.6.3_

---

## present worth

> representation of a cash flow as a single instance at the beginning of the planning horizon (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## presentable

> retrievable and viewable (ISO/IEC/IEEE 15289:2019 Systems and software engineering--Content of life-cycle information items (documentation), 5.18)

---

## presentation device

> device used to present data to the intended user of a system (ISO/IEC 25024:2015 Systems and software engineering - Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) - Measurement of data, 4.30)

---

## prestore

> to store data that are required by a computer program or routine before the program or routine is entered

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## prettyprinting

> use of indentation, blank lines, and other visual cues to show the logical structure of a program

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## preventive action

> action to eliminate the cause of a potential nonconformity or other undesirable potential situation (ISO/IEC 19770-1:2017 Information technology -- IT asset management -- Part 1: IT asset management systems--Requirements, 3.45)

---

## preventive maintenance

> modification of a software product after delivery to correct latent faults in the software product before they occur in the live system

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 14764:2021, Software engineering -- Software life cycle processes -- Maintenance, 3.1.10_

---

## preventive support

> in-service actions undertaken to correct latent faults or vulnerabilities before they are manifested as failures, to replenish or update resources, or to restore initial operating conditions to prevent software aging failures

_Source: IEEE 982:2024, Standard for Measures of the Software Aspects of Dependability, 3.1_

---

## previously developed software

> software that has been produced prior to or independent of the project for which the plan is prepared, including software that is obtained or purchased from outside sources (IEEE 1228-1994

---

## primary data

> data that is collected directly from a subject

_Source: IEEE 7010-2020, IEEE Recommended Practice for Assessing the Impact of Autonomous and Intelligent Systems on Human Well-Being, 2.1_

---

## primary Ent

> entitlement schema (Ent) which encapsulates basic information about an entitlement (ISO/IEC 19770-3:2016 Information technology--IT asset management--Part 3: Entitlement schema, 3.1.19)

---

## primary entity-type

> in Mk II FPA, one of the main entity-types which has the attributes that the application has been designed to process and/or store

_Source: ISO/IEC 20968:2002 Software engineering -- Mk II Function Point Analysis -- Counting Practices Manual, 10_

---

## primary information structure

> information structure to which supplemental information structures may be linked (ISO/IEC 19770-5:2015 Information technology--IT asset management--Overview and vocabulary, 3.24)

---

## primary intent

> intent that is first in importance

_Source: ISO/IEC 20926:2009 Software and systems engineering -- Software measurement -- IFPUG functional size measurement method 2009, 3.43_

---

## primary key

> candidate key selected as the unique identifier of an entity (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765l:2024)

---

## primary user

> person or entity who interacts with the system to achieve the primary goals (ISO/IEC 25019:2023, Systems and software engineering Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) Quality-in- use model, 3.3.8)

---

## primitive

> lowest level for which data is collected (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary)

---

## principal

> party that has delegated authority or a function to another

_Source: ISO/IEC 15414:2015 Information technology -- Open distributed processing -- Reference model -- Enterprise language, 6.6.9_

---

## printed circuit board (PCB)

> configuration of connections between electronic components, typically using metallic paths etched on a non-conducting substrate (board)

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765d:2015_

---

## printed circuit board assembly (PCBA)

> printed circuit board with embedded components and devices

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765d:2015_

---

## printed documentation

> documentation that is either provided in printed form, or provided in electronic form for the customer or user to print (ISO/IEC/IEEE 26514:2022, Systems and software engineering -- Design and development of information for users, 4.33)

---

## printed information for users

> information for users that is either provided in printed form, or provided in electronic form for the customer or user to print (ISO/IEC/IEEE 26514:2022, Systems and software engineering -- Design and development of information for users, 3.1.38)

---

## prioritization matrix

> diagram that plots effort against value so as to classify items by priority (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## prioritization scheme

> methods used to prioritize portfolio, program, or project components, as well as requirements, risks, features, or other product information (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## priority ceiling protocol

> algorithm that provides bounded priority inversion (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## priority interrupt

> interrupt performed to permit execution of a process that has a higher priority than the process currently executing

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## priority inversion

> case where a task's execution is delayed because a lower priority task is blocking it

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## privacy capabilities

> ability to process personal data in a fair and legitimate manner

_Source: IEEE 7002:2022, IEEE Standard for Data Privacy Process, 3.1_

---

## privacy control

> monitoring prescribed for an information system or an organization, designed to achieve a privacy objective

_Source: IEEE 7002:2022, IEEE Standard for Data Privacy Process, 3.1_

---

## privacy framework

> structure that helps the organization meet its privacy requirements, including policies and procedures, roles and responsibilities, training, and governance functions

_Source: IEEE 7002:2022, IEEE Standard for Data Privacy Process, 3.1_

---

## privacy principles

> set of values that guide a system or an organization's personal data actions

_Source: IEEE 7002:2022, IEEE Standard for Data Privacy Process, 3.1_

---

## privacy requirement

> specification for the processing of personal data to meet stakeholders desired privacy outcomes and obligations

_Source: IEEE 7002:2022, IEEE Standard for Data Privacy Process, 3.1_

---

## privacy risk

> combination of the likelihood and impact that individuals or organizations will experience problems resulting from exposure, use, theft, or alteration of personal data

_Source: IEEE 7002:2022, IEEE Standard for Data Privacy Process, 3.1_

---

## private

> responsibility that is visible only to the class or the receiving instance of the class (available only within methods of the class)

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.150_

---

## private type

> data type whose structure and possible values are defined but are not revealed to the user of the type (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## privileged instruction

> computer instruction that can be executed only by a supervisory program

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## PRM

> process reference model

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765l:2024_

---

## proactive approach

> approach of developing an innovative product line or product variations based on organizational predictions that anticipate a stated product need

_Source: ISO/IEC 26553:2018 Information technology -- Software and systems engineering-- Tools and methods for product line realization, 3.15_

---

## probabilistic estimating

> method used to develop a range of estimates along with the associated probabilities within that range (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## probabilistic system

> system whose behavior is described in terms of probabilities, such that its outputs cannot be perfectly predicted

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 29119-11:2020, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 11: Guidelines on the testing of AI-based systems, 3.1.58_

---

## probability

> extent to which an event is likely to occur

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765j:2021_

---

## probability and impact matrix

> grid for mapping the probability of each risk occurrence and its impact on project objectives if that risk occurs (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## probe

> diagnostic process for investigating the organization's readiness to adopt, or ability to succeed with, product line engineering and management (ISO/IEC 26561:2019 Software systems engineering--Methods and tools for product line technical probe, 3.7)

---

## problem

> difficulty, uncertainty, or otherwise realized and undesirable event, set of events, condition, or situation that requires investigation and corrective action

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288:2023 Systems and software engineering--System life cycle processes, 3.26_

---

## problem definition

> statement of a problem, which can include a description of the data, the method, the procedures, and algorithms used to solve it (ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary)

---

## problem report (PR)

> document used to identify and describe problems detected in a product (ISO/IEC/IEEE 14764:2021, Software engineering -- Software life cycle processes -- Maintenance, 3.1.11)

---

## problem state

> in the operation of a computer system, a state in which programs other than the supervisory program can execute (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## problem-oriented language

> programming language designed for the solution of a given class of problems

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## procedural cohesion

> type of cohesion in which the tasks performed by a software module all contribute to a given program procedure, such as an iteration or decision process (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## procedural language

> programming language in which the user states a specific set of instructions that the computer performs in a given sequence (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## procedural programming language

> computer programming language used to express the sequence of operations to be performed by a computer (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering--Vocabulary)

---

## procedure

> information item that presents an ordered series of steps to perform a process, activity, or task (ISO/IEC/IEEE 15289:2019 Systems and software engineering--Content of life-cycle information items (documentation), 5.19)

---

## procedure testing

> type of functional suitability testing conducted to evaluate whether procedural instructions for interacting with a test item or using its outputs meet user requirements and support the purpose of their use

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-1:2022, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 1: General concepts, 3.60_

---

## process

> set of interrelated or interacting activities which transforms inputs into outputs.

---

## process action plan

> plan, usually resulting from appraisals, that documents how specific improvements targeting the weaknesses uncovered by an appraisal will be implemented

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## process action team

> team that has the responsibility to develop and implement process improvement activities for an organization as documented in a process action plan

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## process architect

> person or group that has primary responsibility for creating and maintaining the software life cycle process (SLCP)

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering--Vocabulary_

---

## process architecture

> ordering, interfaces, interdependencies, and other relationships among the process elements in a standard process (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## process area

> cluster of related practices in an area that, when implemented collectively, satisfies a set of goals considered important for making improvement in that area

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary_

---

## process assessment

> disciplined evaluation of an organizational unit's processes against a process assessment model

_Source: ISO/IEC 33001:2015 Information technology--Process assessment--Concepts and terminology, 3.2.15_

---

## process assessment model (PAM)

> model suitable for the purpose of assessing a specified process quality characteristic, based on one or more process reference models (ISO/IEC 33001:2015 Information technology--Process assessment--Concepts and terminology, 3.3.9)

---

## process asset library

> collection of information that can be useful to those who are defining, implementing, and managing processes in the organization.

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## process attribute (PA)

> measurable property of a process quality characteristic (ISO/IEC 33001:2015 Information technology--Process assessment--Concepts and terminology, 3.4.3)

---

## process attribute outcome

> observable result of achievement of a specified process attribute

_Source: ISO/IEC 33001:2015 Information technology--Process assessment--Concepts and terminology, 3.4.4_

---

## process attribute rating

> judgment of the degree of achievement of the process attribute for the assessed process

_Source: ISO/IEC 33001:2015 Information technology--Process assessment--Concepts and terminology, 3.4.5_

---

## process capability

> characterization of the ability of a process to meet current or projected business goals

_Source: ISO/IEC 33020:2019 Information technology--Process assessment--Process measurement framework for assessment of process capability, 3.4_

---

## process capability level

> characterization of a process on an ordinal measurement scale of process capability (ISO/IEC 33020:2019 Information technology--Process assessment--Process measurement framework for assessment of process capability, 3.5)

---

## process context

> set of factors, documented in the assessment input, that influence the judgment, comprehension and comparability of process attribute ratings

---

## process definition

> identification of the process purpose, outcomes, and a sequence of steps involving activities, constraints, and resources that are performed for a given purpose

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## process description

> documented expression of a set of activities performed to achieve a given purpose (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## process dimension

> set of process elements in a process assessment model explicitly related to the processes defined in the relevant process reference models (ISO/IEC 33001:2015 Information technology--Process assessment-- Concepts and terminology, 3.3.10)

---

## process group

> collection of related processes

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary_

---

## process improvement

> actions taken to improve the quality of the organization's processes aligned with the business needs and the needs of other concerned parties

_Source: ISO/IEC 33001:2015 Information technology--Process assessment--Concepts and terminology, 3.1.7_

---

## process improvement objective

> set of target characteristics established to guide the effort to improve an existing process in a specific, measurable way, either in terms of resultant product or service characteristics, such as quality, performance, and conformance to standards, or in the way in which the process is executed, such as elimination of redundant process steps, combination of process steps, and improvement of cycle time

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## process improvement plan

> subsidiary plan of the project management plan that details the steps for analyzing processes to identify activities that enhance their value

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765h:2019_

---

## process improvement support element

> way that an organization expresses support for process improvement projects or initiatives

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 33014:2013 Information technology--Process assessment--Guide for process improvement, 3.2_

---

## process infrastructure

> internal structure of the software life-cycle process, to include lifecycle phases, documentation, baselines, reviews, and products

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary_

---

## process instance

> single specific and identifiable execution of a process (ISO/IEC 33001:2015 Information

---

## process management

> direction, control, and coordination of work performed to develop a product or perform a service

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## process maturity

> extent to which an organizational unit consistently implements processes within a defined scope that contribute to the achievement of its business needs (current or projected)

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 26511:2018 Systems and software engineering--Requirements for managers of information for users of systems, software, and services, 3.1.23_

---

## process measurement framework

> schema for use in characterizing a process quality characteristic of an implemented process

_Source: ISO/IEC 33001:2015 Information technology--Process assessment--Concepts and terminology, 3.4.6_

---

## process outcome

> observable result of the successful achievement of the process purpose (ISO/IEC/IEEE 12207:2026 Systems and software engineering--Software life cycle processes, 3.1.46) (ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288:2023 Systems and software engineering--System life cycle processes, 3.30)

---

## process owner

> person (or team) responsible for defining and maintaining a process (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## process performance

> extent to which the execution of a process achieves its purpose

_Source: ISO/IEC 33001:2015 Information technology--Process assessment--Concepts and terminology, 3.4.7_

---

## process performance indicator

> assessment indicator that supports the judgment of the process performance of a specific process

_Source: ISO/IEC 33001:2015 Information technology--Process assessment--Concepts and terminology, 3.3.12_

---

## process profile

> set of process attribute ratings for an assessed process

_Source: ISO/IEC 33001:2015 Information technology--Process assessment--Concepts and terminology, 3.2.18_

---

## process purpose

> high-level objective of performing the process and the likely outcomes of effective implementation of the process (ISO/IEC/IEEE 12207:2026 Systems and software engineering--Software life cycle processes, 3.1.47) (ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288:2023 Systems and software engineering--System life cycle processes, 3.29)

---

## process quality

> ability of a process to satisfy stated and implied stakeholder needs when used in a specified context

_Source: ISO/IEC 33001:2015 Information technology--Process assessment--Concepts and terminology, 3.4.8_

---

## process quality characteristic

> measurable aspect of process quality

_Source: ISO/IEC 33001:2015 Information technology--Process assessment--Concepts and terminology, 3.4.9_

---

## process quality determination

> systematic assessment and analysis of selected processes against a target process profile

_Source: ISO/IEC 33001:2015 Information technology--Process assessment--Concepts and terminology, 3.2.19_

---

## process quality dimension

> set of elements in a process assessment model explicitly related to the process measurement framework for the specified process quality characteristic

_Source: ISO/IEC 33001:2015 Information technology-- Process assessment--Concepts and terminology, 3.3.14_

---

## process quality indicator

> assessment indicator that supports the judgment of the process quality characteristic of a specific process

_Source: ISO/IEC 33001:2015 Information technology--Process assessment--Concepts and terminology, 3.3.15_

---

## process quality level

> point on a scale of achievement of a process quality characteristic derived from the process attribute ratings for an assessed process

_Source: ISO/IEC 33001:2015 Information technology--Process assessment-- Concepts and terminology, 3.4.10_

---

## process reference model (PRM)

> model comprising definitions of processes in a domain of application described in terms of process purpose and outcomes, together with an architecture describing the relationships between the processes (ISO/IEC/IEEE 12207:2026 Systems and software engineering--Software life cycle processes, 3.1.48)

_Source: ISO/IEC 33001:2015 Information technology--Process assessment--Concepts and terminology, 3.3.16_

---

## process risk determination

> systematic assessment and analysis of selected processes against a target process profile, carried out with the aim of identifying process-related risks to meet a particular specified requirement

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 33015:2019 Information technology--Process assessment--Guidance for process risk determination, 3.1_

---

## process standard

> standard that deals with the series of actions or operations used in making or achieving a product

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## process tailoring

> making, altering, or adapting a process description for a particular end

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## process view

> description of how a specified purpose and set of outcomes can be achieved by employing the activities and tasks of existing processes

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 15026-1:2019 Systems and software engineering--Systems and software assurance--Part 1: Concepts and vocabulary, 3.2.2_

---

## process-related risk

> risk resulting from weaknesses in the performance, management, or deployment of a process

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 33015:2019 Information technology--Process assessment--Guidance for process risk determination, 3.2_

---

## processing logic

> any of the requirements specifically requested by the user to complete an elementary process, such as validations, algorithms, or calculations, and reading or maintaining a file

_Source: ISO/IEC 20926:2009 Software and systems engineering -- Software measurement -- IFPUG functional size measurement method 2009_

---

## processor

> in a computer, a functional unit that interprets and executes instructions (ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary)

---

## procurement management plan

> component of the project or program management plan that describes how a project team will acquire goods and services from outside the performing organization (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## producer object

> object which is the source of the information conveyed

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-2:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Foundations, 13.4.3_

---

## product

> result of a process

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 12207:2026 Systems and software engineering--Software life cycle processes, 3.1.49_

---

## product analysis

> evaluating a product by manual or automated means to determine if the product has certain characteristics

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## product asset instance

> instantiation of a shared asset specific to a member product, automatically produced by the product line engineering (PLE) factory configurator, corresponding to a bill-of-features for that member product (ISO/IEC 26580:2021, Software and systems engineering Methods and tools for the feature-based approach to software and systems product line engineering, 3.14)

---

## product authority

> person or persons with overall responsibility for the capabilities and quality of a product

---

## product backlog

> list of all requirements, normally represented as user stories, that need to be done for a given product (ISO/IEC 29110-1-2:2024, Systems and software engineering Lifecycle profiles for Very Small Entities (VSEs) Part 1-2: Vocabulary, 3.69)

---

## product baseline

> description of the detailed design at a specific point in time, for production, fielding/deployment, and operations and support (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-7:2019, Systems and software engineering Life cycle management Part 7: Application of systems engineering on defense programs, 3.1)

---

## product breakdown structure

> hierarchical structure reflecting a products components and deliverables (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## product configuration identification

> current approved or conditionally approved technical documentation defining a configuration item during the production, operation, maintenance, and logistic support phases of its life cycle

---

## product description

> document stating properties of software, with the main purpose of helping potential acquirers in the evaluation of the suitability for themselves of the software before purchasing it (ISO/IEC 25051:2014 Software engineering -- Software product Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) -- Requirements for quality of Ready to Use Software Product (RUSP) and instructions for testing, 4.1.44)

---

## product engineering

> technical processes to define, design, and construct or assemble a product

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## product identification

> software product name, version, variant, and date information (ISO/IEC 25051:2014 Software engineering -- Software product Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) -- Requirements for quality of Ready to Use Software Product (RUSP) and instructions for testing, 4.1.12)

---

## product instances

> collection comprising the bill-of-features portfolio and product asset instances

_Source: ISO/IEC 26580:2021, Software and systems engineering Methods and tools for the feature-based approach to software and systems product line engineering, 3.15_

---

## product life cycle

> series of phases that represent the evolution of a product, from concept through delivery, growth, maturity, and to retirement (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## product line

> set of products or services sharing explicitly defined and managed common and variable features and relying on the same domain architecture to meet the common and variable needs of specific markets

_Source: ISO/IEC 26550:2015 Software and systems engineering--Reference model for product line engineering and management, 3.16_

---

## product line adoption plan

> plan that describes the changes in process, organization structure, and product building methods to get from the current to product line engineering

_Source: ISO/IEC 26561:2019 Software systems engineering-- Methods and tools for product line technical probe, 3.5_

---

## product line adoption scenario

> scenario that gives concrete sequence of actions related to product line adoption

_Source: ISO/IEC 26561:2019 Software systems engineering--Methods and tools for product line technical probe, 3.6_

---

## product line architecture

> architecture, including both domain architecture and application architecture

_Source: ISO/IEC 26552:2019 Software and systems engineering--Tools and methods for product line architecture design, 3.8_

---

## product line configuration

> snapshot of the product line that contains a collection of revisions of every domain asset and member product in the product line

_Source: ISO/IEC 26563:2022. Software and systems engineering Methods and tools for product line configuration management, 3.4_

---

## product line configuration delta

> difference between the two versions of product line configuration

_Source: ISO/IEC 26563:2022. Software and systems engineering Methods and tools for product line configuration management, 3.5_

---

## product line configuration item

> resulting artefacts that make up a product line (ISO/IEC 26563:2022. Software and systems engineering Methods and tools for product line configuration management, 3.6)

---

## product line configuration management

> coordinated activities to direct and control product line configuration (ISO/IEC 26563:2022. Software and systems engineering Methods and tools for product line configuration management, 3.7)

---

## product line engineering factory

> technological, organizational, and business infrastructure and processes to support a product line engineering (PLE) factory configurator producing product asset instances from shared asset supersets based on a bill-of-features for a member product (ISO/IEC 26580:2021, Software and systems engineering Methods and tools for the feature-based approach to software and systems product line engineering, 3.11)

---

## product line engineering factory configurator

> automated mechanism that produces assets for a specific member product by processing the bill-of-features for that member product, and exercising the shared assets variation points in light of the feature selections made in that bill-of-features (ISO/IEC 26580:2021, Software and systems engineering Methods and tools for the feature-based approach to software and systems product line engineering, 3.12)

---

## product line engineering factory development environment

> toolset for creating, organizing, assembling, and maintaining a collection of elements in a feature catalog, bill-of-features portfolio, shared asset supersets, and a hierarchy of a product line of product lines (ISO/IEC 26580:2021, Software and systems engineering Methods and tools for the feature-based approach to software and systems product line engineering, 3.13)

---

## product line institutionalization

> considering product line engineering as part of working culture by involved managers and staff members

_Source: ISO/IEC 26562:2019 Software and systems engineering--Methods and tools for product line transition management, 3.2_

---

## product line platform

> product line architecture, a configuration management plan, and domain assets, enabling application engineering to effectively reuse and produce a set of derivative products

_Source: ISO/IEC 26550:2015 Software and systems engineering--Reference model for product line engineering and management, 3.18_

---

## product line reference model

> abstract representation of the domain and application engineering life cycle processes; the roles and relationships of the processes; and the assets produced, managed, and used during product line engineering and management (ISO/IEC 26550:2015 Software and systems engineering--Reference model for product line

---

## product line scoping

> process for defining the member products that will be produced within a product line and the major common and variable features among the products, analyzes the products from an economic point of view, and controls and schedules the development, production, and marketing of the product line and its products

_Source: ISO/IEC 26550:2015 Software and systems engineering--Reference model for product line engineering and management, 3.20_

---

## product line test strategy

> scope of domain testing and application testing (ISO/IEC 26554:2018 Information technology--Software and systems engineering-Tools and methods for product line testing, 3.8)

---

## product management

> definition, coordination, and control of the characteristics of a product during its development cycle

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## product owner

> stakeholder responsible for the capabilities, acceptance, and use of a product

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 24587:2021, Software and systems engineering--Agile development--Agile adoption considerations, 3.12_

---

## product quality

> capability of a system or its components to satisfy stated and implied quality needs when used under specific conditions (ISO/IEC 25002:2024, Systems and software engineering Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) Quality model overview and usage, 3.16)

---

## product requirement

> refinement of customer requirements into the developers' language, making implicit requirements into explicit derived requirements

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary_

---

## product risk

> risk that a product can be defective in some specific aspect of its function, quality, or structure

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-2:2021, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 2: Test processes, 3.12_

---

## product roadmap

> timeline with high-level milestones for a product life cycle, particularly the timeline for productive deployment of the product

_Source: ISO/IEC 26556:2018 Information technology--Software and systems engineering-- Tools and methods for product line organizational management, 3.2_

---

## product safety label

> label on a product that informs of one or more potential hazards and describes the safety precautions or actions required to avoid the hazard (IEC/IEEE 82079-1:2019 Preparation of information for use (instructions for use) of products: Part 1: Principles and general requirements, 3.29)

---

## product scope

> features and functions that characterize a product, service or result (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## product scoping

> subprocess of product line scoping that determines the product roadmap, that is 1) the targeted markets; 2) the product categories that the product line organization should be developing, producing, marketing, and selling; 3) the common and variable features that the products should provide in order to reach the long and short term business objectives of the product line organization, and 4) the schedule for introducing products to markets

_Source: ISO/IEC 26550:2015 Software and systems engineering--Reference model for product line engineering and management, 3.20_

---

## product specification

> document that specifies the design to be instantiated in production copies of a system or component

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## product standard

> standard that defines what constitutes completeness and acceptability of items that are used or produced, formally or informally, during the software engineering process

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## product support

> providing information, assistance, and training to install and make software operational in its intended environment and to distribute improved capabilities to users

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## production

> stage in the life cycle when completed software or documentation is prepared, published, and packaged for distribution (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765a:2011)

---

## production library

> software library containing software approved for current operational use (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## production plan

> description of how domain assets are to be used to develop member products in a product line

---

## production rate

> a measure of the amount of work completed per unit of time, such as user stories or features per week (Software Extension to the PMBOK(R) Guide Fifth Edition)

---

## productivity

> ratio of work product to work effort

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765a:2011_

---

## professional standard

> standard that identifies a profession as a discipline and distinguishes it from other professions

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## professionalism

> degree to which the content of the IT service is based on appropriate education, skill, expertise and qualification (ISO/IEC TS 25011:2017 Information technology--Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)--Service quality models, 3.2.5.2)

---

## profile

> subset of appropriate standards processes and their outcomes, activities, and tasks combined to accomplish a particular function (ISO/IEC 29110-1-2:2024, Systems and software engineering Lifecycle profiles for Very Small Entities (VSEs) Part 1-2: Vocabulary, 3.70)

---

## profile group

> collection of profiles which are related either by composition of processes, e.g. activities and tasks, or by capability level, or both (ISO/IEC 29110-1-2:2024, Systems and software engineering Lifecycle profiles for Very Small Entities (VSEs) Part 1-2: Vocabulary, 3.71)

---

## program

> to write a computer program

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## program construct

> set of one or more procedure parts and a control part which may be implicit (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765i:2020)

---

## program design language (PDL)

> specification language with special constructs and, sometimes, verification protocols, used to develop, analyze, and document a program design (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## Program Evaluation and Review Technique (PERT)

> technique for estimating that applies a weighted average of optimistic, pessimistic, and most likely estimates when there is uncertainty with the individual activity estimates

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765h:2019_

---

## program instruction

> computer instruction in a source program (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## program librarian

> person responsible for establishing, controlling, and maintaining a software development library

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## program listing

> printout or other human readable display of the source and, sometimes, object statements that

---

## program maintenance manual

> document that provides the information necessary to maintain a program

_Source: ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary_

---

## program management

> application of knowledge, skills, and principles to a program to achieve the program objectives and obtain benefits and control not available by managing program components individually (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## program mutation

> computer program that has been purposely altered from the intended version to evaluate the ability of test cases to detect the alteration (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## program network chart

> diagram that shows the relationship between two or more computer programs

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## program specification
