> document that describes the structure and functions of a program in sufficient detail to permit programming and to facilitate maintenance

_Source: ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary_

---

## program status word (PSW)

> computer word that contains information specifying the current status of a computer program

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## program synthesis

> use of software tools to aid in the transformation of a program specification into a program that realizes that specification

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## program-sensitive fault

> fault that causes a failure when some particular sequence of program steps is executed (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## programmable breakpoint

> breakpoint that automatically invokes a previously specified debugging process when initiated (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## programmable counter array

> group of counter modules on a microcontroller unit that increases its timing capabilities

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765d:2015_

---

## programmable pulse generator

> part of a microcontroller unit that produces clock signals, adjustable through programming

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765d:2015_

---

## programmable read-only memory (PROM)

> read-only memory unit without a rewrite or an erase data function, which the user can program only once (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765c:2014)

---

## programmable reload timer (PRT)

> reconfigurable device to restart a function, using decrementing, status control, and reload registers

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765e:2015_

---

## programmable terminal

> user terminal that has built-in data processing capability

_Source: ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary_

---

## programmatic reference point

> reference point at which a programmatic interface can be established to allow access to a function (ISO/IEC 10746-2:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model:

---

## programmer manual

> document that provides the information necessary to develop or modify software for a given computer system (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## programming

> general activity of software development

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## programming language

> language used to express computer programs

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## programming support environment

> integrated collection of software tools accessed via a single command language to provide programming support capabilities throughout the software life cycle (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## programming system

> set of programming languages and the support software (e.g., editors, compilers, linkers) necessary for using these languages with a given computer system

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## progressive elaboration

> iterative process of increasing the level of detail in a project management plan as greater amounts of information and more accurate estimates become available (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## prohibition

> prescription that a particular behavior is not allowed

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-2:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Foundations, 11.2.6_

---

## project

> endeavor with defined start and finish criteria undertaken to create a product or service in accordance with specified resources and requirements (ISO/IEC/IEEE 12207:2026 Systems and software engineering--Software life cycle processes, 3.1.50)

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288:2023 Systems and software engineering--System life cycle processes, 3.33_

---

## project balance

> representation, as a series of cash amounts at regular intervals, of the cumulative to-date value

---

## project brief

> high-level overview of the goals, deliverables, and processes for the project (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## project calendar

> calendar that identifies working days and shifts that are available for scheduled activities (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## project charter

> document issued by the project initiator or sponsor that formally authorizes the existence of a project, and provides the project manager with the authority to apply organizational resources to project activities (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## project control

> activities concerned with monitoring the progress of a project, its direction, quality, and resource utilization, as compared with project plan

_Source: ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary_

---

## project file

> central repository of material pertinent to a project (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## project governance

> framework, functions, and processes that guide project management activities in order to create a unique product, service, or result to meet organizational, strategic, and operational goals (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## project lead

> person who helps the project team to achieve the project objectives, typically by orchestrating the work of the project (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## project life cycle

> series of phases that a project passes through from its start to its completion (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## project management (PM)

> application of knowledge, skills, tools, and techniques to project activities to meet the project requirements (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK®)

> term that that describes the knowledge within the profession of project management (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## project management office (PMO)

> management structure that standardizes the project-related governance processes and facilitates the sharing of resources, methodologies, tools, and techniques (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## project management plan

> document that describes how the project will be executed, monitored and controlled, and closed (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## project management process group

> logical grouping of project management inputs, tools and techniques,

---

## project management software

> applications specifically designed to aid the project management team with planning, monitoring, and controlling the project, including cost estimating, scheduling, collaboration, and risk analysis

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## project management team

> members of the project team who are directly involved in project management activities (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## project manager (PM)

> person assigned by the performing organization to lead the team that is responsible for achieving the project objectives (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## project performance

> derived measure that gives an indication of some attribute associated with how well, how quickly, how effectively, or how efficiently a project is carried out

_Source: ISO/IEC 29155-1:2017 Systems and software engineering--Information technology project performance benchmarking framework--Part 1: Concepts and definitions, 2.8_

---

## project phase

> collection of logically related project activities that culminates in the completion of one or more deliverables (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## project plan

> document that describes the technical and management approach to be followed for a project (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## project planning

> activities concerned with the specification of the components, timing, resources, and procedures of a project

_Source: ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary_

---

## project portfolio

> collection of projects that addresses the strategic objectives of the organization

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 12207:2026 Systems and software engineering--Software life cycle processes, 3.1.38_

---

## project procurement management

> Project procurement management includes the processes necessary to purchase or acquire products, services, or results needed from outside the project team. (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## project resource constraint

> limitation or restraint placed on resource usage, such as what resource skills or disciplines are available and the amount of a given resource available during a specified time frame

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## project risk

> risk related to the management of a project (ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-2:2021, Software and systems

---

## project schedule

> output of a schedule model that presents linked activities with planned dates, durations, milestones, and resources (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## project schedule network diagram

> graphical display of the logical relationships among the project schedule activities (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## project scope

> work performed to deliver a product, service, or result with the specified features and functions (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## project scope statement

> description of the project scope, major deliverables, and exclusions (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## project specification

> specification of the objectives, requirements, and scope of a project and its relations to other projects

_Source: ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary_

---

## project team

> project manager, and, for some projects, the project sponsor, and the group of persons who report either directly or indirectly to the project manager and who are responsible for performing project work as a regular part of their assigned duties, including the staff supporting project management

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## project vision statement

> concise, high-level description of the project that states the purpose and inspires the team to contribute to the project (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## project work performance domain

> performance domain that addresses activities and functions associated with establishing project processes, managing physical resources, and fostering a learning environment (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## projectized organization

> organizational structure in which the project manager has full authority to assign priorities, apply resources, and direct the work of persons assigned to the project

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765h:2019_

---

## prolog breakpoint

> breakpoint that is initiated upon entry into a program or routine (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## PROM

> programmable read-only memory

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765c:2014_

---

## prompt

> symbol or message displayed by a computer system, requesting input from the user of the system

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## proof of concept

> realization of an idea or technology to demonstrate its feasibility

_Source: INCOSE Systems Engineering Handbook, 5th ed._

---

## proof of correctness

> formal technique used to prove mathematically that a computer program satisfies its

---

## property

> responsibility that is an inherent or distinctive characteristic or trait that manifests some aspect of an object's knowledge or behavior

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.151_

---

## property to quantify

> property of a target entity that is related to a quality measure element and which can be quantified by a measurement method (ISO/IEC 25021:2012 Software engineering--Software product Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)--Quality measure elements, 4.11)

---

## property-of-interest

> object whose loss is considered as a negative effect (ISO/IEC/IEEE 15026-1:2019 Systems and software engineering--Systems and software assurance--Part 1: Concepts and vocabulary, 3.4.12)

---

## proposal

> supplier's offer to provide a system or service, usually including benefits, costs, risks, opportunities, and other factors applicable to decisions (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765c:2014)

---

## proposed change

> report of anomaly, required or recommended enhancement from the time an idea is recorded until the disposition by a designated change authority (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765i:2020)

---

## proposition

> observable fact or state of affairs involving one or more entities, of which it is possible to assert or deny that it holds for those entities

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-2:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Foundations, 6.2_

---

## protected

> responsibility that is visible only to the class or the receiving instance of the class (available only within methods of the class or its subclasses) (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.152)

---

## protection

> process to secure content

_Source: ISO/IEC 23643:2020, Software and systems engineering--Capabilities of software safety and security verification tools, 3.12_

---

## protection exception

> exception that occurs when a program attempts to write into a protected area in storage (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## protocol

> set of conventions that govern the interaction of processes, devices, and other components within a system

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## protocol object

> engineering object in a channel, which communicates with other protocol objects in the same channel to achieve interaction between basic engineering objects (possibly in different clusters, possibly in different capsules, possibly in different nodes)

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-3:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Architecture, 8.1.12_

---

## prototype

> preliminary type, form, or instance of a system that serves as a model for later stages or for the final, complete version of the system

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765a:2011_

---

## prototyping

> hardware and software development technique in which a preliminary version of part or all of the hardware or software is developed to permit user feedback, determine feasibility, or investigate timing or other issues in support of the development process (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## provision

> expression in the content of a normative document, that takes the form of a statement, an instruction, a recommendation or a requirement (ISO/IEC 14143-2:2011 Information technology -- Software measurement -- Functional size measurement -- Part 2: Conformity evaluation of software size measurement methods to ISO/IEC 14143-1, 3.8)

---

## PRR

> production readiness review

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-8:2019, Systems and software engineering Life cycle management Part 7: Application of systems engineering on defense programs, 3.2_

---

## PRT

> programmable reload timer

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765e:2015_

---

## PSA

> product support analysis

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-9:2023, Systems and software engineering Life cycle management Part 9: Application of system and software life cycle processes in epidemic prevention and control systems, 3.2_

---

## PSB

> preventive support budget

_Source: IEEE 982:2024, Standard for Measures of the Software Aspects of Dependability, 3.2_

---

## PSCI

> Protocol Support for Computational Interactions

_Source: ISO/IEC 14752:2000 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Protocol support for computational interactions, 4_

---

## pseudo instruction

> source language instruction that provides information or direction to the assembler or compiler and is not translated into a target language instruction (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## pseudo-oracle

> independently derived variant of the test item used to generate results, which are compared with the results of the original test item based on the same test inputs (ISO/IEC TR 29119-11:2020, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 11: Guidelines on the testing of AI-based systems, 3.1.59)

---

## pseudocode

> combination of programming language constructs and natural language used to express a computer program design

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## pseudostatic random access memory (PSRAM)

> dynamic random access memory unit with an automatic refresh circuit on the unit

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765c:2014_

---

## PSP

> product support plan

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-8:2019, Systems and software engineering Life cycle management Part 7: Application of systems engineering on defense programs, 3.2_

---

## PSRAM

> pseudostatic random access memory

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765c:2014_

---

## PSW

> program status word

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## psychometrics

> field of study concerned with the theory and technique for developing valid and reliable psychological measures (ISO/IEC 25022:2016, Systems and software engineering -- Systems and software quality requirements and evaluation (SQuaRE) -- Measurement of quality in use, 4.14)

---

## public

> responsibility that is not hidden

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.153_

---

## publisher

> event source that can be connected to an arbitrary number of event sinks, which subscribe to the publisher event source

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765l:2024_

---

## publishing pipeline

> series of defined processing steps that are connected to transform content from its source format into a final deliverable format

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 26531:2023 Systems and software engineering -- Content management for product lifecycle, user and service management information for users, 3.1.25_

---

## pulse width modulation (PWM)

> control of electrical signals with various cycle and duty ratios (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765d:2015)

---

## purpose of the count

> reason for performing the function point count

_Source: ISO/IEC 20926:2009 Software and systems engineering -- Software measurement -- IFPUG functional size measurement method 2009, 3.45_

---

## PV

> planned value (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## PWM

> pulse width modulation

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765d:2015_

---

## QA

> quality assurance

_Source: IEEE 1012-2024 IEEE Standard for System, Software, and Hardware Verification and Validation, 3.2_

---

## QC

> quality control

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## QE

> quality engineering

_Source: IEEE 730-2014 IEEE Standard for Software Quality Assurance Processes, 3.3_

---

## QFP

> quad flat pack

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765c:2014_

---

## QM

> quality management

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765b:2013_

---

## QM-RM

> quality measurement reference model (ISO/IEC 25020:2019 Systems and software engineering--Systems and software product Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)--Quality measurement framework, 4)

---

## QME

> quality measure element (ISO/IEC 25021:2012 Software engineering--Software product Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)--Quality measure elements, 5)

---

## QMS

> quality management system (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765c:2014)

---

## QOS

> Quality of Service

_Source: ISO/IEC 19793:2015 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Use of UML for ODP system specifications, 4_

---

## QPS

> queries per second

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-9:2023, Systems and software engineering Life cycle management Part 9: Application of system and software life cycle processes in epidemic prevention and control systems, 3.2_

---

## QR

> quality requirements

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 14143-4:2002 Information technology -- Software measurement -- Functional size measurement -- Part 4: Reference model, 4_

---

## QTFF

> QuickTime File Format (ISO/IEC/IEEE 26531:2023 Systems and software engineering -- Content management for product lifecycle, user and service management information for users, 3.2)

---

## quad flat pack (QFP)

> surface mount rectangular circuit package with gull-wing shaped leads extending from each of the four sides (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765c:2014)

---

## qualification

> process of demonstrating whether an entity is capable of fulfilling specified requirements

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 12207:2026 Systems and software engineering--Software life cycle processes, 3.1.51_

---

## qualification body

> entity issuing certificates of qualification

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765h:2019_

---

## qualification scheme

> requirements which, when satisfied, result in the issuance of a qualification

_Source: ISO/IEC 24773-1:2019 Software and systems engineering-Certification of software and systems engineering professionals-Part 1: General requirements, 3.15_

---

## qualification testing

> testing conducted to determine whether a system or component is suitable for operational use

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering--Vocabulary_

---

## qualitative risk analysis

> prioritizing risks for subsequent further analysis or action by assessing and combining their probability of occurrence and impact

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## quality

> ability of a product, service, system, component, or process to meet customer or user needs, expectations, or requirements

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 7052:2023, Software engineering--Controlling frequently occurring risks during development and maintenance of custom software, 3.35_

---

## quality analysis

> analysis of rating results for multiple quality properties to determine the objective score or acceptability for the quality of the target entity (ISO/IEC 25040:2024 Systems and software engineering--Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)--Quality evaluation framework, 3.6)

---

## quality assurance (QA)

> part of quality management focused on providing confidence that quality requirements are fulfilled (ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288:2023 Systems and software engineering--System life cycle processes, 3.34)

---

## quality attribute

> characteristic of software, or a generic term applying to quality factors

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765j:2021_

---

## quality characteristic

> inherent characteristic of a product, process, or system related to a requirement (ISO/IEC/IEEE 12207:2026 Systems and software engineering--Software life cycle processes, 3.1.53)

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288:2023 Systems and software engineering--System life cycle processes, 3.35_

---

## quality control (QC)

> monitoring service performance or product quality, recording results, and recommending necessary changes (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765c:2014)

---

## quality evaluation

> systematic examination of the extent to which an entity is capable of fulfilling specified requirements (ISO/IEC 25040:2024 Systems and software engineering--Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)--Quality evaluation framework, 3.7)

---

## quality in use (measure)

> extent to which the system or product, when it is used in a specified context of use, satisfies or exceeds stakeholders needs to achieve specified beneficial goals or outcomes (ISO/IEC 25019:2023, Systems and software engineering Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) Quality-in-use model, 3.1.15)

---

## quality in use measure

> measure of the degree to which a product or system can be used by specific users to meet their needs to achieve specific goals with effectiveness, efficiency, freedom from risk, satisfaction and content coverage in specific contexts of use (ISO/IEC 25000:2014 Systems and software Engineering--Systems and software product Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) -- Guide to SQuaRE, 4.25) (ISO/IEC 25021:2012 Software engineering--Software product Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)--Quality measure elements, 4.12)

---

## quality management

> coordinated activities to direct and control an organization with regard to quality (ISO/IEC/IEEE 12207:2026 Systems and software engineering--Software life cycle processes, 3.1.55)

---

## quality management plan

> component of the project or program management plan that describes how applicable policies, procedures, and guidelines will be implemented to achieve the quality objectives (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## quality measure

> derived measure that is defined as a measurement function of two or more values of quality measure elements (ISO/IEC 25021:2012 Software engineering--Software product Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)--Quality measure elements, 4.13)

---

## quality measure element (QME)

> measure defined in terms of a property and the measurement method for quantifying it, including optionally the transformation by a mathematical function (ISO/IEC 25000:2014 Systems and software Engineering--Systems and software product Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) -- Guide to SQuaRE, 4.26) (ISO/IEC 25021:2012 Software engineering--Software product Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)--Quality measure elements, 4.14)

---

## quality measure on external property

> measure of the degree to which a system or software product enables its behavior to satisfy stated and implied needs for the system including the software to be used under specified conditions (ISO/IEC 25020:2019 Systems and software engineering--Systems and software product Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)--Quality measurement framework, 3.15)

---

## quality measure on internal property

> measure of the degree to which a set of static attributes of a software product satisfies stated and implied needs for the software product to be used under specified conditions (ISO/IEC 25020:2019 Systems and software engineering--Systems and software product Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)--Quality measurement framework, 3.16)

---

## quality metric

> quantitative measure of the degree to which an item possesses a given quality attribute

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## quality metrics

> description of a project or product attribute and how to measure it (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## quality model

> defined set of characteristics and of relationships between them, which provides a framework for

---

## quality of service

> set of quality requirements on the collective behavior of one or more objects

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746- 2:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Foundations, 11.2.2_

---

## quality policy

> basic principles that should govern the organizations actions as it implements its system for quality management (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## quality property

> property of a target entity that is related to a quality measure element, and which can be quantified by a measurement method (ISO/IEC 25019:2023, Systems and software engineering Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) Quality-in-use model, 3.1.18) (ISO/IEC 25002:2024, Systems and software engineering Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) Quality model overview and usage, 3.22)

---

## quality rating module

> set of quality measures, operational environment, and methods for conducting quality measurements and quality ratings on a specific category of target entities (ISO/IEC 25040:2024 Systems and software engineering--Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)--Quality evaluation framework, 3.8)

---

## quality record report

> report which is generated through dynamic test execution and code analysis to record test results and other output (ISO/IEC 30130:2016 Software engineering --Capabilities of software testing tools, 3.1)

---

## quality report

> project document that includes quality management issues, recommendations for corrective actions, and a summary of findings from quality control activities and may include recommendations for process, project, and product improvements (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## quality requirement

> capability of a product to satisfy the stated and implied needs when used under specific conditions

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 24766:2009 Information technology--Systems and software engineering--Guide for requirements engineering tool capabilities, 3.5_

---

## quality subcharacteristic

> set of one or more quality properties that represent a unique aspect of a quality characteristic (ISO/IEC 25019:2023, Systems and software engineering Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) Quality-in-use model, 3.1.20) (ISO/IEC 25002:2024, Systems and software engineering Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) Quality model overview and usage, 3.24)

---

## quantitative risk analysis

> numerical analysis of the effect on overall project objectives of identified risks

---

## query language

> language used to access information stored in a database (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## queue

> list in which items are appended to the last position of the list and retrieved from the first position of the list

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## quiescing

> process of bringing a device or system to a halt by rejecting new requests for work

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## R&D

> research and development (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-8:2019, Systems and software engineering Life cycle management Part 7: Application of systems engineering on defense programs, 3.2)

---

## R&M

> reliability and maintainability (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-8:2019, Systems and software engineering Life cycle management Part 7: Application of systems engineering on defense programs, 3.2)

---

## RACI

> responsible, accountable, consulted, and informed (ISO/IEC TR 29119-13:2022, Software and systems engineering Software testing Part 13: Using the ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119 series in the testing of biometric systems, 3.2)

---

## RAM

> responsibility assignment matrix (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## RAM-C

> reliability, availability, maintainability, and cost

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-8:2019, Systems and software engineering Life cycle management Part 7: Application of systems engineering on defense programs, 3.2_

---

## RAMT

> reliability, availability, maintainability, testability

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24641:2023. Systems and Software engineering--Methods and tools for model-based systems and software engineering, 3.2_

---

## random failure

> failure whose occurrence is unpredictable except in a probabilistic or statistical sense (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## random testing

> specification-based test design technique based on generating test cases to exercise randomly selected test item inputs

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-4:2021 Software and systems engineering -- Software testing -- Part 4: Test techniques, 3.41_

---

## random-access memory (RAM)

> volatile semiconductor storage device which allows data to be written or accessed in approximately the same amount of time, regardless of the data's physical location (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765c:2014)

---

## rapid prototyping

> type of prototyping in which emphasis is placed on developing prototypes early in the development process to permit early feedback and analysis in support of the development process (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## rate-monotonic algorithm

> real-time scheduling algorithm that assigns higher priorities to tasks with shorter periods

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## rating

> action of mapping the measured value to the appropriate rating level (ISO/IEC 25000:2014 Systems and

---

## rating interval

> time interval of the measurement procedure from the time the SUT reaches a stable state of operation to the time the measurement results are fulfilling the required statistical significance

_Source: ISO/IEC 14756:1999 Information technology -- Measurement and rating of performance of computer-based software systems, 4.13_

---

## rating level

> scale point on an ordinal scale, which is used to categorize a measurement scale (ISO/IEC 25000:2014 Systems and software Engineering--Systems and software product Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) -- Guide to SQuaRE, 4.29)

---

## ratio scale

> scale in which the measurement values have equal distances corresponding to equal quantities of the attribute where the value of zero corresponds to none of the attribute (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## RBAC

> role-based access control

_Source: IEEE 7005 2021, IEEE Standard for Transparent Employer Data Governance, 3.2_

---

## RBS

> risk breakdown structure

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025_

---

## RBT

> risk-based testing

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 29119-13:2022, Software and systems engineering Software testing Part 13: Using the ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119 series in the testing of biometric systems, 3.2_

---

## RC

> recovery complete

_Source: IEEE 982:2024, Standard for Measures of the Software Aspects of Dependability, 3.2_

---

## RDA

> Remote Database Access

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-1:1998 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference model: Overview_

---

## RDBMS

> Relational Database Management System (ISO/IEC 25024:2015 Systems and software engineering - Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) - Measurement of data, 5)

---

## RDF

> Resource Description Framework

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 23026:2023 Systems and software engineering--Engineering and management of websites for systems, software, and services information, 3.2_

---

## RDN

> Relative Distinguished Name

---

## reachability graph

> directed graph of nodes and edges, where the nodes correspond to reachable markings, and the edges correspond to transition occurrences

_Source: ISO/IEC 15909-1:2019 Systems and software engineering--High-level Petri nets--Part 1: Concepts, definitions and graphical notation, 3.30_

---

## reachable marking

> marking of the net that can be reached from the initial marking by the occurrence of transitions

_Source: ISO/IEC 15909-1:2019 Systems and software engineering--High-level Petri nets--Part 1: Concepts, definitions and graphical notation, 3.31_

---

## reactivation

> cloning a cluster following its deactivation

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-3:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Architecture, 8.1.26_

---

## reactive approach

> approach of developing a product line or product variations in response to stated needs or customer requirements

_Source: ISO/IEC 26553:2018 Information technology -- Software and systems engineering-- Tools and methods for product line realization, 3.16_

---

## reactiveness

> degree to which the IT service promptly responds to user requests (ISO/IEC TS 25011:2017 Information technology--Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)--Service quality models, 3.2.6.2)

---

## read

> to access data from a storage device or data medium

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## read arc

> special kind of arc that tests the presence of some tokens in the related place, without consumption

_Source: ISO/IEC 15909-3:2021. Systems and software engineering--High-level Petri nets--Part 3: Extensions and structuring mechanisms, 3.5_

---

## read-only

> property that causes no state changes (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.155)

---

## read-only memory (ROM)

> non-volatile semiconductor storage device, from which data cannot be removed once it is written (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765d:2015)

---

## readability

> ease with which a system's source code can be read and understood, especially at the detailed, statement level

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## reader note

> comment made by a reader about a diagram and placed on the diagram page (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765m:2024, 2.1.97)

---

## reading reference

> data storage entity or record, or interface record from another software or system containing data retrieved in a BFC (ISO/IEC 29881:2010 Information technology--Software and systems engineering--FiSMA 1.1 functional size measurement method, 3.8)

---

## ready

> statement on the required quality attributes a backlog item or other work product meets before it starts a specified activity or task (ISO/IEC 33202:2024, Software and systems engineering Core agile practices, 3.14)

---

## ready to use software product (RUSP)

> software product available to any user, at cost or not, to use without the need to conduct development activities (ISO/IEC 25051:2014 Software engineering -- Software product Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) -- Requirements for quality of Ready to Use Software Product (RUSP) and instructions for testing, 4.1.6)

---

## real address

> address of a storage location in the main storage part of a virtual storage system (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## real storage

> main storage portion of a virtual storage system (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## real type

> data type whose members can assume real numbers as values and can be operated on by real number arithmetic operations, such as addition, subtraction, multiplication, division, and square root (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## real-time

> problem, system, or application that is concurrent and has timing constraints whereby incoming events are processed within a given timeframe

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## real-time clock

> integrated circuit that tracks the current time in human units

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765d:2015_

---

## real-time clock (RTC)

> integrated circuit that tracks the current time in human units (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765d:2015)

---

## real-time operating system (RTOS)

> operating system intended to handle transaction requests immediately upon receipt

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765c:2014_

---

## real-time scheduling theory

> theory for priority-based scheduling of concurrent tasks with hard deadlines (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## real-world object

> entity that exists in a three-dimensional form and, by association, implies similar properties or behavior to software functions

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765k:2022_

---

## realization

> representation of interface responsibilities through specified algorithms and representation properties

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.156_

---

## reasonably foreseeable misuse

> use of a product or system in a way not intended by the supplier, but which can result from readily predictable human behavior (IEC/IEEE 82079-1:2019 Preparation of information for use (instructions for use) of products: Part 1: Principles and general requirements, 3.30)

---

## reasoning technique

> form of artificial intelligence that generates conclusions from available information using logical techniques, such as deduction and induction (ISO/IEC TR 29119-11:2020, Software and systems engineering--

---

## recall

> performance metric used to evaluate a classifier that measures the proportion of actual positives that were predicted correctly (ISO/IEC TR 29119-11:2020, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 11: Guidelines on the testing of AI-based systems, 3.1.61)

---

## receptacle

> operation interface in which a computational component plays a client role

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-3:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Architecture, 7.1.16_

---

## recommendation

> provision that conveys advice or guidance

_Source: ISO/IEC 14143-2:2011 Information technology -- Software measurement -- Functional size measurement -- Part 2: Conformity evaluation of software size measurement methods to ISO/IEC 14143-1, 3.9_

---

## record

> set of related data items treated as a unit (ISO/IEC/IEEE 15289:2019 Systems and software engineering-- Content of life-cycle information items (documentation), 5.20)

---

## record element type (RET)

> user-recognizable sub-group of data element types within a data function

_Source: ISO/IEC 20926:2009 Software and systems engineering -- Software measurement -- IFPUG functional size measurement method 2009, 3.46_

---

## records management system

> processes, related control functions, and tools that identify, store, retrieve, and retain data

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765h:2019_

---

## recoverability

> capability of a product in the event of an interruption or a failure to recover the data directly affected and re-establish the desired state of the system (ISO/IEC 25010:2023, Systems and software engineering-- Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)--Product quality model, 3.5.4)

---

## recovery

> restoration of a system, program, database, or other system resource to a state in which it can perform required functions

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## recursion

> process of defining or generating a process or data structure in terms of itself

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## recursive

> pertaining to a software module that calls itself

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## redundancy

> in fault tolerance, the presence of auxiliary components in a system to perform the same or similar functions as other elements for the purpose of preventing or recovering from failures (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## reengineering

> examination and alteration of software to reconstitute it in a new form, including the subsequent implementation of the new form (ISO/IEC TR 19759:2016 Software Engineering -- Guide to the Software Engineering Body of Knowledge (SWEBOK), 5.4.2)

---

## reentrant

> pertaining to a software module that can be entered as part of one process while also in execution as part of another process and still achieve the desired results (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## reentry point

> place in a software module at which the module is reentered following a call to another module (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## refactor

> to restructure software code without altering its behavior for the purpose of improving quality attributes, easing future extension or adaptation, or adhering to an architectural style (Software Extension to the PMBOK(R) Guide Fifth Edition)

---

## reference architecture

> core architecture that captures the high-level architecture concept of domain architecture and application architecture

_Source: ISO/IEC 26552:2019 Software and systems engineering--Tools and methods for product line architecture design, 3.9_

---

## reference body of knowledge

> body of knowledge that is used for the comparison of a particular body of knowledge associated with a certification scheme

_Source: ISO/IEC 24773-1:2019 Software and systems engineering-Certification of software and systems engineering professionals-Part 1: General requirements, 3.12_

---

## reference framework

> structure for understanding significant relationships among the entities of some environment, and for the development of consistent standards or specifications supporting that environment

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24641:2023. Systems and Software engineering--Methods and tools for model-based systems and software engineering, 3.1.26_

---

## reference FSM method

> an FSM method to be used for comparison reasons when verifying the functional size measurement results

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 14143-4:2002 Information technology -- Software measurement -- Functional size measurement -- Part 4: Reference model, 3.3_

---

## reference information

> information that is intended to provide quick access to specific details for users who are generally familiar with the product's functions

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 26514:2022, Systems and software engineering -- Design and development of information for users, 3.1.43_

---

## reference mode

> usage mode that is intended to provide quick access to specific information for software users who are generally familiar with the software functions

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 26514:2022, Systems and software engineering -- Design and development of information for users, 4.42_

---

## reference node

> node of a Petri net that is a representative of another node, possibly defined on another page of the net graph

_Source: ISO/IEC 15909-2:2011 Software and system engineering--High-level Petri nets--Part 2: Transfer format, 4.1.14_

---

## reference place

> reference node that represents a place and refers to either another reference place or to a place

_Source: ISO/IEC 15909-2:2011 Software and system engineering--High-level Petri nets--Part 2: Transfer format, 4.1.15_

---

## reference point

> interaction point defined in an architecture for selection as a conformance point in a specification which is compliant with that architecture

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-2:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Foundations, 10.6_

---

## reference transition

> reference node that represents a transition and refers to either another reference transition to a transition

_Source: ISO/IEC 15909-2:2011 Software and system engineering--High-level Petri nets--Part 2: Transfer format, 4.1.16_

---

## reference user requirement collection (RUR Collection)

> a subset of RUR which is selected to match the purpose in a specific evaluation

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 14143-4:2002 Information technology -- Software measurement -- Functional size measurement -- Part 4: Reference model, 3.5_

---

## reference user requirements (RUR)

> a standard set of user requirements which conforms to the requirements

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 14143-4:2002 Information technology -- Software measurement -- Functional size measurement -- Part 4: Reference model, 3.4_

---

## referential integrity

> guarantee that a reference refers to an object that exists

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.157_

---

## refinement

> process of transforming one specification into a more detailed specification

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-2:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Foundations, 9.6_

---

## reflective construct

> construct that is viewed as the cause of measures in the relationship between a construct and its measures

_Source: ISO/IEC 33003:2015 Information technology--Process assessment--Requirements for process measurement frameworks, 3.13_

---

## reflexive

> in a relationship, the condition when the same data object plays both (binary or many (n-ary)) roles (ISO/IEC 15476-4:2005 Information technology--CDIF semantic metamodel--Part 4: Data models, 6.5)

---

## reflexive ancestor (of a class)

> class itself or any of its generic ancestors (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.158)

---

## refresh

> method to keep data in volatile memory by rewriting the data before it disappears from the memory (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765c:2014)

---

## Regex

> regular expression

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 26531:2023 Systems and software engineering -- Content management for product lifecycle, user and service management information for users, 3.1.26_

---

## regid

> registration identifier

_Source: ISO/IEC 19770-5:2015 Information technology--IT asset management--Overview and vocabulary, 3.27_

---

## register

> written record of regular entries for evolving aspects of a project, such as risks, stakeholders, or defects (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## register bank

> group of registers in a microprocessor chip

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765e:2015_

---

## registration identifier

> unique identifier for an entity (ISO/IEC 19770-5:2015 Information technology--IT asset management--Overview and vocabulary, 3.27)

---

## registry

> book or system for keeping an official list or record of work products and the associated information items (ISO/IEC/IEEE 42020:2019 Software, systems and enterprise -- Architecture processes, 3.18)

---

## regression

> machine learning function that results in a numerical or continuous output value for a given input

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 29119-11:2020, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 11: Guidelines on the testing of AI-based systems, 3.1.62_

---

## regression analysis

> analytic technique where a series of input variables are examined in relation to their corresponding output results in order to develop a mathematical or statistical relationship (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## regression test

> retesting to detect faults introduced by modification

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## regression testing

> testing performed following modifications to a test item or to its operational environment, to identify whether failures in unmodified parts of the test item occur (ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-1:2022, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 1: General concepts, 3.64)

---

## regular expression (Regex)

> string of characters that allows patterns to be used to match search results (ISO/IEC/IEEE 26531:2023 Systems and software engineering -- Content management for product lifecycle, user and service management information for users, 3.1.26)

---

## regulation

> requirement imposed by a governmental body. These requirements can establish product, process or service characteristics, including applicable administrative provisions that have government-mandated compliance (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## regulatory context

> laws, regulations, or any other requirements within the jurisdictions where the system can operate

_Source: IEEE 7002:2022, IEEE Standard for Data Privacy Process, 3.1_

---

## regulatory standard

> standard promulgated by a regulatory agency

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 29119-11:2020, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 11: Guidelines on the testing of AI-based systems, 3.1.63_

---

## reinforcement learning

> task of building a ML model using a process of trial and reward to achieve an objective (ISO/IEC TR 29119-11:2020, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 11: Guidelines on the testing of AI-based systems, 3.1.64)

---

## relation

> set of relationships of the same relationship type

_Source: ISO/IEC 14769:2001 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Type Repository Function, 3.2.3_

---

## relational database management system

> management system for relational database (ISO/IEC 25024:2015 Systems and software engineering - Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) - Measurement of data, 4.34)

---

## relationship

> real-world association among one or more entities

_Source: ISO/IEC 15474-1:2002 Information technology -- CDIF framework -- Part 1: Overview, 4.2_

---

## relationship instance

> association of specific instances of the related classes

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.160_

---

## relationship manager (RM)

> role that develops and manages interactions with customers or suppliers (ISO/IEC 29110-1-2:2024, Systems and software engineering Lifecycle profiles for Very Small Entities (VSEs) Part 1-2: Vocabulary, 3.75)

---

## relationship name

> verb or verb phrase that reflects the meaning of the relationship expressed between the two entities shown on the diagram on which the name appears (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.161)

---

## relationship type

> type of relationship which expresses the number and type of the roles

_Source: ISO/IEC 14769:2001 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Type Repository Function, 3.2.2_

---

## relative address

> address that is adjusted by the addition of an offset to determine the address of the storage location to be accessed (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## relative chain frequency

> relative frequency of using the l-th chain type by an emulated user of the i-th type

_Source: ISO/IEC 14756:1999 Information technology -- Measurement and rating of performance of computer-based software systems, 4.14_

---

## relative estimating

> method for creating estimates that are derived from performing a comparison against a similar body of work, taking effort, complexity, and uncertainty into consideration (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## relative time

> tick from a source that increments it according to a reference activity other than absolute time (IEEE 982:2024, Standard for Measures of the Software Aspects of Dependability, 3.1)

---

## RELAX NG

> regular language description for XML/New Generation

_Source: ISO/IEC 15909-2:2011 Software and system engineering--High-level Petri nets--Part 2: Transfer format, 4.2.6_

---

## release

> particular version of a configuration item that is made available for a specific purpose (IEEE 828-2012 IEEE Standard for Configuration Management in Systems and Software Engineering, 2.12)

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 12207:2026 Systems and software engineering--Software life cycle processes, 3.1.43_

---

## release backlog

> subset of product backlog planned for the next release

_Source: ISO/IEC 33202:2024, Software and systems engineering Core agile practices, 3.24_

---

## release engineer

> person responsible for coordinating development toward a release (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## release map

> a displayed forecast of when software features will be released and how they will be grouped into releases (Software Extension to the PMBOK(R) Guide Fifth Edition)

---

## release package

> collection of configuration items and other artifacts necessary to install or update a software system (IEEE 982:2024, Standard for Measures of the Software Aspects of Dependability, 3.1)

---

## release plan

> plan that describes what portions of system functionality will be implemented in which releases and

---

## release planning

> process of identifying a high-level plan for releasing or transitioning a product, deliverable, or increment of value (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## relevant stakeholder

> stakeholder that is identified for involvement in specified activities and is included in a plan

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## reliability

> capability of a product to perform specified functions under specified conditions for a specified period of time without interruptions and failures (ISO/IEC 25010:2023, Systems and software engineering--Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)--Product quality model, 3.5)

---

## reliability class

> category for subsetting system requirements and behaviors according to 1) the canonical quality characteristics of safety, security, functionality, performance, or utilization, 2) the universal reliability class (no subset of capability types), or 3) one or more system-specific reliability classes (IEEE 982:2024, Standard for Measures of the Software Aspects of Dependability, 3.1)

---

## reliability growth

> improvement in reliability that results from correction of faults

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## reliability testing

> type of testing conducted to evaluate the ability of a test item to perform its required functions, including evaluating the frequency with which failures occur, when it is used under stated conditions for a specified period of time

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-1:2022, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 1: General concepts, 3.66_

---

## relocatable

> pertaining to code that can be loaded into any part of main memory (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017

---

## relocatable address

> address that is to be adjusted by the loader when the computer program containing the address is loaded into memory (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## relocatable code

> code containing addresses that are to be adjusted by the loader to reflect the storage locations into which the code is loaded (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## relocate

> to move machine code from one portion of main memory to another and to adjust the addresses so that the code can be executed in its new location

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## relocating assembler

> assembler that produces relocatable code (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## relocating loader

> loader that reads relocatable code into main memory and adjusts the addresses in the code to reflect the storage locations into which the code has been loaded (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## relocation dictionary

> part of an object module or load module that identifies the addresses that are adjusted when a relocation occurs

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## relocation transparency

> distribution transparency which masks relocation of an interface from other interfaces bound to it

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-3:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Architecture, 4.4.1.5_

---

## relocator

> object that manages a repository of locations for interfaces, including locations of management functions for the cluster supporting those interfaces

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-3:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Architecture, 14.3.1.1_

---

## remote job entry (RJE)

> submission of jobs through a remote input device connected to a computer through a data link (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## remote terminal emulator (RTE)

> data processing system realizing a set of emulated users

_Source: ISO/IEC 14756:1999 Information technology -- Measurement and rating of performance of computer-based software systems, 4.15_

---

## repair

> corrective, emergency, or preventative support action undertaken to mitigate a system fault or a failure

_Source: IEEE 982:2024, Standard for Measures of the Software Aspects of Dependability, 3.1_

---

## reparent

> to move the changes developed under one branch into another (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## repeatability (of results of measurements)

> closeness of the agreement between the results of successive measurements of the same measurand carried out under the same conditions of measurement (ISO/IEC TR 14143-

---

## repetitive addressing

> method of implied addressing in which the operation field of a computer instruction is understood to address the operands of the last instruction executed (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## replaceability

> capability of a product to replace another specified product for the same purpose in the same environment (ISO/IEC 25010:2023, Systems and software engineering--Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)--Product quality model, 3.8.4)

---

## replication

> copying a software product from one medium to another

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 90003:2018 Software engineering -- Guidelines for the application of ISO 9001:2015 to computer software, 3.13_

---

## replication schema

> specification of constraints on the replication of an object including both constraints on the availability of the object and constraints on the performance of the object

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-3:2009 Information technology - - Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Architecture, 16.7.1.1_

---

## replication transparency

> distribution transparency which masks the use of a group of mutually behaviorally compatible objects to support an interface (ISO/IEC 10746-3:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Architecture, 4.4.1.6)

---

## repo bloat

> changes recorded in the repository that do not contribute anything useful to the project's history (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## repo surgery

> changes made directly to the version control system's repository, bypassing the system's commands (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## report

> information item that describes the results of activities such as investigations, observations, assessments, or tests (ISO/IEC/IEEE 15289:2019 Systems and software engineering--Content of life-cycle information items (documentation), 5.21)

---

## report performance

> [Process] the process of collecting and distributing performance information, including status reports, progress measurements, and forecasts (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## report standard

> standard that describes the characteristics of describing results of engineering and management

---

## reporting system

> facilities, processes and procedures used to generate or consolidate reports from one or more information management systems and distribute reports to stakeholders

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765h:2019_

---

## repository

> organized and persistent data storage that allows data retrieval

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 26511:2018 Systems and software engineering--Requirements for managers of information for users of systems, software, and services, 3.1.24_

---

## repository owner

> person or organization that owns and maintains a benchmarking repository (ISO/IEC 29155- 2:2013 Systems and software engineering--Information technology project performance benchmarking framework--Part 2: Requirements for benchmarking, 3.2)

---

## representation

> logical portrayal of a physical, operational, or conceptual image or situation

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765e:2015_

---

## representation property

> property on which an algorithm operates

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.163_

---

## representation standard

> standard that describes the characteristics of portraying aspects of an engineering or management product

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## reproducibility (of results of measurements)

> closeness of the agreement between the results of measurements of the same measurand carried out under changed conditions of measurement (ISO/IEC TR 14143-3:2003 Information technology -- Software measurement -- Functional size measurement -- Part 3: Verification of functional size measurement methods, 3.9) (ISO/IEC 25021:2012 Software engineering--Software product Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)--Quality measure elements, 4.16)

---

## request

> a message sent from one object (the sender) to another object (the receiver), directing the receiver to fulfill one of its responsibilities

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.164_

---

## request for change

> proposal for a change to be made to a system, service, component, or the service management system

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765c:2014_

---

## request for proposal (RFP)

> document used by the acquirer as the means to announce its intention to potential bidders to acquire a specified system, software product, or software service

---

## required inputs

> set of items necessary to perform the minimum verification and validation (V&V) tasks mandated within any life cycle activity

_Source: IEEE 1012-2024 IEEE Standard for System, Software, and Hardware Verification and Validation, 3.1_

---

## required outputs

> set of items produced as a result of performing the minimum verification and validation tasks mandated within any life cycle activity

_Source: IEEE 1012-2024 IEEE Standard for System, Software, and Hardware Verification and Validation, 3.1_

---

## requirement

> statement that translates or expresses a need and its associated constraints and conditions (ISO/IEC/IEEE 12207:2026 Systems and software engineering--Software life cycle processes, 4.1.31)

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288:2023 Systems and software engineering--System life cycle processes, 3.36_

---

## requirement standard

> a standard that describes the characteristics of a requirements specification

---

## requirements allocation

> assignment or budgeting of top-level functional or nonfunctional requirements among the lower-level partitioned functions for accomplishment (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary)

---

## requirements analysis

> process of studying user needs to arrive at a definition of system, hardware, or software requirements

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## requirements attributes

> set of properties associated with requirements

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 24766:2009 Information technology--Systems and software engineering--Guide for requirements engineering tool capabilities, 3.7_

---

## requirements derivation

> changing or translation of a requirement through analysis into a form that is suitable for low-level analysis or design

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## requirements document

> document containing any combination of requirements or regulations to be met by a ready to use software product (RUSP) (ISO/IEC 25051:2014 Software engineering -- Software product Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) -- Requirements for quality of Ready to Use Software Product (RUSP) and instructions for testing, 4.1.13)

---

## requirements documentation

> record of product requirements and other product information, along with whatever is recorded to manage it (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## requirements elicitation

> use of systematic techniques, such as prototyping and structured surveys, to proactively identify and document customer and end-user needs

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018 Systems and software engineering-Life cycle processes-Requirements engineering, 3.1.20_

---

## requirements engineering

> interdisciplinary function that mediates between the domains of the acquirer and supplier to establish and maintain the requirements to be met by the system, software or service of interest (ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018 Systems and software engineering-Life cycle processes-Requirements engineering, 4.1.19)

---

## requirements flow-down

> systematic decomposition of system requirements into allocated and derived requirements, appropriately assigned to low-level functional components

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## requirements management

> activities that help ensure requirements are identified, documented, maintained, communicated and traced throughout the life cycle of a system, product, or service

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018 Systems and software engineering-Life cycle processes-Requirements engineering, 4.1.20_

---

## requirements management plan

> component of the project or program management plan that describes how requirements will be analyzed, documented, and managed (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## requirements partitioning

> separation or decomposing of a top-level requirement or design into successively lower-level detailed requirements or design (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## requirements phase

> period of time in the software life cycle during which the requirements for a software product are defined and documented

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## requirements review

> process or meeting during which the requirements for a system, hardware item, or software item are presented to project personnel, managers, users, customers, or other interested parties for comment or approval (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## requirements specification

> document that specifies the requirements for a system or component (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## requirements specification language

> specification language with special constructs and, sometimes, verification protocols, used to develop, analyze, and document hardware or software requirements (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## requirements traceability

> identification and documentation of the derivation path (upward) and allocation/ flow- down path (downward) of requirements in the requirements set

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018 Systems and software engineering-Life cycle processes-Requirements engineering, 3.1.23_

---

## requirements traceability matrix (RTM)

> structured information artifact that links requirements to their higher-level requirements or needs or to lower-level implementation

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018 Systems and software engineering-Life cycle processes-Requirements engineering, 3.1.24_

---

## requirements traceability tool

> software development tool that establishes a traceability among itemized software requirements specifications, design elements, code elements, and test cases (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## requirements validation

> confirmation that requirements (individually and as a set) define the right system as intended by the stakeholders (ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018 Systems and software engineering-Life cycle processes- Requirements engineering, 4.1.22)

---

## requirements verification

> confirmation by examination that requirements (individually and as a set) are well formed (ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018 Systems and software engineering-Life cycle processes-Requirements engineering, 4.1.23)

---

## requirements-based testing

> specification-based test case design technique based on exercising atomic requirements

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-4:2021 Software and systems engineering -- Software testing -- Part 4: Test techniques, 3.42_

---

## reseller

> organization that purchases goods or services with an intention of selling them to another customer and possibly supporting them

_Source: ISO/IEC 19770-5:2015 Information technology--IT asset management--Overview and vocabulary, 3.29_

---

## reserve

> provision in the project management plan to mitigate cost and/or schedule risk. Often used with a modifier (e.g., management reserve, contingency reserve) to provide further detail on what types of risk are meant to be mitigated (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## reserve analysis

> method used to evaluate the amount of risk on the project and the amount of schedule and budget reserve to determine whether the reserve is sufficient for the remaining risk (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## reserved word

> word in a programming language, of which the meaning is fixed by the rules of that language and which, in certain or all contexts, cannot be used by the programmer for any purpose other than its intended one

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## reset

> to set a variable, register, or other storage location back to a prescribed state (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## reset arc

> special kind of arc that empties an input place

_Source: ISO/IEC 15909-3:2021. Systems and software engineering--High-level Petri nets--Part 3: Extensions and structuring mechanisms, 3.6_

---

## residual control

> microprogramming technique in which the meaning of a field in a microinstruction depends on the value in an auxiliary register (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## residual risk

> risk remaining after risk treatment (ISO/IEC/IEEE 15026-1:2019 Systems and software engineering--

---

## resilience

> degree to which a service recovers its operational condition quickly after a failure occurs (ISO/IEC TS 25052-1:2022, Systems and software engineering--Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE): cloud services--Part 1: Quality model, 3.1.4.2)

---

## resistance

> capability of a product to sustain operations while under attack from a malicious actor (ISO/IEC 25010:2023, Systems and software engineering--Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)- -Product quality model, 3.6.6)

---

## resource

> asset that is utilized or consumed during the execution of a process (ISO/IEC/IEEE 12207:2026 Systems and software engineering--Software life cycle processes, 3.1.45)

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288:2023 Systems and software engineering--System life cycle processes, 3.37_

---

## resource allocation

> allocation (partitioning) of responsibility to different organizational units

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## resource breakdown structure (RBS)

> hierarchical representation of resources by category and type (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## resource leveling

> technique in which start and finish dates are adjusted based on resource constraints with the goal of balancing demand for resources with the available supply (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765h:2019)

---

## resource management plan

> component of the project management plan that describes how project resources are acquired, allocated, monitored, and controlled (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## resource monitor task

> task ensuring sequential access to a resource

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## resource pooling

> feature where physical or virtual resources can be aggregated to provide a service to one or more service customers (ISO/IEC TS 25052-1:2022, Systems and software engineering--Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE): cloud services--Part 1: Quality model, 3.1.1.2)

---

## resource utilization

> capability of a product to use no more than the specified amount of resources to perform its function under specified conditions (ISO/IEC 25010:2023, Systems and software engineering--Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)--Product quality model, 3.2.2)

---

## resource utilization measurement (RUM)

> structure that provides information about resources associated with an IT asset in order to facilitate its management (ISO/IEC 19770-4:2017 Information technology -- IT asset management -- Part 4: Resource utilization measurement, 3.8)

---

## resource-limited schedule

> project schedule whose schedule activity, scheduled start dates and scheduled finish dates reflect expected resource availability

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary_

---

## respecialize

> change by an instance from being an instance of its current subclass to being an instance of one of the other subclasses in its current cluster (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.165)

---

## responding object

> object taking part in a communication, which is not the initiating object

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746- 2:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Foundations, 13.4.2_

---

## response time

> elapsed time between the end of an inquiry or command to an interactive computer system and the beginning of the system's response (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## responsibility

> ability to give account to somebody or some organization for ones actions

_Source: IEEE 7005 2021, IEEE Standard for Transparent Employer Data Governance, 3.1_

---

## responsibility assignment matrix (RAM)

> grid that shows the project resources assigned to each work package (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## responsive web design (RWD)

> method for web page construction to detect the user's screen size and orientation and dynamically change the layout accordingly

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 23026:2023 Systems and software engineering- -Engineering and management of websites for systems, software, and services information, 3.1.20_

---

## responsiveness

> degree to which an IT service responds and provides outcomes in a prompt and timely way (ISO/IEC TS 25011:2017 Information technology--Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)--Service quality models, 3.2.6)

---

## restart
