_Source: IEEE 730-2014 IEEE Standard for Software Quality Assurance Processes, 3.3_

---

## SLCP

> softwarel life cycle processes

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## SLI

> service level indicator

_Source: IEEE 2675-2021, IEEE Standard for DevOps: Building Reliable and Secure Systems Including Application Build, Package, and Deployment, 3.2_

---

## slice

> subset of a downtime span

_Source: IEEE 982:2024, Standard for Measures of the Software Aspects of Dependability, 3.1_

---

## SLO

> service level objective

_Source: IEEE 2675-2021, IEEE Standard for DevOps: Building Reliable and Secure Systems Including Application Build, Package, and Deployment, 3.2_

---

## SLOC

> source lines of code, the number of lines of programming language code in a program before compilation

_Source: ISO/IEC 20968:2002 Software engineering -- Mk II Function Point Analysis -- Counting Practices Manual, 10_

---

## slush

> preparation for a feature or code freeze (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-

---

## SM

> service manager (ISO/IEC 29110-1-2:2024, Systems and software engineering Lifecycle profiles for Very Small Entities (VSEs) Part 1-2: Vocabulary, 3.91)

---

## small and medium enterprise

> enterprise with fewer than 250 persons employed (ISO/IEC 29110-1-2:2024, Systems and software engineering Lifecycle profiles for Very Small Entities (VSEs) Part 1-2: Vocabulary, 3.92)

---

## small outline integrated circuit (SOIC)

> surface-mounted integrated circuit (chip) package with gull-wing pins extending outward

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765d:2015_

---

## small outline package (SOP)

> rectangular surface mount integrated circuit unit with gull-wing leads on the two long sides

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765e:2015_

---

## SMART

> specific, measurable, achievable, relevant, and timely

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765l:2024_

---

## SME

> subject-matter expert

_Source: IEEE 7005 2021, IEEE Standard for Transparent Employer Data Governance, 3.2_

---

## SMS

> service management system

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765i:2020_

---

## SN

> symmetric net

_Source: ISO/IEC 15909-2:2011 Software and system engineering--High-level Petri nets--Part 2: Transfer format, 4.2.7_

---

## SNAP

> software non-functional assessment process

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 32430:2025, Software engineering Software non-functional size measurement, 3.2_

---

## snapshot dump

> dynamic dump of the contents of one or more specified storage areas (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## snoop

> technique for monitoring bus performance in multi-layer memory cache

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765c:2014_

---

## SOA

> service-oriented architecture

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 32430:2025, Software engineering Software non-functional size measurement, 3.2_

---

## SoC

> system on a chip (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765d:2015)

---

## social responsibility

> obligation to wider society to respect the values reigning within it and to act in line with the organizations values, including legal, ethical, environmental, and financial responsibilities

_Source: IEEE 7000:2021, IEEE Standard Model Process for Addressing Ethical Concerns during System Design, 3.1_

---

## societal and ethical risk mitigation

> degree to which an AI system mitigates potential risk to society (ISO/IEC 25059:2023, Software engineering Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) Quality model for AI systems, 3.3.1)

---

## society

> people in general, living together in communities or in geopolitical defined areas (ISO/IEC 25019:2023, Systems and software engineering Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) Quality-in- use model, 3.1.23)

---

## soft copy image

> non-permanent output of information in audio or visual format

_Source: ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary_

---

## soft failure

> failure that permits continued operation of a system with partial operational capability (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## software

> computer programs, procedures and possibly associated documentation and data pertaining to the operation of a computer system

_Source: IEEE 828-2012 IEEE Standard for Configuration Management in Systems and Software Engineering, 2.1_

---

## software acquisition

> process that begins with the decision to obtain a software product or service with the outcome of acceptance of the software

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 41062:2024, Software engineering Life cycle processes Software acquisition, 3.1.17_

---

## software acquisition process

> period of time that begins with the decision to acquire a software product and ends when the product is no longer available for use

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-- Vocabulary_

---

## software agent

> digital entity that perceives its environment and takes actions that maximize its chance of successfully achieving its goals

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 29119-11:2020, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 11: Guidelines on the testing of AI-based systems, 3.1.73_

---

## software artifact

> tangible machine-readable unit or element created during software development (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765l:2024)

---

## software as a service (SaaS)

> access to resources from client devices through thin client interface or program interface (IEEE 2675-2021, IEEE Standard for DevOps: Building Reliable and Secure Systems Including Application Build, Package, and Deployment, 3.1)

---

## software asset

> software that has potential or actual value to an organization

_Source: ISO/IEC 19770-1:2017 Information technology -- IT asset management -- Part 1: IT asset management systems--Requirements, 3.50_

---

## software asset management (SAM)

> control and protection of software and related assets within an organization, and control and protection of information about related assets which are needed in order to control and protect software assets

_Source: ISO/IEC 19770-5:2015 Information technology--IT asset management--Overview and vocabulary, 3.35_

---

## software baseline

> set (one or more) of software configuration items formally designated and fixed at a specific time during the software life cycle

_Source: IEEE 828-2012 IEEE Standard for Configuration Management in Systems and Software Engineering, 2.1_

---

## software characteristic

> inherent, possibly accidental, trait, quality, or property of software

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering--Vocabulary_

---

## software component (SC)

> entity with discrete structure, such as an assembly or software module, within a system considered at a particular level of analysis (ISO/IEC 19770-5:2015 Information technology--IT asset management- -Overview and vocabulary, 3.36)

---

## software configuration item (SCI)

> software entity that has been established as a configuration item (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## software configuration management (SCM)

> process of applying configuration management throughout the software life cycle to help ensure the completeness and correctness of software configuration items (SCI)

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765i:2020_

---

## software consumer

> entity that uses an entitlement of a software package

_Source: ISO/IEC 19770-5:2015 Information technology--IT asset management--Overview and vocabulary, 3.37_

---

## software correction

> change that addresses and implements problem resolutions to recover gaps and to make software operational enough to meet defined operational requirements (ISO/IEC/IEEE 14764:2021, Software engineering -- Software life cycle processes -- Maintenance, 3.1.3)

---

## software creator

> person or organization that creates a software product or package (ISO/IEC 19770-5:2015 Information technology--IT asset management--Overview and vocabulary, 3.38)

---

## software design

> use of scientific principles, technical information, and imagination in the definition of a software system to perform pre-specified functions with maximum economy and efficiency

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## software design audit

> review of a software product to determine compliance with requirements, standards, and contractual documents

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## software design concept

> fundamental idea (such as information hiding) that can be applied to designing a system

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## software design description (SDD)

> representation of software created to facilitate analysis, planning, implementation, and decision-making (IEEE 1012-2024 IEEE Standard for System, Software, and Hardware Verification and Validation, 3.1.28)

---

## software design notation

> means of describing a software design (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## software design verification

> evaluation of a design to determine correctness with respect to stated requirements, conformance to design standards, system efficiency, and other criteria

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## software developer

> person who creates software (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765e:2015)

---

## software development cycle

> period of time that begins with the decision to develop a software product and ends when the software is delivered (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## software development environment

> facilities, hardware, software, procedures, and documentation needed to perform software development (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-5:2017 Systems and software engineering--Life cycle management--Part 5: Software development planning, 3.14)

---

## software development file (SDF)

> collection of material pertinent to the development of a given software unit or set of related units (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## software development library

> software library containing computer readable and human readable information

---

## software development plan (SDP)

> information item that describes the technical approach to be followed for a software development effort (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-5:2017 Systems and software engineering--Life cycle management-- Part 5: Software development planning, 3.15)

---

## software development process

> process by which user needs are translated into a software product (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## software diversity

> software development technique in which two or more functionally identical variants of a program are developed from the same specification by different programmers or programming teams with the intent of providing error detection, increased reliability, additional documentation, or reduced probability that programming or compiler errors influence the end results

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## software element

> system element that is software

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 12207:2026 Systems and software engineering--Software life cycle processes, 3.1.51_

---

## software engineering

> systematic application of scientific and technological knowledge, methods, and experience to the design, implementation, testing, and documentation of software (ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary)

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-5:2017 Systems and software engineering--Life cycle management--Part 5: Software development planning, 3.16_

---

## software engineering environment (SEE)

> environment that provides automated system context services and software-specific services for the engineering of software systems and related domains, such as project management and process management

_Source: ISO/IEC 15940:2013 Systems and software engineering--Software Engineering Environment Services, 2.7_

---

## software entitlement

> software license use rights as defined through agreements between a software licensor and a software consumer (ISO/IEC 19770-5:2015 Information technology--IT asset management--Overview and vocabulary, 3.39)

---

## software entitlement reconciliation

> process of comparing software entitlements owned with those required (granted and deployed), usually to determine compliance with software license agreements

_Source: ISO/IEC 19770-3:2016 Information technology--IT asset management--Part 3: Entitlement schema, 3.1.30_

---

## software feature

> software characteristic specified or implied by requirements documentation

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering--Vocabulary_

---

## software function

> implementation of an algorithm in the software with which the end user or the software can perform part or all of a work task (ISO/IEC 25051:2014 Software engineering -- Software product Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) -- Requirements for quality of Ready to Use Software Product (RUSP) and instructions for testing, 4.1.14)

---

## software hazard

> software condition that is a prerequisite to an accident (IEEE 1228-1994 (R2002) IEEE Standard for Software Safety Plans, 3.1.5)

---

## software identification tag

> information structure containing identification information about a software configuration item

_Source: ISO/IEC 19770-6:2024, Information technology IT asset management Part 6: Hardware identification tag, 3.1.6_

---

## software item

> aggregation of software, such as a computer program or database, that satisfies an end use function and is designated for specification, qualification testing, interfacing, configuration management, or other purposes

_Source: IEEE 828-2012 IEEE Standard for Configuration Management in Systems and Software Engineering, 2.1_

---

## software library

> controlled collection of software and related documentation designed to aid in software development, use, or maintenance (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## software license

> legal rights to use software in accordance with terms and conditions specified by the software licensor (ISO/IEC 19770-5:2015 Information technology--IT asset management--Overview and vocabulary, 3.41)

---

## software licensee

> person or organization granted a license to use a specific software product

_Source: ISO/IEC 19770- 3:2016 Information technology--IT asset management--Part 3: Entitlement schema, 3.1.33_

---

## software licensor

> person or organization who owns or holds the rights to issue a software license for a specific software package

_Source: ISO/IEC 19770-5:2015 Information technology--IT asset management--Overview and vocabulary, 3.43_

---

## software life cycle (SLC)

> project-specific sequence of activities that is created by mapping the activities of a standard onto a selected software life cycle model (SLCM)

_Source: IEEE 730-2014 IEEE Standard for Software Quality Assurance Processes, 3.2_

---

## software maintenance

> totality of activities required to provide support to a software system

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 14764:2021, Software engineering -- Software life cycle processes -- Maintenance, 3.1.12_

---

## software monitor

> software tool that executes concurrently with another program and provides detailed information about the execution of the other program (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary)

---

## software non-functional assessment process (SNAP) category

> group of components, processes or activities that are used to measure the non-functional size of the software (ISO/IEC/IEEE 32430:2025, Software engineering Software non-functional size measurement, 3.1.34)

---

## Software Non-functional Assessment Process (SNAP) counting unit (SCU)

> component, process or activity, in which non-functional size is measured (ISO/IEC/IEEE 32430:2025, Software engineering Software non- functional size measurement, 3.1.35)

---

## Software Non-functional Assessment Process (SNAP) point

> unit of measurement to express the non-functional size of the software (ISO/IEC/IEEE 32430:2025, Software engineering Software non-functional size measurement, 3.1.36)

---

## Software Non-functional Assessment Process (SNAP) sub-category

> component, a process or an activity executed within the project, to meet a non-functional requirement (ISO/IEC/IEEE 32430:2025, Software engineering Software non-functional size measurement, 3.1.37)

---

## software package

> complete and documented set of software supplied for a specific application or function (ISO/IEC 19770-5:2015 Information technology--IT asset management--Overview and vocabulary, 3.44)

---

## software packager

> entity that packages or bundles software created by others

_Source: ISO/IEC 19770-5:2015 Information technology--IT asset management--Overview and vocabulary, 3.45_

---

## software piracy

> illegal use or copying of software products

_Source: ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary_

---

## software product

> set of computer programs, procedures, and possibly associated documentation and data (ISO/IEC 25000:2014 Systems and software Engineering--Systems and software product Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) -- Guide to SQuaRE, 4.31)

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 12207:2026 Systems and software engineering-- Software life cycle processes, 3.1.54_

---

## software product evaluation

> technical operation that consists of producing an assessment of one or more characteristics of a software product according to a specified procedure (ISO/IEC 25000:2014 Systems and software Engineering--Systems and software product Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) -- Guide to SQuaRE, 4.32)

---

## software project

> the set of work activities, both technical and managerial, required to satisfy the terms and conditions of a project agreement (ISO/IEC/IEEE 32430:2025, Software engineering Software non-functional size measurement, 3.1.39)

---

## software project life cycle (SPLC)

> portion of the entire software life cycle applicable to a specific project (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering--Vocabulary)

---

## software project life cycle model (SPLCM)

> framework selected by each using organization on which to map the activities of IEEE Std 1074 to produce the software project life cycle (SPLC)

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering--Vocabulary_

---

## software project life cycle process (SPLCP)

> project-specific description of the process developed by adding the organizational process assets (OPAs) to the software project life cycle (SPLC) and the OPAs (ISO/IEC/IEEE

---

## software quality

> capability of a software product to satisfy stated and implied needs when used under specified conditions (ISO/IEC 25000:2014 Systems and software Engineering--Systems and software product Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) -- Guide to SQuaRE, 4.33)

---

## software quality assurance (SQA)

> set of activities that define and assess the adequacy of software processes to provide evidence that establishes confidence that the software processes are appropriate for and produce software products of suitable quality for their intended purposes (IEEE 730-2014 IEEE Standard for Software Quality Assurance Processes, 3.2)

---

## software quality characteristic

> category of software quality attributes that bears on software quality (ISO/IEC 25000:2014 Systems and software Engineering--Systems and software product Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) -- Guide to SQuaRE, 4.34)

---

## software quality control (SQC)

> a set of activities that measure, evaluate and report on the quality of software project artifacts throughout the project life cycle (Software Extension to the PMBOK(R) Guide Fifth Edition)

---

## software quality evaluation

> systematic examination of the extent to which a software product is capable of satisfying stated and implied needs (ISO/IEC 25000:2014 Systems and software Engineering--Systems and software product Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) -- Guide to SQuaRE, 4.35)

---

## software quality in use

> capability of a software product to enable specific users to achieve specific goals with effectiveness, productivity, safety and satisfaction in specific contexts of use (ISO/IEC 25000:2014 Systems and software Engineering--Systems and software product Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) -- Guide to SQuaRE, 4.54)

---

## software quality management

> coordinated activities to direct and control an organization with regard to software quality

_Source: IEEE 730-2014 IEEE Standard for Software Quality Assurance Processes, 3.2_

---

## software quality measure

> internal measure of software quality, external measure of software quality or software quality in use measure (ISO/IEC 25040:2024 Systems and software engineering--Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)--Quality evaluation framework, 4.60)

---

## software release management

> management of the activities surrounding the release of one or more versions

---

## software reliability

> extent to which a system has operated without countable failures in a specified environment for a specified time span (IEEE 982:2024, Standard for Measures of the Software Aspects of Dependability, 3.1)

---

## software reliability engineering (SRE)

> application of statistical techniques to data collected during system development and operation to specify, estimate, or assess the reliability of software-based systems

_Source: IEEE 982:2024, Standard for Measures of the Software Aspects of Dependability, 3.1_

---

## software reliability management

> process of optimizing the reliability of software through a program that emphasizes software error prevention, fault detection and removal, and the use of measurements to maximize reliability in light of project constraints such as resources (cost), schedule, and performance

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## software repository

> software library providing permanent, archival storage for software and related documentation (IEEE 828-2012 IEEE Standard for Configuration Management in Systems and Software Engineering, 2.1)

---

## software requirement

> software capability needed by a user to solve a problem or to achieve an objective

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 41062:2024, Software engineering Life cycle processes Software acquisition, 3.1.18_

---

## software requirements analysis

> process of studying user needs to arrive at a definition of system, hardware, or software requirements

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## software requirements engineering

> science and discipline concerned with analyzing and documenting software requirements

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## software requirements management

> process of planning and controlling the identification, allocation, and flow-down of requirements from the system level to the module or part level, including interfaces, verification, modifications, and status monitoring (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## software requirements phase

> software development life-cycle phase during which the requirements for a software product, such as functional and performance capabilities, are defined, documented, and reviewed

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## software requirements review (SRR)

> review of the requirements specified for one or more software configuration items to evaluate their responsiveness to and interpretation of the system requirements and to determine

---

## software requirements specification (SRS)

> documentation of the essential requirements (functions, performance, design constraints, and attributes) of the software and its external interfaces

_Source: IEEE 1012-2024 IEEE Standard for System, Software, and Hardware Verification and Validation, 3.1.29_

---

## software requirements verification

> process of ensuring that the software requirements specification complies with the system requirements, conforms to document standards of the requirements phase, and is an adequate basis for the architectural (preliminary) design phase (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary)

---

## software safety

> ability of software to resist failure and malfunctions that can lead to death or serious injury to people, loss or severe damage to property, or severe environmental harm

_Source: IEEE 982:2024, Standard for Measures of the Software Aspects of Dependability, 3.1_

---

## software safety program

> systematic approach to reducing software risks (IEEE 1228-1994 (R2002) IEEE Standard for Software Safety Plans, 3.1.7)

---

## software security

> ability of software to protect its assets from a malicious attacker (ISO/IEC 23643:2020, Software and systems engineering--Capabilities of software safety and security verification tools, 3.22)

---

## software support

> in-service activities undertaken to establish, preserve, amend, or remove conditions and resources necessary for the intended use of a system in an operational environment (IEEE 982:2024, Standard for Measures of the Software Aspects of Dependability, 3.1)

---

## software sustainment

> activities to support, maintain, and operate a software system to help ensure that the software system or service remains operational (ISO/IEC/IEEE 14764:2021, Software engineering -- Software life cycle processes -- Maintenance, 3.1.13)

---

## software system

> system for which software is of primary importance to the stakeholders (ISO/IEC/IEEE 12207:2026 Systems and software engineering--Software life cycle processes, 3.1.55)

---

## software system element

> member of a set of elements that constitute a software system (ISO/IEC/IEEE

---

## software test environment (STE)

> facilities, hardware, software, firmware, procedures, and documentation needed to perform qualification or other testing of software (ISO/IEC 25051:2014 Software engineering -- Software product Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) -- Requirements for quality of Ready to Use Software Product (RUSP) and instructions for testing, 4.1.15)

---

## software test incident

> event occurring during the execution of a software test that requires investigation

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering--Vocabulary_

---

## software testing

> activity in which a system or component is executed under specified conditions, the results are observed or recorded, and an evaluation is made of some aspect of the system or component

_Source: IEEE 730-2014 IEEE Standard for Software Quality Assurance Processes, 3.2_

---

## software testing environment

> facilities, hardware, software, firmware, procedures, and documentation needed to perform testing of software

_Source: IEEE 730-2014 IEEE Standard for Software Quality Assurance Processes_

---

## software tool

> computer program used in the development, testing, analysis, or maintenance of a program or its documentation

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## software unit

> atomic-level software component of the software architecture that can be subjected to standalone testing

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 12207:2026 Systems and software engineering--Software life cycle processes, 3.1.57_

---

## software usage

> consumption against a software entitlement measured as defined by the terms and conditions of that entitlement (ISO/IEC 19770-5:2015 Information technology--IT asset management--Overview and vocabulary, 3.47)

---

## software user documentation

> electronic or printed body of material that provides information to users of software

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 26513:2017 Systems and software engineering--Requirements for testers and reviewers of information for users, 3.35_

---

## software version ID

> explicit and immutable version identifier (name or number) inserted into each configuration item, including each individual release, that can be used to identify the exact version of the configuration item in any instance or repository (IEEE 828-2012 IEEE Standard for Configuration Management in Systems and Software Engineering, 2.1)

---

## software-based system

> computer system controlled by software

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering--Vocabulary_

---

## software-intensive system

> system for which software is a major technical challenge and is perhaps the major factor that affects system schedule, cost, and risk (ISO/IEC/IEEE 32430:2025, Software engineering Software non- functional size measurement, 3.1.40)

---

## software/system element

> element that defines and prescribes what a software or system is composed of (ISO/IEC TR 18018:2010 Information technology--Systems and software engineering--Guide for configuration management tool capabilities, 3.12)

---

## SOI

> system-of-interest (ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288:2023 Systems and software engineering--System life cycle processes, 3.48)

---

## SOIC

> small outline integrated circuit

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765c:2014_

---

## solution

> combination of people, processes, and technologies to implement a desired capability

_Source: IEEE 7005 2021, IEEE Standard for Transparent Employer Data Governance, 3.1_

---

## solution domain

> environment in which a solution or set of solutions resides (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765e:2015)

---

## SOO

> statement of objectives

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-7:2019, Systems and software engineering Life cycle management Part 7: Application of systems engineering on defense programs, 3.2_

---

## SOP

> standard operating procedure

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765e:2015_

---

## SoPC

> system on a programmable chip

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765d:2015_

---

## sort

> symbol representing the name of a set

_Source: ISO/IEC 15909-1:2019 Systems and software engineering--High-level Petri nets--Part 1: Concepts, definitions and graphical notation, 3.33_

---

## sort generator

> generator of new sorts and operators built from a given signature (passed as a parameter)

_Source: ISO/IEC 15909-3:2021. Systems and software engineering--High-level Petri nets--Part 3: Extensions and structuring mechanisms, 3.7_

---

## sorting

> activity of sequencing of rows or records in a transactional function

_Source: ISO/IEC 20926:2009 Software and systems engineering -- Software measurement -- IFPUG functional size measurement method 2009, 3.48_

---

## SoS

> system of systems (ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288:2023 Systems and software engineering--System life cycle processes, 3.49)

---

## SoSE

> system of systems engineering (ISO/IEC/IEEE 21839:2019 Systems and software engineering-- System of systems (SoS) considerations in life cycle stages of a system, 3.2)

---

## SOTIF

> safety of the intended functionality

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 29119-11:2020, Software and systems engineering-- Software testing--Part 11: Guidelines on the testing of AI-based systems, 3.2_

---

## source address

> address of a device or storage location from which data is to be transferred (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## source code

> computer instructions and data definitions expressed in a form suitable for input to an assembler, compiler, or other translator (ISO/IEC TR 7052:2023, Software engineering--Controlling frequently occurring risks during development and maintenance of custom software, 3.41)

---

## source code organization

> arrangement of source code, including layout of code within a single file and

---

## source document

> standard or document defining an existing industry practice

_Source: ISO/IEC 19770-8:2020, Information technology IT asset management Part 8: Guidelines for mapping of industry practices to/from the ISO/IEC 19770 family of standards, 3.2_

---

## source language

> language in which the input to a machine-aided translation process is represented

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## source node

> node associated with the start of an arc

_Source: ISO/IEC 15909-2:2011 Software and system engineering-- High-level Petri nets--Part 2: Transfer format, 4.1.17_

---

## source program

> computer program that is compiled, assembled, or otherwise translated in order to be executed by a computer (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## source statements (SS)

> encoded logic of the software product (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## SOW

> statement of work

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025_

---

## SP

> SNAP (software non-functional assessment process) point

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 32430:2025, Software engineering Software non-functional size measurement, 3.1.39_

---

## space very small entity

> enterprise, organization (e.g. government agency, non-profit organization), department or project having up to 25 people that develops or maintain systems or software in the space domain (ISO/IEC 29110-1- 2:2024, Systems and software engineering Lifecycle profiles for Very Small Entities (VSEs) Part 1-2: Vocabulary, 3.97)

---

## span

> in-service interval whose start and end ticks are measurable

_Source: IEEE 982:2024, Standard for Measures of the Software Aspects of Dependability, 3.1_

---

## spawn action

> dividing action, where the enabled chains will not join

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-2:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Foundations, 13.1.6_

---

## special cause

> source of variation that is not inherent in the system, is not predictable, and is intermittent (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765c:2014)

---

## special function register (SFR)

> register used to control or monitor functions in a microprocessor

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765e:2015_

---

## specialization

> specification of targeted document type definitions that share the common output transformations and design rules developed for more general types and domains

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 26531:2023 Systems and software engineering -- Content management for product lifecycle, user and service management information for users, 3.1.28_

---

## specialize

> change by an instance from being an instance of its current class to being additionally an instance of one (or more) of the subclasses of the current subclass (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.181)

---

## specific profile group

> profile group applicable to VSEs that develop critical systems or critical software and have untypical situational factors (ISO/IEC 29110-1-2:2024, Systems and software engineering Lifecycle profiles for Very Small Entities (VSEs) Part 1-2: Vocabulary, 3.98)

---

## specific symbol

> symbol used when the precise nature or form of, for example, the process or data media is known and when it is necessary to depict the actual medium

_Source: ISO 5807:1985 Information processing -- Documentation symbols and conventions for data, program and system flowcharts, program network charts and system resources charts, 3.2_

---

## specification

> information item that identifies, in a complete, precise, and verifiable manner, the requirements, design, behavior, or other expected characteristics of a system, service, or process (ISO/IEC/IEEE 15289:2019 Systems and software engineering--Content of life-cycle information items (documentation), 5.24)

---

## specification change notice (SCN)

> a document used in configuration management to propose, transmit, and record changes to a specification (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## specification language

> a language, often a machine-processible combination of natural and formal language, used to express the requirements, design, behavior, or other characteristics of a system or component (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## specification tree

> diagram that depicts the specifications for a given system and shows their relationships to one another

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## specification-based testing

> testing in which the principal test basis is the external inputs and outputs of the test item, commonly based on a specification, rather than its implementation in source code or executable software (ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-1:2022, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 1: General concepts, 3.75)

---

## SPI

> schedule performance index (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## spiral model

> model of the software development process in which the constituent activities, typically requirements analysis, preliminary and detailed design, coding, integration, and testing, are performed iteratively until the software is complete (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## SPLC

> software project life cycle

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering--Vocabulary_

---

## SPLCM

> software project life cycle model

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-- Vocabulary_

---

## SPLCP

> software project life cycle process

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-- Vocabulary_

---

## split key

> foreign key containing two or more attributes, where at least one of the attributes is a part of the entity's primary key and at least one of the attributes is not a part of the primary key (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.183)

---

## SPMPI

> Software Project Management Planned Information

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering--Vocabulary_

---

## sponsor

> person or group who provides resources and support for the project, program, or portfolio and is accountable for enabling success (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## spool

> to read input data, or write output data, to auxiliary or main storage for later processing or output, in order to permit input/output devices to operate concurrently with job execution (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## spooler

> program that initiates and controls spooling

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## SPR

> secure processing requirement

_Source: IEEE 7005 2021, IEEE Standard for Transparent Employer Data Governance, 3.2_

---

## spreadsheet program

> program that displays a table of cells arranged in rows and columns, in which the change of the contents of one cell can cause recomputation of one or more cells based on user-defined relations among the cells

_Source: ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary_

---

## sprint

> short time frame, in which a set of software features is developed, leading to a working product that can be demonstrated to stakeholders (ISO/IEC 29110-1-2:2024, Systems and software engineering Lifecycle profiles for Very Small Entities (VSEs) Part 1-2: Vocabulary, 3.99)

---

## sprint backlog

> set of backlog items, e.g. user stories, that are selected for inclusion in a given iteration (ISO/IEC 29110-1-2:2024, Systems and software engineering Lifecycle profiles for Very Small Entities (VSEs) Part 1-2: Vocabulary, 3.100)

---

## SPS

> system performance specification

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-8:2019, Systems and software engineering Life cycle management Part 7: Application of systems engineering on defense programs, 3.1_

---

## SQA

> Software Quality Assurance

_Source: IEEE 730-2014 IEEE Standard for Software Quality Assurance Processes, 3.3_

---

## SQAP

> software quality assurance plan

_Source: IEEE 730-2014 IEEE Standard for Software Quality Assurance Processes, 3.3_

---

## SQC

> software quality control (ISO/IEC 25051:2014 Software engineering -- Software product Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) -- Requirements for quality of Ready to Use Software Product (RUSP) and instructions for testing, 4.2)

---

## SQL

> Structured Query Language

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 23026:2023 Systems and software engineering--Engineering and management of websites for systems, software, and services information, 3.2_

---

## SQO

> service qualitative objectives (ISO/IEC TS 25052-1:2022, Systems and software engineering--Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE): cloud services--Part 1: Quality model, 3.1.8.5)

---

## SQuaRE

> Software product Quality Requirements and Evaluation (ISO/IEC TS 25052-1:2022, Systems and software engineering--Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE): cloud services--Part 1: Quality model, 4)

---

## squiggle

> short S-shaped line attached at one end to an arrow label and at the other end to an arrow segment (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765m:2024, 2.1.101)

---

## SRAM

> static random access memory

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765c:2014_

---

## SRD

> system requirements document

_Source: IEEE 1012-2024 IEEE Standard for System, Software, and Hardware Verification and Validation, 3.2_

---

## SRE

> software reliability engineering

_Source: IEEE 982:2024, Standard for Measures of the Software Aspects of Dependability, 3.1_

---

## SRF

> safety-related function

_Source: IEEE 7009-2024, Fail-Safe Design of Autonomous and Semi-Autonomous Systems, 3.2_

---

## SRMPI

> softwarerelease management planned information

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering--Vocabulary_

---

## SRR

> software requirements review

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## SRS

> software requirements specification

_Source: IEEE 1012-2024 IEEE Standard for System, Software, and Hardware Verification and Validation, 3.1_

---

## SS

> start-to-start

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025_

---

## SSE

> systems and software engineering

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24641:2023. Systems and Software engineering--Methods and tools for model-based systems and software engineering, 3.2_

---

## SSL

> secure sockets layer

---

## SSO

> single sign-on

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 23026:2023 Systems and software engineering--Engineering and management of websites for systems, software, and services information, 3.2_

---

## SSOP

> shrink small outline package (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765c:2014)

---

## SSPL

> software and systems product line

_Source: ISO/IEC 26559:2017 Software and systems engineering -- Methods and tools for variability traceability in software and systems product line, 4_

---

## SSR

> software specification review

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-8:2019, Systems and software engineering Life cycle management Part 7: Application of systems engineering on defense programs, 3.2_

---

## SSS

> system/subsystem specification

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-8:2019, Systems and software engineering Life cycle management Part 7: Application of systems engineering on defense programs, 3.1_

---

## stability

> property that an object has with respect to a given failure mode if it cannot exhibit that failure mode

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-2:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Foundations, 13.6.4_

---

## stability schema

> specification of failure modes which an object will not exhibit

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-3:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Architecture, 16.2.1.1_

---

## stabilization phase

> the time interval of the measurement procedure when the RTE starts submitting tasks until the SUT reaches a stable state of operation

_Source: ISO/IEC 14756:1999 Information technology -- Measurement and rating of performance of computer-based software systems, 4.16_

---

## stable branch

> branch where stability-disrupting changes are discouraged (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## stable process

> process from which all special causes of process variation have been removed and prevented from recurring, so that only common causes of process variation of the process remain

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## stack pointer

> register that stores the address at the top of a stack (the address of the most recent program request)

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765d:2015_

---

## staff-hour

> hour of effort expended by a member of the staff

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## stage

> period within the life cycle of an entity that relates to the state of its description or realization (ISO/IEC/IEEE 12207:2026 Systems and software engineering--Software life cycle processes, 3.1.58) (ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288:2023 Systems and software engineering--System life cycle processes, 3.43)

---

## staged representation

> structure wherein attaining the goals of a set of process areas establishes a maturity level; each level builds a foundation for subsequent levels

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary_

---

## stakeholder

> individual or organization having a right, share, claim, or interest in a system or in its possession of characteristics that meet their needs and expectations (ISO/IEC/IEEE 12207:2026 Systems and software engineering-- Software life cycle processes, 3.1.59) (ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288:2023 Systems and software engineering--System life cycle

---

## stakeholder analysis

> method of systematically gathering and analyzing quantitative and qualitative information to determine whose interests should be taken into account throughout the project (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## stakeholder engagement assessment matrix

> matrix that compares current and desired stakeholder engagement levels (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## stakeholder engagement plan

> component of the project management plan that identifies the strategies and actions required to promote productive involvement of stakeholders in project or program decision making and execution (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## stakeholder equity

> degree of the share or claim a stakeholder has in the system of interest or a portion of the system of interest

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 24766:2009 Information technology--Systems and software engineering--Guide for requirements engineering tool capabilities, 3.9_

---

## stakeholder need

> prerequisite identified as necessary for a stakeholder, or a set of stakeholders, to achieve an intended outcome, implied or stated within a specific context of use (ISO/IEC 25019:2023, Systems and software engineering Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) Quality-in-use model, 3.1.27) (ISO/IEC 25002:2024, Systems and software engineering Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation

---

## stakeholder performance domain

> performance domain that addresses activities and functions associated with stakeholders (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## stakeholder perspective

> way of thinking about an entity of interest, especially as it relates to concerns

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 42010:2022, Software, systems and enterprise Architecture description, 3.18_

---

## stakeholder register

> project document that includes information about project stakeholders including an assessment and classification of project stakeholders (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## stakeholder requirements specification

> structured collection of the requirements [characteristics, context, concepts, constraints and priorities] of the stakeholder and the relationship to the external environment

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018 Systems and software engineering-Life cycle processes-Requirements engineering, 3.1.29_

---

## stakeholder satisfaction

> degree to which stakeholder needs are satisfied when a product or system is used in a specified context of use (ISO/IEC 25022:2016, Systems and software engineering -- Systems and software quality requirements and evaluation (SQuaRE) -- Measurement of quality in use, 4.20)

---

## stand-alone

> pertaining to hardware or software that is capable of performing its function without being connected to other components

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## stand-up meeting

> brief daily project status or planning meeting used in agile development methodologies (ISO/IEC/IEEE 26515: 2018 Systems and software engineering: Developing information for users in an agile environment, 3.14)

---

## standard

> document established by an authority, custom, or general consent as a model or example (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765h:2019)

---

## standard process

> set of definitions of the processes used to guide processes in an organization (ISO/IEC 33001:2015 Information technology--Process assessment--Concepts and terminology, 3.1.8)

---

## Standard Technical English (STE)

> controlled language that includes a set of writing rules and a basic dictionary for writing technical documentation (ISO/IEC/IEEE 26511:2018 Systems and software engineering-- Requirements for managers of information for users of systems, software, and services, 3.1.28)

---

## standardized profile

> internationally agreed-to, harmonized document which describes one or more profiles (ISO/IEC 29110-1-2:2024, Systems and software engineering Lifecycle profiles for Very Small Entities (VSEs) Part 1-2: Vocabulary, 3.102)

---

## standby mode

> operating mode which saves power when a microcontroller unit is not in active use (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765d:2015)

---

## standby redundancy

> in fault tolerance, the use of redundant elements that are left inoperative until a failure occurs in a primary element (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## start-to-finish (SF)

> constraint that an activity cannot finish until another activity has started

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025_

---

## start-to-start (SS)

> constraint that an activity cannot start until another activity has started

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025_

---

## starting address

> address of the first instruction of a computer program in main storage (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## state

> at a given instant in time, the condition of an object that determines the set of all sequences of actions (or traces) in which the object can participate

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-2:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Foundations, 8.8_

---

## state class

> class that represents a set of real or abstract objects that have common knowledge or behavior (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.184)

---

## state data

> data that defines an internal state of the test unit and is used to establish that state or compare with existing states

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering--Vocabulary_

---

## state diagram

> diagram that depicts the states that a system or component can assume, and shows the events or circumstances that cause or result from a change from one state to another

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## state invariant condition

> statement of constraints or relations that can be used to distinguish a subset of particular property states that all satisfy the condition

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765i:2020_

---

## state name

> unique identifier of the state of software execution

_Source: ISO/IEC 11411:1995 Information technology -- Representation for human communication of state transition of software, 2.1_

---

## state transition testing

> specification-based test case design technique based on exercising transitions in a state model

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-4:2021 Software and systems engineering -- Software testing -- Part 4: Test techniques, 3.44_

---

## statement

> in a programming language, a meaningful expression that defines data, specifies program actions, or directs the assembler or compiler (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## statement of work (SOW)

> narrative description of products, services, or results to be delivered by the project (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## statement testing

> structure-based test case design technique based on exercising executable statements in the source code of the test item (ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-4:2021 Software and systems engineering -- Software testing -- Part 4: Test techniques, 3.45)

---

## static

> pertaining to an event or process that occurs without computer program execution (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## static analysis

> process of evaluating a system or component based on its form, structure, content, or documentation (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## static attribute

> element of a hardware identification (HWID) that does not change over the life of the product or is defined at creation

_Source: ISO/IEC 19770-6:2024, Information technology IT asset management Part 6: Hardware identification tag, 3.1.7_

---

## static binding

> binding performed prior to the execution of a computer program and not subject to change during program execution (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## static breakpoint

> breakpoint that can be set at compile time, such as entry into a given routine (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## static dump

> dump that is produced before or after the execution of a computer program (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## static error

> error that is independent of the time-varying nature of an input (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## static model

> model that describes an interrelated set of classes (or subject domains) along with their relationships and responsibilities (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.185)

---

## static product

> non-executable system or software product for reviewing (ISO/IEC 25041: 2012 Systems and software engineering--Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)--Evaluation guide for developers, acquirers and independent evaluators, 4.12)

---

## static program analysis

> sub-field of formal methods concerned with analyzing the properties of software code without executing this code in the target (binary) format

_Source: ISO/IEC 23643:2020, Software and systems engineering-- Capabilities of software safety and security verification tools, 3.24_

---

## static random access memory (SRAM)

> random access memory without refresh process, which keeps data as long as it is powered on (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765c:2014)

---

## static schema

> specification of the state of one or more information objects, at some point in time, subject to the

---

## static testing

> testing in which a test item is examined against a set of quality or other criteria without the test item being executed (ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-2:2021, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 2: Test processes, 3.20)

---

## statistical process control

> statistically based analysis of a process and measures of process performance, which identify common and special causes of variation in process performance and maintain process performance within limits

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## statistically managed process

> process that is managed by a statistically based technique in which processes are analyzed, special causes of process variation are identified, and performance is contained within well- defined limits

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## status code

> code used to indicate the results of a computer program operation (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## status meeting

> regularly scheduled meeting to exchange and analyze information about the current progress of the project and its performance (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## status report

> report on the current status of the project (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## STD

> standard

_Source: IEEE 1012-2024 IEEE Standard for System, Software, and Hardware Verification and Validation, 3.2_

---

## STE

> software test environment

_Source: IEEE 730-2014 IEEE Standard for Software Quality Assurance Processes, 3.3_

---

## steering committee

> advisory body of senior stakeholders who provide direction and support for the project team and make decisions outside the project teams authority (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## step

> one element (numbered list item) in a procedure that tells a user to perform an action (or actions)

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 26514:2022, Systems and software engineering -- Design and development of information for users, 3.1.48_

---

## step-by-step instructions

> procedure consisting of steps to be followed for the safe, effective, and efficient use of a supported product (IEC/IEEE 82079-1:2019 Preparation of information for use (instructions for use) of products: Part 1: Principles and general requirements, 3.38)

---

## stepwise refinement

> software development technique in which data and processing steps are defined broadly at first and then further defined with increasing detail (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary)

---

## stimulus

> whatever causes a unit to exhibit an occurrence of a behavior pattern in the unit's repertoire; something causing or regarded as causing a response (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765i:2020)

---

## stock keeping unit (sku)

> identification, usually alphanumeric, of a particular product that allows it to be tracked for inventory and software entitlement purposes (ISO/IEC 19770-5:2015 Information technology--IT asset management-- Overview and vocabulary, 3.48)

---

## stop

> to terminate the execution of a computer program (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## stop

>

---

## storage

> functional unit into which data can be placed, in which data can be retained, and from which data can be retrieved (ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary)

---

## storage allocation

> element of computer resource allocation, consisting of assigning storage areas to specific jobs and performing related procedures, such as transfer of data between main and auxiliary storage, to support the assignments made (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## storage capacity

> maximum number of items that can be held in a given storage device; usually measured in words or bytes (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## storage efficiency

> degree to which a system or component performs its designated functions with minimum consumption of available storage (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## store

> to place or retain data in a storage device

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary_

---

## story map

> visual model of all the features and functionality desired for a given product, created to give the team a holistic view of what they are building and why (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## story point

> relative measure of the effort needed to develop a user story, compared with what is considered a typical user story by the project team (Software Extension to the PMBOK(R) Guide Fifth Edition)

---

## story-mapping

> making of a two-dimensional representation of part of the backlog that is a visual aid for the user to cut up, group and arrange stories logically in the backlog, which gives an overview of the relations between all the user stories (ISO/IEC TR 7052:2023, Software engineering--Controlling frequently occurring risks during development and maintenance of custom software, 3.43)

---

## STP

> software test plan

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-8:2019, Systems and software engineering Life cycle management Part 7: Application of systems engineering on defense programs, 3.2_

---

## straight-line code

> sequence of computer instructions in which there are no loops

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## straight-line coding

> programming technique in which loops are avoided by stating explicitly and in full all of the instructions that would be involved in the execution of each loop (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## strategic asset management plan (SAMP)

> documented information that specifies how organizational objectives are to be converted into asset management objectives, the approach for developing asset management plans, and the role of the asset management system in supporting achievement of the asset management objectives

_Source: ISO/IEC 19770-1:2017 Information technology -- IT asset management -- Part 1: IT asset management systems--Requirements, 3.53_

---

## strategic IT asset management plan

> documented information that specifies how organizational objectives are to be converted into IT asset management objectives, the approach for developing IT asset management plans, and the role of the IT asset management system in supporting achievement of the IT asset management objectives (ISO/IEC 19770-1:2017 Information technology -- IT asset management -- Part 1: IT asset management systems--Requirements, 3.54)

---

## strategic plan

> high-level document that explains an organizations vision and mission plus the approach adopted to achieve this mission and vision, including the specific goals and objectives to be achieved during the period covered by the document (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## strategy

> organization's overall plan of development, describing the effective use of resources in support of the organization in its future activities (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765c:2014)

---

## strategy artifacts

> documents created prior to or at the start of the project that address strategic, business, or high-level information about the project (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## stratified language

> language that cannot be used as its own metalanguage (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## stream interface

> interface in which all the interactions are flows (ISO/IEC 10746-3:2009 Information technology -

---

## stream interface signature

> interface signature for a stream interface (ISO/IEC 10746-3:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Architecture, 7.1.13)

---

## strengths, weaknesses, opportunities, and threats (SWOT) analysis

> analysis of strengths, weaknesses, opportunities and threats of an organization, project, or option (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## stress testing

> type of performance efficiency testing conducted to evaluate a test item's behavior under conditions of loading above anticipated or specified capacity requirements, or of resource availability below minimum specified requirements (ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-1:2022, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 1: General concepts, 3.79)

---

## strong typing

> feature of some programming languages that requires the type of each data item to be declared, precludes the application of operators to inappropriate data types, and prevents the interaction of data items of incompatible types

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## StRS

> stakeholder requirements specification

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018 Systems and software engineering-Life cycle processes-Requirements engineering, 4.2_

---

## structure chart

> diagram that identifies modules, activities, or other entities in a system or computer program and shows how larger or more general entities break down into smaller, more specific entities (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## structure clash

> in software design, a situation in which a module deal with two or more data sets that have incompatible data structures (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## structure-based testing

> dynamic testing in which the tests are derived from an examination of the structure of the test item (ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-1:2022, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 1: General concepts, 3.80)

---

## structured design

> disciplined approach to software design that adheres to specified rules based on principles such as modularity, top-down design, and stepwise refinement of data, system structures, and processing steps (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## structured program

> computer program constructed of a basic set of control structures, each having one entry and one exit (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## structured programming

> software development technique that includes structured design and results in the development of structured programs

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## structured programming language

> programming language that provides the structured program constructs, namely, single-entry-single-exit sequences, branches, and loops, and facilitates the development of structured programs (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## structured walkthrough

> systematic examination of the requirements, design, or implementation of a system, or any part of it, by qualified personnel

_Source: ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary_

---

## structured writing

> development of content elements according to a predefined and enforced organization of content elements, including metadata in specified templates (ISO/IEC/IEEE 26531:2023 Systems and software engineering -- Content management for product lifecycle, user and service management information for users, 3.1.29)

---

## stub

> skeletal or special-purpose implementation of a software module, used to develop or test a module that calls or is otherwise dependent on it

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## style

> set of language-specific editorial conventions covering grammar, terminology, punctuation, capitalization, and word choice

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 26512:2018 Systems and software engineering--Requirements for acquirers and suppliers of information for users, 3.23_

---

## sub-path

> path that is part of a larger path (ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-4:2021 Software and systems engineering -- Software testing -- Part 4: Test techniques, 3.47)

---

## sub-subcharacteristic

> subdivision of a quality sub-characteristic established by the user of a quality model to provide more granular representation of the quality attributes of a target entity (ISO/IEC 25019:2023, Systems and software engineering Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) Quality-in-use model, 3.1.28) (ISO/IEC 25002:2024, Systems and software engineering Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) Quality model overview and usage, 3.28)

---

## subactivity

> subgraph of an activity which is itself an activity and which satisfies the following condition: for any pair

---

## subclass

> specialization of one or more superclasses

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.186_

---

## subclass cluster

> set of one or more generalization structures in which the subclasses share the same superclass and in which an instance of the superclass is an instance of no more than one subclass

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.187_

---

## subclass responsibility

> designation that a property of a class is overridden in its subclasses (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.188)

---

## subdomain

> domain which is a subset of a given domain

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-2:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Foundations, 10.4_

---

## subject

> person who (voluntarily or involuntarily, or with or without awareness) interacts with the system or whose personal data has been, is, or will be acquired for engaging with or training the system (IEEE 7014-2024, IEEE Standard for Ethical Considerations in Emulated Empathy in Autonomous and Intelligent Systems, 3.1)

---

## subject area

> related collection of meta-object instance definitions (ISO/IEC 15474-1:2002 Information technology -- CDIF framework -- Part 1: Overview, 4.2)

---

## subject domain

> area of interest or expertise (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.189)

---

## subject domain responsibility

> generalized concept that the analyst discovers by asking, &quot;In general, what do instances in this subject domain need to be able to do or to know?&quot; (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.190)

---

## subject-matter expert (SME)

> person responsible for providing technical information or for checking the

---

## submit primitive

> service primitive for which the protocol object is the initiating object of the corresponding communication

_Source: ISO/IEC 14752:2000 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Protocol support for computational interactions, 3.3.12_

---

## subnetwork

> subdivision (fragment) of a project schedule network diagram, usually representing a sub-project or a work package (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765h:2019)

---

## subphase

> a subdivision of a phase. (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) - - Seventh Edition)

---

## subprogram

> separately compilable, executable component of a computer program (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## subproject

> portion of the overall project created when a project is subdivided into more manageable parts

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765h:2019_

---

## subroutine

> routine that returns control to the program or subprogram that called it (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## subroutine trace

> record of all or selected subroutines or function calls performed during the execution of a computer program and, optionally, the values of parameters passed to and returned by each subroutine or function (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## subscription-based license

> license for an entitlement that is for a limited amount of time (ISO/IEC 19770- 3:2016 Information technology--IT asset management--Part 3: Entitlement schema, 3.1.39)

---

## subsetting

> definition or creation of a data subset that includes some but not all data from its sources

_Source: IEEE 982:2024, Standard for Measures of the Software Aspects of Dependability, 3.1_

---

## substitutability

> principle stating that, since each instance of a subclass is an instance of the superclass, an instance of the subclass is acceptable in any context where an instance of the superclass is acceptable (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.191)

---

## subsystem

> secondary or subordinate system within a larger system

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## subtype

> subset of a data type, obtained by constraining the set of possible values of the data type

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## success criteria

> set of conditions to be satisfied by a process instance at completion (ISO/IEC 30103:2015

---

## successful adoption

> extent to which the use of CASE tools can measurably meet an organization's uniquely defined adoption goals

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 14471:2007 Information technology--Software engineering--Guidelines for the adoption of CASE tools, 2.1.1_

---

## succession plan

> process for identifying and developing current employees with the potential to fill key positions in the organization

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 26511:2018 Systems and software engineering--Requirements for managers of information for users of systems, software, and services, 3.1.30_

---

## successor activity

> a dependent activity that logically comes after another activity in a schedule (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## suitability

> extent to which behaviors or outcomes, or both, of a product meet (satisfy) specified quality requirements when used (ISO/IEC 25019:2023, Systems and software engineering Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) Quality-in-use model, 3.2.1.3)

---

## summary activity

> a group of related schedule activities aggregated and displayed as a single activity (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## summative evaluation

> evaluation designed to present conclusions about the merit or worth of the object of evaluation (ISO/IEC 25022:2016, Systems and software engineering -- Systems and software quality requirements and evaluation (SQuaRE) -- Measurement of quality in use, 4.21)

---

## sunk cost

> cost that is irrecoverable by future actions (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## superclass

> class whose instances are specialized into one or more subclasses

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.193_

---

## supercomputer

> class of computers that have the highest processing speeds available at a given time (ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary)

---

## supertype

> relation between type B and type A, in which every &lt;X&gt; which satisfies A also satisfies B (ISO/IEC 10746-2:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Foundations, 9.11)

---

## supervised learning

> task of learning a function that maps an input to an output based on labelled example input-output pairs (ISO/IEC TR 29119-11:2020, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 11: Guidelines on the testing of AI-based systems, 3.1.74)

---

## supervisor state

> in the operation of a computer system, a state in which the supervisory program is executing (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## supervisory program

> computer program, usually part of an operating system, that controls the execution of other computer programs and regulates the flow of work in a computer system (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## supplemental Ent

> Ent which has an &lt;entType&gt; of Supplemental (ISO/IEC 19770-3:2016 Information technology--IT asset management--Part 3: Entitlement schema, 3.1.40)

---

## supplemental information structure

> Information structure that has a subsidiary relationship to another information structure and extends the information in that information structure (ISO/IEC 19770-5:2015 Information technology--IT asset management--Overview and vocabulary, 3.49)

---

## supplementary run

> time interval of the measurement procedure from the time the measurement results fulfill the required statistical significance to the time when all tasks, which were submitted during the rating interval, are completed

_Source: ISO/IEC 14756:1999 Information technology -- Measurement and rating of performance of computer-based software systems, 4.17_

---

## supplier

> organization or individual that enters into an agreement with the acquirer for the supply of a product or service (ISO/IEC/IEEE 12207:2026 Systems and software engineering--Software life cycle processes, 3.1.60)

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288:2023 Systems and software engineering--System life cycle processes, 3.45_

---

## support

> set of activities necessary to ensure that an operational system or component fulfills its original

---

## support activity group

> activity group that is necessary to assure the successful completion of a project, but consists of supporting activities rather than activities directly oriented to the development effort

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering--Vocabulary_

---

## support manager

> role responsible for production support in the very small entity who oversees customer relationships and change management within production environments (ISO/IEC 29110-5-1-2:2025 Systems and software engineering Life cycle profiles for very small entities (VSEs) Part 5-1-2: Software engineering guidelines for the generic Basic profile, 3.36)

---

## support manual

> document that provides the information necessary to service and maintain an operational system or component throughout its life cycle (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## support software

> software that aids in the development or maintenance of other software

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## support staff-hour

> hour of effort expended by a member of the staff who does not directly define or create the software product, but acts to assist those who do

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary_

---

## supportability

> frequency, scope, and consistency of support actions to establish, preserve, and revise conditions and resources necessary for ongoing use of an in-service system (IEEE 982:2024, Standard for Measures of the Software Aspects of Dependability, 3.1)

---

## supported product

> product to which the information for use relates (IEC/IEEE 82079-1:2019 Preparation of information for use (instructions for use) of products: Part 1: Principles and general requirements, 3.40)

---

## surveillance

> systematic iteration of conformity assessment activities as a basis for maintaining the validity of the statement of conformity (ISO/IEC 29110-1-2:2024, Systems and software engineering Lifecycle profiles for Very Small Entities (VSEs) Part 1-2: Vocabulary, 3.106)

---

## survivability

> capability of a product to provide essential services in a timely manner despite the presence of attacks (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765l:2024)

---

## suspension criteria

> criteria used to (temporarily) stop all or a portion of the testing activities

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-1:2022, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 1: General concepts, 3.81_

---
