## TBS

> to be supplied

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018 Systems and software engineering-Life cycle processes- Requirements engineering, 4.1_

---

## TBSF

> time between software failure

_Source: IEEE 982:2024, Standard for Measures of the Software Aspects of Dependability, 3.2_

---

## TCP

> Transmission Control Protocol

_Source: ISO/IEC 14752:2000 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Protocol support for computational interactions, 4_

---

## TCPI

> to complete performance index

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## TDD

> test-driven development

_Source: IEEE 2675-2021, IEEE Standard for DevOps: Building Reliable and Secure Systems Including Application Build, Package, and Deployment, 3.2_

---

## TDP

> technical data package

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-8:2019, Systems and software engineering Life cycle management Part 7: Application of systems engineering on defense programs, 3.2_

---

## team charter

> document that records the team values, agreements, and operating guidelines, as well as establishing clear expectations regarding acceptable behavior by project team members (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## team performance domain

> performance domain that addresses activities and functions associated with the people who are responsible for producing project deliverables that realize business outcomes (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## team selection plan

> document specifying the qualifications, experience and training needs of project staff

---

## technical complexity adjustment

> a factor which attempts to take into account the influence on application size of technical and quality requirements, which can be used to derive the adjusted size (ISO/IEC 20968:2002 Software engineering -- Mk II Function Point Analysis -- Counting Practices Manual, 10)

---

## technical complexity adjustment factors

> the set of 19 factors that are taken into account in the technical complexity adjustment (TCA) (ISO/IEC 20968:2002 Software engineering -- Mk II Function Point Analysis -- Counting Practices Manual, 10)

---

## technical contact

> person responsible for providing an information developer with technical information about a product or for checking the technical accuracy of drafts of information for users (ISO/IEC/IEEE 26512:2018 Systems and software engineering--Requirements for acquirers and suppliers of information for users, 3.24)

---

## technical debt

> cost of deferred work to produce capabilities when they are subsequently needed

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025_

---

## technical independence

> of software quality assurance (SQA), situation in which the SQA effort uses personnel who are not involved in the development of the system or its elements

_Source: IEEE 730-2014 IEEE Standard for Software Quality Assurance Processes, 3.2_

---

## technical management

> application of technical and administrative resources to plan, organize, and control engineering functions

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 12207:2026 Systems and software engineering--Software life cycle processes, 3.1.67_

---

## technical manager

> stakeholder with responsibility for decisions relating to product content and quality achievement (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-5:2017 Systems and software engineering--Life cycle management--Part 5: Software development planning, 3.22)

---

## technical performance measure (TPM)

> measure to assess design progress, compliance to performance requirements, or technical risks and provide visibility into the status of important project technical parameters to enable effective management, thus enhancing the likelihood of achieving the technical objectives of the project

_Source: INCOSE Systems Engineering Handbook, 5th ed._

---

## technical performance measurement

> collection and comparison of technical accomplishments during project execution to the specified level of service, key performance indicators, or planned technical events and accomplishments

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765c:2014_

---

## technical product quality requirement

> product quality requirement on its technically identified properties which are used in its development and maintenance processes (ISO/IEC 25030:2019 Systems and software engineering-- Systems and software quality requirements and evaluation (SQuaRE)--Quality requirements framework, 3.19)

---

## technical requirement

> requirement relating to the technology and environment, for the development,

---

## technical review

> series of systems engineering activities conducted at logical transition points in a system life cycle, by which the progress of a program is assessed relative to its technical requirements using a mutually agreed-upon set of criteria

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-8:2019, Systems and software engineering Life cycle management Part 7: Application of systems engineering on defense programs, 3.1_

---

## technical standard

> standard that describes the characteristics of applying accumulated technical or management skills and methods in the creation of a product or performing a service

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## technique

> method and skill required to carry out a specific activity (ISO/IEC 25001:2014 Systems and software engineering--Systems and software product Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)--Planning and management, 4.5)

---

## technology roadmap

> outline of required and anticipated changes in technologies, with expected dates, which will enable achievement or transformation of a product or product family

_Source: ISO/IEC 26560:2019 Software and systems engineering -- Tools and methods for product line product management, 3.6_

---

## technology viewpoint

> viewpoint on an ODP system and its environment that focuses on the choice of technology in that system

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-3:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Architecture, 4.1.1.5_

---

## TEMP

> test and evaluation main plan

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-8:2019, Systems and software engineering Life cycle management Part 7: Application of systems engineering on defense programs, 3.2_

---

## template

> asset with parameters that can be used to construct an instantiated asset

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765j:2021_

---

## template class

> of an &lt;X&gt;, the set of all &lt;X&gt;s satisfying an &lt;X&gt; template type

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746- 2:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Foundations, 9.23_

---

## template rating module

> quality rating module in which the measurement environment and rating method are parameterized for future use (ISO/IEC 25040:2024 Systems and software engineering--Systems and software Quality

---

## template type

> of an &lt;X&gt;, a predicate defined in a template that holds for all the instantiations of the template and that expresses the requirements the instantiations of the template are intended to fulfill

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-2:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Foundations, 9.22_

---

## temporal clustering

> task-structuring criterion by which activities that are not sequentially dependent, but are activated by the same event are grouped into a task

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary_

---

## temporal cohesion

> type of cohesion in which the tasks performed by a software module are all required at a particular phase of program execution (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## term

> expression comprising constants, variables and operators built from a signature and a set of sorted variables

_Source: ISO/IEC 15909-1:2019 Systems and software engineering--High-level Petri nets--Part 1: Concepts, definitions and graphical notation, 3.35_

---

## terminal

> functional unit in a system or communication network at which data can be entered or retrieved

_Source: ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary_

---

## terminal symbol

> part of the hierarchical definition of a syntax that is not further decomposed in the hierarchy

_Source: ISO/IEC 15474-1:2002 Information technology -- CDIF framework -- Part 1: Overview, 4.2_

---

## terminating behavior

> behavior which breaks down a liaison and repudiates the corresponding contractual context and the corresponding contract (ISO/IEC 10746-2:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Foundations, 13.2.5)

---

## termination construct

> program construct that results in a halt or exit

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## termination deliver

> signal in the implicitly defined signal interface of a client computational object which has the same name and parameters as one of the terminations of an interrogation in the original operation interface

_Source: ISO/IEC 14752:2000 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Protocol support for computational interactions, 3.3.13_

---

## termination submit

> signal in the implicitly defined signal interface of a server computational object which has the same name and parameters as one of the terminations of an interrogation in the original operation interface (ISO/IEC 14752:2000 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Protocol support for computational interactions, 3.3.14)

---

## terminology management system

> software tool specifically designed for collecting, maintaining, and accessing terminological data

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 26511:2018 Systems and software engineering--Requirements for managers of information for users of systems, software, and services, 3.1.34_

---

## test

> activity in which a system or component is executed under specified conditions, the results are observed or recorded, and an evaluation is made of some aspect of the system or component (ISO/IEC 25051:2014 Software

---

## test approach

> high-level test implementation choice, typically made as part of the test strategy design activity (ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-1:2022, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 1: General concepts, 3.83)

---

## test basis

> information used as the basis for designing and implementing test cases (ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-2:2021, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 2: Test processes, 3.22)

---

## test bed

> environment containing the hardware, instrumentation, simulators, software tools, and other support elements needed to conduct a test

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## test case

> set of test inputs, execution conditions, and expected results developed for a particular objective, such as to exercise a particular program path or to verify compliance with a specific requirement

_Source: IEEE 1012-2024 IEEE Standard for System, Software, and Hardware Verification and Validation, 3.3.31_

---

## test case generator

> software tool that accepts as input source code, test criteria, specifications, or data structure definitions; uses these inputs to generate test input data; and, sometimes, determines expected results (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## test case specification

> documentation of a set of one or more test cases

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-2:2021, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 2: Test processes, 3.24_

---

## test class

> designated grouping of test cases

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-- Vocabulary_

---

## test completion process

> test management process that aims to ensure that useful test assets are made available for later use, test environments are left in a satisfactory condition, and the results of testing are recorded and communicated to relevant stakeholders

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-2:2021, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 2: Test processes, 3.25_

---

## test completion report

> report that provides a summary of the testing that was performed (ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119- 2:2021, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 2: Test processes, 3.26)

---

## test condition

> testable aspect of a component or system, such as a function, transaction, feature, quality

---

## test coverage

> degree, expressed as a percentage, to which specified test coverage items have been exercised by a test case or test cases

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-2:2021, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 2: Test processes, 3.28_

---

## test coverage item

> measurable attribute of a test item that is the focus of testing (ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-2:2021, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 2: Test processes, 3.29)

---

## test criteria

> criteria that a system or component must meet in order to pass a given test (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## test data

> data created or selected to satisfy the input requirements for executing one or more test cases

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-2:2021, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 2: Test processes, 3.30_

---

## test data readiness report

> document describing the status of each test data requirement

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-2:2021, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 2: Test processes, 3.31_

---

## test design

> documentation specifying the details of the test approach for a system, software, or hardware feature or combination of features and identifying the associated tests (IEEE 1012-2024 IEEE Standard for System, Software, and Hardware Verification and Validation, 3.1.32)

---

## test design and implementation process

> test process for deriving and specifying test cases and test procedures

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-2:2021, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 2: Test processes, 3.32_

---

## test design technique

> procedure used to create or select a test model, identify test coverage items, and derive corresponding test cases (ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-2:2021, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 2: Test processes, 3.33)

---

## test documentation

> documentation describing plans for, or results of, the testing of a system or component

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## test driver

> software module used to invoke a module under test and, often, provide test inputs, control and monitor execution, and report test results (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## test effort

> activity of performing one or more testing tasks (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software

---

## test environment

> environment containing facilities, hardware, software, firmware, and procedures needed to conduct a test (ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-2:2021, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 2: Test processes, 3.34)

---

## test environment and data management process

> test process for establishing and maintaining a required test environment and corresponding test data

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-2:2021, Software and systems engineering-- Software testing--Part 2: Test processes, 3.37_

---

## test environment item

> element of a test environment that can be considered separately from other parts of the test environment

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-3:2021 Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 3: Test documentation, 3.11_

---

## test environment readiness report

> document that describes the status of the test environment

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-2:2021, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 2: Test processes, 3.35_

---

## test environment requirements

> description of the necessary properties of the test environment

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-2:2021, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 2: Test processes, 3.36_

---

## test execution

> process of running a test on the test item, producing actual results

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-2:2021, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 2: Test processes, 3.38_

---

## test execution engine

> tool that executes test cases by passing data to the test item, triggering the test item to run and receiving data from the test item (ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-5:2024 Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 5: Keyword-driven testing, 3.19)

---

## test execution log

> record of the execution of one or more test procedures

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-2:2021, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 2: Test processes, 3.39_

---

## test execution process

> dynamic test process for executing test procedures created in the test design and implementation process in the prepared test environment and recording the results

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-2:2021, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 2: Test processes, 3.40_

---

## test framework

> structured set of principles, guidelines, and practices used for organizing, selecting, and communicating testing (ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-1:2022, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 1:

---

## test harness

> scaffolding code written for the purpose of exercising lower-level code when the higher-level code that will ultimately exercise it is not yet available (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary)

---

## test incident

> event occurring during the execution of a test that requires investigation

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119- 3:2021 Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 3: Test documentation, 3.15_

---

## test incident reporting process

> dynamic test process for reporting incidents requiring further action that were identified during the test execution process to the relevant stakeholders

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-2:2021, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 2: Test processes, 3.41_

---

## test independence

> degree to which those performing testing have separate responsibilities from those developing the test item

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-1:2022, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 1: General concepts, 3.106_

---

## test interface

> interface to the test item used either to stimulate the test item or to get responses (e.g. actual results), or both (ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-5:2024 Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 5: Keyword- driven testing, 3.21)

---

## test interface layer

> lowest level of abstraction for keywords, which interacts with the test item directly and encapsulates the atomic (lowest level) interactions at the test interface

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-5:2024 Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 5: Keyword-driven testing, 3.22_

---

## test item

> work product to be tested (ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-2:2021, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 2: Test processes, 1)

---

## test item transmittal report

> document identifying test items (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## test lead

> person who leads entire testing process

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 26513:2017 Systems and software engineering-- Requirements for testers and reviewers of information for users, 3.39_

---

## test level

> one of a sequence of test stages, each of which is typically associated with the achievement of particular objectives and used to treat particular risks (ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-2:2021, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 2: Test processes, 3.43)

---

## test log

> chronological record of relevant details about the execution of tests

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## test management

> planning, scheduling, estimating, monitoring, reporting, control, and completion of test activities (ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-2:2021, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 2: Test processes,

---

## test management process

> process used to coordinate, monitor, and control testing (ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119- 2:2021, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 2: Test processes, 3.45)

---

## test model

> representation of a test item that is used during the test case design process

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119- 4:2021 Software and systems engineering -- Software testing -- Part 4: Test techniques, 4.27_

---

## test model specification

> document specifying the test model

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-2:2021, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 2: Test processes, 3.47_

---

## test monitoring and control process

> test management process that aims to ensure that testing is performed in line with a test plan and with organizational test specifications

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-1:2022, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 1: General concepts, 3.113_

---

## test objective

> reason for performing testing

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-2:2021, Software and systems engineering-- Software testing--Part 2: Test processes, 3.49_

---

## test oracle

> source of information for determining whether a test has passed or failed (ISO/IEC TR 29119-11:2020, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 11: Guidelines on the testing of AI-based systems, 3.1.76)

---

## test oracle problem

> challenge of determining whether a test has passed or failed for a given set of test inputs and state

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 29119-11:2020, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 11: Guidelines on the testing of AI-based systems, 3.1.77_

---

## test organization

> management structure responsible for testing within an organization (ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119- 3:2021 Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 3: Test documentation, 3.18)

---

## test participant

> person who provides feedback and allows data collection to test that the information in the software documentation is sufficient to accomplish tasks correctly and form a conceptual understanding of the system (ISO/IEC/IEEE 26513:2017 Systems and software engineering--Requirements for testers and reviewers of information for

---

## test phase

> period of time in the software life cycle during which the components of a software product are evaluated and integrated, and the software product is evaluated to determine whether or not requirements have been satisfied

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## test plan

> document describing the scope, approach, resources, and schedule of intended test activities

_Source: IEEE 1012-2024 IEEE Standard for System, Software, and Hardware Verification and Validation, 3.1.33_

---

## test policy

> executive-level document that describes the purpose, goals, principles, and scope of testing within an organization (ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-3:2021 Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 3: Test documentation, 3.20)

---

## test practice

> conceptual framework that can be applied to the organizational test process, the test management process, or the dynamic test process to facilitate testing (ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-1:2022, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 1: General concepts, 3.119)

---

## test procedure

> sequence of test cases in execution order, with associated actions required to set up preconditions and perform wrap-up activities post execution

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-2:2021, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 2: Test processes, 3.53_

---

## test process

> set of testing activities performed to achieve a test objective (ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-2:2021, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 2: Test processes, 3.55)

---

## test protocol

> list of the steps to be followed in the test

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 26513:2017 Systems and software engineering--Requirements for testers and reviewers of information for users, 3.41_

---

## test readiness review (TRR)

> review conducted to evaluate preliminary test results for one or more configuration items; to verify that the test procedures for each configuration item are complete, comply with test plans and descriptions, and satisfy test requirements; and to verify that a project is prepared to proceed to formal testing of the configuration items (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## test repeatability

> attribute of a test, indicating that the same results are produced each time the test is conducted

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## test report

> document that describes the conduct and results of the testing carried out for a system or component (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## test result

> indication of whether a specific test case has passed or failed, i.e. if the actual results correspond to the expected results or if deviations were observed

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-2:2021, Software and systems engineering-- Software testing--Part 2: Test processes, 3.56_

---

## test scenario

> situation or setting for a test item used as the basis for generating test cases

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 29119- 11:2020, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 11: Guidelines on the testing of AI-based systems, 3.1.78_

---

## test script

> document specifying one or more test procedures (ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-1:2022, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 1: General concepts, 3.124)

---

## test set architecture

> nested relationships between sets of test cases that directly reflect the hierarchic decomposition of the test objectives

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering--Vocabulary_

---

## test specification

> complete documentation of the test design, test cases, and test procedures for a specific test item (ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-2:2021, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 2: Test processes, 3.57)

---

## test status report

> report that provides information about the status of the testing that is being performed in a specified reporting period

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-2:2021, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 2: Test processes, 3.58_

---

## test strategy

> part of the test plan that describes the approach to testing for a specific project, test level, or test type (ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-2:2021, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 2: Test processes, 3.59)

---

## test strategy and planning process

> test management process used to design the test strategy, complete test planning, and create and maintain test plans

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-2:2021, Software and systems engineering-- Software testing--Part 2: Test processes, 3.51_

---

## test sub-process

> test management and dynamic (and static) test processes used to perform a specific test level (e.g. system testing, acceptance testing) or test type (e.g. usability testing, performance testing) normally within the context of an overall test process for a test project (ISO/IEC 29110-5-1-2:2025 Systems and software engineering Life cycle profiles for very small entities (VSEs) Part 5-1-2: Software engineering guidelines for the generic Basic profile, 3.45)

---

## test suite

> set of test cases or test procedures (ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-1:2022, Software and systems engineering-- Software testing--Part 1: General concepts, 3.129)

---

## test target version

> specific version of test target which is used for one-time execution of Dynamic Test Execution or Code Analysis

_Source: ISO/IEC 30130:2016 Software engineering --Capabilities of software testing tools_

---

## test traceability matrix

> document, spreadsheet, or other automated tool used to identify related items in documentation and software, such as requirements with associated tests (ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-3:2021 Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 3: Test documentation, 3.26)

---

## test type

> testing that is focused on specific quality characteristics (ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-2:2021, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 2: Test processes, 3.60)

---

## test unit

> set of one or more computer program modules together with associated control data (for example, tables), usage procedures, and operating procedures that satisfy the following conditions: (a) All modules are from a single computer program; (b) At least one of the new or changed modules in the set has not completed the unit test; (c) The set of modules together with its associated data and procedures are the sole object of a testing process.

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering--Vocabulary_

---

## test-driven development

> development method in which the developers write unit tests before implementing the relevant functionality

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 7052:2023, Software engineering--Controlling frequently occurring risks during development and maintenance of custom software, 3.46_

---

## testability

> extent to which an objective and feasible test can be designed to determine whether a requirement is met

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765g:2018_

---

## testing

> set of activities conducted to facilitate discovery or evaluation of properties of one or more test items

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-2:2021, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 2: Test processes, 3.61_

---

## testing description

> description of the test execution conditions (i.e. test procedure) (ISO/IEC 25051:2014 Software engineering -- Software product Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) -- Requirements for quality of Ready to Use Software Product (RUSP) and instructions for testing, 4.11)

---

## testing pyramid

> graduated series of tests, which includes many simple and automated tests (unit tests) with less frequent integration tests and few lengthy end-to-end or manual tests

_Source: ISO/IEC 33202:2024, Software and systems engineering Core agile practices, 3.26_

---

## testing task iteration

> testing task that is re-performed during maintenance after having been originally performed during development

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## testing tool

> specific or generic tool which is used for test execution and test management such as test results recording, test results display, test results interpretation, generation of test data, generation of test procedure, generation of test scripts, test modelling

_Source: ISO/IEC 30130:2016 Software engineering --Capabilities of software testing tools_

---

## testware

> artifacts produced during the test process required to plan, design, and execute tests

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-1:2022, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 1: General concepts, 3.132_

---

## text

> data in the form of characters, symbols, words, phrases, paragraphs, sentences, tables, or other character arrangements, intended to convey a meaning, and whose interpretation is essentially based upon the reader's knowledge of some natural language or artificial language

_Source: ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary_

---

## text editor

> computer program, often part of a word processing system, that allows a user to enter, alter, and view text (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## text page

> model page that contains textual material related to a specific diagram

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765m:2024, 2.1.102_

---

## text processing

> data processing operations on text, such as entering, editing, merging, retrieving, storing, displaying, or printing (ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary)

---

## texture

> collection of common development rules and constraints for realizing the applications of a product line

_Source: ISO/IEC 26551:2016 Software and systems engineering --Tools and methods for product line requirements engineering, 3.19_

---

## theme

> user stories associated by a common factor, such as functionality, data source, or security level (Software Extension to the PMBOK(R) Guide Fifth Edition)

---

## think time

> elapsed time between the end of a prompt or message generated by an interactive system and the beginning of a human user's response (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## third normal form

> result of a normalization process that transforms groups of data so that each non-key attribute does not depend on any other non-key attribute

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765a:2011_

---

## third party

> person or body that is recognized as being independent of the parties involved, as concerns the issue in question (ISO/IEC 25051:2014 Software engineering -- Software product Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) -- Requirements for quality of Ready to Use Software Product (RUSP) and instructions for testing, 4.1.25)

---

## thrashing

> state in which a computer system is expending most or all of its resources on overhead operations, such as swapping data between main and auxiliary storage, rather than on intended computing functions

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## thread

> chain of actions, where at least one object participates in all the actions of the chain

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-1:1998 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference model: Overview, 13.1.2_

---

## threat

> state of the system or system environment which can lead to adverse effects

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765a:2011_

---

## threat agent

> entity that can adversely act on property-of-interest

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 15026-1:2019 Systems and software engineering--Systems and software assurance--Part 1: Concepts and vocabulary, 3.3.14_

---

## threat modeling

> systematic exploration technique to expose any circumstance or event having the potential to cause harm to a system in the form of destruction, disclosure, modification of data, or denial of service (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering--Vocabulary)

---

## three-address instruction

> a computer instruction that contains three address fields (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## three-plus-one address instruction

> computer instruction that contains four address fields, the fourth containing the address of the instruction to be executed next (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## threshold

> predetermined value of a measurable project variable that represents a limit that requires action to be

---

## threshold coverage

> proportion of neurons exceeding a threshold activation value divided by the total number of neurons in the neural network (normally expressed as a percentage) for a set of tests (ISO/IEC TR 29119-11:2020, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 11: Guidelines on the testing of AI-based systems, 3.1.79)

---

## throughput

> amount of work that can be performed by a computer system or component in a given period of time

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## throughput chart

> diagram that shows the accepted deliverables over time (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## throughput rate

> number of subjects that can be processed by a system per unit of time (ISO/IEC TR 29119- 13:2022, Software and systems engineering Software testing Part 13: Using the ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119 series in the testing of biometric systems, 3.1.32)

---

## throughput rating value

> quotient (corresponding to the j-th task type) of the (actual) throughput and the throughput reference value

_Source: ISO/IEC 14756:1999 Information technology -- Measurement and rating of performance of computer-based software systems, 4.25_

---

## throughput reference value

> minimum throughput required by the set of emulated users

_Source: ISO/IEC 14756:1999 Information technology -- Measurement and rating of performance of computer-based software systems, 4.26_

---

## thumbnail

> miniature image file displayed for quick identification of a larger image or video file

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 23026:2023 Systems and software engineering--Engineering and management of websites for systems, software, and services information, 3.1.22_

---

## tick

> point in absolute time or relative time, or an occurrence of an event counted as a natural unit

_Source: IEEE 982:2024, Standard for Measures of the Software Aspects of Dependability, 3.1_

---

## tier

> grouping of process definitions

_Source: ISO/IEC 19770-5:2015 Information technology--IT asset management-- Overview and vocabulary, 3.50_

---

## TIM

> technical interchange meeting

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-8:2019, Systems and software engineering Life cycle management Part 7: Application of systems engineering on defense programs, 3.2_

---

## time and materials (T&M) contract

> type of contract that is a hybrid contractual arrangement containing aspects of both cost-reimbursable and fixed-price contracts (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## time behavior

> capability of a product to perform its specified function under specified conditions so that the

---

## time class

> time limit, combined with a relative frequency corresponding to the ratio of the number of tasks (of a specific task type) with an execution time less than or equal to the corresponding time limit, to the total number of tasks (of that particular task type), used for comparison with the execution time of a task (of that particular task type)

_Source: ISO/IEC 14756:1999 Information technology -- Measurement and rating of performance of computer-based software systems, 4.27_

---

## time out

> condition that occurs when a predetermined amount of time elapses without the occurrence of an expected event

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## time Petri net

> Petri net with time where timing constraints are associated with transitions

_Source: ISO/IEC 15909-1:2019 Systems and software engineering--High-level Petri nets--Part 1: Concepts, definitions and graphical notation, 3.36_

---

## time sharing

> mode of operation that permits two or more users to execute computer programs concurrently on the same computer system by interleaving the execution of their program (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## time slicing

> mode of operation in which two or more processes are each assigned a small, fixed amount of continuous processing time on the same processor, and the processes execute in a round-robin manner, each for its allotted time, until all are completed

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## time-boxed

> having a prescribed duration limit for a project task (Software Extension to the PMBOK(R) Guide Fifth Edition)

---

## time-critical task

> task that has a firm deadline

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary_

---

## time-scaled schedule network diagram

> project schedule network diagram drawn in such a way that the positioning and length of the schedule activity represents its duration (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765h:2019)

---

## timebox

> prescribed duration limit for a project task (ISO/IEC 29110-1-2:2024, Systems and software engineering Lifecycle profiles for Very Small Entities (VSEs) Part 1-2: Vocabulary, 3.114)

---

## timeliness

> degree to which an IT service delivers outcomes within time limits (ISO/IEC TS 25011:2017 Information technology--Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)--Service quality models, 3.2.6.1)

---

## timeliness function

> description of the user requirements with respect to the execution times of tasks of a specific task type (ISO/IEC 14756:1999 Information technology -- Measurement and rating of performance of computer- based software systems, 4.29)

---

## timeliness rating value

> quotient (corresponding to the j-th task type) of the timely throughput and the total

---

## timely throughput

> throughput of all of those tasks whose execution times are accepted with respect to the timeliness function

_Source: ISO/IEC 14756:1999 Information technology -- Measurement and rating of performance of computer- based software systems, 4.3_

---

## timer event

> stimulus used to periodically activate a task

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## timer pulse unit (TPU)

> microcontroller unit to generate timed pulses

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765d:2015_

---

## timesharing

> operating technique of a data processing system that provides for the interleaving in time of two or more processes in one processor

_Source: ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary_

---

## timing

> process of estimating or measuring the amount of execution time required for a software system or component (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## timing analysis

> method to evaluate the time sequence of logic signals, or the speed of a digital circuit

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765e:2015_

---

## timing analyzer

> software tool that estimates or measures the execution time of a computer program or portion of a computer program, either by summing the execution times of the instructions along specified paths or by inserting probes at specified points in the program and measuring the execution time between probes

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## timing diagram

> diagram showing the time-ordered execution sequence of a group of tasks

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## TINA

> Telecommunication Information Networking Architecture

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-1:1998 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference model: Overview_

---

## tinderbox

> automated build and regression-testing tool (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## TLS

> Transport Layer Security

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 23026:2023 Systems and software engineering--Engineering and management of websites for systems, software, and services information, 3.2_

---

## TM

> trademark

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765h:2019_

---

## TMRR

> technology maturation and risk reduction

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-8:2019, Systems and software engineering Life cycle management Part 7: Application of systems engineering on defense programs, 3.2_

---

## TNR

> true negative rate

_Source: IEEE 1012-2024 IEEE Standard for System, Software, and Hardware Verification and Validation, 3.2_

---

## to-complete performance index (TCPI)

> ratio of the cost to finish the outstanding work to the remaining budget

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025_

---

## TOC

> total ownership cost

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-8:2019, Systems and software engineering Life cycle management Part 7: Application of systems engineering on defense programs, 3.2_

---

## TOE

> target of evaluation

_Source: ISO/IEC 23643:2020, Software and systems engineering--Capabilities of software safety and security verification tools, 3.26_

---

## token

> terminal symbol

_Source: ISO/IEC 15475-2:2002 Information technology -- CDIF transfer format -- Part 2: Syntax SYNTAX.1, 6.1_

---

## tolerable risk

> level of risk that is accepted in a given context based on the current values of society

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 15026-1:2019 Systems and software engineering--Systems and software assurance--Part 1: Concepts and vocabulary, 3.3.15_

---

## tolerance

> quantified description of acceptable variation for a quality requirement (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## tool

> software product that provides support for software and system life cycle processes

_Source: ISO/IEC 15474-1:2002 Information technology -- CDIF framework -- Part 1: Overview, 4.2_

---

## tool bridge

> component that maps and transforms the keywords to code and data to be executed by the test execution engine

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-5:2024 Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 5: Keyword- driven testing, 3.28_

---

## tool-specific information

> information associated with an object of a net graph or with the net graph itself that is specific to a particular tool and is not meant to be used by other tools (ISO/IEC 15909-2:2011 Software and system engineering--High-level Petri nets--Part 2: Transfer format, 4.1.19)

---

## toolbox

> set of tools completing each other in terms of capabilities, to cover a larger area of their intended use (ISO/IEC 23643:2020, Software and systems engineering--Capabilities of software safety and security verification tools, 3.29)

---

## top management

> person or group of people who direct and control an organization at the highest level (ISO/IEC 19770-1:2017 Information technology -- IT asset management -- Part 1: IT asset management systems--Requirements, 3.55)

---

## top-down

> pertaining to an activity that starts with the highest-level component of a hierarchy and proceeds through progressively lower-levels

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## top-down design

> design approach in which a system's functionality is decomposed from high-level concepts into lower-level pieces

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## topic

> coherent part of an information product with a heading that deals with a single subject (IEC/IEEE 82079- 1:2019 Preparation of information for use (instructions for use) of products: Part 1: Principles and general requirements, 3.43)

---

## total

> complete mapping (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.196)

---

## total cluster

> subclass cluster in which each instance of a superclass is an instance of at least one of the subclasses of the cluster (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.197)

---

## total correctness

> in proof of correctness, a designation indicating that a program's output assertions follow logically from its input assertions and processing steps (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary)

---

## total quality management (TQM)

> holistic approach to quality improvement in all life-cycle phases

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## TOV

> target of verification

_Source: ISO/IEC 23643:2020, Software and systems engineering--Capabilities of software safety and security verification tools, 3.26_

---

## TP

> Transaction Processing

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-2:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Foundations, 4_

---

## TPM

> technical performance measure

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018 Systems and software engineering-Life cycle processes-Requirements engineering, 4.2_

---

## TPR

> true positive rate

_Source: IEEE 1012-2024 IEEE Standard for System, Software, and Hardware Verification and Validation, 3.2_

---

## TPS

> transactions per second

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-9:2023, Systems and software engineering Life cycle management Part 9: Application of system and software life cycle processes in epidemic prevention and control systems, 3.2_

---

## TPU

> timer pulse unit

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765d:2015_

---

## TQM

> Total Quality Management

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765c:2014_

---

## TR

> technical requirement

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 14143-4:2002 Information technology -- Software measurement -- Functional size measurement -- Part 4: Reference model, 4_

---

## TRA

> threat and risk assessment

_Source: IEEE 1012-2024 IEEE Standard for System, Software, and Hardware Verification and Validation, 3.2_

---

## trace

> record of the execution of a computer program, showing the sequence of instructions executed, the names and values of variables, or both

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## trace link

> association between two trace artifacts or between an element of variability model and a development artifact

_Source: ISO/IEC 26559:2017 Software and systems engineering -- Methods and tools for variability traceability in software and systems product line, 3.6_

---

## trace link semantics

> purpose or meaning of the trace link, specified in the trace link types

_Source: ISO/IEC 26559:2017 Software and systems engineering -- Methods and tools for variability traceability in software and systems product line, 3.7_

---

## traceability

> discernible association among two or more logical entities, such as requirements, system elements, verifications, or tasks

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288:2023 Systems and software engineering--System life cycle processes, 3.52_

---

## traceability matrix

> matrix that records the relationship between two or more products of the development process

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## traceability with different abstraction level

> level of detail for established traceability

_Source: ISO/IEC 26559:2017 Software and systems engineering -- Methods and tools for variability traceability in software and systems product line, 3.5_

---

## traceable

> having components whose origin can be determined (ISO/IEC/IEEE 15289:2019 Systems and software engineering--Content of life-cycle information items (documentation), 5.25)

---

## trade secret

> formula, process, design, or intellectual property that is protected by non-disclosure

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## trade study

> evaluation of alternatives, based on criteria and systematic analysis, to select the best alternative for attaining determined objectives

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## trade-off

> decision-making actions that select from various requirements and alternative solutions on the basis of net benefit to the stakeholders

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 12207:2026 Systems and software engineering--Software life cycle processes, 3.1.68_

---

## trade-off analysis

> analytical evaluation of design options/alternatives against performance, design-to-cost objectives, and life cycle quality factors

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765f:2016_

---

## trademark

> symbol, word, or phrase used to denote a particular source of goods or services (ISO/IEC/IEEE

---

## trading

> interaction between objects in which information about new or potential contracts is exchanged via a third- party object

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-2:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Foundations, 13.5.5_

---

## trailer

> Identification or control information placed at the end of a file or message (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## trailing decision

> loop control that is executed after the loop body (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## training

> provision of formal and informal learning activities (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## training data

> dataset used to train a machine learning model

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 29119-11:2020, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 11: Guidelines on the testing of AI-based systems, 3.1.80_

---

## transaction

> in software engineering, a data element, control element, signal, event, or change of state that causes, triggers, or initiates an action or sequence of actions

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## transaction analysis

> software development technique in which the structure of a system is derived from analyzing the transactions that the system is required to process (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## transaction file

> file used to collect recent data changes and to periodically update a main file (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765l:2024)

---

## transaction matrix

> matrix that identifies possible requests for database access and relates each request to information categories or elements in the database

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary_

---

## transaction schema

> dynamic schema and an invariant schema defining transactions and their dependencies

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-3:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Architecture, 16.8.1.1_

---

## transaction transparency

> distribution transparency which masks coordination of activities amongst a configuration of objects to achieve consistency

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-3:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Architecture, 4.4.1.8_

---

## transactional function

> elementary process that provides functionality to the user to process data (ISO/IEC 20926:2009 Software and systems engineering -- Software measurement -- IFPUG functional size measurement method

---

## transfer

> to send data from one place and receive it at another

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## transfer file

> file containing data to be interchanged (ISO/IEC 15474-1:2002 Information technology -- CDIF framework -- Part 1: Overview, 4.1)

---

## transfer of an entitlement

> process of assigning a given entitlement to a separate legal entity (ISO/IEC 19770- 3:2016 Information technology--IT asset management--Part 3: Entitlement schema, 3.1.41)

---

## transform analysis

> software development technique in which the structure of a system is derived from analyzing the flow of data through the system and the transformations that are performed on the data (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## transient error

> error that occurs once, or at unpredictable intervals (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## transition

> activities involved in moving a new or changed service, system, or component to or from an environment

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 14764:2021, Software engineering -- Software life cycle processes -- Maintenance, 3.1.14_

---

## transition plan

> [product line] plan that describes product line transition strategy, resources required, responsibilities, detailed transition processes, major changes in product development and success measures for operationalizing the product line transition

_Source: ISO/IEC 26562:2019 Software and systems engineering--Methods and tools for product line transition management, 3.5_

---

## transition scenario

> [product line] scenario including who, what, in what procedures and orders, and how to do their roles and responsibilities for deploying product line transition strategy (ISO/IEC 26562:2019 Software and systems

---

## transition strategy

> [product line] set of plans intended to switch to product line engineering

_Source: ISO/IEC 26562:2019 Software and systems engineering--Methods and tools for product line transition management, 3.7_

---

## translator

> computer program that transforms a sequence of statements expressed in one language into an equivalent sequence of statements expressed in another language (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## transparency

> characteristic of the transfer of information to a stakeholder, which is honest; contains information relevant to the causes of some action, decision, or behavior; and is presented at a level of technicality and in a form that are meaningful to the stakeholder

_Source: IEEE 7000:2021, IEEE Standard Model Process for Addressing Ethical Concerns during System Design, 3.1_

---

## trap

> conditional jump to an exception or interrupt handling routine, often automatically activated by hardware, with the location from which the jump occurred recorded

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary_

---

## tree diagram

> systematic diagram of a decomposition hierarchy used to visualize a systematic set of rules as parent-to-child relationships

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765h:2019_

---

## tree-structured chart

> chart depicting program constructs defined in ISO/IEC 8631 and having the structure of a tree

_Source: ISO/IEC 14568:1997 Information technology -- DXL: Diagram eXchange Language for tree-structured charts, 3.1.1_

---

## trend analysis

> analytical method that uses mathematical models to forecast future outcomes based on historical results (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## triggering event

> event (something that happens) that causes a functional user of the piece of software to initiate (trigger) one or more functional processes (ISO/IEC 19761:2011 Software engineering -- COSMIC: a functional size measurement method, 2.25)

---

## triggers

> indications that a risk has occurred or is about to occur. Triggers may be discovered in the risk identification process and watched in the risk monitoring and control process. Triggers are sometimes called risk symptoms or warning signs. (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh

---

## triple bottom line

> framework for considering the full cost of doing business by evaluating a companys bottom line from the perspective of profit, people, and the planet (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## triple constraint

> framework for evaluating competing demands, such as schedule, cost, and quality (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## TRR

> test readiness review

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-8:2019, Systems and software engineering Life cycle management Part 7: Application of systems engineering on defense programs, 3.2_

---

## true negative

> correct reporting of a failure when it is a failure (ISO/IEC TR 29119-11:2020, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 11: Guidelines on the testing of AI-based systems, 3.1.82)

---

## true positive

> correct reporting of a pass when it is a pass (ISO/IEC TR 29119-11:2020, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 11: Guidelines on the testing of AI-based systems, 3.1.83)

---

## trunk

> software's main line of development; the main starting point of most branches (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## trustworthiness

> extent to which users or stakeholders have confidence that their expectations are met in a verifiable way (ISO/IEC 25019:2023, Systems and software engineering Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) Quality-in-use model, 3.2.3.2)

---

## trustworthy data

> data and related information that is accurate, complete, relevant, readily understood by and available to those authorized users who need it to complete a task

_Source: ISO/IEC 19770-1:2017 Information technology -- IT asset management -- Part 1: IT asset management systems--Requirements, 3.56_

---

## TTR

> time to repair

_Source: IEEE 982:2024, Standard for Measures of the Software Aspects of Dependability, 3.2_

---

## tunnel notation

> pair of short shallow arcs, resembling a pair of left and right parentheses characters, that bracket the arrowhead or the arrowtail of an arrow segment

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765m:2024, 2.1.107_

---

## tunneled arrow

> arrow left undrawn between its attachment to an ancestral box and its appearance as a boundary arrow on some hierarchically consecutive descendent diagram

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765m:2024, 2.1.105_

---

## tunneling

> act of applying tunnel notation to an arrow segment

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765m:2024, 2.1.106_

---

## tuple

> set of fields or data items (ISO/IEC 25024:2015 Systems and software engineering - Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) - Measurement of data, 4.37)

---

## turnaround time

> elapsed time between the submission of a job to a batch processing system and the return of

---

## turnkey

> pertaining to a hardware or software system delivered in a complete, operational state

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## turnkey system

> data processing system that is ready to use when installed and supplied to the user in a ready- to-run condition, possibly customized to a specific user or application (ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary)

---

## tutorial

> instructional procedure in which the user exercises system functions, often using sample data that is supplied with the system or information for users

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 26512:2018 Systems and software engineering-- Requirements for acquirers and suppliers of information for users, 3.26_

---

## two-address instruction

> computer instruction that contains two address fields (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## two-level address

> indirect address that specifies the storage location containing the address of the desired operand (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## two-level encoding

> microprogramming technique in which different microoperations can be encoded identically into the same field of a microinstruction, and the one that is executed depends upon the value in another field internal or external to the microinstruction (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## two-phase acquisition

> segmenting a project into an early phase that focuses on gathering requirements, addressing major risks, and project planning; and a later phase that completes the project if the outcome of the first phase is favorable (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## two-plus-one address instruction

> computer instruction that contains three address fields, the third containing the address of the instruction to be executed next (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## type

> set of values, elements, or items

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 15026-2:2022, Systems and software engineering Systems and software assurance Part 2: Assurance case, 3.1.6_

---

## TZD

> time zone designator

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 23026:2023 Systems and software engineering--Engineering and management of websites for systems, software, and services information, 3.2_

---

## UART

> universal asynchronous receiver/transmitter

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765d:2015_

---

## UAT

> user acceptance testing

---

## UC

> use control

---

## UDF

> unit development folder (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## UE

> uninstall end

_Source: IEEE 982:2024, Standard for Measures of the Software Aspects of Dependability, 3.2_

---

## UF

> uninstall failure

---

## UI

> user interface

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 32430:2025, Software engineering Software non-functional size measurement, 3.2_

---

## ULA

> Upper Layers Architecture

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-1:1998 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference model: Overview_

---

## UML
