## unambiguous

> described in terms that only allow a single interpretation, aided, if necessary, by a definition (ISO/IEC/IEEE 15289:2019 Systems and software engineering--Content of life-cycle information items (documentation), 5.26)

---

## unbinding behavior

> behavior that terminates a binding

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-2:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Foundations, 13.5.4_

---

## unbundle

> separation of arrow meanings, expressed by branching arrow segments (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765m:2024, 2.1.108)

---

## uncertainty

> state, even partial, of deficiency of information related to understanding or knowledge of an event, its consequence, or likelihood

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288:2023 Systems and software engineering--System life cycle processes, 3.39_

---

## uncertainty performance domain

> performance domain that addresses activities and functions associated with risk and uncertainty (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## unconditional jump

> jump that takes place regardless of execution conditions (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## underfitting

> generation of a machine learning model that does not reflect the underlying trend of the training data, resulting in a model that finds it difficult to make accurate predictions

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 29119-11:2020, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 11: Guidelines on the testing of AI-based systems, 3.1.84_

---

## underflow exception

> exception that occurs when the result of an arithmetic operation is too small a fraction to be represented by the storage location designated to receive it (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## understandability

> ease with which a system can be comprehended at both the system-organizational and detailed-statement levels (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## undeveloped argument

> placeholder argument to be replaced by a concrete argument

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 15026- 2:2022, Systems and software engineering Systems and software assurance Part 2: Assurance case, 3.1.7_

---

## undirected graph

> graph in which no direction is implied in the internode connections (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## unidimensionality

> existence of a single trait or construct underlying a set of measures

_Source: ISO/IEC 33003:2015 Information technology--Process assessment--Requirements for process measurement frameworks, 3.15_

---

## Unified Modeling Language (UML)

> graphical language for visualizing, specifying, constructing, and documenting an object-oriented software- intensive system's artifacts

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## uniform resource identifier (URI)

> compact sequence of characters that identifies an abstract or physical resource available on the Internet (ISO/IEC 19770-5:2015 Information technology--IT asset management--Overview and vocabulary, 3.51)

---

## Uniform Resource Locator (URL)

> mechanism for identifying resources on the Internet (such as web pages) by specifying the address of the resource and the access protocol used (ISO/IEC/IEEE 23026:2023 Systems and software engineering--Engineering and management of websites for systems, software, and services information, 3.1.23)

---

## unintended user

> person whom an autonomous/intelligent system (A/IS) creator does not intend an A/IS to be used by, but who nonetheless interacts with the A/IS

_Source: IEEE 7010-2020, IEEE Recommended Practice for Assessing the Impact of Autonomous and Intelligent Systems on Human Well-Being, 2.1_

---

## uniqueness constraint

> constraint stating that no two distinct instances of a class agree on the values of all the properties that are named in the uniqueness constraint

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.199_

---

## unit

> separately testable element specified in the design of a computer software component

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## unit (of measure)

> particular quantity defined and adopted by convention, with which other quantities of the same kind are compared in order to express their magnitude relative to that quantity (ISO/IEC 25021:2012 Software engineering--Software product Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)--Quality measure elements, 4.18)

---

## unit of measurement

> particular quantity defined and adopted by convention, with which other quantities of the same kind are compared in order to express their magnitudes relative to that quantity (ISO/IEC 19761:2011 Software engineering -- COSMIC: a functional size measurement method, 2.26) (ISO/IEC/IEEE 15939:2017 Systems and software

---

## unit requirements documentation

> license for software use as originally purchased or procured, and which can typically be linked directly to purchase records

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-- Vocabulary_

---

## unit test

> testing of individual routines and modules by the developer or an independent tester

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 7052:2023, Software engineering--Controlling frequently occurring risks during development and maintenance of custom software, 3.48_

---

## unit test framework

> environment that facilitates unit testing

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## universal asynchronous receiver/transmitter (UART)

> electronic unit used for serial communications that translates data between parallel and serial forms (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765d:2015)

---

## universal serial bus (USB)

> serial communication interface with two data lines and two power lines between a computer and peripherals

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765d:2015_

---

## unpack

> to recover the original form of one or more data items from packed data (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## unscripted testing

> dynamic testing in which the tester's actions are not prescribed by written instructions in a test case

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-1:2022, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 1: General concepts, 3.133_

---

## UNSP-SC

> United Nations standard products and services code (ISO/IEC 19770-3:2016 Information technology--IT asset management--Part 3: Entitlement schema, 3.2)

---

## unspecialize

> change by an instance from being an instance of its current subclass within a cluster to being an instance of none of the subclasses in the cluster (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.200)

---

## unstratified language

> language that can be used as its own metalanguage

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## unsupervised learning

> task of learning a function that maps unlabeled input data to a latent representation (ISO/IEC TR 29119-11:2020, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 11: Guidelines on the testing of AI-based systems, 3.1.85)

---

## UNTIL

> single-entry, single-exit loop, in which the loop control is executed after the loop body (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## unwind

> in programming, to state explicitly and in full all of the instructions involved in multiple executions of a loop (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## UOD

> Universe of Discourse

_Source: ISO/IEC 19793:2015 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Use of UML for ODP system specifications, 4_

---

## up

> pertaining to a system or component that is operational and in service (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## up time

> period of time during which a system or component is operational and in service; that is, the sum of busy time and idle time (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## updatable argument

> designation given to an operation argument that identifies an instance to which a request can be sent that will change the state of the instance (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.201)

---

## upload

> to transfer programs or data from a connected computer to a computer with greater resources (ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary)

---

## upward compatible

> pertaining to hardware or software that is compatible with a later or more complex version of itself (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## upward compression

> in software design, a form of demodularization in which a subordinate module is copied inline into the body of a superordinate module (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## UR

> user requirements

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 14143-4:2002 Information technology -- Software measurement -- Functional size measurement -- Part 4: Reference model, 4_

---

## URa

> utilize benchmarking results activity

_Source: ISO/IEC 29155-2:2013 Systems and software engineering--Information technology project performance benchmarking framework--Part 2: Requirements for benchmarking, 4_

---

## URI

> uniform resource identifier

_Source: ISO/IEC 19770-5:2015 Information technology--IT asset management--Overview and vocabulary, 3.51_

---

## URL

> uniform resource locator

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 23026:2023 Systems and software engineering--Engineering and management of websites for systems, software, and services information, 3.1.23_

---

## US

> uninstall start

_Source: IEEE 982:2024, Standard for Measures of the Software Aspects of Dependability, 3.2_

---

## usability

> extent to which a system, product or service can be used by specified users to achieve specified goals with effectiveness, efficiency and satisfaction in a specified context of use (ISO TR 25060:2023 Systems and software engineering--Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)--General Framework for Common Industry Format (CIF) for usability-related information, 3.1.1)

---

## usability analyst

> person who observes users performing tasks using the software and documentation and records the actions the user took, problems the user encountered, and comments the user made during the test; and interprets these records to evaluate the results of the testing

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 26513:2017 Systems and software engineering--Requirements for testers and reviewers of information for users, 3.45_

---

## usability defect

> product attribute that leads to a mismatch between user intentions or user actions and the system attributes and behavior (ISO/IEC 25066:2016, Systems and software engineering--Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)--Common Industry Format (CIF) for Usability--Evaluation Report, 3.17)

---

## usability finding

> identified usability defect, usability problem, or positive usability-related attribute (ISO/IEC 25066:2016, Systems and software engineering--Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)- -Common Industry Format (CIF) for Usability--Evaluation Report, 3.18)

---

## usability inspection

> evaluation based on the considered judgment of evaluators who examine the usability- related aspects of an interface with respect to specified criteria (ISO TR 25060:2023 Systems and software engineering-- Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)--General Framework for Common Industry Format (CIF) for usability-related information, 3.4.2)

---

## usability laboratory

> typically, a suite of evaluation and observation rooms fitted with video and audio equipment for recording user responses

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765a:2011_

---

## usability objective

> stated level of usability expressed in terms of effectiveness, efficiency and satisfaction in a specified context of use which can be verified (ISO TR 25060:2023 Systems and software engineering--Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)--General Framework for Common Industry Format (CIF) for usability-related information, 2.15)

---

## usability problem

> situation during use resulting in poor effectiveness, efficiency or satisfaction (ISO/IEC 25066:2016, Systems and software engineering--Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)- -Common Industry Format (CIF) for Usability--Evaluation Report, 3.19)

---

## usability test

> test to determine whether an implemented system fulfils its functional purpose as determined by its users (ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary)

---

## usability testing

> evaluation that involves representative users performing specific tasks with the system to enable the measurement of efficiency, effectiveness, and/or user satisfaction (ISO TR 25060:2023 Systems and software engineering--Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)--General Framework for Common

---

## usability walkthrough

> usability evaluation in which one or more evaluators step through a scenario playing the role of a user and identifying usability problems associated with successful completion of the scenario (ISO TR 25060:2023 Systems and software engineering--Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)-- General Framework for Common Industry Format (CIF) for usability-related information, 2.18)

---

## usage mode

> primary manner in which the documentation developer expects the document to be used

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 26514:2022, Systems and software engineering -- Design and development of information for users, 4.53_

---

## USB

> universal serial bus

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765d:2015_

---

## use

> activity that the user may perform with or on the product during its whole life cycle (IEC/IEEE 82079-1:2019 Preparation of information for use (instructions for use) of products: Part 1: Principles and general requirements, 3.45)

---

## use case

> in UML, a complete task of a system that provides a measurable result of value for an actor

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## use case diagram

> UML diagram that shows actors, use cases, and their relationships (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## use case model

> model that describes a system's functional requirements in terms of use cases

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## use case specification

> document that describes a use case (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## use error

> user action or lack of user action while using the interactive system that leads to a different result than that intended by the manufacturer or expected by the user (ISO/IEC 25066:2016, Systems and software engineering-- Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)--Common Industry Format (CIF) for Usability-- Evaluation Report, 3.20)

---

## use of IT

> planning, design, development, deployment, operation, management, and application of IT to meet the needs of the organization (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765c:2014)

---

## use-related quality requirements

> user requirements that specify the intended outcomes of use of an interactive system and associated quality criteria (ISO TR 25060:2023 Systems and software engineering--Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)--General Framework for Common Industry Format (CIF) for usability-related information, 3.3.6)

---

## user

> individual or group that interacts with a system or benefits from a system during its utilization (ISO/IEC/IEEE 12207:2026 Systems and software engineering--Software life cycle processes, 3.1.70) (ISO/IEC/IEEE 15939:2017 Systems and software engineering--Measurement process, 3.40)

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288:2023 Systems and software engineering--System life cycle processes, 3.53_

---

## user assistance

> capability of a product to be used by people with the widest range of characteristics and capabilities to achieve specified goals in a specified context of use (ISO/IEC 25010:2023, Systems and software engineering--Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)--Product quality model, 3.4.7)

---

## user controllability

> degree to which a user can appropriately intervene in an AI systems functioning in a timely manner (ISO/IEC 25059:2023, Software engineering Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) Quality model for AI systems, 3.2.1)

---

## user documentation

> information to describe, explain, or instruct how to use a system

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765k:2022_

---

## user engagement

> capability of a product to present functions and information in an inviting and motivating manner, encouraging continued interaction (ISO/IEC 25010:2023, Systems and software engineering--Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)--Product quality model, 3.4.5)

---

## user error protection

> capability of a product to prevent operation errors (ISO/IEC 25010:2023, Systems and software engineering--Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)--Product quality model, 3.4.4)

---

## user experience

> user's perceptions and responses that result from the use or anticipated use of a product, system or service (ISO TR 25060:2023 Systems and software engineering--Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)--General Framework for Common Industry Format (CIF) for usability-related information, 3.1.15)

---

## user group

> subset of intended users who are differentiated from other intended users by factors such as age, culture or expertise that can influence usability

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025_

---

## user interaction

> exchange of information between a user and an interactive system via the user interface to complete the intended task (ISO TR 25060:2023 Systems and software engineering--Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)--General Framework for Common Industry Format (CIF) for usability-related information, 2.22)

---

## user interface

> components of an interactive system (software or hardware) that provide information and controls for the user to accomplish specific tasks with the interactive system (ISO/IEC 25063:2014 Systems and software engineering--Systems and software product Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) Common Industry Format (CIF) for usability: Context of use description)

---

## user interface element

> entity of the user interface that is presented to the user by the software (ISO TR 25060:2023 Systems and software engineering--Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)-- General Framework for Common Industry Format (CIF) for usability-related information, 3.2.7)

---

## user interface task

> task that hides the details of the interface to and interacts sequentially with a human user

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## user manual

> document that presents the information necessary to employ a system or component to obtain desired results

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## user need

> prerequisite identified as necessary for a user, or a set of users, to achieve an intended outcome, implied or stated within a specific context of use (ISO TR 25060:2023 Systems and software engineering--Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)--General Framework for Common Industry Format (CIF) for usability-related information, 3.3.3) (ISO/IEC 25064:2013 Systems and software engineering--Software product Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)--Common Industry Format (CIF) for usability: User needs report, 4.19)

---

## user profile

> set of attributes that are unique to a specific user or user group, such as job function or subscription

---

## user requirements (UR)

> set of requirements for use that provide the basis for design and evaluation of interactive systems to meet identified user needs (ISO TR 25060:2023 Systems and software engineering--Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)--General Framework for Common Industry Format (CIF) for usability-related information, 3.3.4)

---

## user story

> simple narrative illustrating a user requirement from the perspective of a persona

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 26515: 2018 Systems and software engineering: Developing information for users in an agile environment, 3.16_

---

## user story point

> relative measure for the scope of user stories, used in agile development methods

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 7052:2023, Software engineering--Controlling frequently occurring risks during development and maintenance of custom software, 3.51_

---

## user system interaction requirements

> user requirements that specify the interactions (e.g. recognizing information, making inputs, making selections, receiving outputs) required by users to achieve the goals (ISO TR 25060:2023 Systems and software engineering--Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)-- General Framework for Common Industry Format (CIF) for usability-related information, 3.3.5)

---

## user terminal

> terminal that enables a user to communicate with a computer (ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information

---

## user type

> classification of emulated users that is defined by the combination of 1) the relative frequencies of the use of chain types; 2) the preparation times (mean values and their standard deviations)

_Source: ISO/IEC 14756:1999 Information technology -- Measurement and rating of performance of computer-based software systems, 4.32_

---

## user view

> functional user requirements as perceived by the user (ISO/IEC 20926:2009 Software and systems engineering -- Software measurement -- IFPUG functional size measurement method 2009, 3.52)

---

## user-based evaluation

> evaluation that involves representative users performing tasks with the system to enable identification of usability problems or measurements of efficiency, effectiveness, user satisfaction, or other user experiences (ISO/IEC 25066:2016, Systems and software engineering--Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)--Common Industry Format (CIF) for Usability--Evaluation Report, 3.22)

---

## user-defined reliability class

> documented collection of certain kinds of system behaviors whose failures are of sufficient interest to warrant specification, observation, and evaluation apart from the canonical reliability classes of safety, security, functionality, performance, and utilization

_Source: IEEE 982:2024, Standard for Measures of the Software Aspects of Dependability, 3.1_

---

## user-friendly

> pertaining to ease and convenience of use by humans

_Source: ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology - - Vocabulary_

---

## user-recognizable

> of requirements for processes or data, agreed upon and understood by both the user and the software developer (ISO/IEC 20926:2009 Software and systems engineering -- Software measurement -- IFPUG functional size measurement method 2009, 3.51)

---

## user-system interaction

> exchange of information between a user and an interactive system via the user interface (ISO TR 25060:2023 Systems and software engineering--Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE)--General Framework for Common Industry Format (CIF) for usability-related information, 3.2.1)

---

## UTC

> Coordinated Universal Time

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765l:2024_

---

## utilitarianism

> ethical decision-making approach to consider the consequences of system design and deployment (harms and benefits) (IEEE 7000:2021, IEEE Standard Model Process for Addressing Ethical Concerns during System Design, 3.1)

---

## utility

> software tool designed to perform some frequently used support function

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## utilization

> in computer performance evaluation, a ratio representing the amount of time a system or component is busy divided by the time it is available (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## utilization bound theorem

> real-time scheduling theorem stating the conditions under which a set of n

---

## V&V

> verification and validation (IEEE 1012-2024 IEEE Standard for System, Software, and Hardware Verification and Validation, 3.2)

---

## VAC

> variance at completion (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## valid

> status of an information structure that follows the specified XML Schema document and is valid from an XML perspective

_Source: ISO/IEC 19770-5:2015 Information technology--IT asset management--Overview and vocabulary, 3.52_

---

## validation

> confirmation, through the provision of objective evidence, that the requirements for a specific intended use or application have been fulfilled (ISO/IEC 25000:2014 Systems and software Engineering--Systems and software product Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) -- Guide to SQuaRE, 4.41)

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 12207:2026 Systems and software engineering--Software life cycle processes, 3.1.71_

---

## validation data

> dataset used to evaluate a candidate machine learning model while tuning it

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 29119- 11:2020, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 11: Guidelines on the testing of AI-based systems, 3.1.86_

---

## validation test

> test to determine whether an implemented system fulfils its specified requirements

_Source: ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary_

---

## value

> number or category assigned to an attribute of an entity by making a measurement (ISO/IEC 25000:2014 Systems and software Engineering--Systems and software product Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) -- Guide to SQuaRE, 4.42)

---

## value at risk

> value that is regarded as being undermined or threatened

_Source: IEEE 7000:2021, IEEE Standard Model Process for Addressing Ethical Concerns during System Design, 3.1_

---

## value baseline

> measure of a set of assets before an optimization, assigning relevant values to each group of assets being tracked

_Source: ISO/IEC 19770-5:2015 Information technology--IT asset management--Overview and vocabulary, 3.53_

---

## value bearer

> system, person, thing, action, or relationship that carries values (IEEE 7000:2021, IEEE Standard Model Process for Addressing Ethical Concerns during System Design, 3.1)

---

## value benefit

> positive state or activity fostering a value

_Source: IEEE 7000:2021, IEEE Standard Model Process for Addressing Ethical Concerns during System Design, 3.1_

---

## value chain analysis

> entire sequence of activities or parties that provide or receive value in the form of products or services

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 26511:2018 Systems and software engineering--Requirements for managers of information for users of systems, software, and services, 3.1.41_

---

## value change coverage

> proportion of neurons activated where their activation values differ by more than a change amount divided by the total number of neurons in the neural network (normally expressed as a percentage) for a set of tests

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 29119-11:2020, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 11: Guidelines on the testing of AI-based systems, 3.1.87_

---

## value class

> class that represents instances that are pure values (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.202)

---

## value cluster

> group containing one core value and several values instrumental to, or related to, the core value (IEEE 7000:2021, IEEE Standard Model Process for Addressing Ethical Concerns during System Design, 3.1)

---

## value delivery office (VDO)

> project delivery support structure that focuses on coaching teams, building agile skills and capabilities throughout the organization, and mentoring sponsors and product owners to be more effective in those roles (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## value delivery system

> collection of strategic business activities aimed at building, sustaining, or advancing an organization (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## value demonstrator

> potential manifestation of a core value, which is either instrumental to the core value or undermines it

_Source: IEEE 7000:2021, IEEE Standard Model Process for Addressing Ethical Concerns during System Design, 3.1_

---

## value disposition

> system characteristic that is an enabler or inhibitor for one or more values

_Source: IEEE 7000:2021, IEEE Standard Model Process for Addressing Ethical Concerns during System Design, 3.1_

---

## value engineering (VE)

> analysis of features, systems, infrastructure, and material selections to realize essential functions at the lowest life cycle cost consistent with required performance, quality, reliability, and safety

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765h:2019_

---

## value harm

> negative state or activity undermining a value

_Source: IEEE 7000:2021, IEEE Standard Model Process for Addressing Ethical Concerns during System Design, 3.1_

---

## value lead

> person assigned to coordinate and conduct tasks related to ethical values elicitation and prioritization and traceability of values through the requirements and design artifacts

_Source: IEEE 7000:2021, IEEE Standard Model Process for Addressing Ethical Concerns during System Design, 3.1_

---

## value list constraint

> constraint that specifies the set of all acceptable instance values for a value class

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.203_

---

## value proposition

> value of a product or service that an organization communicates to its customers (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## value range constraint

> constraint that specifies the set of all acceptable instance values for a value class where the instance values are constrained by a lower and/or upper boundary (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.204)

---

## value register

> information store created for transparency and traceability reasons, which contains data and decisions gained in ethical values elicitation and prioritization and traceability to ethical value requirements

_Source: IEEE 7000:2021, IEEE Standard Model Process for Addressing Ethical Concerns during System Design, 3.1_

---

## value stream map

> display of the critical steps in a process and the time taken in each step, used to identify waste (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## value stream mapping

> lean enterprise method used to document, analyze, and improve the flow of information or materials required to produce a product or service for a customer (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## value-added reseller (VAR)

> company licensed to repackage and support existing products, such as combined software packages (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765g:2018)

---

## value-based system requirement

> system requirement that is traceable from ethical value requirements,

---

## vanity metric

> measure that appears to show some result but does not provide useful information for making decisions (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## VAR

> value-added reseller (ISO/IEC 19770-2:2015 (corr 2017), Information technology -- Software asset management -- Part 2: Software identification tag, 3.2)

---

## variability

> characteristics that can differ among members of the product line

_Source: ISO/IEC 26550:2015 Software and systems engineering--Reference model for product line engineering and management, 3.23_

---

## variability binding

> act of determining the variant of the variation point defined in the variability model

_Source: ISO/IEC 26555:2015 Software and systems engineering--Tools and methods for product line technical management, 3.7_

---

## variability constraint

> constraint relationships between a variant and a variation point, between two variants, and between two variation points

_Source: ISO/IEC 26550:2015 Software and systems engineering--Reference model for product line engineering and management, 3.24_

---

## variability dependency

> relationship between a variation point and a set of variants, which indicates that the variation point implies a decision about the variants

_Source: ISO/IEC 26550:2015 Software and systems engineering--Reference model for product line engineering and management, 3.25_

---

## variability documentation

> detailed description of variability models being used across the member products within a product line

_Source: ISO/IEC 26555:2015 Software and systems engineering--Tools and methods for product line technical management, 3.8_

---

## variability implementation

> variability development in source codes or executable modules

_Source: ISO/IEC 26553:2018 Information technology -- Software and systems engineering-- Tools and methods for product line realization, 3.18_

---

## variability in requirements

> external and internal variability in requirements engineering

_Source: ISO/IEC 26551:2016 Software and systems engineering --Tools and methods for product line requirements engineering, 3.20_

---

## variability in space

> variation that occurs at the same time with a different shape

_Source: ISO/IEC 26555:2015 Software and systems engineering--Tools and methods for product line technical management, 3.9_

---

## variability in test cases

> variability included in domain test cases that will be bound during application testing in order to derive concrete test cases

_Source: ISO/IEC 26554:2018 Information technology--Software and systems engineering- Tools and methods for product line testing, 3.11_

---

## variability in time

> variation that occurs at different times (ISO/IEC 26555:2015 Software and systems engineering--Tools and methods for product line technical management, 3.10)

---

## variability index

> index that captures the level of variations across features, subsystems, components, products, or product lines (ISO/IEC 26564:2022, Software and systems engineering Methods and tools for product line measurement, 3.6)

---

## variability management

> managerial tasks relate to variability and has two dimensions: variability dimension and asset dimension

_Source: ISO/IEC 26550:2015 Software and systems engineering--Reference model for product line engineering and management, 3.26_

---

## variability mechanism

> variability implementation methods in a product line for supporting assembly of domain assets

_Source: ISO/IEC 26555:2015 Software and systems engineering--Tools and methods for product line technical management, 3.11_

---

## variability mechanism operationalization (VMO)

> adequate provision or binding of variability mechanisms at each specific domain or application engineering life cycle stage

_Source: ISO/IEC 26557:2016 Software and systems engineering -- Methods and tools for variability mechanisms in software and systems product line, 3.15_

---

## variability model

> explicit definition for product line variability (ISO/IEC 26550:2015 Software and systems engineering--Reference model for product line engineering and management, 3.27)

---

## variability modeling plan

> documentation that includes schedules, defined roles and responsibilities, and defined quality assurance measures that are applied to variability modelling (ISO/IEC 26558:2017 Software and systems engineering -- Methods and tools for variability modelling in software and systems product line, 3.9)

---

## variability modeling strategy

> variability modelling methodology, strictness degree of variability model validation, rules, constraints, other details for supporting the role of variability model in the whole variability management (ISO/IEC 26558:2017 Software and systems engineering -- Methods and tools for variability modelling in software and systems product line, 3.10)

---

## variability traceability

> trace links established for a variability model with both domain assets and application assets where variants are bound

_Source: ISO/IEC 26555:2015 Software and systems engineering--Tools and methods for product line technical management, 3.12_

---

## variable

> quantity or data item whose value can change

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## variable configuration item

> entity within a configuration that is variable among products

_Source: ISO/IEC 26563:2022. Software and systems engineering Methods and tools for product line configuration management, 3.9_

---

## variable cost

> cost, such as the cost of material, which is directly dependent on the rate of production (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## variable trace

> record of the name and values of variables accessed or changed during the execution of a computer program (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## variance

> quantifiable deviation, departure, or divergence away from a known baseline or expected value (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## variance analysis

> a technique for determining the cause and degree of difference between the baseline and actual performance (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## variance at completion (VAC)

> projection of the amount of budget deficit or surplus, expressed as the difference between the budget at completion and the estimate at completion (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## variant

> fault tolerance, a version of a program resulting from the application of software diversity

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## variant selection

> decision making for a choice of a variant in a variation point

_Source: ISO/IEC 26557:2016 Software and systems engineering -- Methods and tools for variability mechanisms in software and systems product line, 3.18_

---

## variation in space

> existence of different versions of a variable artefact in different shapes and used by different products (ISO/IEC 26563:2022. Software and systems engineering Methods and tools for product line configuration management, 3.10)

---

## variation in time

> existence of different versions of a common or variable artefact at different times (ISO/IEC 26563:2022. Software and systems engineering Methods and tools for product line configuration management, 3.8)

---

## variation point

> representation corresponding to particular variable characteristics of products, domain assets, and application assets in the context of a product line

_Source: ISO/IEC 26550:2015 Software and systems engineering-- Reference model for product line engineering and management, 3.29_

---

## varibility modeling plan

> documentation that includes schedules, defined roles and responsibilities, and defined quality assurance measures that will be applied to variability modelling (ISO/IEC 26558:2017 Software and systems engineering -- Methods and tools for variability modelling in software and systems product line, 3.9)

---

## VCRM

> verification cross-reference matrix (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24748-8:2019, Systems and software engineering Life cycle management Part 7: Application of systems engineering on defense programs, 3.2)

---

## VDD

> version description document

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## VDO

> value delivery office (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## velocity

> measure of a team's productivity rate at which the deliverables are produced, validated, and accepted within a pre-defined interval (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## velocity chart

> chart that tracks the rate at which the deliverables are produced, validated, and accepted within a predefined interval (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## vendor branch

> branch for keeping track of versions of imported software (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## verb phrase

> part of the label of a relationship that names the relationship in a way that a sentence can be formed by combining the first class name, the verb phrase, the cardinality expression, and the second class name or role name

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.206_

---

## verifiable

> can be checked for correctness by a person or tool (ISO/IEC/IEEE 15289:2019 Systems and software engineering--Content of life-cycle information items (documentation), 5.27)

---

## verification

> confirmation, through the provision of objective evidence, that specified requirements have been fulfilled (ISO/IEC 25000:2014 Systems and software Engineering--Systems and software product Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) -- Guide to SQuaRE, 4.43) (ISO/IEC/IEEE 12207:2026 Systems and software engineering-- Software life cycle processes, 3.1.72)

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288:2023 Systems and software engineering--System life cycle processes, 3.55_

---

## verification and validation (V&V)

> process of determining whether the requirements for a system or component are complete and correct, the products of each development phase fulfill the requirements or conditions imposed by the previous phase, and the final system or component complies with specified requirements (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## verification and validation (V&V) effort

> work associated with performing the V&V processes, activities, and tasks

_Source: IEEE 1012-2024 IEEE Standard for System, Software, and Hardware Verification and Validation, 3.1.37_

---

## verification method

> method for producing objective evidence that specified requirements of a system have been fulfilled

_Source: ISO/IEC 23643:2020, Software and systems engineering--Capabilities of software safety and security verification tools, 3.34_

---

## verification sponsor

> the person or organization that requires the verification to be performed and provides financial or other resources to carry it out

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 14143-3:2003 Information technology -- Software measurement -- Functional size measurement -- Part 3: Verification of functional size measurement methods, 3.11_

---

## verification test

> test of a system to prove that it meets all its specified requirements at a particular stage of its development

_Source: ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary_

---

## verification tool

> instrument that can be used during verification to collect information about the target of

---

## verify scope

> [Process] the process of formalizing acceptance of the completed project deliverables (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## Verilog hardware description language (VHDL)

> hardware description language used to design and verify digital circuits

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765e:2015_

---

## version

> initial release or re-release of a computer software configuration item, associated with a complete compilation or recompilation of the computer software configuration item

_Source: IEEE 828-2012 IEEE Standard for Configuration Management in Systems and Software Engineering, 2.1_

---

## version control

> establishment and maintenance of baselines and the identification and control of changes to baselines that make it possible to return to the previous baseline (ISO/IEC/IEEE 26511:2018 Systems and software engineering--Requirements for managers of information for users of systems, software, and services, 3.1.40)

---

## version description document (VDD)

> document that accompanies and identifies a given version of a system or component (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## version identifier

> supplementary information used to distinguish a version of a configuration item from other versions (ISO/IEC TR 18018:2010 Information technology--Systems and software engineering--Guide for configuration management tool capabilities, 3.16)

---

## versioning

> assignment of either unique version names or unique version numbers to unique states of software configuration items, usually for a specific purpose, such as a release of the software product to an external group or the identification of a specific baseline

_Source: IEEE 828-2012 IEEE Standard for Configuration Management in Systems and Software Engineering, 2.1_

---

## vertical microinstruction

> microinstruction that specifies one of a sequence of operations needed to carry out a

---

## very small entity (VSE)

> enterprise, organization, department, or project having up to 25 people (ISO/IEC 29110-1-2:2024, Systems and software engineering Lifecycle profiles for Very Small Entities (VSEs) Part 1-2: Vocabulary, 3.124)

---

## VHSIC

> very high speed integrated circuit

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765e:2015_

---

## video display terminal (VDT)

> user terminal with a display screen and usually equipped with an input unit such as a keyboard (ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary)

---

## video random access memory (VRAM)

> random access memory designed for the frame buffers of graphics cards

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765c:2014_

---

## view

> representation of a system from the perspective of a related set of concerns

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288:2023 Systems and software engineering--System life cycle processes, 3.56_

---

## view component

> separable portion of one or more architecture views that is governed by the applicable model kind or legend (ISO/IEC/IEEE 42010:2022, Software, systems and enterprise Architecture description, 3.19)

---

## view diagram

> graphic representation of the underlying semantics of a view

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.208_

---

## viewpoint

> specification of the conventions for constructing and using a view

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288:2023 Systems and software engineering--System life cycle processes, 3.57_

---

## viewpoint (on a system)

> a form of abstraction achieved using a selected set of architectural concepts and structuring rules, in order to focus on particular concerns within a system

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-2:2009 Information technology - - Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Foundations, 3.2.7_

---

## viewpoint correspondence

> statement that some terms or other linguistic constructs in a specification from one viewpoint are associated with (e.g., describe the same entities as) terms or constructs in a specification from a second viewpoint (ISO/IEC 10746-2:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Foundations, 3.2.8)

---

## violation

> behavior contrary to that required by a rule

_Source: ISO/IEC 15414:2015 Information technology -- Open distributed processing -- Reference model -- Enterprise language, 6.3.8_

---

## virtual

> pertaining to a functional unit that appears to be real, but whose functions are accomplished by other means

_Source: ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary_

---

## virtual address

> in a virtual storage system, the address assigned to an auxiliary storage location to allow that location to be accessed as though it were part of main storage (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## virtual machine (VM)

> pertaining to a functional unit that appears to be real, but whose functions are accomplished by other means

_Source: ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary_

---

## virtual reference

> references made to concepts other than specific meta-entities in a metamodel (ISO/IEC 15474- 1:2002 Information technology -- CDIF framework -- Part 1: Overview, 4.2)

---

## virtual storage

> storage allocation technique in which auxiliary storage can be addressed as though it were part of main storage (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## virtual system of systems

> system of systems (SoS) that lacks a central management authority and a centrally-agreed-upon purpose for the SoS (ISO/IEC/IEEE 21841:2019 Systems and software engineering--Taxonomy of

---

## virtual team

> team that is separated by geography or work schedules and maintains electronic communication (Software Extension to the PMBOK(R) Guide Fifth Edition)

---

## virtual test environment

> test environment where one or more parts are digitally simulated

_Source: ISO/IEC TR 29119- 11:2020, Software and systems engineering--Software testing--Part 11: Guidelines on the testing of AI-based systems, 3.1.88_

---

## virtue

> positive value of human conduct (IEEE 7000:2021, IEEE Standard Model Process for Addressing Ethical Concerns during System Design, 3.1)

---

## visibility

> degree to which a transaction can access object state concurrently with other transactions

_Source: ISO/IEC 10746-3:2009 Information technology -- Open Distributed Processing -- Reference Model: Architecture, 13.7.1.3_

---

## vision statement

> summarized, high-level description about the expectations for a product, such as target market, users, major benefits, and what differentiates the product from others in the market (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## visual data and information

> artifacts that organize and present data and information in a visual format, such as charts, graphs, matrices, and diagrams (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## vital business service

> service that is critical to the success of the business (ISO/IEC 29110-1-2:2024, Systems and software engineering Lifecycle profiles for Very Small Entities (VSEs) Part 1-2: Vocabulary, 3.125)

---

## VMO

> variability mechanism operationalization

_Source: ISO/IEC 26557:2016 Software and systems engineering -- Methods and tools for variability mechanisms in software and systems product line, 3.15_

---

## VMS

> video management software

_Source: IEEE 1012-2024 IEEE Standard for System, Software, and Hardware Verification and Validation, 3.2_

---

## vocabulary

> collection of information related to a specific subset of terms related to a specific domain (ISO/IEC 25024:2015 Systems and software engineering - Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE) - Measurement of data, 4.38)

---

## voice of the customer

> a planning technique used to provide products, services, and results that truly reflect

---

## volatile memory

> unit that stores data only while power is on (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765c:2014)

---

## volatility

> possibility for rapid and unpredictable change (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## volume testing

> type of performance efficiency testing conducted to evaluate the capability of the test item to process specified volumes of data (usually at or near maximum specified capacity) in terms of throughput capacity, storage capacity, or both

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765k:2022_

---

## VRAM

> video random access memory

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765c:2014_

---

## VSE

> very small entity (ISO/IEC 29110-1-2:2024, Systems and software engineering Lifecycle profiles for Very Small Entities (VSEs) Part 1-2: Vocabulary)

---

## vulnerability

> potential flaw or weakness in software design or implementation that can be exercised (accidentally triggered or intentionally exploited) and result in harm to the system

_Source: ISO/IEC 23643:2020, Software and systems engineering--Capabilities of software safety and security verification tools, 3.36_

---

## VVP

> verification and validation plan

_Source: IEEE 1012-2024 IEEE Standard for System, Software, and Hardware Verification and Validation, 3.2_

---

## W3C

> World Wide Web Consortium

_Source: ISO/IEC 19770-3:2016 Information technology--IT asset management--Part 3: Entitlement schema, 3.2_

---

## WAi

> web accessibility initiative (W3C)

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 23026:2023 Systems and software engineering--Engineering and management of websites for systems, software, and services information, 3.2_

---

## waiver

> written authorization to accept a configuration item or other designated item which, during production or after having been submitted for inspection, is found to depart from specified requirements, but is nevertheless considered suitable for use as is or after rework by an approved method (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## walk-through

> static analysis technique in which a designer or programmer leads members of the development team and other interested parties through a segment of documentation or code, and the participants ask questions and make comments about possible errors, violation of development standards, and other problems

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## WAP

> Wireless Application Protocol

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 23026:2023 Systems and software engineering--Engineering and management of websites for systems, software, and services information, 5_

---

## war room

> room used for project conferences and planning, often displaying charts of cost, schedule status, and

---

## warning

> advisory information in information for users that states that performing some action can lead to a potentially hazardous situation which, if not avoided, can result in death or serious injury

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 26514:2022, Systems and software engineering -- Design and development of information for users, 3.1.56_

---

## warning message

> safety-related information that warns about hazards and instructs on how to avoid them (IEC/IEEE 82079-1:2019 Preparation of information for use (instructions for use) of products: Part 1: Principles and general requirements, 3.48)

---

## waste

> work that adds no value to the product or service in the eyes of the customer

_Source: INCOSE Systems Engineering Handbook, 5th ed._

---

## watchdog timer (WDT)

> electronic unit that triggers a reset of an embedded system or other corrective action if the main program, due to some fault condition, fails to periodically signal it

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765d:2015_

---

## waterfall model

> model of the software development process in which the constituent activities, typically a concept phase, requirements phase, design phase, implementation phase, test phase, and installation and checkout phase, are performed in that order, possibly with overlap but with little or no iteration (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## WAV

> Waveform audio file format

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 26531:2023 Systems and software engineering -- Content management for product lifecycle, user and service management information for users, 3.2_

---

## WBS

> work breakdown structure (ISO/IEC/IEEE 16326:2019 Systems and software engineering -- Life cycle processes -- Project management, 5)

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 12207:2026 Systems and software engineering--Software life cycle processes, 3.2_

---

## WCAG

> Web Content Accessibility Guidelines

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 23026:2023 Systems and software engineering-- Engineering and management of websites for systems, software, and services information, 3.2_

---

## WDT

> watchdog timer

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765d:2015_

---

## wearout-failure period

> period in the life cycle of a system or component during which hardware failures occur at an increasing rate due to deterioration (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG)

> set of guidelines that specify how to make content accessible, primarily for people with disabilities, but also for other user agents

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025_

---

## web lead

> person responsible to a website owner for ongoing maintenance of the site's presentation and availability

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 23026:2023 Systems and software engineering--Engineering and management of websites for systems, software, and services information, 3.1.25_

---

## web page

> coherent presentation of a set of content objects and associated interaction objects delivered to users through a browser in accordance with Internet protocols (ISO/IEC/IEEE 23026:2023 Systems and software engineering--

---

## website

> collection of logically connected web pages managed as a single entity (ISO/IEC/IEEE 23026:2023 Systems and software engineering--Engineering and management of websites for systems, software, and services information, 3.1.27)

---

## website owner

> organization responsible for the site content and site design (ISO/IEC/IEEE 23026:2023 Systems and software engineering--Engineering and management of websites for systems, software, and services information, 3.1.28)

---

## website provider

> organization responsible for operation of the website and delivery of site content to users (ISO/IEC/IEEE 23026:2023 Systems and software engineering--Engineering and management of websites for systems, software, and services information, 3.1.29)

---

## weighted milestone method

> earned value method that divides a work package into measurable segments, each ending with an observable milestone, and assigns a weighted value to the achievement of each milestone

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765h:2019_

---

## well-being

> continuous and sustainable physical, mental, and social flourishing of individuals, communities and populations where their economic needs are cared for within a thriving ecological environment

_Source: IEEE 7010-2020, IEEE Recommended Practice for Assessing the Impact of Autonomous and Intelligent Systems on Human Well-Being, 2.1_

---

## well-being metrics

> subjective and objective indicators measuring both internal phenomena and external factors, encompassing the capabilities and subjective well-being approaches, and including but not limited to the domains of a) affect, b) community, c) culture, d) education, e) economy, f) environment, g) human settlements, h) health, i) government, j) psychological well-being/mental well-being, k) satisfaction with life, and l) work

_Source: IEEE 7010-2020, IEEE Recommended Practice for Assessing the Impact of Autonomous and Intelligent Systems on Human Well-Being, 2.1_

---

## what-if scenario analysis

> process of evaluating scenarios in order to predict their effect on project objectives (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## WHILE

> single-entry, single-exit loop in which the loop control is executed before the loop body (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## whitespace

> nondisplaying formatting characters that are embedded within a block of free text

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765n:2025, 3.1.210_

---

## WIA

> well-being impact assessment

_Source: IEEE 7010-2020, IEEE Recommended Practice for Assessing the Impact of Autonomous and Intelligent Systems on Human Well-Being, 2.2_

---

## wideband Delphi

> estimating method in which subject matter experts go through multiple rounds of producing estimates individually, with a team discussion after each round, until a consensus is achieved (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## wiki

> website that allows a group of users to add and edit content collaboratively (ISO/IEC/IEEE 23026:2023

---

## window

> area with visible boundaries that presents a view of a software object or through which a user conducts a dialog with a computer system

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 26514:2022, Systems and software engineering -- Design and development of information for users, 3.1.57_

---

## without prejudice

> without any loss or waiver of the data subjects rights, privileges, or conditions of employment

_Source: IEEE 7002:2022, IEEE Standard for Data Privacy Process, 3.1_

---

## wizard

> procedural form of help that guides a user through each step of a task through dialog with the user

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765k:2022_

---

## WMV

> Windows Media Video

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 26531:2023 Systems and software engineering -- Content management for product lifecycle, user and service management information for users, 3.2_

---

## word

> sequence of bits or characters that is stored, addressed, transmitted, and operated on as a unit within a given computer

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## work breakdown structure (WBS)

> hierarchical decomposition of the total scope of work to be carried out by the project team to accomplish the project objectives and create the required deliverables (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## work breakdown structure dictionary

> document that provides detailed deliverable, activity, and scheduling information about each component in the work breakdown structure (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## work effort

> labor resources required for the production of a specified output (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765a:2011)

---

## work package

> work defined at the lowest level of the work breakdown structure for which cost and duration are estimated and managed (A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK(R) Guide) -- Seventh Edition)

---

## work product

> artifact resulting from the execution of a process

_Source: IEEE 730-2014 IEEE Standard for Software Quality Assurance Processes, 3.2_

---

## work unit

> project task such as constructing or testing a function point, user story, feature, or requirement (Software Extension to the PMBOK(R) Guide Fifth Edition)

---

## workaround

> method to continue operations despite a known error

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765h:2019_

---

## workflow board

> in software development, a visual representation of work for developers who pull tasks from the task backlog; used for on-demand or resource-bound scheduling (Software Extension to the PMBOK(R) Guide Fifth Edition)

---

## working agreement

> statement of the expected working relationship between two or more parties in an agile environment

_Source: ISO/IEC 33202:2024, Software and systems engineering Core agile practices, 3.28_

---

## working metamodel

> definition of the specific meta-objects that can be instantiated in the model section of a CDIF transfer (ISO/IEC 15474-1:2002 Information technology -- CDIF framework -- Part 1: Overview, 4.2)

---

## working set

> in the paging method of storage allocation, the set of pages that are most likely to be resident in main storage at any given point of a program's execution

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering- Vocabulary_

---

## working space

> that portion of main storage that is assigned to a computer program for temporary storage of data (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## workload

> mix of tasks typically run on a given computer system (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## workload model

> model used in computer performance evaluation, depicting resource utilization and performance measures for anticipated or actual workloads in a computer system (ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary)

---

## workstation

> functional unit that usually has special purpose computing capabilities and includes user-oriented input units and output units

_Source: ISO/IEC 2382:2015 Information technology -- Vocabulary_

---

## write

> to record data in a storage device or on a data medium

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765:2017 Systems and software engineering-Vocabulary_

---

## write (-type)

> a data movement type that moves a data group lying inside the functional process to persistent storage (ISO/IEC 19761:2011 Software engineering -- COSMIC: a functional size measurement method, 3.26)

---

## writer

> person designing or developing user documentation (ISO/IEC/IEEE 26515: 2018 Systems and software engineering: Developing information for users in an agile environment, 4.17)

---

## writing reference

> data storage entity or other record, or interface record to another software or system to which data is written in a BFC (ISO/IEC 29881:2010 Information technology--Software and systems engineering--FiSMA 1.1

---

## XAI

> explainable artificial intelligence

_Source: IEEE 1012-2024 IEEE Standard for System, Software, and Hardware Verification and Validation, 3.2_

---

## XFN

> X/Open Federated Naming

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765i:2020_

---

## XHTML

> Extended HyperText Markup Language

---

## XLIFF

> XML Localization Interchange File Format

---

## XMI

> XML Metadata Interchange

## XML

> extensible markup language

_Source: ISO/IEC 15909-2:2011 Software and system engineering--High-level Petri nets-- Part 2: Transfer format, 4.2.9_

---

## XML schema definition

> XML-based language that specifies a set of rules and structure for the creation of XML documents

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 26531:2023 Systems and software engineering -- Content management for product lifecycle, user and service management information for users, 3.1.33_

---

## XML schema document (XSD)

> document that describes the structure of XML information

---

## XP

> extreme programming

---

## XSD

> XML schema definition

_Source: ISO/IEC/IEEE 24765e:2015_

---

## XSL-FO

> XML Stylesheet Language-Formatting Objects

## XSLT

> extensible stylesheet language transformation

---

## yesterday's weather

> a report of work performance in the most recent reporting period (Software Extension to the PMBOK(R) Guide Fifth Edition)
